  - **NPSH** unique calculation — liquid level distance from impeller.

### IIII. FCAW — Flux-Cored Arc Welding (pipe outdoor / windy conditions)
- **FCAW (Flux-Cored Arc Welding)** = arc welding z **drutem rurkowatym wypełnionym flux'em** (zamiast solid wire jak w GMAW). High deposition rate, deep penetration, all-position capable.
- **Dwa typy**:
  - **FCAW-S (Self-Shielded)** — drut sam generuje protective gas (flux się rozkłada w łuku, emituje CO₂ + dymy fluorków). **Brak zewnętrznego cylinder'a gazu**.
    - **Standard dla outdoor work, windy conditions, pipeline construction**.
    - Wire: AWS A5.20 E71T-11, E71T-GS, E71T-8 (najpopularniejsze).
    - **Słabsze mechanical properties** niż FCAW-G (większa porowatość, dym z fluorków).
  - **FCAW-G (Gas-Shielded)** — drut z flux'em + zewn. shielding gas (CO₂ lub 75/25 Ar-CO₂).
    - **Wnętrze hali / wind-free**. Słabsze poza halą.
    - Wire: AWS A5.20 E71T-1, E71T-9, E80T-1 (high strength).
    - **Lepsza jakość spawania** — głębsza penetracja, cleaner welds, lower porosity.
- **Pipeline applications**:
  - **Mainline cross-country pipelines** — często FCAW-S downhill na fill + cap pas'ach (root pass często SMAW E6010 cellulose, hot pass + fill FCAW-S).
  - **Field structural welding** (pipe rack supports, vessel field weld).
  - **Repair welds offshore** — gdzie wind > 5 km/h.
- **Outdoor windy performance**:
  - **Wind tolerance**:
    - **GTAW**: 0-2 km/h. Powyżej = sugar, porosity, contamination.
    - **GMAW**: 5-10 km/h. Powyżej = inadequate gas coverage.
    - **FCAW-G**: 10-15 km/h. Above that lose gas envelope.
    - **FCAW-S**: **30+ km/h** możliwe (self-shielding niezniszczalne).
    - **SMAW (stick)**: 30+ km/h (no gas to blow away).
  - **Pop tent / wind screen** — wymagane dla critical FCAW-G outdoors.
- **Deposition rates** (kg/h spoinowanej masy):
  - **SMAW**: 1.0-2.5 kg/h.
  - **GMAW**: 1.5-4.0 kg/h.
  - **FCAW-S**: 2.0-5.0 kg/h.
  - **FCAW-G**: 2.5-7.0 kg/h.
  - **SAW**: 5-25 kg/h (najszybsze).
- **Process parameters** (typical FCAW-G E71T-1 1.2 mm wire on 6 mm steel):
  - **Voltage**: 22-28 V.
  - **Current**: 150-250 A.
  - **Wire feed speed**: 4-9 m/min.
  - **Travel speed**: 30-50 cm/min.
- **Gun setup**:
  - **Contact tip** — wire connector, regular replacement (1-3 days production).
  - **Nozzle** (FCAW-G) — gas envelope shape. Recess type 5-10 mm.
  - **Anti-spatter spray / nozzle gel** — chroni nozzle od spatter buildup.
- **All-position capability**:
  - **FCAW-S all-position** (E71T-8, E71T-11) — out-of-position (vertical, overhead).
  - **FCAW-G all-position** (T-1, T-9) — wymaga special wires lub pulse mode.
  - **Flat / horizontal only** wires (E70T-1) — production rates higher ale tylko 1G/2G.
- **Wire diameter selection**:
  - **0.9 mm (0.035")**: thin material, all-position.
  - **1.2 mm (0.045")**: STANDARD, większość applications.
  - **1.6 mm (1/16")**: heavy plate, flat position.
  - **2.4 mm (3/32")**: SAW-replacement, mass production.
- **Quality concerns**:
  - **Porosity** — FCAW-S najczęściej; FCAW-G lepiej (proper gas coverage).
  - **Slag inclusions** — between passes; thorough inter-pass cleaning konieczny.
  - **Spatter** — FCAW spatters more niż MIG; nozzle/work piece protection.
  - **Worm holes (gas pockets)** — przepoczwarczenie w pulsed mode (very fine-grained issues).
- **Health & safety**:
  - **Fume rate**: FCAW = **2-3× wyższe** niż SMAW. Manganese particularly wysokie.
  - **Local exhaust ventilation MANDATORY** (patrz iter 16).
  - **PAPR respirator** zalecane dla all-day operations.
  - **Hexavalent chromium** w SS FCAW jak normal (no different than SMAW).

---

## Iteration 29 — 2026-05-25

### JJJJ. CAC-A (Air Carbon Arc Gouging) — back grooving, defect removal
- **CAC-A (Air Carbon Arc Cutting / Gouging)** = wycinanie / wyrównywanie metalu przy użyciu **elektroda węglowa + łuk + sprężone powietrze**. Łuk topi metal, sprężone powietrze wydmuchuje stopiwo z rowka.
- **Główne zastosowania na pipie**:
  - **Back gouging** root pas'a po welding'u jednostronnym (single-V joint) — wycinanie wad root + przygotowanie pod re-weld z drugiej strony.
  - **Defect removal** — wybranie cracks, porosity, slag inclusions z istniejącej spoiny.
  - **Bevel preparation** w field (gdy brak fazowarki).
  - **Cutting plate / pipe** w awaryjnych sytuacjach.
  - **Removal of weld attachments** (lugs, attachments, tack welds).
- **Sprzęt**:
  - **Carbon-graphite electrode** (kopalny węglowy pręt + grafit binder). Średnice **3-19 mm** typowo, długość **300 mm**.
  - **Air supply** — sprężarka 6-7 bar (90-100 psi), min **0.5-2 m³/min** flow (zależy od electrode size).
  - **Power source** — DC welder (200-1500 A) z constant current characteristic. Najczęściej **DC+ (DCEP)** — elektroda dodatnia.
  - **Gouging torch** — uchwyt z gwałtem do air valve + elektroda holder.
- **Typy elektrod**:
  - **DCCP (DC Copper-Plated)** — **NAJPOPULARNIEJSZE**. Pokryte miedzią dla:
    - Dłuższego życia electrod.
    - Stabilnego arc.
    - Uniform groove.
  - **DC Plain** — bez miedzi, tańsze ale szybciej się zużywają.
  - **AC Coated** — dla AC welders (rzadko używane).
  - **Jointed (rounded)** — z łącznikiem stub-to-stub dla long continuous gouging.
- **Amperage selection** (rule of thumb): **125 A per 1/8" (3.2 mm) electrode diameter**.

| Electrode diameter | Amperage range (DCEP) | Air flow |
|--------------------|----------------------:|---------:|
| 3.2 mm (1/8") | 90-150 A | 0.4 m³/min |
| 4.8 mm (3/16") | 200-250 A | 0.5 m³/min |
| 6.4 mm (1/4") | 300-400 A | 0.7 m³/min |
| 9.5 mm (3/8") | 400-700 A | 1.0 m³/min |
| 12.7 mm (1/2") | 600-1000 A | 1.3 m³/min |
| 19 mm (3/4") | 1000-1600 A | 2.0 m³/min |

- **Procedure** krok-po-kroku:
  1. **Pre-mark** linię gouging na pipe surface — soapstone, kreda.
  2. **Setup**: DC welder na CC (constant current), DCEP, amperage per electrode size.
  3. **Air on FIRST** — sprężone powietrze ZAWSZE włączone PRZED dotknięciem electrody do work piece.
  4. **Strike arc** — short tap-and-hold (jak SMAW), ale na **45-60° kąt** do work piece (NIE prostopadle!).
  5. **Travel** — w direction electrody pointed (electrode push, NOT pull). Travel speed **30-50 cm/min** dla średniego gouge.
  6. **Maintain stick-out** — electrode wystaje ~75-100 mm z holder'a; w miarę zużycia, posuwać dalej.
  7. **Air OFF LAST** — najpierw lift electrode, potem air off (zabezpieczenie krater'a).
- **Critical safety rules**:
  - **Ear protection MANDATORY** — CAC-A jest **EKSTREMALNIE HAŁAŚLIWE** (do 110-115 dB).
  - **Spark zone**: HUGE — odpryski roztopionego metalu sypią się 5-10 m w direction'ie gouging. Fire watch + spark zone clearance.
  - **Combustibles** — usunięte 10+ m radius.
  - **Eye protection** — shade 10-12 dla CAC-A high amperage (jaśniejsze niż welding!).
  - **Confined space** — extra ventilation, dymy carbon monoxide + metal oxide intensywne.
  - **PAPR respirator** — recommended.
- **Po gouging — preparation for re-welding**:
  1. **Grinding** rowka — usunięcie carbonized layer (cienka warstwa nawęglona ze electrody i powietrza).
  2. **MT lub PT** — sprawdzić czy nie ma residual defects po gouging.
  3. **Profile inspection** — rowek musi mieć **UNIFORM depth + smooth surface**, brak ostrych krawędzi (stress risers).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Carbon depositi w groove** — niedostateczne grinding po gouging → carbonized weld w re-weld = brittle.
  - **Insufficient air** — molten metal NIE wydmuchany, sticky groove.
  - **Wrong polarity** — DCEN spowoduje burn-up electrode bez efektu cutting.
  - **Electrode angle za płaski** — szeroki rowek, niedostateczna głębokość.
  - **Electrode angle za stromy** — wąski głęboki rowek, trudne dla welding'u (sliv-grooves).
- **CAC-A vs plasma cutting vs grinding**:
  - **CAC-A**: fast removal, dla pipe ≥ 6 mm wall, można field. **Loud + dirty**.
  - **Plasma**: cleaner cut, ale dla cutting (not gouging usually).
  - **Grinding**: slow ale precise, dla finishing po CAC-A.
  - **Reguła**: **CAC-A dla speed** removal → **grinder dla finish**.

### KKKK. Cold Spring (Cold Pull) — pre-stress installation
- **Cold spring (cold pull)** = celowe **wstępne odkształcenie** rurociągu PRZED uruchomieniem operacyjnym — kompensuje POŁOWĘ przewidywanej termicznej ekspansji.
- **Cel**:
  - **Redukcja naprężeń operacyjnych** o ~50% — pipe rośnie z cold position do hot, ale punkt 0-stress jest "w środku" cyklu.
  - **Mniejsze siły na equipment nozzles** (pump, vessel, turbine) — pomp / vessel manufacturer'om wystarcza mniejszy nozzle reinforcement.
  - **Tańsze structural supports** — anchor'y nie muszą wytrzymywać full thermal force.
- **Procedura general**:
  1. **Engineering calculation** — software (CAESAR II) oblicza thermal expansion w punkcie tie-in.
  2. **Cold spring magnitude**: typowo **50% expected expansion** (np. expansion 200 mm → cold spring 100 mm).
  3. **Identification cut point** — gdzie zostawiamy gap w rurociągu (typowo blisko anchor lub equipment).
  4. **Cut pipe shorter** — odcinek "skrócony" o cold spring magnitude (cut short approach), lub:
  5. **Forcefully pull pipes apart** (force overlap approach) — ekstra-długi pipe naciągamy lub kompresujemy.
  6. **Hold under tension** (chain hoist / come-along / hydraulic ram) podczas welding'u final joint.
  7. **Weld in cold-stressed position** — full WPS welding, MT/PT/RT.
  8. **Release tension** AFTER welding — pipe pozostaje w pre-stressed state.
- **B31.3 §319** rules:
  - **Cold spring NOT counted** w **stress range calculation** — uznawane tylko 50-66% jako reduction w **equipment loads** (because field execution rarely perfect).
  - **Maximum cold spring**: typowo ≤ 100% expansion (nigdy więcej, bo by reverse stress).
  - **Heat treatment** (PWHT) — wykonywać po cold spring + welding (sequence: install → cold spring → weld → PWHT → release tension).
- **3 types of cold spring**:
  - **Cut Short**: pipe odcinany **o cold spring amount krótszy** niż between anchor distance. Łączy się z forcefully extending pipe (chain hoist).
  - **Cut Long**: pipe **dłuższy** niż between anchors. Compressowanego do match.
  - **Combined X/Y/Z directional**: dla complex pipe runs (loop + offset + rise). Każda direction has unique pre-load.
- **Tools używane**:
  - **Chain hoist (come-along)** — manualny, 1-10 ton capacity. Tania metoda dla małych pipe.
  - **Hydraulic ram** — 10-50 ton, dla larger pipe / harder pulls.
  - **Spring-loaded** — calibrated load monitoring.
  - **Strain gauges** — track real-time stress during welding (high-precision installations).
- **Critical considerations**:
  - **Cold spring + bolting** — dla flange joint: gradually tighten bolts WHILE applying load. Stress redistributed evenly.
  - **Welding joint w cold spring** — heat input distorts pipe, finishing positions matter. Rotation between passes redistributes.
  - **Documentation** — log force / displacement / weld passes. Audit-able.
- **Wad** cold spring:
  - **Field execution variability** — różne pipefitters dają różne stress.
  - **Long-term creep** — high-T pipe może "set" w pre-stressed position over years (zerowy benefit po creep).
  - **Code conservatism** — kody dopuszczają tylko 2/3 cold spring credit (musi uwzględnić uncertainty).
- **Alternatives** do cold spring:
  - **Expansion loops / bellows** — passive flexibility (patrz iter 7).
  - **Larger pipe stress range** capacity — increased wall thickness lub material upgrade.
  - **Equipment nozzle reinforcement** — pad reinforced nozzle (większy local stress capacity).
- **Application examples**:
  - **Steam main from boiler do turbine** — 100-200 m, ΔT 400°C, expansion ~600 mm. Cold spring 300 mm reduces forces 50%.
  - **Reactor outlet to fractionation column** — high T, vibration. Cold spring eliminuje cyclic anchor loads.
  - **Heat exchanger tube bundle removal** — anchored to floor with cold spring; release tension to remove bundle.

### LLLL. Tank Farm Piping — manifolds, foam fire lines, crude storage
- **Tank farm** = klastry storage tanks (raczej crude oil, refined products, chemicals) z networkiem rurociągów przesyłowych, manifold'ów + fire protection. KRYTYCZNA infrastruktura w terminalach naftowych, refineries, chemical plants.
- **Layout principles**:
  - **Tanks w bunds** (containment dikes) — bermy ziemne lub betonowe wały otaczające tanks. Volume ≥ **110% największego tanku** wewnątrz (NFPA 30, EN 14015).
  - **Piping OUTSIDE bunds** — wszystkie rurociągi POZA earthen berms (NIE wewnątrz). Powód: spillage z bund'a wciągnęłaby pipe'y w fire (CUI burst + thermal damage).
  - **Manifold area** zazwyczaj na obrzeżach tank farm, z dostępem dla maintenance + future expansion.
  - **Above-ground installation** preferowana — easy inspection, no underground corrosion, easy modification.
- **Piping types w tank farm**:
  - **Product piping** (transferring crude/products) — najczęstsze, large diameter (DN 200-600).
  - **Vapor return / vapor recovery** — łączy tank vents z vapor recovery unit (VRU).
  - **Fire water** — for cooling tanks + foam delivery.
  - **Foam piping** (concentrate + premixed solution).
  - **Drainage / slop piping** — collects spills, leaks → slop tank.
  - **Instrumentation** — pressure, temperature, level signals.
- **Manifold design**:
  - **Suction manifold** (from tanks → pumps) — gate valves każdy tank, common header → pumps.
  - **Discharge manifold** (from pumps → loading) — pressure side, valves dla routing.
  - **Cross-connection** (suction ↔ discharge bypass) — dla emergency / back-flushing.
  - **Slope toward drain** — 1:100 minimum (kondensacja vapor, slop accumulation).
- **Foam fire fighting system** (NFPA 11, NFPA 30):
  - **Foam concentrate tank** — typowo 30-50 m³ tank z AFFF (Aqueous Film-Forming Foam) lub AR-AFFF (Alcohol-Resistant) dla polar fluids.
  - **Foam proportioner** — miesza concentrate z wodą w **3% lub 6% ratio** (zależy od fuel type):
    - **3% AFFF**: gasoline, diesel, kerosene.
    - **6% protein**: heavy hydrocarbons.
    - **3% AR-AFFF**: alcohols, methanol, ethanol.
  - **Premix piping** lub **proportioner-at-tank** delivery method.
  - **Discharge devices**:
    - **Foam chambers** w tank roof (fixed) — internal foam application.
    - **Foam pourers (Type II)** — w side of tank.
    - **Sub-surface injection** — foam wpompowywany na dole tank, "rises" through fuel.
    - **Foam monitors** (water cannons) — mobilne lub stationary, manual aim.
    - **Foam sprinklers** dla dispense area, loading docks.
- **Application rates** (NFPA 11):
  - **Crude oil floating roof tank**: **6.5 L/min/m²** dla **65 min**.
  - **Fixed roof tank**: 6.5 L/min/m² dla 30-65 min (zależy od tank diameter).
  - **Subsurface injection**: niższe rates (3.5 L/min/m²) — wydajniejsze.
- **Cooling water** dla tanks:
  - **Sprinkler/spray rings** wokół tank shells w fire emergency.
  - **Application rate**: typowo **4-10 L/min/m²** of shell surface.
  - **Cel**: utrzymać shell T ≤ 100°C → zapobiec boiling fuel + BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapor Explosion).
- **Tank farm safety devices**:
  - **PV valves** (Pressure-Vacuum) — vent atmospheres bez fugitive emissions.
  - **Flame arrestors** — na vent lines, zapobiegają flame propagation z atmosphere do tank.
  - **Foam pourers + chambers** w roof.
  - **Drain valves** — emergency drain do slop.
  - **High-level + Low-level alarms + shutoff**.
  - **Fixed gas detectors** (LEL, H₂S) wokół manifold.
- **Critical distances** (NFPA 30 / API 650):
  - **Tank-to-tank**: minimum **0.5 × tank diameter** spacing.
  - **Tank-to-property line**: zależy od tank class i Class location.
  - **Manifold-to-tank**: ≥ 5 m typowo (NFPA 30 specifics by tank class).
  - **Pump house location**: 15-30 m od tanks (safe distance).
- **Loading docks**:
  - **Loading arm** lub **hose connection** (patrz iter 25).
  - **Vapor recovery hookup** mandatory dla VOC reduction (volatile organic compounds — regulatory).
  - **Spill containment** — concrete pad with bermed perimeter, drain to slop.
  - **Earthing**: critical dla static dissipation (truck + arm bonding).
- **Pigging** capability — niektóre tank farms instalują pig launcher/receiver dla long-distance crude pipelines (off-loading from terminal to pipeline).

---

## Iteration 30 — 2026-05-25

### MMMM. SAW (Submerged Arc Welding) — pipe manufacturing + heavy duty
- **SAW (Submerged Arc Welding)** = łuk między drutem ciągłym a workpiece **CAŁKOWICIE UKRYTY pod warstwą flux'a** (proszek lub granulat). Flux: 
  - **Chroni łuk** od atmosfery (zero shielding gas needed).
  - **Topi się i tworzy slag** który chroni stopiwo do schłodzenia.
  - **Re-cyklowalny** (slag scrap → re-melt → re-use).
- **Deposition rate**: **5-25 kg/h** — **NAJWYŻSZA** z arc welding processes. Stąd główne zastosowanie: **mass production**.
- **Manufacturing pipe**:
  - **LSAW (Longitudinal SAW) Pipe** — z **płyt stalowych** rolled w cylinder, **jedna longitudinal seam** spawana SAW.
    - **Proces JCOE**: J-Press → C-Press → O-Press → Edge prep → Welding (single longitudinal seam).
    - **Sizes**: DN 325-1420 mm (12"-56").
    - **Wall**: do 75 mm typowo.
    - **Standard**: API 5L PSL2, EN ISO 3183.
    - **Inside + outside SAW pass** — full penetration.
    - **Aplikacje**: high-pressure oil & gas pipelines, structural, offshore risers.
  - **SSAW (Spiral SAW / SAWH = Helical) Pipe**:
    - **Strip coil** (taśma stalowa) rolled na **angle** do osi pipe → tworzy **helical seam**.
    - **Continuous welding** — taśma podawana bez przerwy.
    - **Mniej steel waste** vs LSAW (taśma wykorzystywana w 95-99%).
    - **Sizes**: DN 200-2500 mm (8"-100"). **Większy zakres** niż LSAW.
    - **Wall**: do 25 mm typowo (cieńsze niż LSAW).
    - **Aplikacje**: large diameter water + gas pipelines, structural piles, casing.
- **LSAW vs SSAW comparison**:

| Aspect | LSAW | SSAW |
|--------|------|------|
| Seam | 1 straight longitudinal | Helical, długi |
| Diameter range | 12"-56" | 8"-100" |
| Wall thickness | Do 75 mm | Do 25 mm |
| Cost per ton | Higher | Lower |
| Pressure rating | Higher | Moderate |
| Inspection | Easier (straight seam) | Difficult (helical RT) |
| Aplikacje | Critical pipeline, offshore | Water, large gas distribution |

- **SAW process variants**:
  - **Single-wire SAW** — basic, jednych elektrod.
  - **Twin-wire SAW** — 2 druty równolegle, wyższe deposition + lepsza penetration.
  - **Tandem SAW** — 2-3 druty seriall, **mass production heavy plates**.
  - **Tubular electrode SAW** — drut z flux'em w środku (jak FCAW ale w SAW).
  - **Strip cladding SAW** — szeroka taśma elektrody do cladding (np. inconel na CS pressure vessels).
- **Process parameters** (typical LSAW DN500 thick wall):
  - **Wire diameter**: 4.0-6.4 mm.
  - **Current**: 600-1200 A.
  - **Voltage**: 30-38 V.
  - **Travel speed**: 30-100 cm/min.
  - **Flux consumption**: ~1.0-1.5 kg flux per kg wire.
- **Flux types**:
  - **Fused flux** — pre-melted, granular. Stable arc, deep penetration.
  - **Agglomerated (bonded) flux** — mixed powder + binder. Better mechanical properties (toughness).
  - **Mixed (hybrid)** — bonded core + fused outer.
- **Limitations SAW**:
  - **Tylko flat / horizontal positions** — flux nie da się utrzymać na vertical lub overhead.
  - **NIE field work** — typowo shop / manufacturing only (specjalne field SAW setups istnieją ale rzadkie).
  - **Bulk equipment** — flux hopper, recovery system, dedicated workstation.
  - **Brak visibility** — operator nie widzi pool'a (must rely on parameters + experience).
- **Pipeline application** (specialty):
  - **Field SAW** — w bardzo dużych pipeline'ach (DN ≥ 600), automatic tractor-mounted system. Rzadko ale używane.
  - **Mainline pipelines** dziś preferują **GMAW-P (pulsed)** lub **mechanized FCAW** dla speed + portability.

### NNNN. Sample Point Design — process piping sampling stations
- **Sample point / sampling station** = miejsce w pipe gdzie operator może pobrać **reprezentatywną próbkę** medium dla:
  - **Lab analysis** (skład chemiczny, zanieczyszczenia, ICP-MS, gas chromatograph).
  - **Process analyzer** (online instruments: pH, conductivity, density, viscosity, IR composition).
  - **Corrosion coupons** (weight loss monitoring).
  - **Biocidal / microbial monitoring** (cooling water, fuel).
- **Sample point components**:
  - **Sample probe** — wchodzi w środek pipe (NIE z boku — boundary layer może być niereprezentatywna). **Isokinetic probe** dla two-phase / particulate sampling — same velocity inside vs outside probe.
  - **Isolation valves** (typowo 2 in series):
    - **Block valve** — primary isolation (ball valve preferred dla quick on/off).
    - **Sample valve** — needle valve dla flow control.
    - **Secondary block** — failsafe.
  - **Pressure indicator** — verify line pressure przed opening.
  - **Pressure relief / vent** — bezpieczne odprowadzenie podczas opening.
  - **Sample container connection** — quick-disconnect, septum (gas), sample bomb (high-P).
- **Standard designs**:
  - **Atmospheric vent type** (low pressure / low toxicity): single valve + vent to atmosphere lub drain.
  - **Closed-loop sampling** (toxic / VOC) — sample container connected w sealed loop, returning to process. Bez emission.
  - **Quick-disconnect bottle sampling** (Welker-style):
    - Pressurized bottle z septum.
    - Sample valve + needle penetration.
    - Sample injected do bottle pod ciśnieniem (no atmospheric exposure).
  - **Process analyzer sample system** — continuous flow przez analyzer, return to process. Built-in conditioning (filter, pressure regulator, temperature control).
- **Critical design rules**:
  - **Avoid dead legs** — sample point pipe długość ≤ **6× ID** (dead leg = stagnant fluid, niereprezentatywne).
  - **Top of pipe** dla liquid sample, **side** dla gas sample (avoid liquid carryover).
  - **Velocity przy probe**: ≥ 0.3 m/s (turbulent enough dla representative).
  - **Slope** sample line back to process: 1:50 minimum (drain back).
- **Coupon monitoring** (corrosion):
  - **Weight-loss coupon** — strip metal podobny do pipe material, ważony przed/po expozycji.
  - **Calculate corrosion rate** od loss / area / time.
  - **Coupon installation/removal**:
    - **Retrievable through ball valve** — coupon holder na long rod, slides przez ball valve. Pull-back, close ball valve, then full retraction.
    - **Hot tap coupon installation** — live installation z special tool.
    - **High-pressure coupon access fitting** — specialized fitting (Cosasco, Caproco).
  - **Exposure period**: typowo **30-90 dni** (krótszy nie representative, dłuższy = uszkodzony coupon).
- **Online analyzers sample conditioning**:
  - **Filtration**: 5-50 µm depending on analyzer.
  - **Pressure regulator**: zredukować z process P (50 bar) do analyzer P (1-2 bar).
  - **Temperature control**: heated lub cooled do analyzer operating T (often 25-50°C constant).
  - **Trap / knockout drum**: dla two-phase removal.
  - **Vaporizer** dla liquid sample → gas chromatograph.
- **Sample station enclosure**:
  - **Sample shelter / panel** — weather + safety enclosure.
  - **Drainage** — collect spills.
  - **Vapor recovery** dla VOC / toxic.
  - **Emergency wash / eyewash** zalecane.
  - **PPE storage** — gloves, respirator, splash guards.
- **HSE considerations**:
  - **Lockout / Tagout** dla planned sampling (positive isolation).
  - **Toxic gas detection** dla H₂S, CO services.
  - **Hot work permit** jeśli welding rework w sampling area.
  - **Confined space considerations** dla underground sample pits.

### OOOO. Welding Cable Sizing — amperage + length + voltage drop
- **Welding cable** = wysokoprądowy elastyczny kabel łączący welder z elektrodą + ground clamp. Krytyczny komponent — niewłaściwy size = voltage drop = poor arc + welder overheating.
- **AWG (American Wire Gauge)** sizing — odwrotna skala: **mniejszy numer = grubszy kabel**:

| AWG | Diameter [mm] | Cross-section [mm²] | Resistance [mΩ/m] |
|-----|--------------:|--------------------:|------------------:|
| #6 | 4.1 | 13.3 | 1.30 |
| #4 | 5.2 | 21.2 | 0.82 |
| #2 | 6.5 | 33.6 | 0.52 |
| #1 | 7.3 | 42.4 | 0.41 |
| #1/0 | 8.3 | 53.5 | 0.32 |
| #2/0 | 9.3 | 67.4 | 0.26 |
| #3/0 | 10.4 | 85.0 | 0.20 |
| #4/0 | 11.7 | 107.2 | 0.16 |

- **Amperage rating** (continuous duty, 30°C ambient):

| AWG | 100% duty cycle | 60% duty | Max short runs |
|-----|----------------:|---------:|---------------:|
| #6 | 100 A | 130 A | 150 A |
| #4 | 135 A | 175 A | 200 A |
| #2 | 190 A | 250 A | 280 A |
| #1 | 215 A | 285 A | 320 A |
| #1/0 | 250 A | 330 A | 380 A |
| #2/0 | 300 A | 395 A | 450 A |
| #3/0 | 350 A | 460 A | 530 A |
| #4/0 | 405 A | 530 A | 600 A |

- **Length compensation rule**: dodaj **+1 AWG** (grubszy size) na każde **15 m (50 ft)** całkowitej długości obwodu.
- **Voltage drop calculation**:
  - **V_drop = I × R × L** gdzie I=amperage, R=resistance/m, L=cable length (one-way × 2 dla round trip).
  - **Limit**: V_drop < **4%** of welder output voltage (typowo < 1-2 V).
  - **Przykład**: 200 A welder, 25 m cable, #2 AWG (R=0.52 mΩ/m):
    - V_drop = 200 × 0.00052 × 50 (round trip) = **5.2 V** — **ZA WYSOKIE!**
    - Zwiększyć do #1/0 (R=0.32 mΩ/m): V_drop = 200 × 0.00032 × 50 = **3.2 V** — OK.
- **Total circuit length** = electrode cable + ground (work) cable. Oba są częścią obwodu!
  - **Common error**: tylko electrode cable jest sized, ground cable jest niedobudowany. Wzrost resistance, arc instability.
- **Cable construction**:
  - **Fine stranded copper** (najpopularniejsze) — flexible, durable.
  - **Class K / Class M stranding** — różne flexibility (K = standard, M = extra-flex).
  - **EPDM lub neoprene jacket** — heat + abrasion resistant.
  - **HOSE rated** (jak SAE 1006-G2) — oil-resistant dla industrial.
- **Voltage rating**:
  - **600 V / 750 V** — standard welding cable.
  - **2000 V** — special high-frequency TIG (przepięcia start)
- **Polarity considerations**:
  - **DC welding** — kabel polarity nie matter (electron flow, no AC issue).
  - **AC welding** — kabel rates same way.
- **Wymiana / inspection**:
  - **Insulation cracks** = trash. Brak negotiation — risk shock + arc flash.
  - **Connector / lug corrosion** — clean lub replace.
  - **Resistance test** — pomiar Ω od end-to-end. Każdy wzrost > 10% manufacturer spec = replace.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Lincoln Electric** — Genuine LE Cable, premier choice.
  - **Direct Wire** — premium quality.
  - **Nassau National Cable** — value option.
  - **Carol / General Cable** — premium industrial.
- **Storage / handling**:
  - **Nie zwijaj kabla pod prądem** — generuje pole magnetyczne (induced EMF), heating w spirali.
  - **Nie ciągnij za connector lug** — wyrwie się ze ścianki.
  - **Nie spalaj jacket** — szczególnie EPDM toksyczny przy spaleniu.
- **Welder duty cycle** w relation do cable:
  - **Welder 60% duty cycle** w 300 A → kabel sized **dla 300 A continuous** (60% duty = 36 min ON / 60 min) nie 60% rating.
  - **Welder 100% duty cycle dla industrial** → kabel sized **dla full rated current**.

---

## Iteration 31 — 2026-05-25

### PPPP. GMAW / MIG Welding na pipe — short circuit + pulse mode
- **GMAW (Gas Metal Arc Welding) / MIG (Metal Inert Gas)** — wire continuously fed przez welding gun, shielded by gas (Ar, CO₂, Ar/CO₂ mix). **High deposition + high speed** w porównaniu do SMAW/GTAW. **Wymaga gas → indoor / windscreen outdoor**.
- **Metal transfer modes** — KRYTYCZNE dla pipe welding:
  - **Short Circuit (GMAW-S)** — drut dotyka pool'a 50-200×/sek, każdy contact = mini-arc + metal transfer:
    - **Najniższy heat input** — minimum HAZ + distortion.
    - **All-position capable** — overhead, vertical-up/down, pipe 5G/6G.
    - **STANDARD dla root pass pipe** (otwarty root).
    - **Wire**: 0.9-1.2 mm.
    - **Gas**: 75% Ar + 25% CO₂ (carbon steel) lub 100% CO₂ (cheap, more spatter).
    - **Voltage**: 16-21 V. Current: 90-200 A.
    - **Slag traveling** — drut może "rzucić cold lap" / brak penetration (LOF). Skill-dependent.
  - **Spray Transfer (GMAW)**:
    - **Continuous metal stream** w postaci fine droplets pod high current.
    - **Wysokie deposition + smooth weld**.
    - **TYLKO flat / horizontal positions** — molten pool zbyt płynny.
    - **Threshold**: > 24 V + > 200 A typical (specific for wire size).
    - **Gas**: 80% Ar + 20% CO₂ lub mostly Ar dla spray.
  - **Globular Transfer**:
    - **Niepożądane** — duże kropla, niestabilna, spatter.
    - Zazwyczaj między short circuit i spray (transition zone).
    - **Avoid** — adjust parameters poza tę strefę.
  - **Pulsed Spray (GMAW-P)**:
    - **Hybryd**: peak current → 1 droplet per pulse → background current. Powtarza się 50-300 Hz.
    - **All-position spray-quality** — droplet kontrolowany, heat input zbliżony do short circuit.
    - **IDEAL dla pipe root pass + thin material**.
    - **Wymaga inverter welder** z pulse control.
- **Modified Short Circuit Processes** (proprietary):
  - **Lincoln STT (Surface Tension Transfer)** — micro-controlled current przy short circuit. Hands-off operation.
  - **Miller RMD (Regulated Metal Deposition)** — adaptive control droplet transfer.
  - **ESAB SMC (Smart Modulated Current)** — feedback adjust each cycle.
  - **EWI Reciprocating Wire Feed** — wire driven push-pull → controlled short circuit frequency.
  - **Zalety**: bridges gaps 1.5-3 mm, **less skill required**, **wyższe penetration** vs traditional GMAW-S.
- **Pipe root pass — modified short circuit (np. Lincoln STT)**:
  - **Open root joint**, gap 2.5-3.2 mm.
  - **5G or 6G position**.
  - **Wire 0.9-1.2 mm** ER70S-6.
  - **Gas**: 90% Ar + 10% CO₂ lub 95/5.
  - **Single welder root + hot pass + fill in sequence** — bardzo szybkie vs SMAW root.
- **Spray transfer dla fill / cap passes**:
  - **2G position** (rotating pipe) lub flat pipe sections.
  - **Wire 1.2-1.6 mm**.
  - **Gas 90/10 Ar/CO₂**.
  - **High deposition 4-8 kg/h**.
- **Comparison pipe processes**:

| Process | Root pass | Fill / Cap | Speed | Quality |
|---------|-----------|------------|-------|---------|
| SMAW E6010/E7018 | Standard pipeline | Standard | Medium | Very good |
| GTAW (TIG) | Premium quality | Slow | Slow | Best |
| GMAW-S | Modern pipeline | Sometimes | Fast | Good (skill-dep) |
| Modified short circuit (STT) | Excellent | Sometimes | Fast | Very good |
| FCAW-S | After root (downhill) | Standard pipeline | Fast | Good |
| GMAW pulse | Premium new field | Premium | Medium | Excellent |

- **Critical issue dla MIG na pipe**:
  - **Wind sensitivity** — outdoor GMAW = porosity unless tent.
  - **Lack of fusion** — common w open root with poor technique (cold lap).
  - **Tip wear** — wymiana contact tip co 1-3 dni.
  - **Wire feed troubles** — drive rolls wear, kinked liner → erratic arc.
- **Equipment**:
  - **Welder**: inverter 200-500 A, multi-process (CC + CV + pulse).
  - **Wire feed**: 2- or 4-roll drive system w gun side.
  - **Gun**: 200-400 A air-cooled, water-cooled dla high duty.
  - **Push-pull gun** — pojedyncza wire feed roll w gun + second w welder dla flexibility.
- **Vendors**: Lincoln Electric, Miller, ESAB, Fronius, Kemppi, EWM.

### QQQQ. Hot Bolting & Single Stud Replacement — online flange maintenance
- **Hot bolting** = sekwencyjny removal/replace bolt'ów w **operating flange** (under reduced pressure, but unit running). Maintenance bez shutdown.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Corroded / damaged bolts** — wymiana przed bolt failure.
  - **Wrong material** discovery (np. B7 zamiast B7M for sour service).
  - **Re-torque** flanges które poluzowały podczas thermal cycling.
  - **Hot service** (steam, hot oil) — niemożliwe na zimno, must be done online.
- **Pre-conditions / risk assessment**:
  - **Pressure ≤ 50% design** — typical safety limit (lower pressure = less catastrophic if blow-out).
  - **Temperature within bolt material spec** — high-T bolts (np. A193 B16) tylko gdy T < 540°C.
  - **Gasket condition assessment** — old / hard gasket może blow przy reduced bolt load.
  - **Engineering analysis** — flange separation modeling (FEM lub Akselos type analysis).
  - **Permit to work** + lockout (mimo "live", positive isolation downstream).
- **Hot bolting procedure**:
  1. **Cleaning + lubricant** — wire brush istniejące bolts, anti-seize compound (Never-Seez, Molykote G-Rapid Plus, Tef-Gel).
  2. **Mark all bolts** for identification (np. permanent paint marker numbers 1-N).
  3. **CRISS-CROSS sequence** — NIE clockwise! Standard star pattern (patrz iter 2).
  4. **Loosen ONE bolt** in sequence (np. bolt #1, najpiero położone najdalej od pivot point).
  5. **Remove bolt** carefully — monitor gasket dla extrusion / leak.
  6. **Inspect bolt** — corrosion, thread damage, head crack.
  7. **Replace** z new bolt + new nut + new washers (always! reused = unknown stress).
  8. **Hand-tight** then snug → **30% torque** w cross-pattern overall (z całego flange'u, nie tylko nowego bolt'a).
  9. **60% torque** w cross-pattern.
  10. **100% target torque** w cross-pattern.
  11. **Repeat** dla each subsequent bolt (1 → 2 → 3...).
- **Critical: TOOLING**:
  - **Hydraulic tensioner lub torque wrench** (NIE manual ratchet wrench — accuracy insufficient).
  - **Bolt heater** dla cold-pressing operations (heat bolt → expand → tighten → cool stretches bolt to preload).
- **Safety**:
  - **Stand-off** — operator nie at line of force in case bolt blow-out.
  - **PPE**: face shield, FR clothing, hearing protection.
  - **Gas / fluid spray containment** — splash guards / shrouds.
  - **Emergency response** — fire watch + emergency block valves ready.
- **Single stud replacement (SSR)**:
  - **Wariant hot bolting** — wymieniamy TYLKO uszkodzone bolt'y (znane przez inspection), nie wszystkie.
  - **Mniej ryzykowne** niż full hot bolting (1 bolt removed at a time = mniej de-loading).
  - **Faster** — typowo 30 min per bolt.
- **Standardy**:
  - **API RP 686 (Recommended Practices for Machinery Installation)**.
  - **Energy Institute Hot Bolting Guidelines**.
  - Internal company procedures (Shell DEPs, ExxonMobil GP, BP standards).
- **Documentation**:
  - **Hot bolting permit** authorized by area engineer + supervisor.
  - **Bolt log** — każdy removed + replaced bolt: serial #, lot #, torque, sequence.
  - **Post-job inspection** — leak check after all bolts replaced.

### RRRR. LDAR (Leak Detection & Repair) — Fugitive Emissions Program
- **LDAR (Leak Detection And Repair)** = systematyczny program wykrywania + reprezentacji **fugitive emissions** (VOC, HAP, methane) z piping components (valves, flanges, connectors, pumps, compressors).
- **Regulatory drivers**:
  - **EPA Clean Air Act §111 (NSPS)** — New Source Performance Standards. Subpart VVa/GGGa dla refineries, OOOOa dla oil & gas production.
  - **EPA OOOOb/c (2024)** — strict methane rules dla oil & gas.
  - **EU Industrial Emissions Directive (IED)** — BAT (Best Available Techniques) reference.
  - **CARB** (California Air Resources Board) — najostrzejsze stanowe rules.
- **Components covered** (typowo):
  - **Valves** — gate, globe, ball, butterfly, control valves.
  - **Flanges + connectors** (threaded, compression fittings).
  - **Pumps + compressors** — shaft seals.
  - **Pressure relief valves (PRV)**.
  - **Open-ended lines** (vent stacks bez plug'a).
  - **Sampling connections** (patrz iter 30).
- **Detection methods**:
  - **EPA Method 21** — **portable VOC analyzer** (FID/PID) measured at component surface.
    - **Threshold**: typowo **500-10,000 ppmv** (zależy od component type + service):
      - Light liquid valves: 500 ppmv leak threshold.
      - Heavy liquid valves: 10,000 ppmv.
      - Gas valves: 500-1,000 ppmv.
    - **Procedure**: probe at suspected leak point, sweep 1-2 cm/s, hold reading 2× response time.
  - **Optical Gas Imaging (OGI)** — IR camera (FLIR GFx320, GF77a) — visualize gas leaks. Better dla:
    - Hard-to-reach components (top of column, deep w racks).
    - Methane (transparent to standard cameras, visible w 3.2-3.4 µm IR band).
    - **Quicker** — visual screen entire area, then probe with Method 21 only suspected leaks.
  - **Drone-mounted sensors** — emerging dla flare line, tank farm overview.
  - **Continuous monitoring** — fixed gas detectors at critical equipment.
  - **Acoustic emission** — ultrasonic detection dla high-pressure gas leaks.
- **LDAR program structure**:
  - **Component inventory** — every flange / valve / connector counted + tagged.
  - **Risk-based prioritization** — high-risk first (light HCs, high-stream conc).
  - **Inspection frequency**:
    - **Monthly** dla critical (PRV, control valves on light liquid/gas).
    - **Quarterly** dla standard valves.
    - **Annual** dla low-risk (heavy oil, water service).
  - **Targets** ("performance" categories):
    - **Subpart VVa**: < 2% leakers permitted, repair within 5 days, resolution 15 days.
    - **CARB**: more stringent (0.5% target).
- **Repair priorities**:
  - **First attempt repair** within **5 days** typically.
  - **Final repair** within **15 days**.
  - **Delay of Repair (DOR)** — gdy repair requires shutdown, can be deferred to next planned outage (typowo ≤ 2 lata).
  - **Repair methods**:
    - **Tighten packing / gland nuts** — easy fix dla valves.
    - **Replace gasket** — wymaga isolation.
    - **Replace component** — drugi attempt dla persistent leak.
- **Records / reporting**:
  - **Per-component log** — date, inspection result (ppm reading), repair date, follow-up reading.
  - **Annual report** — % leakers, average emissions, year-over-year trend.
  - **Audit-able** dla EPA inspections (5+ years retention).
- **Calculation of emissions** (after detection):
  - **EPA correlation equations** — emission rate function of leak rate concentration.
  - **Bagging studies** — direct measurement (wrap component in bag, measure flow).
  - **Total leak inventory** — sum of all components per service, per facility.
- **Effectiveness**:
  - **Well-implemented LDAR**: **40-60% reduction** w fugitive emissions vs baseline.
  - **Top performers** (with OGI + monthly inspection): up to 80% reduction.
  - **Cost-effectiveness**: $200-2000 per ton CO₂ equivalent reduced (methane-rich streams).
- **2024+ trend — Methane regulations**:
  - **EPA OOOOb (new sources)** — stricter than Method 21, requires OGI quarterly.
  - **EPA OOOOc (existing sources)** — phased compliance 2027+, mandatory leak detection + repair.
  - **EU Methane Regulation** (2024) — direct measurement for super-emitters.
  - **Pipefitter implication**: more frequent inspection access requirements, design with **bolt-on quick-access** sample / repair points.

---

## Iteration 32 — 2026-05-25

### SSSS. Victaulic & Grooved Mechanical Coupling — pipe joining bez spawania
- **Grooved mechanical coupling (Victaulic to brand)** = pipe joining przez **rowek wycięty na końcu pipe + obejma z gasket'em**. Bez spawania, bez gwintowania, bez flange'a.
- **Komponenty grooved joint**:
  1. **Pipe** z **circumferential groove** na końcu (rolled lub cut do precyzyjnej geometrii).
  2. **Elastomer gasket** (EPDM, Buna-N, FKM/Viton) — owijający OD obu rur w obszarze groove.
  3. **Housing (coupling, 2-piece)** — dwie połówki obejmy z key/lip pasującym w groove. Bolted together.
- **Groove preparation**:
  - **Roll grooving** — rolka ciśnienie zewn., shrink wall lokalnie, tworzy groove bez usuwania materiału. **Standard dla większości pipe**.
  - **Cut grooving** — tokarka usuwa materiał, zostawiając groove. Dla heavy wall (Sch 80+) gdzie roll grooving niewystarczający.
  - **Groove dimensions standardized** wg Victaulic OGS (Original Groove System) lub AGS (Advanced Groove System) dla DN ≥ 350.
- **Types of couplings**:
  - **Rigid coupling** — sztywne, brak axial / angular movement. Tłumaczy się jak weld'ed joint w stress analysis. Zalety: vibration-proof, eliminate pipe sway.
  - **Flexible coupling** — allows **angular deflection** (±1-2° per joint) + **axial movement** (±0.05-0.15" per joint). Acts jak **micro expansion joints** — pipe natural flexibility absorbs thermal expansion.
- **Application zalety**:
  - **No hot work** — eliminuje hot work permits, fire watch, weld inspection.
  - **Speed**: **5-10× szybsze** niż welded joint. Single coupling installable w **1-3 min** dla skilled.
  - **No skill** — fitter wystarczy, nie certified welder.
  - **Pre-fabricated** — pipe spool z fittingami można **pre-assemble + groove** w shopie, install na site.
  - **Field-adjustable** — pipe można rotate, repositioning easy.
  - **Permanent yet serviceable** — easy disassembly dla maintenance / repair.
- **Pressure ratings**:
  - **Standard groove (OGS)**: 300-1000 psi (20-70 bar).
  - **Advanced groove (AGS)** dla DN ≥ 350: 250-365 psi.
  - **High-pressure (Vic-HP)**: do **3000 psi** dla oil & gas.
- **Size range**:
  - **¾" do 24"** (DN 20-DN 600) standard.
  - **30" do 60"** (DN 750-1500) — large diameter, water utilities.
- **Aplikacje pierwsza klasa**:
  - **Fire protection / sprinkler systems** — Victaulic **industry standard** dla wet sprinklers. Cieczna kontrola.
  - **HVAC** — chilled water, hot water, condenser water w komercyjnych budynkach.
  - **Plumbing** — kompletna distribution domestic water.
  - **Mining + tunneling** — flexible dla ground movement.
  - **Marine** — easy assembly w confined ship engine rooms.
  - **Mission-critical facilities** (data centers, hospitals) — easy maintenance.
- **Limitations**:
  - **High temperature**: do 230°C (450°F) dla EPDM standard, 480°C (900°F) dla FKM/Viton specials.
  - **NIE dla cryogenic** (gasket elastomer cracks).
  - **NIE dla highly aggressive chemicals** (acid, caustic) — gasket failure.
  - **Carbon steel pipe minimum** SCH 5/10 (Sch 40 too thick for rolling).
  - **Sealing wymaga gasket condition** — long-term elastomer aging.
- **Standardy**:
  - **AWWA C606** (USA water industry standard).
  - **NFPA 13** dla fire sprinklers (Victaulic specifically listed).
  - **FM Approval / UL Listed** dla fire protection coupling.
- **Vendors**: **Victaulic** (original, 1925), Gruvlok (Anvil), Shurjoint, Tyco/Johnson Controls. Generic **grooved-end** często compatible (verify gasket + housing tolerances).

### TTTT. Material Test Reports (MTR / MTC) — EN 10204 standardy
- **MTR (Material Test Report) / MTC (Mill Test Certificate)** = dokument certyfikujący że material spełnia spec (chemical composition, mechanical properties, heat treatment). Krytyczne dla **traceability** w piping systems.
- **EN 10204** — standard europejski definiujący 4 typy MTR (USA equivalent: ASTM A370 + project-specific).
- **Typy MTR**:
  - **Type 2.1** — **Declaration of compliance** with order. **NAJTAŃSZY**, **GENERIC**:
    - Brak heat-specific test results.
    - Producent oświadcza że produkt zgodny ze spec.
    - **Brak chemical analysis or mechanical test data**.
    - Stosowane dla: low-criticality, commodity products (standard fasteners, generic pipe).
  - **Type 2.2** — **Test report** w typowych testach:
    - **Generic test data** (z reprezentatywnej heaty, NIE konkretnej serii).
    - Wskazuje typowe properties dla material grade.
    - Brak traceability do specific batch.
    - Stosowane dla: medium-risk industrial applications.
  - **Type 3.1** — **Inspection certificate** z **specific heat traceability**:
    - **Chemical analysis + mechanical tests** wykonane na **rzeczywistej produkcji** (actual heat).
    - **Każdy single piece** w shipment traceable do batch / heat number.
    - **Validated by manufacturer's QA dept** (musi być **independent od production team**).
    - **STANDARD dla większości piping applications** (process piping, pressure vessels, structural).
    - **Cost**: ~5-15% premium nad 2.1.
  - **Type 3.2** — **Inspection certificate WITNESSED**:
    - Wszystko co w 3.1 + **independent third-party inspector** witnesses + co-signs tests.
    - **Inspector typowo**: TÜV, DNV-GL, Lloyd's Register, ABS, Bureau Veritas, klientowy QA representative.
    - **Najwyższy poziom assurance**.
    - **Cost**: ~20-50% premium nad 3.1.
    - **Lead time impact**: +2-4 tygodnie (inspector coordination).
    - **Stosowane dla**: subsea pipelines, nuclear, high-pressure/high-temp service, aerospace, critical safety equipment.
- **Content każdego MTR (3.1, 3.2)**:
  1. **Material designation** (e.g., ASTM A106 Gr.B, ASTM A312 TP316L).
  2. **Heat number / batch ID** — unique traceability identifier.
  3. **Chemical composition** — % wt każdego pierwiastka (C, Mn, Si, P, S, + alloying jeśli applicable).
  4. **Mechanical properties** — tensile, yield, elongation, hardness (HRB/HRC/HV).
  5. **Charpy impact testing** (jeśli wymagane przez spec) — energy absorbed at specific T.
  6. **Heat treatment** condition (annealed, normalized, quenched + tempered, solution annealed).
  7. **NDE results** (jeśli wymagane) — RT, UT testing of mother pipe.
  8. **PMI (Positive Material Identification)** results — XRF lub OES verification.
  9. **Dimensions** + tolerances.
  10. **Inspector signature** (3.1) + **third-party signature** (3.2).
- **Specific test data required (typical for pipe)**:
  - **Tensile strength** (UTS) — np. A106 Gr.B min 415 MPa.
  - **Yield strength** (0.2% offset) — min 240 MPa dla A106 Gr.B.
  - **Elongation** — min 30% (A106 Gr.B, 50 mm gauge length).
  - **Charpy V-notch impact** — często min 27 J at 0°C lub spec'd lower T.
  - **Hardness** — max wg NACE MR0175 (22 HRC dla sour) lub spec.
- **PMI (Positive Material Identification)** — field verification of material:
  - **XRF (X-Ray Fluorescence)** — handheld Olympus Vanta lub Bruker S1 Titan. Identifies alloy by elemental signature.
  - **OES (Optical Emission Spectroscopy)** — sparks small zone, atom signatures. Najbardziej accurate dla C content.
  - **PMI 100%** standardowo dla SS/alloy piping (avoid mix-up z carbon steel pipe).
- **Traceability chain**:
  - **Heat number → pipe → spool → installed location** w as-built drawings.
  - **Each shipment**: packing list + MTR per pipe length.
  - **5-30+ year retention** for critical service.
- **Niewłaściwe MTR**:
  - **Brak MTR** = material NIE może być zainstalowany dla regulated service (NDT inspection nie podpisze).
  - **MTR 3.1 vs spec'd 3.2** — material returned to vendor, project delay.
  - **PMI failure** — material za 100% wymianę.

### UUUU. Steam Blowing — pre-commissioning cleaning power plant + steam lines
- **Steam blowing** = mechaniczne czyszczenie steam lines (boiler → turbine) **wysokoenergetyczną parą** przy starcie nowej instalacji lub po major maintenance. Usuwa scale, weld slag, debris, mill scale.
- **Cel**:
  - **Czysty rurociąg PRZED first steam-up turbiny** — debris w turbinie = **wybicie łopatek** (multi-million $ damage).
  - **Verification metoda**: target plates pokazują że pipe jest clean.
- **Procedura general**:
  1. **Pre-clean / hydrolaundering** — wash with water removed loose scale, debris.
  2. **Install temporary steam blow piping** — bypass turbine, blow to atmosphere (z noise silencer typowo 130-150 dB).
  3. **Install target plates** (impact specimens) w temporary piping outlet — najczęściej **aluminum lub stainless steel mirror plates**.
  4. **Start boiler at reduced pressure** (cold start sequence).
  5. **Pressurize do steam blow pressure** — typowo **20% HIGHER** niż normal operating pressure.
  6. **Open steam blow valve QUICKLY** (sudden release) → high-velocity steam carries debris through pipe.
  7. **Hold blow** 30-60 sekund.
  8. **Cooling time** — minimum 3 h between blows (allows pipe to cool + thermal cycle dla loosening more debris).
  9. **Insert target plate** at outlet after 2-3 blows (early blows mają zbyt dużo debris).
  10. **Inspect target plate** — wzrokowo + microscope.
  11. **Repeat** until acceptance criteria met.
- **Cleanliness Factor Ratio (CFR)** — KRYTYCZNY parameter:
  - **CFR = (P_blow × Specific Volume_blow) / (P_op × Specific Volume_op)**.
  - **CFR ≥ 1.2** — minimum dla skuteczny cleaning (steam dynamic pressure 20% wyższe niż operating).
  - **CFR ≥ 1.5** — recommended dla critical service (high-pressure turbines).
- **Target plate acceptance** (typowo):
  - **No more than 5 pits < 1 mm diameter** w central zone area.
  - **No deformed edges** (wskazane przez large debris impact).
  - **No pitting w rim zone** (outer 10% radius — local impact concentration).
  - **Mirror finish** restored po blow — niewidoczne particles.
- **Inspection points**:
  - **Main steam line** boiler → turbine.
  - **Reheater line** (jeśli applicable).
  - **Extraction lines** to feedwater heaters.
  - **Auxiliary steam headers**.
- **Types of steam blow**:
  - **Discontinuous blow (puff blow)** — short bursts (30-60 s), cooling between.
    - Pros: aggressive cleaning, thermal cycling loosens debris.
    - Cons: thermal stress na pipe (z reguły OK dla new pipe — designed for cycles).
  - **Continuous blow** — long sustained blow (30-60 min).
    - Pros: gentle thermal, dla critical / aged pipe.
    - Cons: less aggressive cleaning, dłuższy total time.
  - **Alternating blow** — switch between two outlets, cyclically.
- **Safety considerations**:
  - **NOISE**: 120-150 dB przy outlet. **Hearing protection mandatory** + restricted area (300 m radius).
  - **Steam plume hazard** — atmospheric release. Visible kilometr.
  - **Thermal shock** w pipe — designed-for. Inspection po każdym blow dla cracking signs.
  - **Steam fall-back** — temporary piping designed z slope away from boiler.
  - **Equipment isolation** — turbine + downstream equipment isolated POZYTYWNIE (blind, spectacle blind).
- **Alternative methods** (newer technology):
  - **Chemical cleaning + steam blow combined** — pre-wash z citric acid lub ammonium citrate solution, removes oxide layer chemically. Then mild steam blow dla wykończenie.
  - **Air blow** — instead steam, sprężone powietrze (dry). Less aggressive ale safer (no thermal stress).
  - **Nitrogen blow** — clean inert atmosphere, dla critical service. Drogie.
- **Duration & cost**:
  - **Typowy power plant** 600 MW: 100-200 blows × 30 s każdy = **3-6 weeks total**.
  - **Cost**: $500k - $5M zależnie od plant size + duration.
- **Documentation**:
  - **Steam blow plan** (engineering document) — approval przez plant + insurer.
  - **Per-blow log** — pressure, T, duration.
  - **Target plate photos** + inspector signatures.
  - **Acceptance certificate** for turbine first-roll.

---

## Iteration 33 — 2026-05-25

### VVVV. 3D Laser Scanning — pipe alignment + tie-in surveys
- **3D laser scanning** = capture entire pipe-rack lub plant geometry **w punktach (point cloud)** — milimetrowa dokładność. **Standard dla brownfield projects** (modyfikacje istniejących instalacji).
- **Scanner technologies**:
  - **Terrestrial scanner** (Faro Focus, Leica RTC360, Trimble X9) — tripod mounted, 360° × 270° FOV, **2-50 m range**, **1-5 mln points/sek**.
  - **SLAM (Simultaneous Localization And Mapping) scanner** — handheld lub backpack-mounted (Faro Orbis, NavVis VLX, GeoSLAM ZEB Horizon). **Moving while scanning** — w ruchu po plant'cie. Niższa dokładność (±5-10 mm vs ±2-3 mm terrestrial) ale **10× szybsze**.
  - **Mobile mapping (vehicle-mounted)** — large pipeline corridors, refineries.
  - **Drone LiDAR** — outdoor pipeline routes, ROW (right-of-way).
- **Field workflow**:
  1. **Survey planning** — identify scan positions, tie-in points, control targets.
  2. **Control targets** placement — special **black/white checkerboard targets** lub **spheres** placed in scan area. Each ma known coordinates (GPS lub project grid).
  3. **Scanning** — multiple scan positions, **overlap 30-50%** between adjacent scans.
  4. **Registration** — software (Faro Scene, Leica Cyclone, Autodesk ReCap) aligns scans przez **target-based** lub **cloud-to-cloud** matching.
  5. **Georeferencing** — link point cloud to project coordinate system (GPS + survey control).
  6. **Modeling** — convert point cloud do **3D model** (Revit, AVEVA E3D, Bentley OpenPlant) via "scan-to-BIM" workflow.
- **Pipe modeling z point cloud**:
  - **Cylinder fitting** — algorithms detect cylindrical surfaces, fit pipe geometry.
  - **Centerline extraction** — pipe axis traced w 3D.
  - **Diameter + wall verification** — measured OD vs design.
  - **Tie-in dimensions** — exact field measurements za-built positions.
- **Aplikacje pipefitter / construction**:
  - **As-built documentation** — exact positions of existing pipe + equipment dla retrofit projects.
  - **Pre-fabrication tie-in** — measure exact field dimensions, send do shop dla pre-fab spool with custom angles / lengths.
  - **Clash detection** — check new pipe routing przeciw existing equipment / structures (avoid interferences).
  - **Volume calculations** — tank, vessel internal volume verification.
  - **Pipe sag** + deflection measurement — detect supports issues.
- **Accuracy levels**:
  - **Terrestrial scanner**: ±1-3 mm (with proper control + registration).
  - **SLAM**: ±5-15 mm (drift over distance).
  - **Drone LiDAR**: ±10-50 mm (depends on altitude + GNSS quality).
- **Inner-wall scanning** dla pipe inspection:
  - **Pipe inspection robot** — wheeled / crawler robot z **line laser + camera** moving wewn. pipe.
  - **Average deviation ID + length: ≤ 0.13 mm** — wystarczające dla pipe condition assessment.
  - **Stosowane**: pipeline integrity, found wall thinning + ovality + erosion.
- **Survey control / coordinate system**:
  - **GNSS positioning** — RTK rover z base station, ±10 mm horizontal.
  - **Total station** — laser-based angle + distance, ±1-3 mm.
  - **Project coordinate system** — usually plant-local grid (e.g., refinery 0,0 at control point).
  - **Vertical datum** — typically MSL (mean sea level) lub plant grade.
- **Cost / time** comparison:
  - **Traditional tape measure tie-in survey**: 8-16 h dla typical spool tie-in (low accuracy ±25 mm).
  - **3D laser scanning**: 2-4 h field + 8-16 h office post-processing (high accuracy ±5 mm).
  - **Net advantage**: 50-70% **rework reduction** w fab + install (eliminates iterative field adjustments).
- **Software stack**:
  - **Capture**: Faro Scene, Leica Cyclone Field 360, Trimble Realworks.
  - **Registration + cleaning**: Autodesk ReCap Pro, ClearEdge3D EdgeWise.
  - **Modeling**: ClearEdge3D Verity (compare scan to model), Aveva LFM.
  - **CAD integration**: SolidWorks, Inventor, Plant 3D, AVEVA E3D Design.

### WWWW. Drone Inspection — Pipeline Aerial Survey + Methane Detection
- **UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) / Drone inspection** = aerial inspection rurociągów przez drone z multi-sensor payload. **Standardowa praktyka dla long-distance + offshore pipelines**.
- **Drone types**:
  - **Quadcopter / Multirotor** — agile, hover, close-up inspection (10-60 min flight time):
    - DJI Matrice 350, Skydio X10, Freefly Astro, Autel EVO Max 4T.
    - Aplikacje: tank inspection, refinery component inspection, sky-side pipe.
  - **Fixed-wing** — long endurance (3-6 h flight time), large area coverage:
    - WingtraOne, Quantum Systems Trinity, Delair UX11.
    - Aplikacje: long-distance pipeline ROW (right-of-way) patrol.
  - **VTOL Hybrid** — combine vertical takeoff + fixed-wing efficiency.
- **Payload sensors**:
  - **Visual RGB camera** (high-resolution 50 MP+) — basic visual inspection, photogrammetry, asset condition assessment.
  - **Thermal IR (Infrared camera)** — detect:
    - **Methane leaks** (cold spot from Joule-Thomson expansion if gas leaking under pressure).
    - **Insulation damage** (hot spots in lines that should be insulated).
    - **Pump bearing failure** (hot spots).
    - **Fire / hot spot identification**.
  - **OGI (Optical Gas Imaging)** — specialized IR detecting methane/VOC absorption bands. FLIR GFx320, Sierra Olympus GasFIR.
  - **TDLAS (Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy)** — quantitative methane:
    - **Range**: 5-300 m detection.
    - **Sensitivity**: ≤ 5 ppm·m (5 ppm methane in 1 m path = detectable).
    - **Response**: 5 ms.
    - Detection minimum: **0.35 g/hr methane** w some systems.
  - **LiDAR** — 3D point cloud mapping pipeline corridors:
    - Above-ground pipeline routing.
    - Vegetation encroachment.
    - Slope stability monitoring.
    - Settlement / subsidence detection.
  - **Multispectral** — chlorophyll/vegetation health (early warning leak via plant stress).
- **Mission types**:
  - **Routine surveillance** — weekly / monthly visual + methane patrol.
  - **Post-incident response** — immediate drone deployment after pressure drop alarm.
  - **Construction monitoring** — track progress on new pipeline construction.
  - **Right-of-way encroachment** — detect unauthorized excavation, building construction.
  - **Insurance claim** — document damage post-event (storm, earthquake, sabotage).
  - **Stack inspection** — flare stack visual + thermal.
- **Regulatory frameworks**:
  - **FAA Part 107** (USA) — commercial drone operations.
  - **PHMSA pipeline regulations** — drones acceptable for certain mandatory inspections.
  - **EASA Specific Category** (EU).
  - **BVLOS (Beyond Visual Line Of Sight)** — special permit dla long-distance pipeline patrols.
- **Cost benefits vs traditional inspection**:
  - **Helicopter patrol**: $1000-3000/hour.
  - **Drone patrol**: $200-800/hour all-in.
  - **Coverage rate**: drone **2-10× faster** dla same area (no landings, no refuel midway).
  - **Safety**: zero ground personnel exposure to hazardous areas.
- **Data processing**:
  - **Cloud processing** — automated detection (AI/ML) flag anomalies (leaks, encroachments).
  - **GIS integration** — overlay drone data on existing pipeline maps.
  - **Reports** — auto-generated dla regulators, audit trails.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Weather** — wind > 15 m/s grounded większość drones.
  - **Battery / flight time** — typowo 30-60 min per battery (multirotor).
  - **BVLOS regulations** — restrict long pipeline coverage.
  - **Resolution vs coverage tradeoff** — high altitude faster ale less detail.
- **Vendors / service providers**: Industrial SkyWorks, Avitas Systems (GE), Volatus Aerospace, Flyability (indoor pipe), SkyWatch.AI, Skydio.

### XXXX. Hydrogen Piping (H₂) — material compatibility + ASME B31.12
- **Hydrogen embrittlement (HE)** — H atoms wnikają w metal, propagate cracks pod stress. **Nieusuwalny problem dla high-strength steels** w pure H₂ environment.
- **ASME B31.12** ("Hydrogen Piping and Pipelines") — dedykowany kod dla H₂ piping. Plus complementary:
  - **AIGA 087** (Asia Industrial Gases Association).
  - **EIGA Doc 121** (European Industrial Gases Association).
  - **API RP 941** dla HTHA (High Temp Hydrogen Attack — refining hydrocracking).
- **3 forms of hydrogen damage**:
  - **HE (Hydrogen Embrittlement)** — atom H w material lattice → cracks initiate + propagate. Reversibilne (degassing recovery possible).
  - **HIC (Hydrogen-Induced Cracking)** — blistering, step-wise cracking w sour service (typowo z H₂S source).
  - **HTHA (High-Temperature Hydrogen Attack)** — T > 200°C + H₂ → reaction z C w stali, decarburization. **Irreversibly damages** steel. **Catastrophic** failure mode.
- **Materials selection (B31.12)**:
  - **Austenityczna SS (304L, 316L, 321)** — **PREFERRED dla high pressure H₂**:
    - **Stable austenite** (face-centered cubic) — H₂ atomy mają niski diffusivity.
    - **L-grades (low carbon)** lepsze (mniej ferrite phase precipitation).
    - **316L** dla wet H₂ service (lepsza pitting resistance).
  - **Nickel alloys (Inconel 625, 718)** — premium dla extreme service:
    - Limited HE susceptibility.
    - High strength + corrosion resistance.
    - Drogie ($$$).
  - **Carbon steel (A106, A333)** — **OK dla low-pressure + low-purity H₂** (< 100 bar typically):
    - **Risk increases z higher pressure + higher purity** (less moisture inhibition).
    - **Strength limit**: SMYS ≤ 414 MPa (HRC ≤ 22) per B31.12 Option B requirements.
    - **NIE używać** quenched + tempered HSLA (high-strength low-alloy) w pure H₂.
  - **Aluminum alloys (5083, 6061)** — **immune to HE** dla cryogenic LH₂. Niska wytrzymałość ogranicza pressure rating.
  - **Copper alloys (CuNi, brass)** — immune to HE ale niska wytrzymałość.
  - **NIE używać**:
    - High-carbon steel (> 0.30% C).
    - HSLA high-strength steels (jak API 5L X70+).
    - Cast iron (porowate, microcracks).
    - Titanium (hydride formation = cracks).
- **B31.12 design approach**:
  - **Option A**: standard pipeline code (B31.8 etc.) z reduced allowable stress (typowo 0.5-0.72× normal).
  - **Option B**: advanced approach — z **higher allowable stress** ale **specific material qualification + testing** (slow strain rate tests, fatigue crack growth w H₂).
- **Welding hydrogen pipe** — krytyczne practices:
  - **Avoid hard HAZ** — preheat + interpass + PWHT carefully spec'd.
  - **Hardness limit**: ≤ 235 HV w HAZ (same as NACE MR0175 22 HRC).
  - **Low-hydrogen filler metals** mandatory (E7018-H4, ER70S-G).
  - **Strict moisture control** — wszystko musi być suche (electrode oven, gas dryers).
  - **NDE 100% RT** dla critical service.
- **Service pressures + applications**:
  - **Low pressure (< 100 bar)**: hydrogen distribution networks, refineries, ammonia plants.
  - **Medium pressure (100-350 bar)**: hydrogen fueling stations.
  - **High pressure (350-700 bar)**: fuel cell vehicle dispensers, on-board H₂ tanks.
  - **Ultra-high (700-1000 bar)**: H₂ research, electrolysis test loops.
- **Hydrogen-specific design considerations**:
  - **Smaller molecule than air** — wykrywanie leak bardzo trudne. **Helium leak test** mandatory (He podobnej molecular size).
  - **Wide flammability range**: **4-75% in air** (vs methane 5-15%). Practically anything ignites H₂.
  - **Auto-ignition** at very low energy (0.02 mJ vs 0.28 mJ methane).
  - **Invisible flame** w UV - special **UV/IR fire detectors** wymagane.
  - **Low molecular weight** — buoyant, escapes upward in ventilation. Top-of-room vents mandatory dla H₂ rooms.
  - **Joule-Thomson cooling** — H₂ EXPANDS HOTTER (unlike most gases). Pressure regulator stage może powodować ignition.
- **Connections**:
  - **Welded preferred** (no fugitive paths).
  - **Compression fittings** (Swagelok) — OK z proper qualification.
  - **NIE używać NPT threads** dla wysoko-ciśnieniowych H₂ (fugitive leak risk).
  - **NIE używać gasketed flanges** powyżej low-pressure service (graphite gaskets degrade w H₂).
- **Inspection**:
  - **VT + PT** dla all surface welds.
  - **RT lub PAUT 100%** dla high-pressure.
  - **Periodic helium leak test** w-service.
  - **Hardness monitoring** w HAZ po PWHT.

---

## Iteration 34 — 2026-05-25

### YYYY. WPS Essential Variables — ASME IX detailed (QW-250)
- **WPS (Welding Procedure Specification)** = written instructions guiding welders. **PQR (Procedure Qualification Record)** validates WPS przez physical testing. Każda zmiana **essential variable** wymaga re-qualifikacji PQR.
- **3 categories of variables** (ASME IX QW-250):
  - **Essential Variables** — change AFFECTS mechanical properties (strength, ductility). Re-qualification required.
  - **Supplementary Essential Variables** — applies ONLY gdy impact testing required (np. low-temperature service, NACE MR0175). Becomes essential w tych przypadkach.
  - **Non-essential Variables** — change DOESN'T affect mechanical properties. Just edit WPS, no re-qualification.
- **Essential Variables — typical examples** (vary by process, QW-252 SMAW / QW-253 GTAW / QW-254 GMAW / QW-256 SAW):
  - **P-number** (material group) — change to different P-number = re-qualify.
  - **F-number** (filler metal group) — change = re-qualify.
  - **A-number** (weld metal analysis) — change deposit composition (different alloying) = re-qualify.
  - **Thickness range** — qualified range from PQR. Going outside = re-qualify:
    - **PQR coupon = 1.5T to 2T** (1.5-2× coupon thickness) typically.
    - **Thicker than qualified**: re-qualify with thicker coupon.
    - **Thinner than 0.5T qualified**: also re-qualify (rare issue).
  - **Filler metal classification** — różne klasy (e.g., E7018 vs E7016) require re-qualification.
  - **Gas composition** (GTAW/GMAW) — change shielding gas mix = re-qualify.
  - **Heat treatment (PWHT)** — adding/removing PWHT = re-qualify.
  - **Preheat ≥ −55°C** decrease — re-qualify (lower preheat = higher cooling rate = different microstructure).
  - **Welding process** — SMAW vs GTAW vs GMAW = different WPS entirely.
- **Non-essential Variables** (free to adjust without re-qualification):
  - **Pipe diameter** — qualifying on DN50 (2") covers most diameters! However:
    - **Position qualification rule** applies (next bullet).
    - **Wall thickness within qualified range**.
    - **Practical note**: most operators verify with brief test bend even na new diameter.
  - **Joint design** (within reasonable range — V vs J groove).
  - **Type / size of electrode** (within same F-number).
  - **Welding position** (1G/2G/5G/6G) — qualifying in **6G covers ALL positions** (gold standard).
  - **Backing** (with backing → without, if essential variables unchanged).
  - **Travel direction** (uphill/downhill) — usually non-essential.
- **Supplementary Essential Variables** (impact-test required service):
  - Position becomes essential — must qualify each position separately.
  - Heat input becomes essential.
  - Preheat decrease becomes essential.
  - PWHT cooling rate becomes essential.
- **Position qualification rules** (QW-461):
  - **1G** (flat) — qualifies only 1G welding.
  - **2G** (horizontal, pipe vertical) — qualifies 1G + 2G.
  - **5G** (pipe horizontal fixed) — qualifies 1G + 5G + uphill / downhill.
  - **6G** (pipe 45° fixed) — qualifies ALL positions (1G, 2G, 5G, 6G, 6GR with restriction). **GOLD STANDARD**.
  - **6GR** (with restriction ring) — qualifies all + restricted positions (T-, K-, Y-joints).
- **PQR essentials**:
  - **Actual values used** during qualification welding (not specifications).
  - **Test results**: tensile, bend, hardness, impact, macro.
  - **Signed by manufacturer's authorized representative**.
  - **One PQR can support multiple WPSs** (within qualified ranges).
- **WPQ / WPS Qualification documentation**:
  - **WPS**: instruction, allows ranges (e.g., "current 90-180 A").
  - **PQR**: specific values used (e.g., "tested at 130 A").
  - **Welder Performance Qualification (WPQ)**: individual welder certified to specific WPS.

### ZZZZ. HDD (Horizontal Directional Drilling) — pipeline crossing installation
- **HDD (Horizontal Directional Drilling)** = trenchless installation method dla pipeline przejść pod rzekami, autostradami, środowiskowo wrażliwymi obszarami. **Pipeline installed UNDER obstacle bez wycinania trench'a**.
- **3-stage process**:
  1. **Pilot hole** — small diameter (75-300 mm) directional drilled hole from entry to exit point. Drill steered using **electromagnetic guidance system** (walking transducer aboveground).
  2. **Reaming (pre-reaming)** — multiple passes enlarge hole to **120-150% pipe OD** (np. DN600 pipe → 750-900 mm reamed hole).
  3. **Pull-back installation** — pre-fabricated pipeline pulled backward through enlarged hole.
- **Equipment**:
  - **HDD rig** — track-mounted (mid-size) lub stationary (large rigs). Power: 50-1000 ton pull capacity.
  - **Drill rod** — sectional pipe joined as drilling proceeds.
  - **Pilot drill bit** — directional, sterowany.
  - **Reamer** — różne wielkości, multiple sizes used progressively.
  - **Drilling fluid (mud)** — bentonite-based, cools tool + carries cuttings + stabilizes borehole.
- **Pull-back specifics**:
  - **Pulling head** — fabricated cone attached do **front pipe**, hooked to drill string. Welded onto pipe.
  - **Swivel joint** between pulling head and drill string — pipe doesn't rotate during pull-back (avoid torsional damage).
  - **Roller bands** lub **rollers w trench** support pipe entrance angle (typowo 8-20°).
  - **Continuous pulling** — bez przerw, ciągły pull dla minimum friction (stop = bentonite settles, pipe sticks).
- **Pipe preparation pre-HDD**:
  - **Above-ground welding + testing** — pipe spawany na **roller stands** w ROW (right-of-way) na całej długości.
  - **Continuous welding** procedure pre-tested for quality (no PWHT typically possible mid-string).
  - **Hydrotest** PRZED pull-back (z reguły — bo trudno potem).
  - **External coating** + **abrasion-resistant overlay** dla HDD (np. **3LPE + 3LPP overlay** lub **HDD field joint coatings**).
- **Sizing capabilities**:
  - **Diameter**: DN 50 do DN 1500.
  - **Length**:
    - **Short** (< 300 m) — utility crossings (gas, water, fiber).
    - **Medium** (300-1500 m) — typical pipeline crossings (river, highway, railway).
    - **Long** (1500-5000 m) — major river crossings (Mississippi, Rhine).
    - **Extreme** (> 5000 m) — shore approach offshore pipelines (e.g., 7000 m+ from beach to subsea).
- **Drilling fluids (mud) management**:
  - **Bentonite + additives** (polymers dla shale stability, surfactants dla pipe lubrication).
  - **Mud weight** 8-12 lb/gal (1-1.4 kg/L).
  - **Volume**: 2-5× borehole volume (recycled through pumps + tanks).
  - **Spill risk**: **frac-out** = mud breakthrough surface (rivers, marshes). **Major environmental concern**.
- **Quality control**:
  - **Drilling profile** continuously monitored (depth + heading).
  - **Bore path acceptance**: typowo ±1 m horizontal, ±0.5 m vertical at exit.
  - **Coating integrity** post pull-back — **DCVG (Direct Current Voltage Gradient)** test po install.
  - **Hydrotest** post-install (verification że pipe nie uszkodzony podczas pull-back).
- **HDD vs alternative methods**:
  - **Open-cut trench**: dla short shallow crossings. Disruptive ale tani.
  - **HDD**: medium-long crossings, deep under obstacles.
  - **Microtunneling**: jeszcze precyzyjniejsze, dla short crossings z high-accuracy requirements (sewers, utilities).
  - **Pipe jacking**: large diameter (3-6 m), short, under highways.
- **Cost** (typical):
  - **Open-cut**: $200-1000/m.
  - **HDD**: $1500-15000/m (zależy od length + diameter + soil).
  - **Microtunnel**: $5000-30000/m.
- **Risks specific to HDD**:
  - **Bit deviation** — pilot hole drifts off-target (corrected by reaming).
  - **Cobble / boulder zones** — bit jamming.
  - **Frac-out** (mud breakthrough surface) — environmental fines + cleanup cost.
  - **Pipe damage during pull-back** — coating damage, pipe buckling.
  - **Drilling fluid loss** — into permeable formations (porous limestone, fractured rock).

### AAAAA. Orifice Plate Flow Measurement — installation requirements
- **Orifice plate flow meter** = differential pressure device. Plate z drilled hole installed w pipe, **fluid przechodzi z restriction = pressure drop**. ΔP correlates do flow rate via Bernoulli + discharge coefficient.
- **Construction**:
  - **Plate**: SS 316L typowo, **3-6 mm thick**, sharp upstream edge.
  - **Orifice (bore)**: concentric (centered) lub eccentric (offset, dla wet gas / slurry).
  - **Bore types**:
    - **Concentric (standard)** — hole in geometric center.
    - **Eccentric** — hole at bottom (for gas with condensate) lub top (for liquid with gas).
    - **Segmental** — half-moon shape (for slurries with solids).
  - **Beta ratio (β)**: ratio of orifice diameter (d) to pipe ID (D). **Typically 0.2-0.7**.
- **Critical installation rules**:
  - **Upstream straight pipe**: minimum **10D** without disturbance. Increase do **20-40D** jeśli istnieją upstream disturbances (elbows, valves, reducers).
  - **Downstream straight pipe**: minimum **5D**.
  - **Flow conditioner** może być użyty dla skrócenia straight-pipe requirements (np. zubehör flow conditioner pozwala 2-3D upstream).
- **ISO 5167 / API 14.3** — international + USA standards dla orifice flow measurement requirements.
- **Plate orientation**:
  - **Sharp edge UPSTREAM** — KRYTYCZNE! Sharp edge facing flow direction. Reverse = nieaccurate, większy ΔP loss.
  - **Beveled side downstream** — taper allowed dla flow recovery.
  - **Marking**: typowo **stamped tag w "ear" plate** wskazujący flow direction.
- **Pressure taps**:
  - **Flange taps** — w flange'ach po obu stronach plate'a. 1" upstream, 1" downstream od face plate'a.
  - **Corner taps** — w corner między plate i flange face.
  - **D + D/2 taps** — 1D upstream, D/2 downstream — bardziej accurate dla some applications.
  - **Pipe taps** — 2.5D upstream, 8D downstream (rzadkie).
- **Vena contracta** — najtroższe miejsce stream'u tuż za plate'em (max velocity, min pressure). Pressure drop measurement most accurate przy vena contracta location.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Reversed plate** — sharp edge downstream → 5-15% measurement error.
  - **Damaged sharp edge** — wear / corrosion makes edge dull → error.
  - **Wrong gasket** — gasket protrudes into bore = error + erosion.
  - **Off-center plate** — eccentricity > 0.5% pipe ID = error.
  - **Insufficient straight pipe** — eddies / swirl = error.
- **Discharge coefficient (Cd)**:
  - **0.6** typowo dla concentric plate w long straight pipe.
  - **Varies with Re (Reynolds number)** + β (beta ratio).
- **Calibration**:
  - **Wet calibration** w factory (pipe + plate + flowmeter calibrated as system).
  - **Theoretical calculation** wg ISO 5167 (validated for standard installations).
  - **In-situ calibration** za pomocą reference meter (turbine, Coriolis).
- **Pressure transmitter**:
  - **Differential pressure (DP)** transmitter — measures pressure across plate.
  - **Mounted close to plate** — short impulse lines reduce dead-leg effects.
  - **Impulse lines** sloped 1:10 down do transmitter (drain condensate dla gas service; vent gas dla liquid).
  - **Stub-tap connections** with isolation valves dla maintenance.
- **Manifold (3-valve manifold)**:
  - 2× **block valves** (isolate transmitter from process).
  - 1× **equalizer valve** (zero transmitter under no-flow).
  - Allows safe transmitter calibration without process shutdown.
- **Annular ring (chamber tap)** — alternatywa do flange/corner taps:
  - **Pressure ring** integrated z plate holder.
  - **Multiple peripheral taps** averaged through annular chamber.
  - **Better dla unequal pipe wear** lub erosion in upstream pipe.
- **Beta ratio selection**:
  - **β = 0.5-0.7** dla high flow accuracy (less pressure drop).
  - **β = 0.2-0.4** dla wide flow range (rangeability).
  - **β > 0.7** = poor accuracy (flow too unrestricted).
  - **β < 0.2** = excessive pressure drop, energy waste.
- **Permanent pressure loss**:
  - **(1 − β²)** times measured ΔP = permanent loss.
  - **β = 0.6 → 64% of measured ΔP** is permanent loss.
  - Energy cost — orifice meters expensive to operate vs **Coriolis** (~zero pressure drop).

---

## Iteration 35 — 2026-05-25

### BBBBB. Coriolis Mass Flow Meter — installation orientation rules
- **Coriolis principle**: 2 U-tubes (lub straight) vibrating at natural frequency. Flow przez tube → **Coriolis force** twist tubes proportionally do mass flow rate. Pickoff sensors measure phase shift → flow rate.
- **Zalety vs orifice / turbine / mag-meter**:
  - **Mass flow direct** (nie volumetric → density conversion).
  - **No moving parts** (oprócz vibrating tubes).
  - **No straight-pipe requirements** (immune do upstream disturbances).
  - **Density + viscosity** simultaneously measured.
  - **Accuracy ±0.1%** — najlepsza w industry.
  - **Wide turndown** 100:1 (Coriolis works od 0.1 to 100% scale).
- **Wady**:
  - **Drogie** ($5k-$50k+ per unit, vs $500-2000 orifice plate).
  - **Limited diameter** — typowo do DN 200, max ~DN 350 (large coriolis are huge + expensive).
  - **Sensitive do vibration** — proper mounting essential.
  - **Limited for two-phase flow** (chociaż new models handle better).
- **Orientation rules (KRYTYCZNE dla accuracy)**:
  - **Vertical upward flow** — IDEAL dla wszystkich fluids (forces gas/condensate UP, away from sensor).
  - **Horizontal — housing DOWN (flag down)** dla **liquids** (prevent air pockets w tubes).
  - **Horizontal — housing UP (flag up)** dla **gases** (prevent condensate trapped w tubes).
  - **Vertical downward flow** — **AVOID** (gas accumulates w tubes, accuracy degraded).
  - **Slurry / viscous liquid** — **vertical (flag-like)** to prevent particulate settling.
- **Two-phase flow handling**:
  - **Housing-down vertical orientation** preferred (gas escapes upward, liquid drains down).
  - **Self-draining design** wymaga dla intermittent flow.
- **Mounting requirements**:
  - **Rigid support** — mocowanie do solid pipe rack / structure (no swaying).
  - **Vibration isolation** — keep meter > **0.6 m od large motors, transmitters, vibrating equipment** (cross-talk z EMF).
  - **No mechanical bypass** through meter housing (any external force = measurement error).
- **Pipe alignment**:
  - **Coriolis acts as expansion bellows** — slight misalignment OK, ale **shouldn't bear axial load**.
  - **Adjacent supports** — pipe supported na own brackets, nie via Coriolis.
- **Zero adjustment**:
  - **Per-installation zeroing** — after install + before commissioning.
  - **Process must be**: at operating T + P, with **no flow** (close valves both sides).
  - **Repeat zero monthly** dla high-precision applications.
- **Mass flow vs volumetric**:
  - **Coriolis = mass directly** — independent of density changes (heating, P changes).
  - **Volumetric flow** computed by dividing mass / density (also measured by Coriolis).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Air entrainment** w liquid service — false low reading.
  - **Liquid w gas service** — false high.
  - **Cavitation downstream** (control valve close to meter) — flow disturbance.
  - **External vibration sources** (compressor, pump close to meter) — noise w signal.
- **Custody transfer applications**:
  - **API 5.6** dla Coriolis liquid hydrocarbon metering — accuracy requirements.
  - **OIML R 117** (international legal metrology) — calibration + verification.
  - **Custody transfer = $$ accuracy critical**: oil pipeline tariff disputes, LNG cargo measurement.
- **Vendors / models**: Emerson Micro Motion (market leader), Endress+Hauser Promass, Yokogawa ROTAMASS, Krohne Optimass.

### CCCCC. P91 Welding — chromium-moly steel boiler & high-T piping
- **P91 (ASTM A335 P91 / SA-335 P91)** = **modified 9Cr-1Mo-V steel** = "Creep Strength Enhanced Ferritic" (CSEF) family. Standard dla nowoczesnych boilers, supercritical steam pipe, USC turbines.
- **Chemistry** (typical):
  - **C**: 0.08-0.12% (low, dla weldability).
  - **Cr**: 8.0-9.5% (creep + oxidation resistance).
  - **Mo**: 0.85-1.05% (high-T strength).
  - **V**: 0.18-0.25% (carbide formation, creep strength).
  - **Nb**: 0.06-0.10% (grain refinement).
  - **N**: 0.030-0.070% (carbide stabilization).
- **Microstructure**: **tempered martensite** w as-supplied condition (normalized + tempered N&T heat treatment).
- **Service**:
  - **Temperature**: do **650°C** (1200°F) — high-pressure superheater outlet headers, main steam lines.
  - **Pressure**: do **30 MPa** (300 bar) — supercritical / ultra-supercritical boilers.
  - **Aplikacje**: power plant boilers, HRSG (Heat Recovery Steam Generators), steam piping.
- **Welding challenges** — **najtrudniejsze pipe welding** w industry:
  - **Hardenable** — wszystkie pasy spawania tworzą martenzyt w HAZ → kruche, podatne na cold cracking.
  - **Carbide precipitation** — niedostateczne PWHT = soft type IV creep zone w HAZ.
  - **Type IV cracking** — premature creep failure w fine-grained HAZ region (around 5 mm od fusion line).
- **WELDING PROCEDURE** (mandatory steps):
  1. **Preheat**: **204°C minimum** (Mr martenzyt transformation finish). Higher preheat (250°C) better.
  2. **Interpass T**: 200-300°C maintained (NIE cool below 150°C między pasami — risk crack).
  3. **Filler metal**:
     - **Matching filler**: SMAW E9015-B91 / E9018-B91, GTAW ER90S-B9 / B9L (low Mn variants).
     - **Critical chemistry**: **Mn + Ni ≤ 1.5%** w filler (above = upset Ac1 = post-tempering issue).
