  4. **Hydrogen control**: low-hydrogen electrodes mandatory (≤ 4 mL/100g diffusible H₂). Properly baked + holding ovened.
  5. **Heat input control**: typically **0.5-2.0 kJ/mm** (specific WPS values).
  6. **Hydrogen bake-out** (between welding + PWHT): **300-350°C, 2-4 h hold** — diffuses out residual H₂ before martensite forms.
- **POST-WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)** — **MANDATORY**:
  1. **Cool from welding to ≤ 96°C (Mf)** — ensure full martensite transformation. Pomiar termoparami.
  2. **Heat slowly to PWHT temperature**: **ramp ≤ 50°C/h** (typical), max 100°C/h.
  3. **Soak at 740-760°C**: **typical 760°C ±5°C**. Hold **2-4 h per inch thickness** (typically 3-6 hours total).
  4. **Cool slowly**: **ramp ≤ 100°C/h** to 300°C, then air cool.
- **PWHT temperature sensitivity**:
  - **Below 720°C**: insufficient tempering → hard, brittle weld.
  - **Above 770°C**: **dangerous** — exceeds Ac1 transformation T → re-austenitization → fresh martensite forms on cool-down → **catastrophic loss of properties**.
  - **Ac1 ≈ 800°C** — never cross under heat treatment!
- **Inspection**:
  - **Hardness** post-PWHT: should be **180-265 HV10** (NB 235 HV typical). HAZ NIE może być > 250 HV po PWHT.
  - **NDE 100% RT** lub PAUT.
  - **Macro etching** dla coupon welds — verify martensite + tempering.
- **Common failures**:
  - **Type IV cracking** — w fine-grained HAZ, after few years service. Result of microstructural mismatch + creep.
  - **Hard zones** — incomplete PWHT (under-temped). HRC > 30 = imminent SSC w sour service + lower fatigue life.
  - **Soft zones** — over-temped PWHT (T > Ac1) → re-austenization issue.
  - **Cold cracking** — missed preheat + hydrogen.
- **Process selection** dla P91:
  - **GTAW (TIG)** dla root pass — wymagana czystość, kontrola heat input.
  - **SMAW** dla fill (manual welding flexibility).
  - **GMAW/FCAW** acceptable z proper WPS — advanced controlled-arc modes (Lincoln STT, modified short-circuit) successful.
  - **SAW** automatic dla longitudinal welds w shop fabrication.
- **Other CSEF steels**: P92, P122, P911 — similar challenges. T22 (2.25Cr-1Mo), T11 (1.25Cr-0.5Mo) — easier but lower performance.

### DDDDD. FRP / GRP (Fiberglass Reinforced Plastic) Piping — corrosion-resistant
- **FRP / GRP / RTRP** = composite pipe with **glass fiber reinforcement** + **thermosetting resin matrix**. Pioneer w aggressive chemical environments where metal piping by korodował.
- **Acronyms** (regional):
  - **FRP** (Fiberglass Reinforced Plastic) — USA, Canada.
  - **GRP** (Glass-fiber Reinforced Plastic) — rest of world.
  - **RTRP** (Reinforced Thermosetting Resin Pipe) — official ASTM.
  - **GRE** (Glass Reinforced Epoxy) — specific resin type.
  - **GRV** (Glass Reinforced Vinyl ester) — another type.
- **Construction**:
  - **Resin types**:
    - **Polyester** (isophthalic) — water + general chemicals, niska temp limit (~80°C).
    - **Vinyl ester** — broad chemical resistance, oxidizing acids, do 110°C.
    - **Epoxy** — premier choice dla oil & gas, do 120-140°C.
    - **Furan** — bardzo agresywne acids (HF, conc H₂SO₄), do 150°C.
  - **Glass fibers**:
    - **E-glass** (electrical grade) — standard, dobra wytrzymałość, względnie tanio.
    - **ECR-glass** (E-glass + Cr Resistant) — lepsza dla aggressive environments.
    - **Continuous filament winding** vs **chopped strand mat** — winding zapewnia higher strength + directionality.
- **Manufacturing methods**:
  - **Filament winding** — continuous glass fiber pasma wound at controlled angle (typically ±55°), saturated z resin. STANDARDOWE dla industrial pipe.
  - **Centrifugal casting** — resin + fibers cast w rotating mold, distribute przez centrifugal force.
  - **Hand lay-up / Contact molding** — manual, dla custom shapes (fittings, tanks).
- **Properties** (typical filament-wound epoxy GRP):
  - **Hoop tensile strength**: 150-400 MPa.
  - **Axial tensile strength**: 50-100 MPa (lower bo less axial reinforcement).
  - **Modulus E**: 12-25 GPa (much lower niż steel 200 GPa → big deflection możliwy).
  - **Density**: 1.8-2.0 g/cm³ (lekki, **2-3× lżejszy** niż steel).
  - **CTE**: ~25 ×10⁻⁶ (2× steel) — significant thermal expansion management.
  - **Temperature limit**: zależy od resin, typowo **80-140°C**.
  - **Pressure rating**: do **70 bar** standard, do **300 bar** specialized.
- **Joining methods**:
  - **Adhesive bonded joints** — taper-tape socket, applied z 2K epoxy. Standard dla GRP/FRP. Cure 8-24 h.
  - **Threaded** — like NPT but pipe threads cut w GRP. Limited pressure (low pressure water service).
  - **Flanged** — bolt-on flange (GRP flange lub metal-flange adapter). **Stub-end + lap joint** approach.
  - **Laminated joints** — manual lay-up over joint, hand-saturated resin. Dla repair lub on-site custom.
  - **Mechanical couplings** — Victaulic-style, dla specific FRP grades.
- **Industries / applications**:
  - **Chemical processing** — kwasy (sulfuric, HCl, FeCl₃), bases (NaOH, hipochloriny).
  - **FGD (Flue Gas Desulfurization)** — wet scrubber piping in power plants. **STANDARDOWE FRP application**.
  - **Water + wastewater treatment** — large diameter (DN 1000+) gravity sewers, force mains.
  - **Offshore platforms** — seawater piping, firewater (corrosion-immune w marine environment).
  - **Oil & gas** — produced water, brine injection, gathering systems.
  - **Mining** — slurry pipelines, mill water.
- **Advantages**:
  - **Korozja: zero** dla compatible chemicals.
  - **Lekki**: easier installation, mniejsze support requirements.
  - **Smooth bore**: lower friction loss vs steel (Hazen-Williams C ~150 vs 100-130 dla steel).
  - **No coating maintenance** — material itself is corrosion barrier.
  - **Long life**: 50+ years w properly designed system.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Lower mechanical strength** vs steel (compensated by larger wall).
  - **Brittle failure mode** — sudden burst (vs steel plastic deformation warning).
  - **UV degradation** — outdoor exposed FRP musi mieć **UV-protective coating** lub gel coat.
  - **Fire** — FRP burns. Limited use w fire-rated areas.
  - **Wymagane special training** dla installers.
- **Standards**:
  - **ASTM D2992 / D2997 / D2310** dla RTRP design + testing.
  - **API 15LR / 15HR** dla low-pressure / high-pressure GRP.
  - **ISO 14692** (oil + gas).
  - **AWWA C950** (water service).
- **Installation considerations**:
  - **Support spacing**: 50-75% of steel pipe (lower modulus = more deflection).
  - **Thermal expansion loops**: more needed (higher CTE).
  - **Flange connections**: torque limits much lower than steel (over-torque cracks).
  - **Cutting**: diamond blade, water-cooled. NIE flame cut (resin burns).
  - **Drilling**: low-speed, fresh drill bits (glass abrades drills fast).
  - **Health hazard**: glass fiber dust → respirator + protective clothing.

## Iteration 36 — 2026-05-25

### EEEEE. Link-Seal — Modular Pipe Wall Penetration Sealing
- **Link-Seal** = brand (Garlock / Pyplok / Eaton) modular mechanical seal dla **sealing pipe przejść przez ściany, podłogi, sufity**. Eliminuje stare metody jak lead-oakum joints, mastics, manual flashings.
- **Konstrukcja**:
  - **Modular rubber links** (EPDM, Buna-N, Silicon, Neoprene, EPR) — łańcuch elastomerów połączonych bolt'ami.
  - **Stainless steel bolts + pressure plates** (304 SS lub 316 SS).
  - **Compressible design** — gdy bolt'y dokręcane, rubber expands **radialnie** → tight seal między pipe OD a wall casing ID.
- **Materials per service**:
  - **EPDM** — standard, water + ozone + UV resistant. Temp range −40°C do +120°C.
  - **Buna-N (NBR)** — oils, fuels. Temp do +100°C.
  - **Silicone** (model FD = Fire Damper) — **fire-rated**, do **+204°C** ciągłe lub spike do 400°F (200°C). **2-3 hour fire rating** (UL Classified).
  - **Neoprene** — chemical resistant, alkalies, mild acids.
  - **EPR (Ethylene Propylene Rubber)** — high temperature water/steam (do +150°C).
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Mechanical pipe penetrations** — water, HVAC, fire main, fuel oil przez beton walls/floors.
  - **Electrical conduit penetrations** — sealing EMT/RGS conduit through walls.
  - **Process pipe penetrations** w industrial facilities.
  - **Tank wall penetrations** — fuel tanks, water storage.
  - **Subgrade utility penetrations** — buried entry points to buildings (waterproofing critical).
- **Fire-rated installations** (FD model):
  - **UL Listed** + **FM Approved**.
  - **2-hour fire rating** dla model FD.
  - **3-hour fire rating** dla heavier-duty models.
  - Required wszystkie commercial buildings z fire-rated walls (separation walls, vertical shafts).
- **Pressure rating**:
  - **20 psig (40 ft head)** — typical Link-Seal pressure rating (modest, NOT designed dla high pressure containment).
  - Dla wyższych ciśnień: **specialty high-pressure seals** (PSI-Plus) lub flange spool przez wall.
- **Wall casing options**:
  - **Standard schedule pipe sleeve** — embedded w beton during construction.
  - **Century-Line wall sleeve** — pre-fabricated casing z weep collar.
  - **Cell-Cast disks** — temporary forms removed after concrete cures.
  - **Existing wall penetration** — drilled hole, sleeve installed retroactively.
- **Sizing**:
  - **Pipe OD + casing ID** = annular space.
  - **Link-Seal selection chart** matches pipe OD do casing ID, recommends specific model.
  - **Belt installation**: number of links per circumference = (pipe OD × π) / link length.
- **Installation procedure**:
  1. **Position pipe** centered w wall casing (use pipe centering spacers if needed).
  2. **Wrap Link-Seal belt** wokół pipe w annular space.
  3. **Join ends** of belt — connecting bolts at "end caps".
  4. **Initial hand-tight** bolts.
  5. **Tighten bolts** w **cross pattern** (jak flange) — typowo 30%, 60%, 100% pass'y. Spec'd torque z installation manual (10-25 ft-lbf typically).
  6. **Inspect**: belts should be evenly compressed, no gaps.
  7. **Pressure test** (jeśli applicable) — leakage check pod static head lub mild pressure.
- **Zalety**:
  - **Installation w 1/4 czasu** vs traditional methods.
  - **Re-usable design** — can be loosened, pipe repositioned, re-tightened.
  - **Self-supporting** — eliminates pipe weight load na wall penetration.
  - **Fire + water + gas seal** in single product.
  - **Field-adaptable** — dla różne pipe sizes + casing diameters.
- **Code compliance**:
  - **NFPA 13/20** (sprinkler) — Link-Seal w fire-rated walls.
  - **IBC (International Building Code)** — through-penetration firestop systems.
  - **NEC** (National Electrical Code) — sealed conduit penetrations.

### FFFFF. Carbon Equivalent (CE) — Weldability Assessment & Preheat
- **Carbon Equivalent (CE)** = single number representing **hardenability + crack susceptibility** of steel. Higher CE = harder welding, more preheat required.
- **Najpopularniejsze formuły**:
  - **CE (IIW)** = **C + Mn/6 + (Cr + Mo + V)/5 + (Cu + Ni)/15** [wt %]
    - **Standard formula** adoptowana przez IIW (International Institute of Welding) w 1967.
    - **Dla większości plain carbon + carbon-manganese steel**.
  - **CE (AWS D1.1)** = **C + (Mn + Si)/6 + (Cr + Mo + V)/5 + (Ni + Cu)/15**
    - Slight modification of IIW — includes Si.
    - **Standard dla structural steel** welding w USA.
  - **Pcm (Japanese / Ito-Bessyo)** = **C + Si/30 + (Mn + Cr + Cu)/20 + Ni/60 + Mo/15 + V/10 + 5B**
    - **Dla low-carbon HSLA steels** — bardziej accurate dla nowoczesnych stale.
  - **CEN (Yurioka)** — kompleksowa formuła dla **martenzytyczna prediction w HAZ + Δt8/5** (czas chłodzenia z 800 do 500°C).
  - **CET (German DIN EN 1011-2)** — uses heat input + plate thickness + H₂ level dla preheat calculation.
- **Preheat selection based on CE (IIW)**:

| CE [%] | Preheat | Notes |
|--------|---------|-------|
| < 0.40 | None (≥ 10°C ambient OK) | Dobra weldability, niskie HIC risk |
| 0.40-0.50 | 80-120°C | Moderate — często potrzebny dla thicker section (> 25 mm) |
| 0.50-0.60 | 120-180°C | Mandatory dla większości thicknesses |
| 0.60-0.70 | 180-250°C | High HIC risk, hard HAZ |
| > 0.70 | 250-350°C + PWHT | Very high risk, special procedures |

- **Inne factors wpływające na preheat** (oprócz CE):
  - **Thickness** — thicker = more cooling rate = harder martensite = higher preheat.
  - **Hydrogen content of consumables** — H4 (low) vs H8 (medium) vs H16 (high) electrodes.
  - **Heat input** — higher heat input = slower cooling = less preheat needed.
  - **Restraint** — high-restraint joints (e.g., pipe-to-tubesheet) require more preheat.
  - **Ambient T** — cold ambient = faster cooling = more preheat.
  - **AWS D1.1 Annex** — tabular preheat selection based on CE + thickness combo.
- **Example calculation** — A106 Gr.B carbon steel:
  - C = 0.30%, Mn = 0.90%, Si = 0.10%, Cr = 0.04%, Mo = 0.02%, V = 0.02%, Cu = 0.20%, Ni = 0.10%.
  - **CE (IIW)** = 0.30 + 0.90/6 + (0.04 + 0.02 + 0.02)/5 + (0.20 + 0.10)/15 = 0.30 + 0.15 + 0.016 + 0.02 = **0.486**.
  - Recommendation: **preheat ≥ 80-120°C** dla thick wall, brak preheat dla cienkościennych w ciepły dzień.
- **High-CE materials w pipingu**:
  - **API 5L X65**: CE ~ 0.40-0.43 — minimal preheat.
  - **API 5L X70**: CE ~ 0.43-0.45.
  - **API 5L X80**: CE ~ 0.45-0.48 — typowo 50-100°C preheat.
  - **API 5L X100**: CE ~ 0.50-0.55 — 100-150°C preheat.
  - **HSLA 100**: CE ~ 0.55 — careful PWHT control.
- **Sour service implications (NACE MR0175)**:
  - CE ≤ 0.43% **plus** hardness limit (≤ 22 HRC) — combined requirements.
  - Niska CE alone niewystarczająca — hardness MUST be verified.
- **Low-temperature toughness vs CE**:
  - High CE often correlates z lower toughness — special impact testing dla cryogenic service.
  - A333 Gr.6 (low temp) has CE limit + Charpy impact requirement.

### GGGGG. Welding Gas Mixtures — MIG / TIG selection chart
- **Shielding gas function**:
  - **Protect molten weld pool** from atmosphere (O₂, N₂, H₂O moisture).
  - **Influence arc characteristics** (stability, penetration, droplet transfer mode).
  - **Affect weld metal mechanical properties** (toughness, ductility).
- **Pure gases**:
  - **Argon (Ar)** — inert, dla wszystkich nonferrous (Al, Cu, Ti) + standard dla TIG steel:
    - **Density** > air, sinks → effective coverage.
    - **Low ionization potential** — łatwy arc start.
    - **Smooth arc** — minimal spatter.
    - **Limited penetration** w MIG (wymagana wyższa heat input dla deep welds).
  - **Helium (He)** — inert, dla aluminium MIG + dodatkowy heat input:
    - **Hotter arc** — wyższa heat input (use dla thick aluminium, thick copper).
    - **Less dense than air** — escapes upward → higher flow rates needed.
    - **Wider, flatter bead profile** vs argon.
    - **Expensive** ($$$ vs Ar).
  - **CO₂** — semi-inert, decompose w łuku w O₂ + CO. **TYLKO MIG / FCAW na steel**:
    - **Deep penetration** — high ionization energy.
    - **Cheap** (czysty CO₂ ~30% kosztów Ar).
    - **More spatter** — globular transfer dominant.
    - **NIE dla TIG** (utleniałby tungsten).
    - **NIE dla nonferrous** (CO₂ reaguje z Al, Cu).
- **MIG gas mixtures** (carbon steel):
  - **75% Ar + 25% CO₂ (C-25)** — **NAJPOPULARNIEJSZE** dla mild steel:
    - Smooth arc, decent penetration, acceptable spatter.
    - Stosowane dla: structural, automotive, general fabrication.
    - **Penetration**: between pure Ar (low) and pure CO₂ (high).
  - **90% Ar + 10% CO₂** — thinner / cleaner welds:
    - Sheet metal, thin tube fabrication.
    - **Spray transfer** możliwa.
    - Less spatter than C-25.
  - **85% Ar + 15% CO₂** — middle ground.
  - **80% Ar + 20% CO₂** — slightly more penetration than 90/10, cleaner than C-25.
- **MIG gas mixtures (stainless steel)**:
  - **Tri-mix 90% He + 7.5% Ar + 2.5% CO₂** — premium dla 300-series stainless. Smooth arc, deep penetration, minimal carbide precipitation.
  - **98% Ar + 2% O₂** — alternative, dobre dla thin gauge.
  - **98% Ar + 2% CO₂** — economical, low carbon pickup.
  - **NIE używać** > 5% CO₂ na stainless (raises C content w spoinie → sensitization).
- **MIG gas mixtures (aluminum)**:
  - **100% Ar** — STANDARD dla thin to medium aluminum.
  - **75% Ar + 25% He** — better penetration dla 6-12 mm.
  - **50% Ar + 50% He** — heavy plate (> 12 mm) aluminum.
  - **100% He** — extreme heat input dla very thick plate (rzadko).
- **TIG gas selection**:
  - **100% Ar** — STANDARD dla all materials w TIG (steel, SS, Al, Ti, Cu).
  - **95% Ar + 5% H₂** — **stainless steel TIG** improvement:
    - **Hotter arc** → deep penetration.
    - **Slag reduction** — cleaner appearance.
    - **NIE używać** na carbon steel (HIC risk z H₂).
    - **NIE na titanium** (hydride formation).
  - **75% Ar + 25% He** — thick aluminum TIG, gdy więcej heat input needed.
  - **5N argon (99.999%)** — premium dla TI, super-duplex, BPE.
- **Special mixtures**:
  - **Bright welding mixtures** — proprietary (Linde HELISTAR-A, Praxair StainShield) z H₂ + CO₂ traces.
  - **Hi-purity backing gas** — N₂ (cheap dla CS roots), Ar (universal).
- **Flow rate (recap with gas type)**:
  - **MIG mild steel C-25**: 25-35 CFH (12-17 L/min).
  - **MIG aluminum 100% Ar**: 35-50 CFH (17-24 L/min) — Ar leaves cup szybciej w aluminium application.
  - **TIG steel 100% Ar**: 12-20 CFH (6-9.5 L/min).
  - **TIG aluminum 100% Ar AC**: 17-25 CFH (8-12 L/min).
- **Tank colors** (US OSHA color code):
  - **CO₂** — silver / aluminum tank.
  - **Argon** — green w yellow shoulder.
  - **Helium** — brown.
  - **Mixed gases** — varies, marked w label.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong gas dla process** — np. CO₂ na TIG (tungsten contamination instantly).
  - **CO₂ na stainless TIG** — carbide precipitation, sensitization.
  - **Ar na CS MIG w outdoor wind** — gas blown away, porosity. Trzeba CO₂ lub wind screen.
- **Costs**:
  - **CO₂**: cheapest (~$50 per cylinder).
  - **Ar/CO₂ mix (C-25)**: $150-250.
  - **100% Ar**: $200-400.
  - **Tri-mix stainless**: $400-800.
  - **He, He blends**: $500-1500 (expensive z importowanej helium).

## Iteration 37 — 2026-05-25

### HHHHH. Magnetic Particle Inspection (MPI / MT) — wet vs dry, fluorescent vs visible
- **MPI / MT (Magnetic Particle Testing)** = surface + sub-surface NDE method dla **ferromagnetic materials** (CS, low-alloy, martensitic SS). NIE dla austenitic SS, Al, Cu, Ti (non-magnetic).
- **Zasada**:
  - Apply **magnetic field** do part (wzdłuż lub poprzecznie).
  - **Defect (crack, lack of fusion)** zaburza linie strumienia magnetycznego → tworzy **leakage field** above surface.
  - Apply **ferromagnetic particles** (proszek lub w cieczy) — gromadzą się w leakage field → **wizualnie wskazują defekt**.
- **Particle types (4 kombinacje)**:
  - **Dry visible (color contrast)**:
    - **Suchy proszek** w kolorach (czerwone, szare, żółte — kontrastowe do tła).
    - **Aplikacja**: drying powder bulb / sprayer.
    - **Białe tło (white contrast paint)** często aplikowane PRZED particles.
    - **Stosowane**: field inspection na poziomie shop, weld surface visible inspection. Rough surfaces, elevated temp.
    - **Sensitivity**: MEDIUM — wykrywa cracks ≥ 0.025 mm (1 mil) głębokie.
  - **Dry fluorescent**:
    - Rzadko używane — większość dry method = visible.
  - **Wet visible (color contrast)**:
    - **Particles in carrier fluid** (woda + corrosion inhibitor lub light oil).
    - **Aerosol can** lub spray gun.
    - **Better dla fine cracks** — particles migrate freely do leakage field.
    - **Sensitivity**: HIGHER — wykrywa cracks ≥ 0.013 mm (0.5 mil).
  - **Wet fluorescent (FMP — Fluorescent Magnetic Particle)**:
    - **Particles z fluorescent coating** w carrier fluid.
    - **Inspection pod UV-A light (365 nm "black light")** w darkened booth.
    - **HIGHEST SENSITIVITY** — wykrywa cracks ≥ 0.008 mm (0.3 mil).
    - **Stosowane**: aerospace, nuclear, critical pressure vessels.
- **Magnetization methods**:
  - **Yoke (najczęstsza dla pipe welds)**:
    - **AC yoke** lub **DC yoke** — portable electromagnetic, podkowowa kształt.
    - **AC**: surface defects only (skin effect).
    - **DC**: subsurface defects do ~6 mm głębokości.
    - **NIE umieść yoke direct na pool** — yoke między dwoma legs.
    - **2 perpendicular orientations** — sprawdzić raz wzdłuż weld + raz poprzecznie (catch all crack orientations).
  - **Prods (electrical contacts)**:
    - **Direct current przez part** między dwoma probe tips.
    - **Higher current capability** — sub-surface deeper.
    - **Risk arc strike** — może uszkodzić powierzchnię. Limited use.
  - **Coil / Solenoid** — encircling coil dla cylindrical part.
  - **Permanent magnets** — backup / field, mniej common dla pipe welds.
- **Procedure** (typowo dla pipe weld):
  1. **Surface cleanup** — wire brush, remove scale, paint (≤ 0.05 mm thickness film acceptable). Solvent wipe.
  2. **Apply white contrast paint** (jeśli używamy visible particles na ciemnym CS).
  3. **Position yoke** wzdłuż weld (longitudinal orientation).
  4. **Energize yoke** + **apply particles** (powder dry lub spray wet).
  5. **Inspect** — krytyczne miejsca: weld toes, ripples, undercut zones.
  6. **Mark indications** marker permanent.
  7. **Reposition yoke** poprzecznie do weld (transverse orientation).
  8. **Re-energize + re-apply particles**.
  9. **Inspect second time** w nowej orientacji.
  10. **Demagnetize part** (jeśli wymagane — most aplikacje NIE wymagają).
  11. **Cleanup** — wipe particles + paint.
- **Procedure standards**:
  - **ASTM E709** — Standard Guide for MT.
  - **ASTM E1444** — Standard Practice for MT.
  - **ASME Section V Article 7** — pressure equipment + piping.
  - **EN ISO 17638** — non-destructive testing of welds.
  - **AWS D1.1 Section 6** — structural welding MT acceptance.
- **Acceptance criteria** (typowo):
  - **ASME B31.3 §344.3**: **brak relevant indications** (relevant = ≥ 1.6 mm linear, ≥ 3 mm rounded).
  - **AWS D1.1**: depend on stress category, allowable accumulated 6-50 mm w 6"length.
  - **Crack-like indications**: **always rejected** regardless of size.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Tylko ferromagnetic materials** — NIE dla SS 304/316 austenitic.
  - **Surface + max ~6 mm subsurface** (DC yoke).
  - **Skill-dependent** — operator interpretation.
  - **Demagnetization** może być wymagana dla parts going to further machining (residual magnetism affects machining + wear).

### IIIII. Rigging Sling Capacity — wire rope, chain, synthetic + lift planning
- **Sling capacity** = **Working Load Limit (WLL)** = safe load dla danej sling geometry. Critical dla **safe lifting** spool, valve, equipment.
- **Sling types**:
  - **Wire rope sling**:
    - **Construction**: 6×19, 6×37 (most common), IWRC (Independent Wire Rope Core) lub FC (Fiber Core).
    - **Design Factor (DF) = 5** dla standard wire rope sling (WLL = MBL / 5).
    - **Sizes**: 1/4" do 4" diameter wire rope.
    - **Aplikacje**: general industrial, oil & gas, construction.
  - **Chain sling (Grade 80 / 100 / 120)**:
    - **Higher strength per diameter** vs wire rope.
    - **Grade 80**: standard, **Grade 100**: 25% stronger, **Grade 120**: 50% stronger.
    - **Resistant to abrasion, sharp edges, high temperature**.
    - **Aplikacje**: heavy industrial, hot environments (jeszcze użyteczne do 200°C).
  - **Synthetic sling**:
    - **Nylon, Polyester webbing** (flat strap) lub **roundsling** (tubular).
    - **Soft on load** — NIE damage delicate surfaces (machined parts, painted equipment).
    - **Color-coded WLL**: green=2 ton, yellow=3 ton, etc.
    - **NIE dla high temp** (degrades > 90°C dla nylon, > 100°C polyester).
    - **NIE dla sharp edges** — corner protectors needed.
- **Hitch configurations** (sposób podpięcia):
  - **Vertical / single-leg** — sling visi pionowo, full WLL.
  - **Choker hitch** — sling owinięta wokół load, hooked back to itself → **75% WLL** (friction loss at choker point).
  - **Basket hitch** — sling pod load, both ends do crane hook → **2× WLL** (z każdej strony), but only **at vertical angle**.
  - **Multi-leg bridle** (2-, 3-, 4-leg) — leg force depend on **horizontal angle** (sin trig).
- **Sling angle effect** (KRYTYCZNY):

| Sling angle (from horizontal) | Capacity factor |
|-------------------------------|----------------:|
| 90° (vertical) | 100% |
| 60° | 86.6% |
| 45° | 70.7% |
| 30° | **50%** |
| Below 30° | **NIE STOSOWAĆ** |

- **Reduction reason**: lower angle → wyższa tension w sling leg dla samego load.
- **Example calculation**:
  - 2-leg bridle, 5000 kg load.
  - **Angle 60°**: każdy leg widzi 5000/(2×0.866) = 2887 kg per leg.
  - **Angle 30°**: każdy leg widzi 5000/(2×0.5) = 5000 kg per leg (= całość!).
  - **Angle 15°**: każdy leg widzi 5000/(2×0.259) = 9650 kg per leg (= almost 2× load!).
- **Pre-lift inspection checklist** dla each sling:
  - **Wire rope**: broken wires (limit: 6 in one lay, 3 in one strand), kinks, crushing, corrosion.
  - **Chain**: stretched links (≥ 5% elongation = trash), nicks, cracks, twist.
  - **Synthetic**: cuts, abrasion, UV degradation, melting from heat, chemical damage.
  - **Hardware**: hooks, shackles, master links — same inspection.
- **Tag / identification**:
  - **Capacity tag** — required by OSHA 1910.184. Permanent or laminated tag with: WLL, manufacturer, serial #, last inspection date.
  - **Missing tag** = **destroy sling** (no provenance, unsafe).
- **OSHA / ASME standards**:
  - **OSHA 1910.184** — slings (USA general industry).
  - **OSHA 1926.251** — slings (construction).
  - **ASME B30.9** — Slings standard.
  - **ASME B30.20** — Below-the-Hook Lifting Devices.
- **Lift planning**:
  - **Lift plan document** — required dla critical lifts (heavy, high consequence).
  - **Engineered lift plan** > 75% crane capacity OR awkward load OR multi-crane.
  - **Inclusions**: load weight, center of gravity, crane size + position, rigging selection, swing path, hazards.
  - **Pre-lift meeting** — supervisor + rigger + signalman + crane operator.
- **Hand signals** — universal OSHA signals dla cranes:
  - **Hoist**: index finger pointing UP, hand circling.
  - **Lower**: index finger pointing DOWN, circling.
  - **Boom up / down**: thumb up / down.
  - **Trolley right / left**: hand horizontal, thumb pointing direction.
  - **STOP**: fist closed, raised.
  - **Emergency stop**: both arms swung in front of body.

### JJJJJ. Electrode Amperage Chart — SMAW (Stick) Settings Guide
- **General rule**: **~1 amp per 0.001"** of electrode diameter dla mild steel flat position, DCEP.
- **Common electrode diameters** + amperage:

| Diameter | E6010 (DCEP) | E6011 (AC/DCEP) | E7018 (DCEP/AC) |
|----------|-------------:|----------------:|-----------------:|
| 1/16" (1.6 mm) | 20-40 A | 25-45 A | — |
| 3/32" (2.4 mm) | 40-85 A | 50-90 A | 70-100 A |
| 1/8" (3.2 mm) | 75-125 A | 80-130 A | 90-140 A |
| 5/32" (4.0 mm) | 110-170 A | 120-175 A | 125-220 A |
| 3/16" (4.8 mm) | 140-210 A | 150-215 A | 170-275 A |
| 7/32" (5.6 mm) | 170-250 A | 180-260 A | 210-320 A |
| 1/4" (6.4 mm) | 210-320 A | 220-325 A | 275-430 A |

- **Position adjustment**:
  - **Flat (1G/1F)**: standard chart values.
  - **Horizontal (2G/2F)**: same or slight reduction.
  - **Vertical-up (3G/5G uphill)**: **reduce 10-20%** (puddle control).
  - **Vertical-down (5G downhill, pipeline)**: **same or slight increase** (gravity helps).
  - **Overhead (4G/6G top)**: **reduce 10-20%** + smaller electrode preferred.
- **Polarity rules**:
  - **DCEP (DC reverse polarity, electrode +)**: deepest penetration. **STANDARD dla 7018, 6010**.
  - **DCEN (DC straight polarity, electrode −)**: less penetration, faster melt-off. Rare dla SMAW, common dla GTAW.
  - **AC**: between DCEP and DCEN. **OK dla 6011, 7018**. **NIE dla 6010** (6010 designed for DC).
- **Material thickness vs electrode diameter**:

| Plate thickness | Recommended electrode diameter |
|-----------------|-------------------------------:|
| ≤ 3 mm | 1/16" - 3/32" |
| 3-6 mm | 3/32" - 1/8" |
| 6-10 mm | 1/8" - 5/32" |
| 10-20 mm | 5/32" - 3/16" |
| > 20 mm | 3/16" - 1/4" |

- **Electrode classification (AWS A5.1)**:
  - **E[strength][position][coating]**.
  - **E6010**: 60 ksi tensile, all positions, cellulose sodium coating (DC only).
  - **E6011**: 60 ksi, all pos, cellulose potassium (AC + DC).
  - **E6013**: 60 ksi, all pos, rutile potassium (DC + AC). Easy strike, smooth weld.
  - **E7014**: 70 ksi, iron powder rutile. Higher deposition rate.
  - **E7018**: 70 ksi, all pos, iron powder low-hydrogen (DC + AC).
  - **E7024**: 70 ksi, flat/horiz only, iron powder heavy. High deposition, NO position welding.
- **Voltage** (not directly controlled — function of arc length):
  - Typical arc voltage: **20-30 V** dla mild steel SMAW.
  - **Long arc** = higher voltage, more spatter.
  - **Short arc** = lower voltage, may stick electrode.
- **Practical setup tips**:
  - **Start lower amp** dla test bead, then adjust up.
  - **Listen do arc sound**: smooth "bacon-frying" = right amp; popping = too low; harsh hissing = too high.
  - **Check bead profile**: smooth, even ripple = right amp; high crown narrow = too low; flat wide spreading = too high.
  - **Crater fill** technique: stop, hold arc, retreat at end of bead.
- **Polarity testing** (jeśli nie pewny):
  - **DCEP** (electrode +): deeper bead, electrode burns slower.
  - **DCEN** (electrode −): wider bead, electrode burns faster, may smoke more dla 7018.

## Iteration 38 — 2026-05-25

### KKKKK. Pump-Motor Shaft Alignment — laser, dial, rim & face methods
- **Shaft alignment** = bringing pump shaft + motor shaft do **collinear position** (within tolerance) PRZED running. Misalignment = #1 cause of pump bearing/seal failure.
- **Misalignment types**:
  - **Parallel offset (rim misalignment)** — shafts parallel ale nie współliniowe (offset w X lub Y).
  - **Angular misalignment (face misalignment)** — shafts intersect at angle (NIE parallel).
  - **Combined** — kombinacja obu.
  - **Soft foot** — baseplate nieodpowiednio podparta, foot rocks gdy bolt'y loosened.
- **Alignment tolerances** (industry standard rotating equipment, typical for direct-coupled motor-pump):

| RPM | Excellent | Acceptable | Maximum |
|-----|----------:|-----------:|--------:|
| ≤ 1800 | 0.05 mm | 0.10 mm | 0.15 mm |
| 1800-3600 | 0.025 mm | 0.05 mm | 0.10 mm |
| > 3600 | 0.015 mm | 0.025 mm | 0.05 mm |

- **Maximum shaft runout** (out-of-true): 0.1 mm (0-1500 RPM), 0.05 mm (1500-3000 RPM), 0.025 mm (> 3000 RPM).
- **Methods of alignment**:
  - **Rim & Face Dial Indicator** (classical):
    - **2 dial indicators**: jeden na rim (sprawdza parallel offset), jeden na face (angular).
    - **Rotation both shafts together** (coupled lub manually) — measurements every 90°.
    - **Readings**: T (top), B (bottom), L (left), R (right).
    - **Calculations**: (T+B)/2 = vertical offset; (L+R)/2 = horizontal offset.
    - **Time**: 2-4 hours dla skilled millwright.
    - **Accuracy**: ±0.025 mm (limited by indicator + bar deflection).
  - **Reverse Dial Indicator** (improved classical):
    - **2 indicators**: jeden czyta motor shaft od pump side, drugi pump shaft od motor side.
    - **Eliminates bar sag** (largest error source w rim & face).
    - **Better accuracy** than rim & face.
  - **Laser alignment** (modern standard):
    - **Two laser/detector pods** mounted na shafts.
    - **Computer-controlled** — rotation, calculations, real-time feedback.
    - **Accuracy**: ±0.001-0.005 mm.
    - **Time**: 30-60 min total.
    - **Step-by-step guidance**: "Add 0.15 mm under front motor foot, 0.08 mm under rear..."
    - **Vendors**: Pruftechnik Rotalign, Fluke 830, SKF TKSA, Easy-Laser, Ludeca.
- **Pre-alignment checks**:
  - **Baseplate condition** — bez pęknięć, deterioration, grout intact, **supported under entire area**.
  - **Anchor bolts torque** — equal torque na wszystkich.
  - **Soft foot check** — KRYTYCZNY!
    - Loosen each foot one-by-one (only one at a time).
    - Measure with dial indicator how much foot rises (or pump shaft drops).
    - **Limit**: ≤ 0.05 mm soft foot na każdym foot.
    - **Correction**: shim under affected foot.
  - **Pipe strain** — disconnect pipe from pump nozzles podczas alignment.
    - Pipe pushing on pump = forced misalignment after start-up.
    - **API 686** wymaga pipe strain checks (≤ 0.05 mm shaft movement when piping disconnected/reconnected).
  - **Thermal growth compensation** — pumps z hot service (np. boiler feed) **rosną w running condition**:
    - Cold align z **negative offset** dla compensate growth.
    - **Example**: pump shaft grows 0.3 mm vertical during operation; cold-aligned ze shaft 0.3 mm low → runs collinear hot.
- **Procedure** (laser alignment):
  1. **Pre-checks** (baseplate, soft foot, pipe strain).
  2. **Mount laser/detector pods** na shafts.
  3. **Initial dimensions** entered (distance laser-to-laser, foot-to-coupling).
  4. **Rotate shafts 360°** (manually lub motor barring).
  5. **Computer calculates** offset values.
  6. **Adjust motor (preferred)** — moveable component. Pump rzadko ruszany (anchored z pipe).
  7. **Shims under motor feet** (correct vertical).
  8. **Horizontal slides** — push/pull motor sideways (correct horizontal).
  9. **Verify** — re-measure, repeat if outside tolerance.
- **Shim materials**:
  - **Stainless steel shims** (preferred) — corrosion-resistant, precise thickness.
  - **Carbon steel shims** — economic ale rusty.
  - **AVOID**: brass (creeps), washers (stack of 5+ unstable), copper (galvanic).
  - **Pre-cut shim packs** (0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0 mm thicknesses) — Loctite Smart Shim, McMaster.
- **Post-alignment**:
  - **Re-torque anchor bolts** (per spec).
  - **Final verification** — rotate again, check final readings.
  - **Record alignment log** — initial vs final, shim count, operator signature.
  - **Couplings** installed only AFTER alignment verified.

### LLLLL. Boroscope / Videoscope — BPE Orbital Weld Inspection
- **Boroscope (rigid) / Videoscope (flexible)** = optical inspection tool wnętrza pipe / vessels / casted parts. **MANDATORY dla ASME BPE pharma piping** — 100% orbital weld inspection.
- **Construction**:
  - **Insertion tube** — flexible (videoscope) lub rigid (boroscope), 2-30 mm OD.
  - **Camera tip** — CMOS / CCD sensor, LED illumination.
  - **Articulation joint** — 2-way (up/down) lub 4-way (all directions), 130-180° bend radius.
  - **Display** — LCD/LED monitor (4.5"-10"), often touchscreen.
  - **Recording** — video + still photo capture na SD card lub USB.
- **Resolution**:
  - **Industrial/general**: 320×240 to 640×480 pixels.
  - **Pharma BPE / nuclear**: **minimum 640×480**, preferowane **1280×720** (HD) lub **1920×1080** (Full HD).
  - **4K resolution** dostępne (Olympus IPLEX, Karl Storz) — premium dla forensic / regulatory.
- **ASME BPE requirements**:
  - **100% boroscope inspection** of orbital welds (vs sampling dla regular piping).
  - **Each weld documented** — photo + ID number + welder stamp.
  - **Records archived** dla life of plant (FDA GMP requirement).
  - **Inspection criteria**:
    - **Penetration**: pełny przepływ, ID koncentryczny.
    - **Discoloration**: silver/gold OK; blue/purple = excessive heat; black/sugar = no purge.
    - **Geometry**: hi-lo ≤ 15% wall, NO concavity > 10% wall, NO convexity > 10% wall.
    - **Contamination**: brak tungsten inclusions, brak particle embedding.
- **Inspection technique**:
  1. **Insert scope** w pipe end (typowo przez ferrule end z removed clamp).
  2. **Articulate camera tip** dla full circumferential view of weld.
  3. **Rotate around** — można scope inspect every 30° + zoom dla detail.
  4. **Photo + annotate** każdy weld.
  5. **Compare** z reference (acceptable / unacceptable image library).
  6. **Sign-off** by Level II VT inspector.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Diameter limit** — small ID pipe (DN ≤ 15) trudne do navigate.
  - **Length limit** — typowo 1-3 m insertion (longer needs special long scopes).
  - **Bends** — flexible only do 180° tight radius (rigid boroscope ograniczone).
  - **Lighting issues** — odbicia od polished SS ID może maskować defects.
- **Common vendors**:
  - **Olympus IPLEX GAir** — premium, full HD, robotic articulation.
  - **Evident (Olympus medical brand)**.
  - **GE / Mentor Visual iQ**.
  - **Karl Storz Endoscopy**.
  - **Mil-Spec Industries** — military-grade ruggedized.
- **Image quality factors**:
  - **Resolution + sensor quality** — main driver.
  - **Illumination intensity** — high-output LED arrays (2000-5000 lumens).
  - **Camera tip cleanliness** — smudges destroy image.
  - **Camera focus** — fixed lub variable focus.
- **3D scanning videoscope** (advanced) — Olympus Stereo Measurement:
  - Two lenses + structured light → 3D point cloud of weld profile.
  - **Quantitative measurement** of defect depth + width (calibrated).
  - **Acceptance ujmowane numerycznie**, nie subjective.
- **Cost**:
  - **Entry-level industrial**: $300-2000 (handheld, USB-connected).
  - **Professional**: $5,000-15,000 (HD, articulation).
  - **BPE / pharma-grade**: $25,000-100,000+ (4K, software analysis).

### MMMMM. ITP (Inspection & Test Plan) — Piping QC Document
- **ITP (Inspection and Test Plan)** = document defining **WHAT inspections** + **WHEN** + **BY WHOM** + **TO WHAT CRITERIA** dla piping construction. Project-specific QC blueprint.
- **Structure** (typical ITP):
  - **Activity list** — each construction step (material receiving, cut/bevel, fit-up, welding, NDE, hydrotest, painting, insulation).
  - **Inspection requirements** — what to check per step.
  - **Method** — visual, dimensional, RT, UT, etc.
  - **Acceptance criteria** — code reference (ASME B31.3, AWS D1.1, project spec).
  - **Frequency** — 100%, 10%, sampling.
  - **Responsibility** — contractor QC, client, third-party inspector (TPI), regulatory body.
  - **Intervention type** — H (Hold), W (Witness), S (Surveillance), R (Review).
  - **Documentation** — record type (checklist, report, certificate).
- **Intervention types** (KRYTYCZNE pojęcia):
  - **Hold Point (H)** — **WORK STOPS** until designated party (typowo TPI lub Client) signs off:
    - Used dla critical irreversible operations.
    - Cannot proceed bez release.
    - **Examples**: raw material receiving, post-weld NDE, hydrotest, final acceptance.
  - **Witness Point (W)** — **NOTIFICATION required** to designated party, but work proceeds even if absent:
    - Authority MAY attend (their discretion).
    - **Examples**: fit-up inspection, in-progress welding, intermediate dimensional check.
    - **Notification time**: typowo **48-72 h advance**.
  - **Surveillance Point (S)** — random inspection at authority's option, no advance notice.
  - **Review (R)** — document review only, no physical inspection at activity.
- **Typical piping ITP activities** (high-level):
  1. **Material receiving inspection** (HOLD): MTR verification, PMI, visual.
  2. **Cutting + beveling**: dimensional verification.
  3. **Fit-up inspection** (WITNESS): root gap, mismatch, alignment, square cut.
  4. **Welder qualification**: WPS valid, welder stamp current.
  5. **Welding monitoring** (SURVEILLANCE): WPS adherence, electrode storage, preheat.
  6. **Visual inspection** (HOLD): each weld 100%.
  7. **NDE — RT/UT/MT/PT** (HOLD lub WITNESS depending criticality): per project plan.
  8. **PWHT** (HOLD if applicable): chart record review.
  9. **Hardness testing** (HOLD if NACE service): post-PWHT verification.
  10. **Dimensional control** — pre-fab spool tolerances.
  11. **Pressure test / hydrotest** (HOLD): test pressure, hold time, leak inspection.
  12. **Coating + painting** (WITNESS): surface prep, DFT, holiday detection.
  13. **Insulation install** (SURVEILLANCE).
  14. **Final inspection / handover** (HOLD): punch list, system test, documentation review.
- **Code references** dla piping ITP:
  - **ASME B31.3** §341 (Examination), §345 (Testing) — process piping.
  - **ASME B31.1** dla power piping.
  - **API 1104** dla pipelines.
  - **AWS D1.1** dla structural welds.
  - **Project specifications** — owner overlay (usually more stringent than code).
- **ITP responsibilities** (typical):
  - **Contractor QC** — internal inspector, day-to-day operation.
  - **Owner's representative** — project supervisor's QC engineer.
  - **TPI (Third Party Inspector)** — independent (Bureau Veritas, DNV-GL, TÜV, Lloyd's Register, SGS) — verifies on behalf of regulator lub insurance.
  - **AHJ (Authority Having Jurisdiction)** — government inspector (national boiler inspector, pressure equipment authority).
- **Document control**:
  - **ITP Rev 0** = Issued For Comment (IFC).
  - **ITP Rev 1+** = approved revisions.
  - **Project records** — each activity tagged z ITP item number for traceability.
- **Practical pipefitter aspects**:
  - **Witness Point timing** — schedule inspector's arrival; don't proceed past activity bez notification.
  - **Hold Point release**: pisemny release required przed continuing (signature, date, on traveler / checklist).
  - **Pre-construction meetings** — contractor explains ITP do crew. Crew should understand hold points (not just complete work bez signature!).
- **ITP examples / templates**:
  - **Power industry**: API piping pressure-test sections, ASME B31.1 power piping.
  - **Oil & gas**: API 5L pipe + API 1104 welding sections.
  - **Pharma**: ASME BPE + cGMP overlay.

## Iteration 39 — 2026-05-25

### NNNNN. Bug-O System / Pipe Welding Tractor — automated track welding
- **Bug-O System** = founded 1970s w Canonsburg, PA — pioneer w **mechanized welding tractors** (carriages). Automatyzuje wszystkie procesy welding/cutting/grinding tools. **Standardowe** w heavy industrial fabrication.
- **Concept**: motorized **carriage (tractor)** carries welding gun (lub torch, lub grinder) wzdłuż **track** (rail) lub **trackless** path. Operator sets parameters → tractor consistent travel speed → uniform welds.
- **Advantages vs hand welding**:
  - **Consistent travel speed** — eliminuje welder fatigue effect na bead quality.
  - **Higher deposition rate** — tractor utrzymuje optimum arc time vs hand stops/starts.
  - **Better penetration** — constant heat input.
  - **Reduced operator skill** dla simple long welds (pipefitter not full welder).
  - **Safety** — operator further from hot zone, less smoke exposure.
- **Track types**:
  - **Rigid rail (straight)** — aluminum lub steel, **3-12 m sections** linked. Dla long horizontal welds (storage tanks, ships).
  - **Flexible rail (curved)** — magnetic attachment, follows pipe curvature. Dla **pipe circumferential welds** (5G/6G).
  - **Magnetic track** — clamps na ferrous workpiece (CS, low-alloy).
  - **Vacuum track** — non-magnetic materials (SS, aluminum), suction cups attach do surface.
- **Bug-O products** (main lines):
  - **Modular Drive System (MDS)** — base carriage z multiple drives + attachments. Configurable dla różnych applications.
  - **GO-FER IV** — portable rugged tractor, ALL positions włącznie z overhead.
  - **Piper Bug Pipe Welding System** — dedicated dla pipe 5G/6G welds, programmable, integrated z Lincoln Electric Invertec.
  - **Universal Bug-O-Matic (BUG-5700/5702/5704)** — combined drive + oscillator w one housing. Lightweight (one operator).
  - **Track Cutting Machines** — dla oxy-fuel / plasma cutting (straight cuts).
  - **Trackless Welding Carriage** — magnetic wheels follow workpiece directly, no rail needed.
- **Oscillator function**:
  - **Programmable** side-to-side weave pattern.
  - **Amplitude**: 0-100 mm typically.
  - **Frequency**: 0-60 cycles/min.
  - **Dwell time** at ends (slow at edge dla full fusion):
    - **Symmetric dwell** dla square welds.
    - **Asymmetric dwell** dla compensate gravity (vertical-up welding).
- **Pipe welding application** (typical 6G overhead pipe):
  1. **Position pipe** w 5G/6G orientation.
  2. **Wrap magnetic track** wokół pipe (clamp at start/end).
  3. **Mount tractor** on track + welding gun w torch holder.
  4. **Set parameters**: travel speed, oscillation amplitude/frequency/dwell, voltage, wire feed (jeśli MIG).
  5. **Start program** — tractor moves wokół pipe, depositing weld bead.
  6. **Auto-shutoff** at end of programmed path.
  7. **Inspect** root pass + ground if needed.
  8. **Re-program** dla fill/cap passes (different parameters).
- **Heights control + seam tracking** (advanced MDS features):
  - **Automatic height control (AHC)** — sensors maintain tip-to-work distance constant w CV mode.
  - **Seam tracking** — laser/visual sensors keep gun on joint center even w wobbled tracks.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Complex 3D paths** (twisted, irregular pipe) — manual welding still better.
  - **Tight access** — Bug-O may not fit w pipe rack interference areas.
  - **Setup time** — 15-30 min per weld (track mount, parameter programming). Versus 5 min hand setup → only beneficial dla longer welds.
- **Vendors / competitors**:
  - **Bug-O Systems** — market leader.
  - **Abicor Binzel ABICAR** — German welding tractors.
  - **Lincoln Electric Cruiser** — pipe-focused.
  - **Magnatech** — premium orbital pipe systems (overlap z Bug-O dla larger pipe).
  - **Gullco** — Canadian, KAT (Krawler) series.
- **Cost**:
  - **Basic tractor + track**: $3,000-15,000.
  - **Full MDS with AHC + seam tracking**: $20,000-50,000.
  - **Orbital head dedicated pipe welder**: $15,000-100,000+.
- **Productivity gains** dla typical user: **30-100% weld speed increase** + reduced rework. Payback dla shop with regular long welds: 6-18 months.

### OOOOO. Pre-Commissioning Oil Flush — API 686 lube oil + hydraulic systems
- **Oil flush** = pre-commissioning **mechanical cleaning** of oil piping (lube oil, seal oil, hydraulic, control oil) via **circulating clean oil** at high flow + filtration → removes manufacturing residues, weld slag, dirt, water.
- **Cel**:
  - **Protect turbomachinery bearings** — typowo bearing clearance 0.05-0.15 mm; particle > clearance → bearing damage.
  - **Protect hydraulic actuators** — fine clearances (0.005-0.025 mm) — sensitive.
  - **Eliminate corrosion products** (rust scale from manufacturing).
- **API 614 / API 686** standards:
  - **API 614** — specification for lubrication, shaft-sealing, control-oil systems.
  - **API 686** — Recommended Practice for **pre-commissioning** machinery installations.
  - **Cleanliness target** zwykle wg ISO 4406 (np. 18/16/13 = max particle counts at 4 µm/6 µm/14 µm size ranges).
  - **NAS 1638** alternative cleanliness rating.
- **Pre-flush preparation**:
  - **Manual cleaning** lube oil tank wnętrza — wire brush, vacuum, wipe.
  - **Inspect** + remove visible scale, weld slag, foreign objects.
  - **Air/steam blow** all interconnecting piping (with target plates jak iter 32) PRZED final assembly.
  - **Bypass machinery bearings** — install **temporary jumper piping** bypassing pump/turbine/compressor bearings.
  - **Disconnect control valves** — temporary spool replaces.
- **Flush procedure**:
  1. **Charge lube oil** do main reservoir, filtered through fine filter (≤ 25 µm).
  2. **Start auxiliary lube oil pump** at moderate flow.
  3. **Heat oil** to typowo **65-95°C** (lower viscosity = better suspension of particles).
  4. **Pump discharge T controlled** at **155°F (68°C) ±5°F** typowo.
  5. **Circulate** through full piping circuit + back to tank via temporary jumpers.
  6. **Periodic filter changes** — coarse pre-filters (50 µm) → medium (25 µm) → fine (10 µm) → ultra-fine (5 µm).
  7. **Sample at fine filter** — particle count analysis (ISO 4406, laser particle counter).
  8. **Repeat until cleanliness target met** — typically requires 8-72 hour flush + multiple filter changes.
  9. **Loop-by-loop progression**:
     - **Stage 1**: main reservoir + immediate piping.
     - **Stage 2**: extends to first bearing housing (still bypassed).
     - **Stage 3**: continues to second bearing housing.
     - Continue until full circuit clean.
- **Critical considerations**:
  - **Flow rate during flush**: typowo **3-5× operating flow** (vigorous flushing dislodges particles).
  - **Turbulence**: Re > 4000 minimum (turbulent flow keeps particles suspended).
  - **Hot/cold cycling** — temperatura cycling between 30°C i 90°C helps loosen scale through thermal expansion.
  - **Brass swabbing fittings** — installed at critical bearing inlet locations dla in-line particle sampling.
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - **ISO 18/16/13** (typical industrial lube oil).
  - **ISO 17/15/12** (premium for turbomachinery).
  - **ISO 14/12/9** (servo hydraulic — extreme cleanliness).
- **Documentation**:
  - **Flush log** — start time, T, flow, sample IDs.
  - **Particle count reports** — each sample analyzed.
  - **Filter records** — # filters changed, last filter post-flush DP.
  - **Acceptance certificate** signed by client + supplier.
- **Post-flush activities**:
  - **Replace bearing bypass jumpers** z real machinery components.
  - **Replace control valves**.
  - **Final filter installation** (operating filter spec).
  - **Lube oil change** — fresh charge (flushed oil discarded jako contaminated).
  - **Pre-startup checklist** — interlocks, alarms, instrumentation.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Skipping pre-flush mechanical cleaning** — flush won't remove gross debris.
  - **Insufficient turbulence** — laminar flow lets particles settle.
  - **Cold flush only** — solids don't suspend well, contaminants remain.
  - **Permanent filter installed during flush** — gets immediately fouled, must be replaced post-flush.

### PPPPP. Flame Straightening — heat distortion correction
- **Flame straightening (heat straightening)** = **controlled local heating + cooling** of welded structures dla correcting distortion. Heated zone wants to expand ale **restrained by cool surrounding material** → after cooling, **plastic shrinkage** w heated zone pulls structure back into shape.
- **Underlying principle**:
  - **Metal expands when heated** — w restraint, expansion forced into compression (upper yield stress).
  - **Compression plastically deforms** metal w heated zone.
  - **Cooling shrinkage** does NOT recover full expansion (plastic deformation persistent).
  - **Net result**: heated zone shrinks slightly relative to original size.
- **Heat patterns** (apply w specific shapes dla różnych correction):
  - **Spot heat** — okrągłe spot ~25-50 mm dia. Heated do **600-650°C** (dark cherry red w mild steel). Spot shrinks dia, pulling material radially inward. **Standardowe dla local distortion correction**.
  - **Linear heat** — straight line długości ~50-150 mm. Heats wzdłuż line, line shrinks longitudinally. **Used dla beam bowing correction**.
  - **Wedge / V-heat** — wedge-shaped pattern, narrow end towards inside of curve. Powerful shrinkage gradient → bend correction. **Standard dla beam straightening**.
  - **Cross / star heat** — kombinacja patterns dla complex 3D corrections.
- **Critical temperature limits**:
  - **Mild carbon steel**: **600-650°C max** (dark cherry red). **Above 750°C** transforms structure → permanent property change.
  - **HSLA / low-alloy steel**: **550-600°C max** (more sensitive).
  - **Stainless steel** (304/316): **400-500°C max** — above causes carbide precipitation / sensitization.
  - **Aluminum**: **150-200°C max** (low yielding T).
  - **NIE używać** dla high-strength steels (Q&T) — permanent property loss.
- **Process control**:
  - **Temperature indicators**:
    - **Tempilstik chalks** — melts at specific T (e.g., Tempilstik 650 melts when reached 650°C). Most common.
    - **Pyrometr IR** — non-contact, calibrated for emissivity.
    - **Color of heated steel** (visual):
      - Black-red (550°C) — too low for straightening.
      - **Dark cherry red (650°C)** — RIGHT for mild steel.
      - Bright cherry red (700°C) — getting too high.
      - Salmon (800°C) — TOO HIGH, structure damage.
- **Cooling methods**:
  - **Air cool** (natural) — slowest, most common. Allows thermal cycle to develop properly.
  - **Water spray** — accelerated cooling. NIE dla high-restraint structures (rapid quench = cracking risk). Used carefully.
  - **NIGDY ice water lub liquid nitrogen** — thermal shock damages material.
- **Common applications**:
  - **Welded beam bowing correction** — primary application. V-heats on convex side pull beam straight.
  - **Plate flatness correction** — spot heats relieve buckling.
  - **Pipe straightening** — pipe heated locally at specific clock position (e.g., 12 o'clock dla bow correction).
  - **Ship structures** — welding-induced buckling correction.
  - **Bridge girders** — fabrication + service repair.
  - **Storage tank shell** — out-of-round correction.
- **Tools**:
  - **Oxy-acetylene torch** — most common. Standard rosebud lub Vanguard-style large heating tip.
  - **Propane torch** — slower heating, OK dla thinner sections.
  - **Induction heating** — premium method, controlled heat zone, precise. Drogo (sprzęt $50k+).
- **Procedure** (mild steel beam straightening, V-heat):
  1. **Measure distortion** — laser, straightedge, plumb bob. Identify magnitude + direction.
  2. **Plan heat pattern** — calculate # of V-heats needed (typowo 1-3 mm correction per V-heat).
  3. **Mark heating points** z chalk.
  4. **Pre-restrain structure** (optional) — clamps lub strongbacks aplikowane (enhance effect).
  5. **Heat to target T** — heat narrow end of V first, expand outward. Total heated zone ~50-150 mm.
  6. **Hold T uniformly** for 30-60 seconds.
  7. **Remove heat** + allow natural cool.
  8. **Re-measure** distortion. Re-heat if needed.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Overheating** — structure damage, hot-spot brittleness.
  - **Insufficient heat T** — no plastic deformation, no correction.
  - **Wrong location** — heating wrong side moves distortion wrong direction.
  - **Multiple consecutive heats same area** — material fatigue, cracking.
  - **Water cooling rapid quench** — induces internal cracks, embrittles.
- **Effectiveness**: skilled operator can correct **10-50 mm of distortion** w long beams (depends on size + material). Multiple cycles often needed.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Time-intensive** — 2-8 h per typical structure.
  - **Skill-dependent** — wrong technique makes distortion worse.
  - **Limited to ductile materials** — embrittled steels NIE candidates.
  - **Strength reduction** — slight (typowo < 5%) w heated zone. NDE often verifies post-straightening.
- **Codes / standards**:
  - **AISC 360** + **AISC LRFD** allow flame straightening for structural steel (specific limits).
  - **AWS D1.1 §5.26** specifically covers heat straightening dla structural welds.
  - **AASHTO** standards dla bridge structures.

---

## Iteration 40 — 2026-05-25

### QQQQQ. AWWA Water Utility Valves — C500 / C509 / C515 / C504 / C516
- **AWWA** (American Water Works Association) — standardy dla **water/wastewater utilities** w USA. Różne od ASME/API kodów (które są dla industrial / oil & gas / pressure equipment).
- **Gate valves** (water main shutoff):
  - **AWWA C500** — **METAL-SEATED gate valves**:
    - Wedge metal-on-metal seal.
    - Heavy duty, durable, ale **leakage allowance** (small drips OK).
    - Coated z fusion-bonded epoxy lub powder coating.
    - **Stosowane**: legacy installations, niektóre transmission mains.
    - **Wycofywane** — większość utilities specyfikuje teraz C509/C515.
  - **AWWA C509** — **RESILIENT-SEATED gate valves** (full body):
    - **Wedge encapsulated w EPDM rubber** — zero leakage allowance.
    - **Smooth seal** even z particles w water.
    - More corrosion-resistant coating wymagana niż C500.
    - **Standard** dla water distribution DN ≤ 300.
  - **AWWA C515** — **REDUCED-WALL resilient-seated gate valves**:
    - **Same resilient seat** jako C509, ale **thinner body** wall (reduced casting weight).
    - **Lighter + cheaper** niż C509.
    - **2-3× longer service life** vs C500 metal-seated.
    - **STANDARD nowoczesny** — większość water utilities now specs C515.
    - Sizes: **3"-48"** (DN75-DN1200), ratings 250-350 psi.
- **Wedge construction (C509 / C515)**:
  - **Ductile iron core** (ASTM A536 65-45-12).
  - **EPDM (Ethylene Propylene Diene Monomer) rubber** — **standard** dla potable water (NSF/ANSI 61 certified).
  - **NBR** (Nitrile rubber) — opcja dla oil contamination service.
  - **Full encapsulation** — bottom + sides + top, brak metal-to-metal contact gdy closed.
- **Stem configurations**:
  - **NRS (Non-Rising Stem)** — stem rotates ale nie podnosi się. Standard dla buried services + valve boxes (mniej space wymagana powyżej valve).
  - **OS&Y (Outside Screw & Yoke / Rising Stem)** — visible stem podnosi się gdy valve opens (clear visual indicator). Standardowe dla industrial + fire systems.
- **End connections**:
  - **Mechanical joint (MJ)** — bolted gland follower z gasket dla cast iron pipe (CIP) lub ductile iron pipe (DIP).
  - **Flanged** — ANSI/AWWA Class 125 lub 250.
  - **Push-on (Tyton)** — fast connection do PVC lub DIP push-on bell.
  - **Threaded** (small sizes only ≤ DN50).
- **Operating mechanism**:
  - **Wrench nut (operating nut)** — 2" standard square head dla **valve key (T-handle wrench)** access through valve box.
  - **Handwheel** — exposed installations.
  - **Bevel gear operator** — dla DN ≥ 400 (mechanical advantage dla heavy wedge).
  - **Position indicator** — paint mark, rising stem visual, lub built-in counter (turns to close).
- **AWWA Butterfly Valves**:
  - **AWWA C504** — **Rubber-seated butterfly valves**:
    - Standard dla DN ≥ 100 water distribution (gate valves get expensive at large sizes).
    - **Rubber seat**: vulcanized in body lub on disc.
    - Class 25B (25 psi), 75B (75 psi), 150B (150 psi).
  - **AWWA C516** — **Large diameter rubber-seated**:
    - Sizes DN 750-2500 (30"-100"+) — distribution mains, raw water.
    - Bell + spigot rubber-encapsulated disc.
  - **Quarter-turn** operation — fast on/off, lower torque niż gate valve.
  - **Triple-offset (specialty)** — dla wastewater + slurry (limited AWWA application).
- **AWWA Check Valves (C508, C510)**:
  - **C508** — Swing check valves dla water service.
  - **C510** — Triple duty valve (gate + check + flow regulation).
- **AWWA Plug Valves (C517)**:
  - Eccentric plug valves dla wastewater. Self-cleaning (plug rotation scrapes seat).
- **Inspection / Test (AWWA standards)**:
  - **Hydrostatic shell test**: 2× rated pressure (zero leakage).
  - **Hydrostatic seat test**: 1.1× rated, leakage ≤ allowable.
  - **Operability test**: open + close 100% range bez binding.
- **NSF / ANSI 61** — drinking water material safety certification. Mandatory dla potable systems.
- **Coatings**:
  - **Fusion-bonded epoxy (FBE)** — inside + outside. **NSF 61 certified**.
  - **Liquid epoxy** — alternative dla retrofit.
  - **DFT (Dry Film Thickness)**: 8-10 mil exterior, 4-6 mil interior.

### RRRRR. Welder Qualification Test (WQT) — 6G AWS D1.1 + ASME IX
- **WQT (Welder Qualification Test)** = **individual welder certification** to specific WPS. Different from **WPS qualification** (PQR validates procedure, WQT validates person).
- **6G position significance**:
  - **Pipe at 45° fixed** — najbardziej demanding position dla welder.
  - **Qualifies ALL positions** w pipe + plate (1G, 2G, 3G, 4G, 5G, 6G).
  - **STANDARD test dla professional pipe welders** — most jobs require 6G certification.
- **AWS D1.1 vs ASME IX**:
  - **AWS D1.1**: dla structural steel welding (buildings, bridges, oil platforms). Standard w USA construction.
  - **ASME IX**: dla pressure piping + boilers + pressure vessels. Standard w refineries, power plants.
  - **Different test details** — but very similar.
- **Test coupon details (6G AWS D1.1)**:
  - **Pipe size**: typowo DN 50-150 (2"-6") dla diameter qualification limits.
  - **Wall thickness**: chosen do qualify range needed (e.g., 0.375" wall qualifies 0.187"-0.75").
  - **Pipe length**: typowo 150-200 mm (6-8") per side.
  - **Material**: P-No.1 carbon steel (A106 Gr.B typical) dla generic qualification.
- **Joint preparation**:
  - **Single V bevel, 60° included angle** (30° per side).
  - **Root gap**: 1/16" to 1/8" (1.6-3.2 mm).
  - **Root face**: 0 (open root) lub 1.5 mm landing.
  - **No backing strip** typically (open root test).
- **Welding sequence**:
  1. **Tack weld** — 4 tacks at 12/3/6/9 o'clock.
  2. **Root pass** — typowo SMAW E6010 (cellulose) lub GTAW dla TIG-qualified.
  3. **Hot pass** — burn out remaining slag.
  4. **Fill passes** — bring up to flush with bevel.
  5. **Cap pass** — final reinforcement (1-3 mm above pipe surface).
- **Testing**:
  - **Visual inspection (VT)** — pierwsza brama:
    - **No cracks**, no overlap.
    - **Reinforcement** ≤ 1/16" (1.6 mm) for thin, ≤ 1/8" (3.2 mm) for thick.
    - **Undercut** ≤ 1/32" (0.8 mm) depth.
    - **Concavity** root: ≤ 1/16".
  - **Bend testing**:
    - **2 root bends** + **2 face bends** dla AWS D1.1 (specimens cut at 90° intervals around pipe).
    - **180° around mandrel** — bend test jig pin diameter zależy od material (2-3× thickness typical).
    - **Acceptance**: no crack lub open defect > 1/8" (3.2 mm) na bent surface.
  - **Radiographic option (substitute dla bend)**:
    - **RT (X-ray)** wzdłuż full pipe circumference.
    - **Acceptance criteria** wg D1.1 / ASME IX (specific defect limits).
    - **Preferred dla pipe** — bend test on pipe coupons trudne, RT cleaner.
  - **UT alternative** — niektórym kodom alternatywa do RT (cheaper, no radiation safety).
- **Certificate / Continuity**:
  - **Welder ID number** assigned (often called "Card number" lub "stamp number").
  - **Validity**: certificate valid **6 months** w AWS D1.1 (3 months ASME IX) bez recertification.
  - **Continuity record** — welder MUST weld with same process within validity period, else retake test.
  - **Production weld testing** — random RT samples z welder's production welds counted as continuity proof.
  - **Renewal**: re-test if continuity broken, lub if welder failed a production weld inspection.
- **Welder card content**:
  - Name + welder ID number.
  - Test date + valid until date.
  - Position qualified (6G qualifies all).
  - Process qualified (SMAW / GTAW / GMAW / FCAW).
  - Material P-No. or F-No. qualified.
  - Thickness range qualified.
  - Backing condition (with backing / without).
  - Issuing authority (employer, AWS, ASME, NCPWB).
- **Essential variables** (changes that require re-qualification):
  - **Process change** (SMAW → GMAW = new test).
  - **Backing addition/removal**.
  - **Position change downward** (6G qualifies all, ale 1G qualifies only 1G).
  - **P-No.** group change (CS → SS = new test).
  - **F-No.** filler metal group change.
  - **Thickness** outside qualified range.
- **Production retest** if welder's production weld fails:
  - **Immediate retest** required after defects found.
  - **Single re-test allowed** by some codes; multiple failures = invalidate cert.

### SSSSS. Pre-Fabrication Spool Dimensional Tolerances — ASME B31.3
- **Pre-fab tolerances** = **dimensional acceptance criteria** dla shop-fabricated spool pieces. Critical dla **field fit-up** without strain on equipment / piping.
- **Standard tolerances (ASME B31.3 §335, also referenced w industry)**:
  - **Center-to-center distance** (między centerlines kolan / tees / fittingów): **±3 mm (±1/8")**.
  - **Face-to-face distance** (flange face to flange face): **±3 mm (±1/8")**.
  - **Center-to-face distance**: **±3 mm**.
  - **Attachment location** (lug, bracket, drain tap): **±3 mm**.
- **Angular tolerances**:
  - **Branch angle** (e.g., tee 90°, lateral 45°): **±0.5° (30 minutes of arc)**.
  - **Flange face rotation** (bolt hole pattern alignment): **±1.6 mm** (1/16") = ~0.5° angular dla DN ≤ 200, smaller dla larger flange.
- **Pipe alignment / misalignment limits**:
  - **NPS ≤ 24** (DN ≤ 600): max misalignment **1.6 mm (1/16")** at butt joint.
  - **NPS ≥ 26** (DN ≥ 650): max misalignment **3.2 mm (1/8")** if permitted by code.
- **Bend ovality** (po bending lub forming):
  - **Internal pressure design**: flattening ≤ **8%** of OD.
  - **External pressure (vacuum) design**: flattening ≤ **3%** of OD (more sensitive).
  - Measurement: D_max − D_min ≤ tolerance × D_nominal.
- **Pipe ID drift test**:
  - **Drift mandrel** = solid bar 6 mm smaller than pipe ID (typowo). Passes through to verify no internal restriction.
  - **Reasons for failure**: weld root protrusion > spec, ovality after bend, internal scaling.
- **Wall thickness tolerance** (after welding):
  - Cap reinforcement: ≤ 1.6 mm (1/16") dla thin wall, ≤ 3.2 mm dla thick.
  - Root reinforcement (ID side): ≤ 1.5 mm typical (depends on service).
  - **Concavity** (ID): ≤ specific % wall.
- **Length tolerance dla cuts**:
  - **Production cut (oxy-fuel)**: ±2 mm.
  - **Cold cut (saddle machine)**: ±1 mm.
  - **Precision cut (CNC)**: ±0.5 mm.
- **Field fit-up allowance**:
  - **One spool piece per circuit** zostaje z **100-150 mm (4-6")** longer plain end (no bevel).
  - **Field adjustment** — cut to exact length on site dla tie-in.
  - **Final bevel** wykonany w field przed welding.
- **Hardness tolerance** (post-PWHT):
  - **NACE MR0175**: ≤ **22 HRC (250 HV10)** dla CS + low alloy.
  - **Standard pipe (non-sour)**: ≤ **350 HV** typical, no specific upper limit dla many codes.
- **Pipe end square-cut tolerance**:
  - **Perpendicularity**: ≤ 1° deviation from axis.
  - Measured by **pipe square** (right-angle gauge).
  - **Out-of-square** = uneven bevel after cutting = bad fit-up.
- **Bevel tolerance**:
  - **Bevel angle**: 37.5° ± 2.5°.
  - **Land (root face)**: per WPS, ± 0.8 mm.
  - **Bevel face surface**: clean, no scale, Ra ≤ 12.5 µm.
- **Verification methods**:
  - **Steel tape measure** — basic length checks (±1 mm).
  - **Vernier caliper** — diameters, depth.
  - **Pipe square + level** — perpendicularity + level.
  - **Bevel gauge** — angle of fazy.
  - **Hi-lo gauge** — internal mismatch measurement.
  - **Weld gauge** — reinforcement, undercut.
  - **3D laser scanning** — premium dla complex spools (patrz iter 33).
- **Dimensional Report (DR)**:
  - **Per-spool measurement** sheet.
  - **Compared to drawing** dimensions.
  - **Deviations** > tolerance flagged.
  - **Sign-off** by QC inspector przed releasing spool to coating / shipping.
- **Why tolerances matter**:
  - **Tight tolerance** = good field fit-up, low strain on equipment, designed performance.
  - **Loose tolerance** = field rework (recut, re-weld), strain on equipment nozzles (overload), invalid stress analysis.
  - **Critical: NIGDY force pipe into position** — induces residual stress that may cause SCC, fatigue, equipment damage.
- **Inspection points** (per ITP iteration 38):
  - **Pre-fab QC checkpoint**: hold point before painting / shipping.
  - **Field receipt inspection**: spool received from shop, verified against drawing + DR.
  - **Field fit-up**: verify before tack welding.

## Iteration 41 — 2026-05-25

### TTTTT. Plasma Arc Cutting — CNC pipe + bevel preparation
- **Plasma arc cutting (PAC)** = thermal cutting używający **ionized gas (plasma)** rozgrzanego do 20,000-30,000°C przez electric arc. Plasma jet topi + wydmuchuje metal z kerf.
- **Process basics**:
  - **Plasma gas** (compressed air, N₂, O₂, Ar/H₂ mix) przepływa przez **constricted nozzle** + electric arc → ionizes do plasma stream.
  - **High-velocity plasma jet** (300-1000 m/s) topi metal + ejects motten metal z kerf.
  - **Conductive materials only** — CS, SS, Al, Cu, brass, Ti (electric arc requires conductivity).
  - **Cutting speed**: 10-150 cm/min zależy od material thickness.
- **System components**:
  - **Power supply** — DC constant current, 20-1000 A.
  - **Torch** — z **electrode (hafnium tip lub copper)** + **nozzle (water-cooled lub air-cooled)**.
  - **Gas regulator** — kontroluje plasma gas + shield gas flow.
  - **Cooling system** — water-cooled torch dla high duty cycles.
  - **CNC controller** — programmable path control.
- **Types of plasma cutting**:
  - **Conventional (standard)** — single gas (air typowo). 0.6-25 mm thickness range.
  - **High-definition (HD)** — multi-gas (O₂ + air), narrower kerf, square cut edge. Better dla precision parts. Up to 50 mm.
  - **Underwater plasma** — torch + workpiece pod wodą (red. noise + fumes, slower).
  - **Fine plasma** — extreme precision, do 3 mm material głównie.
- **CNC pipe plasma cutting machines**:
  - **5-axis CNC pipe plasma** — pipe rotation (axis A) + torch X/Y/Z + bevel A-axis. Cuts complex shapes:
    - **Square cuts** — straight perpendicular.
    - **Mitered cuts** — angle to pipe axis (45°, 30°, custom).
    - **Saddle cuts (fishmouth)** — branch pipe contour for tee/stub-in.
    - **Hole cuts** — for lateral connections.
    - **Bevel cuts** — weld prep w one step (no separate beveling machine).
- **Bevel angles** programmable:
  - **30°** — thin material, low filler.
  - **37.5°** — standard for SMAW/GTAW pipe weld.
  - **45°** — thick wall, deep penetration.
  - **Compound bevel (J-groove, U-groove)** — multi-axis tilt sequence.
- **Torch Height Control (THC)** — KRYTYCZNE dla quality:
  - **Capacitive sensor** lub **arc voltage feedback** — keeps torch 1-5 mm above workpiece surface.
  - **Automatic adjustment** dla warped material, pipe ovality.
  - **Pierce + cut height different**: pierce starts higher (5-10 mm), drops to cutting height once arc established.
- **Cutting parameters** (typical CS pipe, air plasma):

| Wall thickness | Amperage | Speed | Gas |
|----------------|---------:|------:|-----|
| 1 mm | 30 A | 5000 mm/min | Air |
| 5 mm | 80 A | 2500 mm/min | Air |
| 10 mm | 130 A | 1500 mm/min | Air |
| 25 mm | 260 A | 600 mm/min | Air or O₂ |
| 50 mm | 400 A | 300 mm/min | O₂ |

- **Plasma vs oxy-fuel cutting**:
  - **Plasma faster** dla ≤ 25 mm material.
  - **Oxy-fuel cheaper** dla > 25 mm (gas + flow rate).
  - **Plasma cleaner edge** — minimal slag.
  - **Plasma cuts SS + Al** (oxy-fuel cannot — no exothermic oxidation reaction).
  - **Oxy-fuel cuts thicker** material (do 500 mm+).
- **Plasma vs laser cutting**:
  - **Laser cleaner cut + finer detail**.
  - **Plasma faster + cheaper** dla > 5 mm material.
  - **Plasma works on rusty/painted surface** (laser needs clean).
  - **Laser energy efficient** dla thin material < 5 mm.
- **Quality criteria**:
  - **Kerf width**: 1-3 mm typical (zależy od amperage + nozzle).
  - **Cut surface roughness**: Ra 6-25 µm (zależy od material + speed).
  - **Bevel angle accuracy**: ±2° dla mechanized, ±5° dla handheld.
  - **Heat affected zone (HAZ)**: 1-3 mm shallow vs 5-10 mm dla oxy-fuel.
  - **Hardness in HAZ** — temporary hardness increase, often acceptable bez tempering.
- **Safety considerations**:
  - **UV / IR radiation** — shade 5-9 helmet zależy od amperage.
  - **High noise** — 90-120 dB. Hearing protection.
  - **Fume** — gęsta, metal oxide. LEV ventilation mandatory.
  - **Spatter** — molten metal flies up to 10 m. Fire protection.
  - **High-voltage pilot arc** — 10-25 kV. Insulated cables, ground clamps.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Hypertherm** — premium (Powermax, MAXPRO).
  - **Esab Vector / Powercut**.
  - **Lincoln Electric Tomahawk**.
  - **Messer / Koike** — large CNC pipe systems.

### UUUUU. API 6A — Wellhead + Christmas Tree Valves
- **Wellhead** = pressure-containing equipment **at top of oil/gas well** controlling pressure + flow during production + workover.
- **Christmas tree** = vertical stack of valves above wellhead, named jak choinka (Christmas tree) because of vertical arms.
- **API 6A** ("Specification for Wellhead and Christmas Tree Equipment") — primary standard. Pressure ratings: 2,000-20,000 psi (140-1400 bar).
- **Pressure classes** (API 6A):
  - **2K** (2,000 psi / 138 bar).
  - **3K** (3,000 psi / 207 bar).
  - **5K** (5,000 psi / 345 bar).
  - **10K** (10,000 psi / 690 bar) — common shallow gas / oil.
  - **15K** (15,000 psi / 1,034 bar) — deepwater, high-pressure gas.
  - **20K** (20,000 psi / 1,380 bar) — ultra-deepwater.
- **Tree components (bottom-up)**:
  - **Casing head spool** — bottom, connects do casing.
  - **Tubing head spool** — connects do production tubing.
  - **Tubing hanger** — holds production tubing.
  - **Lower master valve (LMV)** — **primary barrier**, manual lub actuated:
    - **NIGDY operated production** — only shut-off for emergencies.
    - **Bubble-tight seal** required.
    - **Backup if upper master fails**.
  - **Upper master valve (UMV)** — primary operational shutoff:
    - **Daily operation** dla starting/stopping production.
    - **First seal point** at wellhead.
  - **Tree cap / swab valve** — top, removable dla wireline / coiled tubing access:
    - **Swabbing** = pulling tools through to clean / measure / test.
  - **Flow cross / tee** — distributes do wing arms.
  - **Wing valves** (2-4 sztuki, side outlets):
    - **Production wing valve** — main flow do production line / separator.
    - **Kill wing valve** — emergency well-killing port.
    - **Annulus access** — between casing layers (for monitoring lub injecting).
    - **Choke valve** — see below.
- **Choke valve** (KLUCZOWY):
  - **Function**: regulate well flow rate + pressure drop across tree.
  - **Two choke configuration** (common):
    - **Positive choke (fixed)** — bean (orifice) of specific diameter installed.
    - **Adjustable choke (regulating)** — needle-and-seat mechanism, manually lub hydraulically operated.
  - **Pressure drop** across choke: typowo 50-90% of inlet pressure.
  - **Materials** dla harsh service: Inconel 718 trim, tungsten carbide bean/seat, ceramic inserts dla erosion resistance.
- **Subsea trees** (specjalne):
  - **Mounted on seabed** dla offshore wells.
  - **Hydraulic actuated valves** (impossible diver access).
  - **Vertical tree** lub **horizontal tree** configuration.
  - **Connections**: hot-stab connections dla ROV (Remotely Operated Vehicle) access.
  - **Operating depth**: do 3000 m water depth (current limit).
- **Tree types**:
  - **Conventional dual-master tree**: lower + upper master, standard.
  - **Conventional single-master tree**: cheaper, used dla low-pressure wells.
  - **Triple-master tree**: extra redundancy, deepwater.
  - **Production tree**: standard wellhead.
  - **Christmas tree (more arms)**: complex production well z multi-zone completion.
  - **Workover tree**: temporary use during workover operations.
- **Materials** (API 6A class designations):
  - **AA / BB**: general service, no H₂S.
  - **CC**: moderate H₂S service.
  - **DD/EE**: sour service per NACE MR0175.
  - **FF**: severe sour + CO₂.
  - **HH**: special applications.
- **Operation**:
  - **Production**: UMV open, LMV open, production wing open, choke set, others closed.
  - **Shut-in**: production wing closed → UMV closed (positive isolation).
  - **Kill operation**: kill wing opens, pumps heavy fluid → production wing closed.
  - **Workover**: LMV closed (positive isolation), tree disassembled above LMV.
- **Inspection / testing**:
  - **Factory hydrostatic test**: shell test 1.5× rated, seat test 1.1× rated.
  - **Annual function test**: full open/close per local regulation (some jurisdictions).
  - **Wireline/Coiled tubing pre-job**: verify swab + tree caps function.
- **Vendors**: Cameron (Schlumberger), Halliburton, Baker Hughes, Aker Solutions, TechnipFMC, Vetco (Aker), GE Vetco Gray, NOV.

### VVVVV. HFW / ERW Pipe Manufacturing — High Frequency Welded
- **ERW (Electric Resistance Welding)** = pipe manufacturing process gdzie **steel strip** (skelp) jest **cold-formed w cylinder** + **welded longitudinally** via electric resistance heating na seam edges.
- **HFW (High Frequency Welding)** = modern subset of ERW. **Wszystkie nowoczesne ERW pipes są HFW** — older low-frequency ERW (50-450 Hz) zastąpiony przez **HFW (100-500 kHz)**.
- **Manufacturing process**:
  1. **Steel coil** uncoiled + leveled.
  2. **Edge milling** — strip edges precyzyjnie obrobione (right angle, clean).
  3. **Cold roll forming** — progressive roll stages bend strip w cylindryczny kształt (15-20 form rolls).
  4. **High-frequency current** induced w strip edges via **induction coil** lub **direct contact**.
  5. **Edge heating** — strip edges heat to **forging temperature** (~1450°C, near melting).
  6. **Squeeze rolls** force heated edges together — solid-state weld (NO molten pool, NO filler metal).
  7. **External weld upset (bead)** removed by **scarfing tool** (cutting blade) — flush with OD.
  8. **Internal bead** sometimes removed (depends on spec).
  9. **Inline heat treatment** — seam annealing / normalizing dla restoring properties.
  10. **Cooling**, sizing, straightening.
  11. **Cut to length** + end finishing (bevel for welded pipe, plain for threaded).
- **Standards**:
  - **API 5L** — line pipe specification (oil + gas pipelines). HFW mandatory for **PSL 2 grade** (Product Specification Level 2 — premium quality).
  - **ASTM A53** — general use carbon steel pipe.
  - **ASTM A106** — high-temp service (mostly seamless, ale some ERW grades).
  - **ASTM A252** — piling.
  - **API 5CT** — casing + tubing dla wells.
- **HFW pipe properties**:
  - **Diameter range**: DN 50-1200 (2"-48").
  - **Wall thickness**: 2-25 mm typical.
  - **Material grades**: API 5L Grades A, B, X42, X52, X60, X65, X70, X80 (yield strength MPa designation).
  - **Strength**: comparable lub better niż seamless (depends on grade).
  - **Weld zone**: post-anneal, microstructure refined → tougher niż original strip.
- **Inspection** (mandatory dla API 5L PSL 2):
  - **Online UT (ultrasonic)** — 100% of seam, automated.
  - **Hydrotest** — 100% of pipe, 1.5× design pressure typically.
  - **MT (magnetic particle)** — seam surface.
  - **Random destructive tests** — bend, flattening, tensile (from sample lengths).
  - **Charpy V-notch** dla low-T service grades.
- **HFW vs seamless**:
  - **HFW cheaper** (mass production efficiency).
  - **Seamless preferred** dla high-pressure / sour service (no weld susceptibility).
  - **HFW excellent** dla onshore + offshore gas pipelines, water main, structural.
  - **HFW + post-weld annealing** approaches seamless properties.
- **HFW vs SAW pipe**:
  - **HFW**: smaller diameters (≤ DN 600 typically), thinner walls, high production rate.
  - **SAW (LSAW)**: larger diameters, thicker walls, dedicated longitudinal seam.
- **Common defects HFW (historical)**:
  - **Cold weld** — insufficient temperature na strip edges → poor fusion. Modern HFW eliminuje (better controls).
  - **Hook crack** — edge contamination → cracks at seam HAZ. Online UT detects 100%.
  - **Lack of bond** — incomplete forging. Detected by hydrotest + UT.
  - **Mismatch** (hi-lo on seam) — edges not aligned. Visual + UT detection.
- **Modern HFW quality** — comparable do seamless dla 99% applications. Some jurisdictions (notably oil & gas downstream + petrochem) still spec seamless dla high-criticality, ale this is gradually changing as HFW track record grows.
- **Vendors**:
  - **JFE Steel** (Japan).
  - **POSCO** (Korea).
  - **Tenaris** (Argentina, US — both ERW + seamless).
  - **Vallourec** (France — mostly seamless ale some ERW).
  - **EVRAZ NA** (US/Canada).
  - **TMK / Russia**.
  - **Welspun India**.
