## Iteration 48 — 2026-05-25

### OOOOOO. Storage Tank Floor Inspection — MFL Scanner + Robotic Crawler
- **Storage tank floor inspection** = critical NDE dla **aboveground petroleum storage tanks (ASTs)**. Tank floor most susceptible part — soil-side corrosion from underneath + product contact + condensation + biological activity.
- **API 653** standard ("Tank Inspection, Repair, Alteration, and Reconstruction") — governs in-service tank inspection.
- **Inspection frequency**:
  - **External**: monthly (visual) + 5 years (formal).
  - **Internal**: **out-of-service every 10-20 years** depending on corrosion rate + product service.
  - **Online inspection** (without emptying tank): emerging technology dla long-life tanks.
- **MFL (Magnetic Flux Leakage)** — primary NDE method dla tank floor scanning:
  - **Strong magnets** w scanner head saturate floor plate magnetically.
  - **Defects (corrosion pits, cracks)** disrupt magnetic flux → leakage detected by sensors.
  - **Signal interpretation** identifies defect location + size.
  - **Coverage**: surface + subsurface (top + bottom-side corrosion).
- **Equipment types**:
  - **Manual MFL scanner**:
    - **Handheld** lub trolley-mounted unit.
    - **Operator pushes** across floor pattern (typowo 50% overlap).
    - **Standard tools**: Silverwing FloormapX, MFE Mark IV, Alphanetics MST-10, Eddyfi FloormapX.
    - **Productivity**: ~500-1000 m² per shift.
  - **Automated robotic crawler**:
    - **4-wheeled robot** z multiple NDE sensors (MFL + UT).
    - **Magnetic wheels** dla floor adhesion.
    - **Remote operated** via cable lub wireless.
    - **In-service (online)** inspection — tank stays operational, crawler navigates floor underwater (oil-side).
    - **96% floor coverage** typical (gaps: under support columns, drain lines).
    - **Vendors**: Diakont (Russia / USA), Square Robot (USA), Inspectah.
  - **Combined MFL + UT array**:
    - **MFL** scans large area quickly identifying suspect zones.
    - **UT** provides precise wall thickness confirmation w suspect areas.
    - Two-step approach combines speed + accuracy.
- **Detection capabilities**:
  - **General corrosion**: 5-100% wall loss.
  - **Pitting**: ≥ 20% wall loss detectable.
  - **Cracks**: limited (UT or PT better dla crack characterization).
  - **Top-side vs bottom-side** discrimination via magnetic flux direction analysis.
- **API 653 acceptance criteria**:
  - **Minimum bottom plate thickness** based on next inspection interval + corrosion rate.
  - **Maximum corrosion pit depth** allowed = function of remaining thickness + design pressure.
  - **Critical zone** = 12" (300 mm) around shell-to-bottom weld — most stringent (water + product accumulate).
- **Online inspection benefits**:
  - **No empty tank** — saves $500K-$5M per tank (cleaning + sludge disposal + product loss).
  - **Faster inspection** — multiple days vs weeks-months out-of-service.
  - **Frequent inspection** possible — annual vs decadal traditional.
- **In-service crawler operation**:
  1. **Crawler lowered** through manway into tank.
  2. **Operator at control panel** outside tank.
  3. **Navigates floor in pattern** (raster scan).
  4. **Real-time data** transmitted to surface dla analysis.
  5. **Anomaly mapping** generated.
  6. **Crawler retrieved**, surface cleanup of any product on equipment.
- **Limitations of MFL**:
  - **Plate thickness** > 12-15 mm — magnetic saturation difficult.
  - **Liner / coating** thick — reduces sensitivity.
  - **Surface preparation** required (clean floor, no heavy sludge).
- **Floor repairs** (post-inspection):
  - **Doubler plates** — welded patches over corroded areas (allowed only outside critical zone).
  - **Full plate replacement** — entire damaged plate cut out + new plate welded in.
  - **Chime plate replacement** — critical 300 mm band w shell-to-bottom weld.
  - **Re-coating** — internal liner reapplication.
- **Pipefitter / welder role**:
  - **Doubler plate welding** — full penetration fillet welds.
  - **Plate replacement** — cut + remove + weld new plate (welder w PPE inside tank, confined space permits).
  - **Welder qualifications** — typowo SMAW + FCAW dla tank repairs.

### PPPPPP. Stop Check Valve — Gate + Check Combination dla Boiler Service
- **Stop check valve (SCV) / non-return valve / boiler stop valve** = combination valve **stop valve + check valve** w one body. **Two functions w one device**:
  - **Stop function** — operator can manually close valve to prevent flow direction (manual on/off).
  - **Check function** — automatic non-return prevents reverse flow if stem raised (acts like check valve).
- **ASME requirement**:
  - **ASME Section I (Boiler Construction)** **mandates SCV** as **first valve** on boiler discharge line when **multiple boilers connected to common header**.
  - **Protects individual boiler** from reverse flow disturbance z others.
- **Construction**:
  - **Body**: globe valve-like, z **inlet and outlet on perpendicular axes** (T-pattern lub Y-pattern):
    - **T-pattern**: stem perpendicular to flow, internal disc moves vertically. Standard.
    - **Y-pattern**: stem + disc at 30-45° angle. Less pressure drop, dla high-flow lines.
  - **Disc**: **NOT attached to stem** — KEY DIFFERENCE od regular globe valve.
  - **Stem**: raises / lowers, controls **maximum disc lift**.
  - **Seat**: matches disc surface, seals when disc lands.
- **Operating modes**:
  - **Stem fully closed (down)**: stem pushes disc onto seat → **fully closed** (positive shut-off, like gate valve).
  - **Stem partially raised**: disc free to lift/lower based on flow direction:
    - **Forward flow**: disc lifts (lift check function).
    - **Reverse flow**: disc drops onto seat (check function).
  - **Stem fully open**: disc free to lift maximum, **flow control limited by stem position**.
- **Applications**:
  - **Boiler discharge** (main steam outlet) — first valve, protects against reverse flow from header.
  - **Boiler feedwater pump discharge** — protects pump against backflow.
  - **Turbine cooling water** — bypass + safety.
  - **Multiple boiler installations** — each boiler has SCV between boiler outlet + common steam header.
  - **Multiple pump parallel systems** — each pump's discharge.
- **Sequence dla boiler startup** (typical):
  1. **All boilers OFF, SCV closed (stem down)**.
  2. **Start boiler 1**, raise to operational pressure.
  3. **Open SCV stem on boiler 1** — disc free to lift, forward flow OK.
  4. **Disc lifts** under pressure, boiler 1 steam joins header.
  5. **Start boiler 2** — similarly open SCV stem.
  6. **If boiler 1 trips** — pressure drops on boiler 1 side, disc closes automatically, header retains pressure (no reverse flow into shut-down boiler).
- **Sizes + ratings**:
  - **DN 50-500** typowy zakres.
  - **Class 150-2500** depending on service.
  - **High-pressure**: 4500 psi (310 bar) dla supercritical boilers, premium body materials.
- **Materials**:
  - **Body**: A216 WCB (CS), A105 (forged), A182 F11/F22 (Cr-Mo dla high-T).
  - **Internal trim**: 316 SS, Stellite-faced disc dla wear resistance.
  - **Stem**: 410 SS lub special alloy.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Stem packing** — gland packing standard (patrz iter 42).
  - **Internal inspection** — every few years, replace seat + disc if worn.
  - **Hydrotest** — periodic per code.
- **Related valves** (similar concept):
  - **Free-floating ball check valve** — ball lifts/falls dla flow direction.
  - **Tilting disc check** — disc nachylony, faster closing (less slam).
  - **Spring-assisted check valve** — spring closes disc instantly when reverse flow detected.
- **Stop check NIE same as**:
  - **Globe valve** — globe valve disc attached to stem (operator-controlled flow rate). Stop check disc independent.
  - **Gate valve + separate check** — 2-piece valve assembly. Stop check = 1-piece compact.

### QQQQQQ. Welder + Pipefitter Hand Tools — Chipping Hammer, Wire Brush, Slag Tools
- **Hand tools** = welder + pipefitter's daily essentials. NIE high-tech ale **CRITICAL** dla weld quality + productivity.
- **Slag removal tools** (post-SMAW / FCAW):
  - **Chipping hammer**:
    - **Cone tip** on one end (pointed), **chisel edge** on other (flat blade).
    - **7-12" long handle**, often **spring-coil** lub fiberglass dla shock absorption.
    - **Weight**: 10-16 oz typical.
    - **Steel head** — hardened, replaceable.
    - **Use**: light tap on weld bead at toe (avoid hitting weld face — can damage).
    - **Brands**: Lincoln Electric KH588, Klein H80612, Hobart Welder's, Vsenkes.
  - **Slag scraper / brush combo** — wire brush + chipping head w one tool.
  - **Needle scaler (pneumatic)**:
    - **Multiple steel needles** in pneumatic chamber.
    - **Hammers needles** against work surface, high frequency.
    - **Removes heavy slag** + rust + scale fast.
    - **Anvil-action** — multiple impact points.
    - **Drawback**: noisy (100+ dB), tool vibration (carpal tunnel concern long-term).
  - **Wire brush** (manual):
    - **Steel bristles** (CS dla CS weld, SS dla SS weld — NIE mix!).
    - **Hand size** lub long handle.
    - **Cleaning between passes** + final cleanup.
  - **Wire wheel** (powered):
    - **Drill / die grinder attachment**.
    - **Knotted wire** lub stringer wheel — different aggression.
    - **Crimped wire wheel** — gentler.
  - **Grinding wheel** (angle grinder):
    - **Cut-off disc**: thin, dla cutting steel.
    - **Grinding disc**: thick, dla material removal.
    - **Flap disc**: layered abrasive cloth, dla finishing.
    - **CRITICAL**: stainless steel wymaga **SS-only abrasive discs** (CS-contaminated discs contaminate SS surface, causing rust).
- **Measuring tools**:
  - **Pipefitter's square** — specialty square z scribed angles dla pipe fitting calculations (saddle cuts, mitered cuts).
  - **Carpenter's square** — basic perpendicularity check.
  - **Tape measure** — 25-50' typical.
  - **Caliper** — vernier lub digital, ±0.02 mm.
  - **Pipe protractor** — kątownik magnetyczny dla pipe angle measurement.
  - **Bevel gauge** — sprawdza kąt fazy.
  - **Hi-lo gauge** — mierzy internal mismatch.
  - **Weld gauge** — multiple gauges w one: reinforcement, undercut, leg size, throat.
- **Fit-up tools**:
  - **Soapstone** — biała chalk-like rysik dla mark'owania na CS.
  - **Silver pencil / scriber** — dla SS i wycenia precyzyjnego.
  - **Centerline punch** — punktak.
  - **Pipe wrench** — Stillson type, dla loosening / tightening NPT threads.
  - **Chain wrench** — alternatywa dla pipe wrench na large diameters.
  - **Pliers** — needle nose, lineman's, slip-joint.
- **Layout + cutting**:
  - **Pipe alignment clamps** (patrz iter 4): single chain, spider, cage.
  - **Wrap-around template** (patrz iter 18).
  - **Bandsaw / portable bandsaw** dla cutting pipe.
  - **Pipe cutter** (rotary cutting wheel): manual dla DN ≤ 100 cleanly.
  - **Saddle machine / bevel machine** — production tool.
- **Marking + identification**:
  - **Welder's stamp set** — letter + number stamps dla welder ID na pipe (per welder qualification system).
  - **Permanent marker** — chemical-resistant, weather-proof.
  - **Heat-resistant marker** (Markal Pro-Line, Sakura Solid Marker) — survives welding heat zones.
- **Personal tools** (PPE):
  - **Welding helmet** — auto-darkening preferred.
  - **Gloves** — TIG (thin), MIG/SMAW (medium), heavy-duty.
  - **Leather jacket / sleeves** — flame-resistant.
  - **Safety glasses** under helmet.
  - **Steel-toe boots**.
  - **Hearing protection** — earplugs lub muffs.
  - **Respirator** — N95 minimum, P3 dla SS, PAPR dla critical service.
- **Specialty pipefitter tools**:
  - **Hi-Lo gauge / Hi-Lo welding gauge** — mierzy mismatch internal pipe.
  - **Tap & die set** — NPT thread cutting/repair.
  - **Pipe threader** (electric, manual) — Ridgid 535, 535M.
  - **Pipe vise** — Yost lub Ridgid pipe vise, holds pipe during work.
  - **Pipe levelmaster** — magnetic levels designed dla curved pipe surfaces.
  - **Plumb bob** — vertical reference dla pipe rack work.
- **Storage organization**:
  - **Tool belt / vest** — fitter's tools always accessible.
  - **Welder's gang box** — large lockable box dla shop / job site.
  - **Tool roll** — fabric organizers dla wrench sets.
  - **Magnetic tool tray** — keeps small items from rolling away.
- **Care + maintenance**:
  - **Daily cleaning** — slag removed from tools.
  - **Wire brush replacement** — replace when bristles broken or rusty.
  - **Sharpening** — chisels + chipping hammer tips.
  - **Lubrication** — pipe wrench moving parts.
  - **Storage** — dry place, prevent rust.

## Iteration 49 — 2026-05-25

### RRRRRR. Welder ID Stamp / Hot Stamping Pipe — Weld Traceability
- **Welder ID stamp** = **physical hand-stamped mark** w pipe **adjacent to weld** identifying who welded it. Critical dla **traceability + accountability** w piping fabrication.
- **Purpose**:
  - **Quality control** — defect attribution do specific welder dla retraining / re-qualification.
  - **Regulatory compliance** — pressure vessel + piping codes require traceability.
  - **As-built documentation** — each weld linked to welder, WPS, date, NDE results.
  - **Repair history** — failed welds tracked do welder dla statistical analysis.
- **Stamp construction**:
  - **Hardened steel hand stamps** — letter + number combinations.
  - **Engraved by CNC**, hardened + nickel-plated dla corrosion protection.
  - **Standard size**: 5-8 mm character height (visible bez magnification but small enough no significant stress concentration).
  - **Low-stress stamps** dla stainless / critical service:
    - **Rounded dot matrix** characters (NOT sharp corners — sharp = stress riser, crack initiation).
    - Round-dot stamp preferred dla SS, alloy steels, sour service.
  - **Sharp character (block letters)** OK dla CS general service.
- **Code requirements**:
  - **ASME B31.3 §328.5.1**: each qualified welder + welding operator assigned identification symbol. **Each pressure-containing weld** lub adjacent area marked w identification symbol (unless engineering design specifies otherwise).
  - **ASME Section VIII (PV)** — similar; welder shall stamp the weld.
  - **API 650 (storage tanks)**: identification mark hand- or machine-stamped **adjacent to + at intervals not exceeding 1 m (3 ft) along completed welds**.
  - **API 1104 (pipelines)**: welder ID can be marked through other identification systems (paint marker, weld log) — physical stamp not always required.
  - **AWS D1.1 (structural)**: doesn't strictly require stamps; just identifiability through some system.
- **Stamp formats**:
  - **Single letter + number** (e.g., "A12", "J45") — most common dla individual welders.
  - **Company prefix + welder number** (e.g., "ABC-15", "XYZ-08") — projects z multiple contractors.
  - **Date code optional** — month + year added dla long-term traceability.
- **Stamping location**:
  - **Within 50-100 mm of weld** typically (close enough to identify, not on weld itself).
  - **Top of pipe** (12 o'clock) — visible from walkway.
  - **For long seam welds**: every 1 m max (per API 650).
  - **Multiple welders na one weld** (e.g., root by one, fill/cap by another) — all stamps adjacent.
- **Procedure** (typical):
  1. **Welder completes weld** + cleans area.
  2. **Self-inspection** — verify no obvious defects.
  3. **Hand stamps** ID adjacent (light hammer blow, single strike).
  4. **NDE inspector verifies** stamp present + readable post-NDE.
  5. **Weld log** updated z welder ID, date, WPS, NDE results.
- **Alternative identification** (when physical stamp NOT allowed):
  - **High-stress alloy pipe** (P91, super-duplex) — stamping verboten (stress riser).
  - **Permanent marker** + **detailed weld log** maintained.
  - **Photo documentation** of each weld w GPS / location ID.
  - **QR code labels** — modern approach, links do digital weld record.
- **Welder qualification expiration**:
  - **Continuity proof** — welder must show recent qualified work (typowo 6-month validity ASME, 3-month AWS).
  - **Continuity log** maintained by employer.
  - **Welder card** carried (laminated, current).
- **Production weld monitoring**:
  - **Random RT spot checks** of welder's production work — failures attribute to welder, re-qualification potentially required.
  - **Higher failure rate** = additional training / supervision.
  - **Defect statistics** per welder maintained — performance metrics.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Pickardt-USA** — premier welder stamps + engraved tools.
  - **Margreiter (Germany)** — European supplier.
  - **Durable Technologies** — custom engraved stamps.
  - **Pryor Marking Technology** — automated marking systems.

### SSSSSS. API 5CT — Oil Well Casing + Tubing + Premium Connections
- **API 5CT** ("Specification for Casing and Tubing") — standard dla **downhole pipe** w oil + gas wells. Casing dla wellbore stability, tubing dla production flow.
- **Casing function**: stabilizes wellbore, isolates formations, supports surface equipment.
- **Tubing function**: carries production fluid (oil, gas, water) from formation to surface.
- **Sizes** (API 5CT):
  - **Casing**: 4-1/2" (114 mm) to 20" (508 mm) OD typowy.
  - **Tubing**: 1.05" (27 mm) to 4-1/2" (114 mm) OD.
- **Grades** (yield strength denoted, MPa or ksi):
  - **H40**: 40 ksi (275 MPa) — shallow wells.
  - **J55 / K55**: 55 ksi (380 MPa) — standard wells.
  - **N80**: 80 ksi (550 MPa) — deeper / higher pressure.
  - **L80**: 80 ksi z impact test — sour service.
  - **C90 / T95**: 90/95 ksi — H₂S corrosion resistance + high strength.
  - **P110**: 110 ksi (760 MPa) — high-pressure deepwater.
  - **Q125**: 125 ksi — extreme pressure.
  - **V150**: 150 ksi — ultra-high pressure (specialty).
- **API standard threaded connections**:
  - **STC (Short Threaded + Coupled)** — short triangular threads. Simple, cheap. Limited torque + pressure rating.
  - **LTC (Long Threaded + Coupled)** — longer thread length, higher rating.
  - **BTC (Buttress Threaded + Coupled)** — square thread profile, much higher tensile rating.
  - **Round thread (RND)** — older design.
- **Premium connections** (proprietary, exceed API performance):
  - **VAM connections** (Vallourec):
    - **VAM TOP, VAM 21, VAM Xtreme** — popularne dla deep/HP/HT wells.
    - **Radial cone seals** + robust shoulders.
    - **VAM 21**: 100% pipe-body strength (tension, compression, burst).
  - **Tenaris/TenarisHydril Wedge™ series**:
    - **Trapezoidal thread flank** (wedge-shaped).
    - **Exceptional torque performance** — high makeup torque without galling.
    - **Wedge 441, Wedge 461, Blue, Dopeless®**.
  - **Hydril (NOV)**:
    - **8RD, 15SC, 15HC** — gas-tight integral connections.
    - **Large diameter casing** specialty.
  - **JFE Bear** (Japan).
  - **Atlas Bradford** (US).
- **Premium vs API standard**:
  - **Gas-tight seal** (premium) vs threaded leak path (API).
  - **Higher torque + makeup** rating.
  - **Better corrosion resistance** (special metal seals).
  - **Cost**: 2-5× API standard.
- **Service classifications**:
  - **Standard**: dry wells, low pressure.
  - **Sour service**: H₂S present → NACE MR0175 compliant materials (L80, C90, T95, C110).
  - **HP/HT (High-Pressure / High-Temperature)**: > 10,000 psi + > 150°C — premium connections + materials.
  - **Deepwater**: > 1000 ft water depth — Tier 1 connections.
  - **Thermal (SAGD)**: cyclic temperature 200-300°C dla heavy oil recovery — special grades + connections.
- **Connection makeup**:
  - **Pipe wrench / power tongs** — apply torque per specification.
  - **Torque turn monitoring** — real-time torque + rotation tracking.
  - **Makeup torque tables** — manufacturer-specific dla each connection.
  - **Lubricant ("dope")** — applied to threads (Bestolife, API modified, environmentally friendly variants).
- **Inspection**:
  - **Visual** — thread inspection dla damage post-running.
  - **EMI (Electromagnetic Inspection)** — wall thickness + defects.
  - **Hydrostatic test** — connection pressure integrity test.
  - **Make-and-break testing** — connection durability dla repeated use.
- **Field handling**:
  - **Thread protectors** — plastic / steel caps protecting threads during transport / storage.
  - **Stabbing protectors** — slip-on rubber sleeves during running.
  - **Lifting subs** — special connections dla rig crane.

### TTTTTT. Welding Safety Toolbox Talks — Daily HSE Meetings
- **Toolbox Talk (TBT)** = **short safety meeting** (5-15 min) at start of shift covering specific hazard topic relevant do day's work. **Daily ritual** w industrial workplaces.
- **Purpose**:
  - **Reinforce safe work practices** — repetition builds habits.
  - **Identify hazards** — focus on day's specific risks.
  - **Open communication** — crew shares concerns, near-misses.
  - **Regulatory compliance** — OSHA records require evidence of safety training.
  - **Safety culture** — daily focus signals "safety first" commitment.
- **Welding-specific TBT topics — "Three F's of Welding"**:
  - **FIRE** — sparks, hot metal, combustibles.
  - **FUMES** — toxic, carcinogenic dust + gases.
  - **FACE** — eyes, skin from UV/IR + spatter + arc flash.
- **Common welding TBT topics**:
  1. **Hot work permit procedure** (per iter 20).
  2. **Confined space welding** — gas test, ventilation, attendant.
  3. **Electric shock** — wet conditions, damaged cables, grounding.
  4. **Welding fume exposure** — Cr⁶⁺ stainless, Mn carbon steel.
  5. **PPE selection** — helmet shade dla welding type + amperage.
  6. **Compressed gas cylinder safety** — chained upright, regulator inspection.
  7. **Fire watch responsibilities** — 30 min post-weld monitoring.
  8. **Confined space — Permit-Required Confined Space (PRCS)**.
  9. **Lockout / Tagout (LOTO)** procedure.
  10. **Working at heights** — fall protection, scaffolding inspection.
  11. **Heat stress / cold stress** — hydration, layered clothing.
  12. **Pneumatic test safety** — exclusion zone, stored energy.
  13. **Crane signals + rigging** — communication, sling inspection.
  14. **Eye protection — UV / IR / arc flash**.
  15. **Slips, trips, falls** — cable management, housekeeping.
  16. **Manual handling** — proper lifting, pipe handling.
  17. **Burns + skin injuries** — protective clothing, first aid.
  18. **Drug + alcohol policy** — fitness for duty.
  19. **Emergency procedures** — evacuation routes, muster point.
- **TBT structure**:
  1. **Opening** (1 min): introduce topic, why important today.
  2. **Specific incident / hazard** (3-5 min): real example, recent near-miss, lesson learned.
  3. **Discussion** (3-5 min): crew questions, share experiences.
  4. **Key takeaways** (1-2 min): 3 specific actions for today.
  5. **Attendance** (1 min): sign-in sheet, record keeping.
- **Records required**:
  - **Attendance sheet** — date, topic, signatures.
  - **Topic content** — written summary of what discussed.
  - **Action items** — follow-ups from prior TBT.
  - **Archived** dla 3-5 years (OSHA requires).
- **Frequency**:
  - **Daily** (start of shift) — standard w industrial facilities.
  - **Weekly summary** of week's TBT topics — supervisor review.
  - **Special TBT** dla high-risk activities (turnaround, hot tap, confined space entry).
- **Resources / sources**:
  - **OSHA toolbox talks** — free, public domain.
  - **HSE Study Guide** — extensive TBT library.
  - **Safety Toolbox Topics** — industry-specific.
  - **Construction Safety Council** — daily talks dla construction.
- **Quality TBT characteristics**:
  - **Short + focused** — not 30-minute lectures.
  - **Specific** — about today's actual work / hazards.
  - **Two-way** — discussion, not monologue.
  - **Actionable** — specific behaviors changed.
  - **Documented** — but not bureaucratic.
- **Common failure modes** (poorly run TBT):
  - **Signed but not discussed** — crew signs sheet but no real meeting.
  - **Generic topics** — same hot work talk every day (loses impact).
  - **One-way** — supervisor talks, crew tuned out.
  - **Token compliance** — minutes-long mumbled, no substance.
- **Industry stats**:
  - **Properly executed TBTs** correlate z **20-40% reduction** w incident rates.
  - **OSHA** strongly emphasizes daily TBT w construction + heavy industry.

## Iteration 50 — 2026-05-25

### UUUUUU. Cathodic Disbondment Testing — ASTM G8 / G80 / G95
- **Cathodic disbondment (CD) testing** = laboratory test verifying **pipeline coating's resistance** do **cathodic protection-induced disbondment**. Critical dla buried pipeline coatings.
- **Background problem**:
  - **CP (Cathodic Protection)** generates **alkali (OH⁻ ions)** at coating defect site (holiday).
  - **High pH attacks coating-steel adhesive bond** — coating gradually disbonds from substrate.
  - **Disbonded coating** + electrolyte trapped underneath = **shielded corrosion** (CP can't reach disbonded area).
  - **Worst-case scenario**: coating peels but no visible damage → invisible corrosion progresses → catastrophic failure.
- **Coating qualification** requires CD test PRZED field application — verifies coating won't disbond under typical CP service conditions.
- **ASTM G8** ("Standard Test Methods dla Cathodic Disbonding of Pipeline Coatings"):
  - **Test setup**: coated pipe sample **submerged w electrolyte** (3% NaCl typically).
  - **Artificial holiday** drilled w coating exposing bare steel.
  - **Cathodic potential** applied (typowo −1.5 V vs Cu/CuSO₄ reference).
  - **Test duration**: 30 days (Method A) lub 180 days (Method B).
  - **Test temperature**: room T (22-25°C).
  - **Result**: measure **radial disbondment** from artificial holiday — coating peeled back distance after test.
- **ASTM G80** — similar to G8 but **fixed test conditions** (no options).
- **ASTM G95** ("Attached Cell Method"):
  - **Test cell ATTACHED do coating surface** (NOT submerged whole pipe).
  - **Electrolyte filled w cell**, voltage applied through cell electrode.
  - **More practical** dla testing field samples + sections of larger pipe.
  - **Heating allowed**: cell can be heated on hot plate dla elevated T testing.
  - **Test temperatures**: room T (22-25°C) lub elevated (60-95°C).
- **ASTM G42** ("Elevated Temperature CD Test") — similar to G8 ale at high T (60-95°C).
- **Test parameters**:
  - **Electrolyte**: 3% NaCl + 1% Na₂SO₄ + 1% Na₂CO₃ (typical formula).
  - **Potential**: −1.5 V do −3.5 V vs Cu/CuSO₄ (more negative = more aggressive CD).
  - **Time**: 24 h, 30 days, 90 days, 180 days depending on test method.
  - **Holiday size**: 3.2 mm drilled hole standard.
- **Acceptance criteria** (typowo per coating spec):
  - **FBE coating** at 65°C: max **6 mm disbondment radius** after 30 days.
  - **3LPE at 50°C**: max **8 mm** after 30 days.
  - **Coal tar enamel**: more liberal (older spec).
  - **High-T epoxy (geothermal)**: max 10 mm at 95°C after 30 days.
- **Why critical**:
  - **Coating failure** w field invisible until catastrophic.
  - **CD test predicts field performance** under combined CP + service conditions.
  - **Approving new coatings**: pass CD test = OK dla pipeline use.
- **Industry practices**:
  - **3LPE manufacturer** must demonstrate CD compliance before sales.
  - **FBE coatings**: pre-qualified to API RP 5L7 + ASTM G8 standards.
  - **Field-applied coatings** (epoxy patches, FJC, repair): site-specific CD compliance.

### VVVVVV. API 1169 — Pipeline Construction Inspector Certification
- **API 1169** = certification dla **pipeline construction inspectors**. Mid-career certification, focused on **NEW onshore pipeline construction** quality oversight.
- **Scope**: pipeline construction phases — survey/staking → stringing → welding → coating → backfill → hydrotest → commissioning.
- **NOT covered**: offshore pipelines, in-service operation, maintenance.
- **Examination format**:
  - **115 questions** (100 scored + 15 unscored experimental).
  - **3 hours** duration.
  - **Computer-based** test (Prometric / Pearson VUE centers).
  - **Multiple choice** format.
  - **Reference materials provided** on screen (US + Canadian government refs):
    - DOT PHMSA regulations (49 CFR 192 + 195).
    - API RP 1110 — Pressure Testing Steel Liquid Pipelines.
    - API 1104 — Welding of Pipelines.
    - NACE SP0169 — Cathodic Protection.
    - API RP 1102 — Steel Pipelines Crossing Railroads + Highways.
- **Body of Knowledge** (BoK) topics:
  1. **Inspection / Management Knowledge Areas**:
     - QA / QC processes.
     - Safety + HSE.
     - Environmental + pollution control.
     - Documentation + record keeping.
  2. **Front-End Construction**:
     - Survey + staking.
     - Right-of-way (ROW) preparation.
     - Permits + regulatory compliance.
     - Material receiving + inspection.
  3. **Installation Construction**:
     - Stringing — laying out pipe sections along ROW.
     - Bending — induction lub field bending.
     - Welding + NDE — visual, RT, AUT (already covered iter 47).
     - Trenching + ditching.
     - Crossing (road, river, railway) — HDD, bored crossings.
     - Coating — FBE, 3LPE, field joint coating.
     - Lowering-in — pipe placed into trench.
     - Tie-in — final connections (welds joining sections).
  4. **Back-End Construction**:
     - Cathodic Protection installation + commissioning.
     - As-built drawings + records.
     - Restoration — ROW reseeding, fence repair, landowner relations.
     - Hydrostatic testing.
     - Pigging operations.
  5. **Post-Construction**:
     - Line list closeout.
     - Punch list resolution.
     - Handover do operations.
- **Eligibility**:
  - Combination of education + experience required.
  - Typowo **5+ years experience** w pipeline construction inspection.
  - Engineering degree reduces experience requirement.
- **Recertification**: every **3 years** — continuing education credits + re-exam if lapses.
- **Career benefits**:
  - **Required by many contractors** dla bidding pipeline projects.
  - **Higher pay** vs uncertified inspectors (20-40% premium).
  - **Project management roles** often require API 1169.
- **Other API ICP (Individual Certification Programs)**:
  - **API 510** — Pressure Vessel Inspector.
  - **API 570** — Piping Inspector (covered iter 25).
  - **API 653** — Storage Tank Inspector.
  - **API 580/581** — RBI (Risk-Based Inspection).
  - **API 936** — Refractory Personnel.
- **Renewal credits**:
  - **CEUs (Continuing Education Units)** — attend conferences, take courses.
  - **Job experience** documented.
  - **Re-exam** if recertification missed.

### WWWWWW. HDPE Electrofusion Fitting — Coupler + Saddle Installation
- **Electrofusion (EF)** = alternative to butt fusion (covered iter 20) dla joining HDPE pipe. **Fitting z embedded electrical heating wire** — current heats fitting + pipe surfaces do fusion temperature.
- **Advantages vs butt fusion**:
  - **Tight spaces** — no need dla large butt fusion machine clamping.
  - **Repair work** — joining cut ends of in-service pipe.
  - **Saddle connections** — service tap-ins on existing main without cutting whole line.
  - **No alignment perfection** needed — fitting accommodates pipe variations.
  - **Faster setup** — pipe scraped + insert + clamp + plug in.
- **Disadvantages vs butt fusion**:
  - **More expensive fittings** — embedded wire adds cost.
  - **Power source required** — EF processor unit + cables.
  - **Smaller diameter range** typically — most ≤ DN 250.
  - **More steps** — surface preparation more critical.
- **Common EF fitting types**:
  - **EF coupler** — straight pipe-to-pipe joint.
  - **EF reducer** — different diameter pipes.
  - **EF elbow** — 45° lub 90° direction change.
  - **EF tee** — branch connection.
  - **EF saddle** — service tap (gas/water distribution main z service line).
  - **EF transition** — HDPE to metallic adapter.
- **Installation procedure** (EF coupler — common):
  1. **Cut pipe ends square** (square cut, NO bevel).
  2. **Measure pipe diameter** — verify within OD spec dla fitting size.
  3. **Out-of-round correction** — re-round clamps if oval (> 1.5% ovality).
  4. **Mark insertion depth** on pipe — typowo half the fitting length.
  5. **SCRAPE pipe surface** — KRYTYCZNE step:
     - **Peeling tool** preferred (consistent depth ~0.2 mm).
     - **Hand scraper** alternative dla emergencies.
     - **Full circumference** scraping to insertion depth mark.
     - **Removes oxidation layer** (UV-degraded outer surface w outdoor stored pipe).
  6. **Clean** — wipe with **70% isopropyl alcohol** + lint-free towel. **NO water, no contamination**.
  7. **Insert pipe ends** into fitting do marked depth — **straight push, NO twisting** (twisting drags contaminants in).
  8. **Clamp pipe and fitting** — restraining clamps prevent movement during fusion + cooling.
  9. **Connect EF processor cables** do fitting terminals.
  10. **Scan barcode / select fitting size** on EF processor (auto-determines voltage + time).
  11. **Run fusion cycle** — typowo 100-300 seconds at 40 V (small fittings) to 80 V (large fittings).
  12. **Indicator pins** — small pins extrude as melted PE expands. Visible confirmation of fusion.
  13. **Cooling time** — typowo 30-60 min (do NOT disturb).
  14. **Remove clamps** + visual inspection — indicator pins extended evenly, no melt-out, no gaps.
- **EF saddle installation** (service tap):
  1. **Mark hole position** on main pipe.
  2. **Scrape main pipe** at saddle location.
  3. **Position saddle** + bolt it onto main with under-clamp.
  4. **Run fusion cycle** — saddle fused to main.
  5. **After cool**: drill through saddle + main using **integral built-in cutter** (saddle has cutting head with rotation drive).
  6. **Cap or cap valve** finished saddle dla future service connection.
- **Quality criteria**:
  - **Indicator pins** flush with body lub slightly extended (NIE under, NIE excessive bulge).
  - **No melt-out** (PE oozing from joint).
  - **No charring** (overheating from voltage spike).
  - **Uniform appearance** all-around.
- **Inspection / testing**:
  - **Visual inspection** post-cool.
  - **Hydrotest** dla water service.
  - **Pressure test** specific fitting per manufacturer.
  - **Destructive testing** dla qualification (peel test, tensile).
- **Common failure modes**:
  - **Inadequate scraping** — oxidized layer prevents fusion.
  - **Contamination** — oil, water, dirt on prepared surface.
  - **Insufficient cooling** — premature handling deforms fusion zone.
  - **Wrong voltage / time** — incorrect EF processor settings.
  - **Out-of-round pipe** — gap between pipe + fitting too large.
- **Standards / regulations**:
  - **ASTM F1055** — Electrofusion joining of polyethylene pipe + fittings.
  - **ASTM F2620** — Butt fusion (covered iter 20).
  - **49 CFR 192.285** — Qualification of persons making fusion joints (gas service mandatory).
  - **AGA Plastic Pipe Manual** — industry guide.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Natural gas distribution** — STANDARD dla gas mains + service lines (small to medium diameter).
  - **Water distribution + irrigation** — large scale.
  - **Industrial chemical** — corrosive media (chemical-compatible HDPE grade).
  - **Mining slurry** — abrasion resistance.

## Iteration 51 — 2026-05-25

### XXXXXX. API 6D — Pipeline + Piping Valves Specification
- **API 6D** ("Specification for Pipeline and Piping Valves") = standard dla **transmission + distribution pipeline isolation valves**. Different from **API 6A** (wellhead) i **ASME B16.34** (general process valves).
- **Scope**: oil + gas pipeline service. **Trunk lines + station valves**.
- **Covered valve types**:
  - **Ball valves** — najczęstsze (full bore + reduced bore).
  - **Gate valves** — through-conduit, slab gate.
  - **Check valves** — non-return dla one-way pipeline flow.
  - **Plug valves** — older technology, still used niektórych aplikacjach.
- **Full bore requirement** — kluczowa charakterystyka API 6D:
  - **Valve bore ≥ pipe ID** dla pigging passage.
  - **NO restriction** dla pipeline inspection gauges (pigs), cleaning tools, smart pigs.
  - **Required dla pipeline applications** (vs reduced-bore for in-plant piping).
- **Trunnion-mounted ball** (dominant design):
  - **Ball z trunnion shafts** (upper + lower) supported na bearings.
  - **Seats absorb line pressure** (NOT ball — pressure pushes seats onto ball).
  - **Low operating torque** vs floating ball (10-30% torque dla equivalent floating).
  - **Standard dla DN ≥ 250 + Class ≥ 600**.
  - **Self-cleaning seats** w some designs (pressure-energized).
- **Floating ball** (less common w API 6D):
  - **Smaller sizes** + lower pressure (DN ≤ 200, Class ≤ 300).
  - **Pressure pushes ball** onto downstream seat.
  - **Higher torque** at high pressure.
- **Pressure ratings (API 6D)**:
  - Class **150, 300, 600, 900, 1500, 2500**.
  - Sizes **DN 50-900** (NPS 2-36").
  - **Beyond Class 2500 + DN 900**: typowo API 17D (subsea) lub manufacturer-specific.
- **Standard features**:
  - **Trunnion ball** w larger sizes.
  - **Soft seats** (PEEK, Devlon, PTFE filled) dla bubble-tight seal.
  - **Metal seats** dla high-T lub erosive service.
  - **Fire-safe** rated per API 607 / 6FA — seal retains z burn-out elastomer.
  - **Anti-static**: bonded ball-to-stem-to-body dla static discharge.
  - **Double Block + Bleed (DBB)**: 2 seats z body cavity pressure relief.
  - **Stem packing**: graphite + PTFE rings, certified low-emission (per ISO 15848).
- **Testing requirements (API 6D)**:
  - **Hydrostatic shell test**: 1.5× rated, 2 minutes minimum, no visible leak.
  - **Hydrostatic seat test**: 1.1× rated, **Class A acceptance** (no visible leak) lub Class B (allowable rate).
  - **Pneumatic seat test**: optional dla critical service, bubble count.
  - **Anti-static test**: electrical continuity ball-to-body (≤ 10 Ω).
  - **Operating torque**: measured + recorded.
  - **Fire test**: API 607 dla soft-seated, 6FA dla metal-seated.
- **API 6D vs API 598 vs ASME B16.34**:

| Spec | Scope | Pressure rating |
|------|-------|----------------:|
| **API 6D** | Pipeline isolation | Class 150-2500 |
| **API 6A** | Wellhead + Christmas tree | 2K-20K psi |
| **API 598** | Industrial valve testing | All classes |
| **ASME B16.34** | Pressure-temperature ratings general | Class 150-4500 |

- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Mainline block valves (MBV)** — pipeline isolation co 10-30 km dla emergency shutdown.
  - **Pig launchers / receivers** — block + bleed at pigging stations.
  - **Compressor station inlet/outlet** valves.
  - **Pumping station** isolation.
  - **Custody transfer skids** — measurement piping.
- **Actuation**:
  - **Manual gear operator** — handwheel, dla maintenance.
  - **Pneumatic actuator** — typowo air-spring fail-safe close (FC) lub fail-safe open (FO).
  - **Hydraulic actuator** — large valves, high torque.
  - **Electric actuator** — remote operation, SCADA integration.
  - **Gas-over-oil** — pipeline-pressure powered actuator (uses line gas via accumulator).
- **Critical pipeline integrity**:
  - **MBV (Mainline Block Valves)** must close within **2-4 minutes** w emergency (per PHMSA regulations).
  - **Pipeline rupture detection** — pressure drop triggers automatic MBV closure (isolation sections).

### YYYYYY. RTP — Reinforced Thermoplastic Pipe (Spoolable Composite)
- **RTP (Reinforced Thermoplastic Pipe)** = composite pipe construction z **HDPE/PEX inner liner + reinforcing fibers + HDPE outer cover**. **Spoolable** = pre-fabricated long lengths on reels (50-1000 m), unrolled at site.
- **Layered structure** (typical):
  - **Inner liner** — HDPE, PEX, PA11/PA12 (Polyamide), PVDF — chemical compatibility z fluid.
  - **Reinforcement layer** — high-strength fiber:
    - **Aramid (Kevlar)** — flexible, light, expensive.
    - **High-strength polyester** — economy choice.
    - **Glass fiber** — stiffer, less flexible.
    - **Carbon fiber** — premium dla extreme pressures.
    - **Steel cord** — heaviest, OK dla larger diameter.
  - **Outer cover** — HDPE jacket, UV-stable, abrasion-resistant.
- **Manufacturing**:
  - **Continuous extrusion** of inner liner.
  - **Helically wrapped fibers** at controlled angle (typowo ±54.7° dla optimal hoop/axial strength balance).
  - **Outer jacket extruded** over reinforcement.
  - **Coiled onto reels** continuous lengths.
- **Performance properties**:
  - **Pressure rating**: 5-100 bar (depends on construction).
  - **Diameter**: typowo DN 50-200 (2"-8") commercial range.
  - **Temperature**: −40°C do +110°C (HDPE matrix limit).
  - **Service life**: 20+ years verified, 50+ years theoretical (validated by accelerated aging).
- **Advantages vs steel pipe**:
  - **Lightweight**: 1/5 weight of equivalent steel pipe — 90% reduced transportation costs.
  - **Corrosion-immune**: no internal/external corrosion (chemical resistant).
  - **No coating**: skip FBE, 3LPE coating + holiday detection.
  - **No CP**: cathodic protection not needed dla non-metallic pipe.
  - **Spoolable**: 1 km lengths possible, **no field welding** between joints.
  - **Faster installation**: 3-5× faster than steel pipeline construction.
  - **Cost**: 20-40% lower installed cost vs steel.
- **Joining systems**:
  - **Mechanical end couplings** — proprietary swage fittings (Magma Global, NOV Tuboscope, Soluforce).
  - **Crimped + bolted** end fittings — metal connector swaged onto RTP end.
  - **No fusion / welding** field requirements.
- **Aplikacje (oil & gas)**:
  - **Water injection lines** — produced water back to formation.
  - **Flowlines** — wellhead do gathering station.
  - **Gas gathering** — low-medium pressure gas distribution.
  - **CO₂ injection** (enhanced oil recovery).
  - **Hydrogen** (emerging — but careful, permeation through plastic).
- **Aplikacje (other industries)**:
  - **Mining** — slurry transport.
  - **Industrial fluids** — chemical, water, heated process.
  - **District heating** — pre-insulated RTP available.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Magma Global** (UK) — m-pipe™ thermoplastic composite pipe.
  - **NOV Tuboscope** — Fiber Tube products.
  - **Soluforce / Pipelife** — Soluforce RTP.
  - **Baker Hughes** — onshore composite flexible.
  - **Future Pipe Industries**.
  - **CNPS (China)** — large-volume manufacturer.
- **Standards**:
  - **API 15S** — Spoolable Reinforced Plastic Line Pipe.
  - **API 17J / 17K** — flexible riser specifications (offshore).
  - **ASTM D2992** — long-term hydrostatic pressure rating.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Permeation** — small molecules (H₂, CO₂) can diffuse przez plastic over time.
  - **UV degradation** — outdoor exposed RTP needs UV-stabilized jacket lub burial.
  - **Higher pressure limits** vs steel (max ~100 bar for most RTPs).
  - **Fire** — plastic burns. Limited use w fire-rated areas.
- **Field deployment**:
  - **Reel truck** transports RTP coil.
  - **Reel handler** spools pipe along ROW.
  - **Trench + cover** — buried installation.
  - **End fitting installation** at terminations.
  - **Hydrotest** — same standards as steel pipe testing.

### ZZZZZZ. Refrigerant Copper Pipe Brazing — HVAC + Refrigeration
- **Brazing** = joining metals by melting filler metal at > 450°C (vs soldering < 450°C). **Standard dla copper refrigerant lines** w HVAC, refrigeration, chillers.
- **Difference from welding**: base metal NIE topi się (vs welding gdzie base + filler melt together). Brazing relies on **capillary action** drawing molten filler into joint clearance.
- **Joint design**:
  - **Cup + tube** — tube wsuwa się w cup (socket fitting).
  - **Cup ID slightly larger** than tube OD: **0.05-0.15 mm clearance** dla capillary action.
  - **Insertion depth** typowo 12-25 mm (per fitting size).
- **Filler rod types**:
  - **BCuP-2 (94% Cu + 6% P)** — copper-phosphorus, no silver. Cheapest. **Copper-to-copper only**.
  - **BCuP-3 (89% Cu + 5% Ag + 6% P)** — 5% silver, copper-to-copper.
  - **BCuP-5 (80% Cu + 15% Ag + 5% P)** — **15% silver, MOST POPULAR**. Standard dla refrigeration. Self-fluxing on copper-copper.
  - **BAg-1 (45% Ag + 15% Cu + 16% Zn + 24% Cd)** — silver brazing alloy. Versatile, strong, BUT **CADMIUM TOXIC** (use w well-ventilated areas).
  - **BAg-5 (45% Ag + 30% Cu + 25% Zn)** — Cd-free silver alloy, modern preferred.
- **Phosphorus self-fluxing** (BCuP series):
  - **P reacts with copper oxide** during brazing → cleans surface → no separate flux needed.
  - **ONLY dla copper-to-copper**.
  - **NIE używać** BCuP na steel/brass — embrittles iron (forms iron phosphide).
- **Flux usage** (BAg series, brass joints):
  - **Black flux** — high-temp (silver brazing).
  - **White paste** — lower-T standard.
  - **Apply BEFORE heat** — flux dehydrates first, then melts as brazing T approached.
- **Nitrogen purging** (KRYTYCZNE dla HVAC/refrigeration):
  - **Purpose**: prevent **copper oxide (scale)** formation inside pipe podczas heating.
  - **Internal scale problems**:
    - Loose particles → carried do compressor → bearing damage.
    - Clog **TXV (Thermostatic Expansion Valve)** — refrigerant flow disrupted.
    - Acid formation w refrigerant.
  - **N₂ flow**: **2-4 CFH (1-2 L/min)** — gently flushes air without creating pressure.
  - **Too much N₂**: causes pinhole in molten filler (gas escape through filler).
  - **Too little**: oxide forms.
  - **Connection**: nitrogen tank → regulator → flexible hose → fitting on remote pipe end.
- **Procedure** (refrigerant line braze):
  1. **Cut pipe square** — tubing cutter (rotary wheel, NOT hacksaw — leaves burrs).
  2. **Deburr inside + outside** — debur tool, ensure no chip remains.
  3. **Clean joint surfaces** — emery cloth lub Scotch-Brite. Remove oxide layer.
  4. **Wipe with clean rag** — no oil, no water residue.
  5. **Insert pipe into fitting** — verify depth to mark.
  6. **Start N₂ purge** at remote pipe end (or use trickle continuous through entire system).
  7. **Heat fitting** with oxy-acetylene torch (lub MAPP gas dla smaller jobs):
     - **Heat fitting body first** (sucks rod toward heat).
     - **Move flame around joint** uniformly.
     - **Reduction flame** (slight excess fuel) — protects copper from oxidation.
     - **Avoid heating pipe directly** — keeps wall thinner pipe from overheating.
  8. **Touch rod do joint** when copper turns dull cherry red (650-750°C).
  9. **Capillary action** draws molten alloy into joint — small puddle should flow around full circumference.
  10. **Remove heat** + **continue N₂ flow** during cooling (~2-3 min).
  11. **Wipe joint** with damp cloth (removes scale/dirt only, NOT immediately — wait until joint solidified).
- **Quality checks**:
  - **360° fillet** — even bead all around.
  - **No icicles** — bottom of joint doesn't have drip pattern.
  - **No charred copper** — overheating darkens surface.
  - **Leak test** — pressurize z N₂ to 200-400 psi, soap solution lub electronic leak detector.
- **Common errors**:
  - **No N₂ purge** — internal scale, compressor damage downstream.
  - **Overheating** — copper anneals weak, may crack later.
  - **Underheating** — rod doesn't flow, cold joint, pinhole.
  - **Dirty surface** — flux can't compensate dla heavy oxide.
  - **Wrong rod** — BCuP on brass = embrittle.
- **Refrigerant compatibility**:
  - **R-22, R-410A, R-32, R-134a** — standard brazing OK.
  - **R-744 (CO₂)** — high pressure (50-100 bar), thicker wall pipe, premium fittings.
  - **R-717 (NH₃ ammonia)** — **NOT copper** (NH₃ corrodes copper!). Use steel piping + welding.
  - **R-1234yf** — newer low-GWP refrigerant, copper compatible.
- **Safety**:
  - **Oxy-acetylene** — combustion gases. Ventilation.
  - **Flux fumes** — toxic (especially Cd in BAg-1). Respirator + ventilation.
  - **Hot work permit** w industrial setting.
  - **Refrigerant management** — EPA Section 608 certification dla refrigeration work (USA).
  - **Eye protection** — brazing glasses (shade 4-5).

## Iteration 52 — 2026-05-25

### AAAAAAA. Wireless Welding Monitor / Data Logger — Digital Welding Management
- **Wireless welding monitor / arc data logger** = electronic system **continuously recording welding parameters** (voltage, current, wire feed, travel speed, gas flow) **per weld + per welder + per workstation**. Real-time + cloud-uploaded data dla quality + productivity analysis.
- **Trend**: digital transformation of welding from "feel" to **data-driven** quality control.
- **Cel**:
  - **Quality assurance** — verify weld parameters within WPS limits.
  - **Productivity tracking** — operator factor, arc time vs total time.
  - **Cost analysis** — wire usage, gas consumption, labor.
  - **Compliance audit** — automatic record-keeping dla ASME/AWS code requirements.
  - **Welder training** — identify poor welders / parameters via data.
  - **Predictive maintenance** — wear patterns identified before failure.
- **Major systems**:
  - **Lincoln Electric CheckPoint** — flagship cloud-based welding production monitoring:
    - **Production tracking** — operator factor, arc time, deposition rate.
    - **Real-time dashboards** — KPI per cell, shift, plant.
    - **Trend projections** — predict productivity based on historical patterns.
    - **Cloud + mobile** — view from phone, tablet, PC anywhere.
    - **Generation 5** (released 2021) — modern UI, advanced analytics.
  - **Lincoln Arc Tracker** (K3019-1):
    - **Standalone data monitor** — connects to existing welder.
    - **Logs A/V/wire feed** za każdy łuk.
    - **USB export** dla offline analysis.
  - **Lincoln CWT WDL-3** (Weld Data Logger):
    - **Standalone unit** — logs up to 4,000 welds internally.
    - **Export to PC** dla analysis.
  - **Miller Insight** (suite):
    - **Centerpoint** — arc data monitoring + logging software.
    - **Insight Reporter** — analytics dashboard.
    - **Wireless Aux 1** (XMT 350 FieldPro) — wireless remote control + data logging.
  - **NEXTime (Lincoln)** — wireless module dla Ranger engine drives. Connects to smartphone via app dla preventive maintenance alerts.
- **Data captured**:
  - **Welder ID** — RFID badge identification.
  - **WPS number** active.
  - **Voltage** (real-time, average per pass).
  - **Amperage**.
  - **Wire feed speed**.
  - **Travel speed** (z encoder if mechanized).
  - **Gas flow rate**.
  - **Heat input** (J/mm) calculated.
  - **Arc time** (productive time) vs idle.
  - **Total deposited material** (kg of wire used).
  - **Defects / repairs** (manual entry).
- **Wireless connectivity**:
  - **Wi-Fi** standard dla shop applications.
  - **Cellular** dla field welding (job sites).
  - **Bluetooth** dla local data dump.
  - **CAN-bus** integration z robotic welders.
- **Reports + analytics**:
  - **Per-welder scorecard** — productivity rank, quality stats.
  - **Per-WPS compliance** — % of welds within parameter window.
  - **Cost per weld** — including labor + consumables.
  - **OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness)** — uptime, performance, quality combined.
  - **Trend analysis** — week-over-week improvements / declines.
- **Integration**:
  - **ERP** (SAP, Oracle) — links welding data do production orders.
  - **MES (Manufacturing Execution System)** — shop floor visibility.
  - **CMMS** — maintenance management of welders.
  - **QC software** — defect tracking, weld map integration.
- **Pricing**:
  - **Standalone logger** (Arc Tracker): $1,000-3,000 per machine.
  - **CheckPoint subscription**: $50-200 per machine/month.
  - **Enterprise installations**: $50K-$500K+ initial + ongoing.
- **ROI** (typical):
  - **5-15% productivity improvement** w large shops.
  - **20-30% quality improvement** w arc time tracking + parameter compliance.
  - **Payback period**: 6-18 months.

### BBBBBBB. Pipe Straightening + Re-Rounding — out-of-round / kinked pipe repair
- **Out-of-round pipe** = pipe deformation z impact, transportation damage, thermal stress, etc. **Critical issue** dla:
  - **Fit-up quality** — wrong geometry prevents proper bevel mating.
  - **Welding** — uneven gap, varying mismatch.
  - **Operating life** — locked-in stresses → fatigue, cracking.
- **Causes**:
  - **Impact damage** during transport / handling.
  - **Mishandling** z slings (pinch points).
  - **Heavy load on pipe rack** during fabrication.
  - **Thermal warpage** z welding (especially thin wall).
  - **Compaction** w buried pipeline (heavy traffic above).
- **Acceptance criteria** (typowo):
  - **ASME B31.3 §304.2.2** — pipe ovality ≤ **8% of nominal OD** dla internal pressure design.
  - **External pressure** (vacuum): ≤ **3% ovality** (more sensitive).
  - **Pipeline construction**: typically 1-2% max acceptable.
  - **Bend OD reduction** after bending: ≤ 5%.
- **Re-rounding methods** (small diameter, copper / soft alloy):
  - **REED Round It tool** — manual rerounding tool, fits inside pipe, provides leverage. **DN 25-50 typowo**.
  - **Pipe expander** (manual / hydraulic) — internal expanding head pushes pipe walls outward.
  - **Rubber mallet** — tap external circumference (light, careful — avoid further damage).
  - **Wooden dowel / mandrel inside** + external taps — restore ID + OD.
  - **Steel rod / mandrel** dla heavier copper tubing.
- **Re-rounding methods** (medium diameter, CS / SS):
  - **Hydraulic jack** — placed inside pipe ID, expands outward against deformed area.
  - **Cross-bow type bender** — hydraulic press w configuration squeezing pipe back into shape.
  - **Squeeze chuck** — large clamping tool dla manufacturing scale.
  - **Pipe sizing rings** — heavy steel rings shaped do correct ID, slip onto pipe end + pushed dla forming.
- **Heat-assisted methods** (CS / large diameter):
  - **Local heating** of deformed area do ~600°C (cherry red).
  - **Mechanical force** applied during heated state.
  - **Cool naturally** — pipe retains new shape.
  - **WARNING**: must NOT exceed PWHT temperature dla pipe specification (avoid material property change).
- **Pipeline crawler systems** (long-distance corrective work):
  - **Mechanical rerounder crawler** — runs inside pipe, rerounds locally where needed.
  - **Used w pipeline replacements**, retrofit, post-incident repairs.
- **Pipe straightening** (axial / bow):
  - **Bowed pipe** (banana-curved) — straightened by:
    - **Flame straightening** (covered iter 39).
    - **Hydraulic press** — mechanical straightening.
    - **Rotation + pressure roller** — pipe rolled past adjustable rollers, gradually straightens.
- **Acceptance / inspection after re-rounding**:
  - **OD measurement** at multiple points (top, bottom, 3-9 o'clock).
  - **Ovality calculation**: (OD_max − OD_min) / OD_nominal × 100%.
  - **Drift mandrel test** — straight bar slightly smaller than ID passes through (verifies no internal restriction).
  - **Wall thickness UT** — verify no thinning during re-rounding (cold work could thin).
  - **Hardness test** (CS / alloy) — verify no hardening from cold work.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Permanent damage** if pipe yielded too far — cracks, embrittlement.
  - **Cold-worked metal** properties change — may need anneal.
  - **CS large diameter ≥ DN 600** — often easier to cut out + replace section than re-round.
- **Damage prevention** (better than rerounding):
  - **Pipe protectors** during transport (end caps + circumferential supports).
  - **Soft slings** dla lifting (NOT bare chain).
  - **Even support spacing** dla long pipe runs.
  - **Climate-controlled storage** dla coiled HDPE / RTP.

### CCCCCCC. Storage Tank Hydrostatic Test — API 650 commissioning + settlement
- **API 650** ("Welded Tanks for Oil Storage") — standard dla **above-ground storage tanks** (AST) construction + testing.
- **Hydrotest purpose** (new tank commissioning):
  - **Weld integrity verification** — full mechanical loading reveals welding defects under stress.
  - **Foundation settlement** — confirm uniform tank settle pod loaded weight.
  - **Functional test** — all accessories (valves, gauges, manways) under operational pressure.
  - **Baseline reference** — settlement readings serve future-reference dla long-term monitoring.
  - **Safer than first-fill with product** — water is non-toxic, controllable.
- **Pre-test preparation**:
  - **Tank construction completed** including shell, floor, roof.
  - **All welds NDE** (visual, vacuum box for floor seams, RT/UT for shell).
  - **Internal cleaning** — remove construction debris.
  - **Manways closed**.
  - **Drain provisions** in place dla post-test water discharge.
  - **Settlement monuments installed** wokół tank shell base.
- **Water filling sequence** (API 650 §7.3):
  - **Fill rate**: typowo **0.5-2 m water level rise/hour** (depends on tank size).
  - **Quality**: clean fresh water — chlorides + minerals impact subsequent product service.
  - **Sometimes inhibitor added** (sodium nitrite, sodium silicate) dla post-test corrosion protection.
- **Settlement measurement points** (mandatory 6 readings per API 650):
  1. **Before test start** (empty tank, baseline).
  2. **1/4 fill height**.
  3. **1/2 fill height**.
  4. **3/4 fill height**.
  5. **Maximum fill** held **24 hours minimum** (acclimation period).
  6. **After complete draining** (recovery measurement).
- **Settlement tolerances** (API 650 Annex B):
  - **Uniform settlement** ≤ **50 mm (2")** — acceptable.
  - **Differential settlement** ≤ **13 mm per 10 m circumference (1/2" per 32 ft)** — acceptable.
  - **Exceeding tolerances**: stop filling + report to purchaser dla evaluation.
  - **Excessive differential** = foundation problem, requires investigation + repair.
- **Inspection during test**:
  - **Visual leakage check** — walk perimeter every 1-2 hours initially.
  - **Anchor bolt straps** — verify no foundation lifting.
  - **Roof seal** (if floating roof) — function check.
  - **Vapor recovery system** — if installed.
  - **Pressure/vacuum valves** — functional check.
- **Hold duration**:
  - **24 hours minimum** at maximum height.
  - **48-72 hours** typowy dla critical service tanks.
  - **Major projects**: 7-14 days hold dla foundation stabilization.
- **Post-test activities**:
  - **Drain water** controlled — typowo 0.5-2 m level fall/hour (avoid vacuum collapse).
  - **Drying** — internal blower + heat lub forced ventilation.
  - **Touch-up paint** if damaged.
  - **Final dimensional check** — diameter, roundness, vertical (out-of-plumb).
  - **Final settlement readings**.
- **Water disposal**:
  - **Discharge permit** required (regulatory).
  - **API water separator** treatment if oil contaminated.
  - **Reuse** to fill adjacent tanks (planning helps).
- **Settlement reasons** (foundation issues):
  - **Soil compaction** (loose foundation soils).
  - **Insufficient bearing capacity** (foundation design error).
  - **Differential consolidation** (varying soil moisture / density across tank base).
  - **Soft spots** (locally weak foundation).
- **Remediation options**:
  - **Uniform > 50 mm**: tank lift + foundation work, or live with it (some tanks design dla expected settlement).
  - **Differential > tolerance**: jacking + re-leveling, foundation grouting, partial replacement.
- **Inspection after first year of service**:
  - **API 653 §3.3** — settlement re-measurement annual.
  - **Trend curve**: settlement vs time. Stable curve = no concern. Increasing curve = problem.
- **Special considerations**:
  - **Heated tanks**: pre-heat during hydrotest if specified (avoid water-to-product thermal shock).
  - **Insulated tanks**: hydrotest BEFORE insulation install (saves cleanup if leak).
  - **Cryogenic / LNG tanks**: hydrotest with **specific test medium** (sometimes glycol-water).

## Iteration 53 — 2026-05-25

### DDDDDDD. ASME PCC-1 — Bolted Flange Joint Assembly Guidelines
- **ASME PCC-1** ("Guidelines for Pressure Boundary Bolted Flange Joint Assembly") = **best-practice recommended procedures** dla bolted flange assembly. Complements **ASME B16.5 (flange dimensions)** + **B16.20 (gaskets)** + **B16.34 (P-T ratings)**.
- **NOT mandatory standard** — guidelines + practices that codes (B31.3, BPVC) refer to dla flange joint integrity.
- **Scope**: pressure-boundary flanges w piping + pressure vessels.
- **Key topics covered (sections + appendices)**:
  1. **Installation prep** — flange face condition, alignment, gasket selection.
  2. **Bolt + gasket selection** — material, dimensions, condition.
  3. **Bolt + nut numbering** — sequential dla cross-pattern tightening.
  4. **Tightening sequence** — multiple-pass criss-cross (covered iter 2).
  5. **Target torque determination** — bolt preload calculations.
  6. **Pressure + tightness testing** — leak-tight verification.
  7. **Records** — assembly logs.
  8. **Joint disassembly** — proper procedure dla maintenance.
- **Appendices** (detailed methodologies):
  - **Appendix A**: Training + qualification of personnel.
  - **Appendix D**: Guidelines for assembly bolt patterns.
  - **Appendix F**: Bolt root and tensile stress areas.
  - **Appendix J**: **Target torque method** (calculated).
  - **Appendix K**: **Nut factor method** (alternative simpler approach).
  - **Appendix L**: **Leak management** post-assembly.
  - **Appendix M**: **HSE (Health, Safety, Environment)** considerations.
  - **Appendix O**: **Hot-bolting procedure** (covered iter 31).
- **Target torque calculation** (Appendix K — nut factor method):
  - **T = K × D × F**
  - T = torque [Nm or in-lbf].
  - K = nut factor (friction-dependent, typically 0.10-0.20).
  - D = nominal bolt diameter [mm or in].
  - F = target bolt preload [N or lbf].
  - **Nut factor K** depends on:
    - Lubricant type (Never-Seez K ≈ 0.13, Molykote K ≈ 0.11, dry K ≈ 0.20).
    - Bolt material + surface condition.
    - Nut + washer condition.
- **Target preload** (typically dla gasketed joints):
  - **F = (G × A_gasket × m) + (P × A_pressure)**
  - G = gasket factor (per ASME II Part D / Mfr data).
  - m = gasket maintenance factor.
  - Plus pressure-induced separation force.
- **Lubricant compatibility** (KRYTYCZNE):
  - **NEVER use random lubricant** — must be compatible z process fluid (in case leak), bolt material (no SCC), gasket material.
  - **Common acceptable**: nickel-graphite paste, Molykote G-Rapid Plus, Never-Seez Nuclear Grade.
  - **AVOID** dla oxygen service: NO oils (combustion risk).
- **Bolt + flange numbering system** (Appendix D):
  - **Cross-pattern sequence**: bolt 1-2-3-4 standardized — opposite each time.
  - **4-bolt flange**: 1 → 3 → 2 → 4 (or 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 crisscross).
  - **8-bolt flange**: 1 → 5 → 3 → 7 → 2 → 6 → 4 → 8.
  - **12-bolt flange**: 1 → 7 → 4 → 10 → 2 → 8 → 5 → 11 → 3 → 9 → 6 → 12.
  - **Pre-numbered** wokół flange w shop (paint marker lub permanent stamps).
- **Pass sequence** (multi-pass tightening):
  - **Pass 1**: 30% of target torque, full criss-cross.
  - **Pass 2**: 60% of target torque, full criss-cross.
  - **Pass 3**: 100% of target torque, full criss-cross.
  - **Pass 4 (verification)**: 100% torque, full circumferential check (no bolt rotates → done).
- **Start-up retorque** (after first heat-up):
  - **Thermal cycle** alters nut factor (lubricant breakdown, gasket relaxation).
  - **Retorque** at operating T (if possible) lub immediately after first cool-down.
  - **Adjust target torque** dla temperature-dependent nut factor.
- **Joint disassembly**:
  - **Loosening pattern** = REVERSE of tightening criss-cross.
  - **Crack-pass first** — slight loosening of each bolt before full removal.
  - **AVOID** removing bolts sequentially around perimeter (warps flange).
- **Personnel qualification**:
  - **Trained + tested** operator required dla critical assemblies.
  - **Documentation** of training maintained.
  - **Periodic recertification**.

### EEEEEEE. Magnetic / Vortex / Ultrasonic Flow Meters — Installation
- **Beyond orifice plate** (covered iter 34), other flow meters dla industrial piping:
- **Magnetic flow meter (Mag-meter, EM flow meter)**:
  - **Principle**: Faraday's Law — conductive liquid przepływający przez magnetic field generates voltage proportional do velocity.
  - **No moving parts** — extremely reliable.
  - **No pressure drop** — full bore flow.
  - **Material requirements**: **CONDUCTIVE liquid** only (water, slurries, acids — NOT pure hydrocarbons / DI water bez dodatków).
  - **Conductivity minimum**: ~5 µS/cm (5 microsiemens/cm).
  - **Liner materials**: PTFE, neoprene, polyurethane (chosen dla chemical compatibility).
  - **Electrodes**: 316 SS, Hastelloy, Tantalum (per service).
  - **Installation requirements**:
    - **5-10D upstream straight pipe**, **2-5D downstream**.
    - **Full pipe always** — flow meter must be filled (NOT siphon flow with air gaps).
    - **Pump outlet preferred**, NOT inlet (cavitation = bubbles = error).
    - **Vertical pipe** preferred (drains air upward).
    - **Grounding critical** — meter body grounded to pipe + earth ground.
    - **Cable shielding** dla noise immunity.
  - **Aplikacje**: water + wastewater, slurry, food, beverage, chemical processing, mining.
  - **Accuracy**: ±0.5% of reading typical (premium ±0.2%).
- **Vortex flow meter**:
  - **Principle**: shedder bar inserted w flow generates **alternating vortices** (von Kármán street). Frequency of vortex shedding proportional do flow velocity.
  - **Versatility**: liquids, gases, steam.
  - **No moving parts** — reliable.
  - **Installation**:
    - **Upstream straight pipe**: minimum **10-20D** (depending on disturbance).
    - **Downstream**: minimum **5D**.
    - **Away from**: strong magnetic fields, mechanical vibrations (false signals).
    - **Avoid** mounting close to pumps, valves, fittings.
    - **Bidirectional flow** OK z some models.
  - **Sensor element**: piezoelectric (heat-stable up to 425°C steam).
  - **Aplikacje**: steam metering (major application), gas flow, liquid w cleaner services.
  - **Limitations**:
    - Minimum flow threshold — vortex shedding stops below Reynolds ~10,000.
    - **NOT dla viscous fluids** (no vortex shedding).
    - **Two-phase flow** issues.
  - **Accuracy**: ±1% of reading.
- **Ultrasonic flow meter**:
  - **Two types**:
    - **Transit-time** — most common, dla clean fluids:
      - Two sensors send ultrasonic pulses upstream + downstream.
      - Time difference proportional do velocity.
      - **Accuracy**: ±1-2%.
    - **Doppler** — dla fluids z particles or bubbles:
      - Single sensor sends ultrasonic.
      - Frequency shift z particles in flow gives velocity.
      - **Less accurate** (±2-5%) but works for slurries.
  - **Clamp-on installation** — transducers mounted **on outside of pipe** without cutting:
    - **Coupling gel** between transducer + pipe surface (acoustic coupling).
    - **NO pipe modification** required.
    - **Temporary installation** common (rental dla audit measurements).
    - **Permanent** inline versions also available (transducers w pipe wall).
  - **Installation requirements**:
    - **10D upstream + 5D downstream** straight pipe.
    - **Pipe material** matters — works on most metals, plastic, FRP. Cast iron problematic (porosity).
    - **Pipe wall thickness** known + entered into meter dla calibration.
    - **Pipe material velocity** known (different materials transmit sound differently).
  - **Aplikacje**:
    - **HVAC + chiller flow**.
    - **Pipeline check meters** (audit, leak detection).
    - **Water utility** flow auditing.
    - **Custody transfer** dla some products.
  - **Accuracy**: ±1-3% clamp-on, ±0.5% inline transit time.
- **Comparison chart** dla flow meter selection:

| Meter | Conductive fluid required | Pressure drop | Accuracy | Cost | Aplikacje |
|-------|--------------------------|---------------|----------|------|-----------|
| **Orifice** | No | High | ±2% | $ | Universal, gas + liquid |
| **Vortex** | No | Medium | ±1% | $$ | Steam, gas, clean liquid |
| **Mag-meter** | **YES** | None | ±0.5% | $$ | Water, slurry, conductive chemicals |
| **Coriolis** | No | Low | ±0.1% | $$$$ | Mass flow, custody transfer |
| **Ultrasonic clamp-on** | No | None | ±2% | $$ | Audit, non-invasive measurement |
| **Ultrasonic inline** | No | None | ±0.5% | $$$ | Permanent install, clean fluids |
| **Turbine** | No | Medium | ±0.5% | $$ | Liquid, gas, custody transfer |
| **Positive displacement** | No | Medium | ±0.2% | $$$ | Viscous liquids, custody |

### FFFFFFF. Friction Stir Welding (FSW) — Solid-State Joining
- **FSW (Friction Stir Welding)** = **SOLID-STATE welding process** (no melting). **Rotating non-consumable tool** plunged w joint, traverses → generates friction heat + mechanically intermixes metal → forged joint.
- **Process basics**:
  - **Rotating tool** (special pin profile + shoulder) — typowo 200-2000 RPM.
  - **Plunged into joint** between two workpieces (butt configuration).
  - **Tool moves along joint** at controlled feed rate (cm/min).
  - **Heat generated by friction + plastic deformation** (NOT external arc / flame).
  - **Material softens** but NEVER melts — stays solid.
  - **Plasticized material extruded around tool**, forged behind.
  - **Result**: dense, fine-grained weld nugget.
- **Materials suitable**:
  - **Aluminum + alloys** — primary FSW application (especially Al-Li difficult-to-fusion-weld).
  - **Magnesium + alloys**.
  - **Copper + alloys**.
  - **Titanium + alloys**.
  - **Stainless steel** (challenging — high tool wear).
  - **Low-carbon steel** (growing application).
  - **Nickel alloys**.
  - **Dissimilar metals** (Al-Cu, Al-steel, etc.) — major advantage of FSW.
  - **Thermoplastics** — FSW variants available.
- **Advantages**:
  - **No fusion zone problems**: NO porosity, NO solidification cracking, NO liquation cracking.
  - **No shielding gas** — solid-state, no oxidation issues.
  - **No filler metal** required (solid-state forging).
  - **Lower distortion** vs fusion welding.
  - **Higher mechanical properties** w weld zone (fine grain w nugget).
  - **Energy efficient** — 75-90% less energy vs arc welding.
  - **Environment-friendly** — no fumes, no spatter, no UV radiation.
  - **Repeatable + automation-friendly** — CNC-controlled.
- **Limitations**:
  - **Joint configurations limited** — primarily butt + lap. Difficult dla complex 3D shapes.
  - **Requires backing anvil** — workpiece supported od dołu.
  - **Tool wear** dla harder materials (steel, Ti) — exotic tools (W-Re alloys, PCBN).
  - **Investment**: $500K-$3M dla machine + tooling (vs $10K-100K arc welder).
  - **NOT portable** — fixed equipment, not field-deployable typically.
  - **Capable thickness**: 1-50 mm typowo, special tools up to 100 mm.
- **FSW dla pipe**:
  - **Aluminum pipe** — FSW of small diameter (DN 25-50) commercial. **Dissimilar Al-Cu pipe joints** dla heat exchanger industry.
  - **Stainless steel pipe** — research stage + niche industrial. Tool wear major issue.
  - **Carbon steel pipeline** — limited adoption, experimental.
  - **Future**: nuclear waste storage pipe (sealed Cu containers), aerospace fuel lines.
- **FSW pipe machine**:
  - **Pipe rotator** holds pipe horizontally + rotates.
  - **FSW tool head** stationary, plunges + traverses across rotating pipe.
  - **OR rotating pipe head** + stationary pipe (less common).
- **Welding parameters**:
  - **Tool rotation speed**: 300-1500 RPM dla Al, 100-600 RPM dla Ti/Steel.
  - **Travel speed**: 5-50 cm/min depending on material + thickness.
  - **Tool plunge force**: 5-50 kN (high for steel).
  - **Tool tilt angle**: 1-3° (tool leans forward dla forging action behind).
- **Tool design**:
  - **Shoulder** — generates surface heat + contains material.
  - **Pin (probe)** — extends below shoulder, mixes joint material.
  - **Pin profiles**: cylindrical, conical, threaded, tri-flat, MX Triflute (proprietary).
  - **Tool materials**:
    - **H13 steel** — Al welding.
    - **W-Re alloys** — Ti, stainless.
    - **PCBN (Polycrystalline Cubic Boron Nitride)** — most demanding, super-hard.
    - **Tungsten carbide** — moderate-hard materials.
- **Quality control**:
  - **Visual** — exit hole at end of weld (always present unless backfilled).
  - **RT / UT** — internal defect detection.
  - **Macrosection** — nugget zone characterization.
  - **Tensile + bend** — mechanical property verification.
- **Industrial adoption**:
  - **Aerospace**: Boeing Apollo capsule, F-22 fuselage panels, ESA Ariane rocket tanks.
  - **Automotive**: Ford F-150 truck Body, Honda Accord trunk, Tesla battery enclosures.
  - **Shipbuilding**: aluminum hulls + decks (high-speed ferries).
  - **Rail**: high-speed train aluminum body sections.
  - **Pipe**: emerging, limited dla now.
