## Iteration 66 — 2026-05-25

### QQQQQQQQ. Vacuum-Jacketed Pipe (VJP) — Cryogenic Transfer Lines
- **Vacuum-jacketed pipe (VJP / Vacuum Insulated Pipe — VIP)** = **double-walled pipe** z **inner carrier pipe + outer jacket + evacuated vacuum annulus** z multilayer insulation. **Premier insulation** dla cryogenic transfer (LN₂, LO₂, LAr, LNG, LH₂, LHe).
- **Construction layers** (z inside out):
  - **Inner pipe (carrier)** — typowo SS 304L/316L, transports cryogenic liquid.
  - **Multi-Layer Insulation (MLI)** wrap on inner pipe — alternating layers:
    - **Radiant heat barrier** — aluminized Mylar / Kapton (reflective).
    - **Spacer material** — fiberglass mat lub paper (non-conductive).
    - Typowo **30-60 layers** total.
  - **Getter material** — molecular sieve / activated charcoal — adsorbs out-gassed molecules, maintains vacuum over years.
  - **Vacuum annulus** — pumped down to **≤ 9 microns Hg (1.2 Pa)** — eliminates conductive + convective heat transfer.
  - **Outer jacket** — typowo SS 304L lub carbon steel painted.
- **Performance**:
  - **Heat leak**: **50× more effective than foam insulation**.
  - **Heat leak**: **200× more effective than bare copper**.
  - **Typical heat leak**: 1-2 W/m dla DN50 pipe (vs 50-100 W/m dla foam-insulated equivalent).
  - **LN₂ boil-off**: < 1% per day w typical industrial VJP system (vs 10-20% z foam).
- **Critical performance metric**:
  - **NER (Normal Evaporation Rate)** — % cryogen evaporated per day.
  - **Specified per project** — typowo ≤ 1-2%/day dla industrial.
- **Vacuum maintenance**:
  - **Getter material critical** — maintains vacuum przeciwko slow gas permeation.
  - **Vacuum pump-out port** — initial evacuation + periodic re-pumping if needed.
  - **Cold pumping**: when pipe cools z room T do cryogenic T, residual gas freezes (cryopumping) — naturally improves vacuum.
- **Internal supports** dla inner pipe:
  - **Centralizers / spacers** — keep inner pipe centered w outer (prevent contact = thermal short).
  - **Materials**: thermally insulating (PTFE, fiber-reinforced plastic).
  - **Spacing**: typowo every 0.5-1 m.
- **Expansion handling**:
  - **Inner pipe contracts** when cooled (huge thermal contraction — covered iter 25).
  - **Bellows** built into inner pipe at intervals — absorb contraction.
  - **External bellows** w outer jacket dla outer pipe thermal motion (less severe).
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Industrial gas supply** — LN₂/LO₂/LAr distribution between tank + use point.
  - **LNG terminals** — ship-to-shore + tank-to-vaporizer transfer.
  - **LH₂ infrastructure** — hydrogen fueling stations, energy applications.
  - **Cryogenic research** — particle accelerators, superconductor cooling.
  - **Space industry** — rocket fuel transfer (LOX, LH₂).
- **Sizes**:
  - **DN 15-300** typical commercial range.
  - **DN 600+** specialty dla LNG terminals.
  - **Lengths**: typowo 6-20 m sections, field-joined.
- **Field joining**:
  - **Bayonet connections** — most common. Male-female interlocking design + vacuum seal.
  - **Welded extensions** — for permanent joints, complex z inner + outer pipe welds + vacuum re-pump.
  - **Flanged** — specialty, w expensive bayonet alternative.
- **Bayonet design**:
  - **Male end** — slim probe extending from one pipe.
  - **Female end** — receiving socket on other pipe.
  - **Cryogenic seal** between male/female — typowo PTFE lip seal at warm end.
  - **Vacuum continuity** — through bayonet design (not always 100% — some heat leak).
- **Vendors**:
  - **Chart Industries** — premier USA.
  - **Technifab Products** — vacuum jacketed pipe specialty.
  - **CRYOWorks** — cryogenic transfer.
  - **OPW CES (Cryogenic Engineering Specialists)**.
  - **Crane CryoFlo** — petroleum + chemical applications.
  - **REGO** (Linde subsidiary) — Europe.
- **Standards**:
  - **CGA H-3** — Compressed Gas Association — vacuum jacketed equipment.
  - **EN 13458** — Cryogenic vessels, static vacuum insulated.
  - **ASME B31.3** — process piping (applies to VJP installation).
- **Common issues**:
  - **Vacuum loss** — getter saturated, leaks at welds — performance degrades.
  - **Internal pipe contact w outer** — manufacturing defect lub displacement during cycling.
  - **Mechanical damage** — outer jacket dented w transport, may impact vacuum.
- **Inspection**:
  - **Vacuum gauge port** — periodic check of vacuum level.
  - **External visual** — dents, paint damage, jacket integrity.
  - **Performance test** — measure boil-off rate vs baseline.
  - **Helium leak detection** dla suspected vacuum leaks.

### RRRRRRRR. Heat Exchanger Floating Head — Tube Bundle Removal Procedure
- **Floating head heat exchanger** = TEMA Type **AES / BES / AET / BET** (per iter 56) — **removable tube bundle** z **internal split-ring floating head** allowing thermal expansion. **Most common refinery exchanger** dla fouling service.
- **Why removable bundle**:
  - **Fouling service** (crude preheaters, hot oil) — frequent cleaning required.
  - **Tube replacement** — failed tubes plugged, eventually replaced.
  - **Inspection** — internal NDE access.
  - **High thermal differential** — floating head absorbs expansion bez stressing tubesheet.
- **Components** dla floating head removal:
  - **Channel head** (Type A — removable cover lub B — bonnet) at stationary tubesheet end.
  - **Stationary tubesheet** — welded to shell.
  - **Tube bundle** — tubes between two tubesheets.
  - **Floating tubesheet** — at "back" end, slides inside shell as tubes expand.
  - **Split ring** — backing ring holding floating head; **split in half** dla disassembly.
  - **Floating head cover** — bolts onto floating tubesheet.
  - **Shell cover (rear)** — closes shell at floating end, removable.
- **Removal procedure step-by-step**:
  1. **Isolate exchanger** — block valves closed both shell + tube sides.
  2. **Drain** both sides z product. **LOTO** isolation valves.
  3. **Depressurize** — vent both sides do atmospheric.
  4. **Cool down** — wait dla safe-to-touch temperature (< 60°C).
  5. **Blind tube + shell side inlet/outlet nozzles** — positive isolation per LOTO procedure.
  6. **Disconnect + remove distributor / channel** (front end):
     - Loosen channel head bolts (cross-pattern).
     - Remove channel head + gasket.
     - Now stationary tubesheet face exposed.
  7. **Disconnect + remove rear shell cover** (back end):
     - Bolts cross-pattern loosening.
     - Remove cover + gasket.
     - Rear floating head + split ring now accessible.
  8. **Remove floating head cover bolts** — exposing split ring.
  9. **Remove split ring halves** carefully — typowo lifted out (heavy, crane assist).
  10. **Floating head** disconnects from tubesheet (typically slides off).
  11. **Attach bundle puller** at stationary tubesheet (front end):
     - **Hydraulic bundle puller** (per iter 46) — attached via tubesheet bolt pattern.
     - Or **crane lift** if bundle has accessible lifting eyes.
  12. **Pull bundle** out of shell — slow, controlled (1-2 m/min):
     - Pulling force applied at stationary tubesheet end.
     - Bundle slides through baffles, baffle spacers, against shell ID.
     - Inspect during pull — visible scale, damaged tubes, broken baffles.
  13. **Set bundle on cradles** — wooden lub steel cradles supporting tube bundle horizontally.
  14. **Bundle cleaning + inspection** (per iter 46 hydroblast + ECT).
- **Reinstallation procedure**:
  - **Reverse of removal**.
  - **Critical alignment** — tubes thread through baffle holes (can be tedious).
  - **Pre-position floating head + split ring** before bundle fully inserted.
  - **Hydrotest** post-installation:
    - **Tube side hydrotest** (1.5× design pressure) — verifies tube + tube-to-tubesheet integrity.
    - **Shell side hydrotest** — verifies channel cover + floating head sealing.
    - **Both sides simultaneously** sometimes possible.
- **Common issues**:
  - **Stuck bundle** — corrosion holding bundle to shell. Use hydraulic puller + sometimes thermal cycling.
  - **Bent tubes** — bundle damage during pull lub bend test fail.
  - **Damaged baffles** — replaced as needed.
  - **Tube-to-tubesheet leaks** — found in post-installation hydrotest, plug damaged tubes.
- **Field tools** required:
  - **Hydraulic bundle puller** (truck-mounted, $5K-25K rental per job).
  - **Crane** — minimum 25-100 ton depending on bundle size.
  - **Spreader bars** — distribute lift force evenly.
  - **Slings** — synthetic dla finished surfaces.
  - **Bolt heaters** — for hot bolting of stuck flange bolts.
- **Lift planning**:
  - **Bundle weight** typowo 5-50 ton dla industrial exchangers.
  - **Pre-lift inspection** of crane, slings, rigging.
  - **Tag lines** dla controlled positioning.
  - **Pre-rig** simulation w 3D modeling dla complex jobs.
- **Refurbishment**:
  - **Re-tubing** — replace all tubes (expensive, every 15-30 years).
  - **Tube plugging** — preemptive for failed tubes (limit ~10% before re-tube).
  - **Baffle replacement** — degraded baffles.
  - **Tubesheet repair** — re-rolling holes if eroded.

### SSSSSSSS. Pipe Scaffolding — Tube + Coupler Scaffolds dla Pipefitter Work
- **Scaffold** = temporary elevated platform dla worker access. Pipefitter / welder routinely works at heights (pipe rack levels, vessel access, exchanger maintenance) — scaffolding critical safety equipment.
- **OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart L** ("Scaffolds") = primary USA regulation. Applies do all construction scaffolds.
- **Scaffold types** (typowo used dla industrial pipefitter work):
  - **Tube + Coupler Scaffold** — pre-cut tubes (1.9" / 48 mm OD typically) connected z **mechanical couplers** (clamps + bolts):
    - **Most flexible** — built do any height + shape, fits around obstacles.
    - **Heavy loads** OK (commonly 25-75 psf live load).
    - **Multi-story possible** (50+ ft heights common).
    - **Disadvantage**: requires skilled assembly, time-consuming.
    - **Standard dla petrochemical, refinery work**.
  - **System scaffold (modular / cup-lock / ring-lock)** — pre-fabricated frames + ledgers:
    - **Cuplock (Cup-Lok)** — British system, cups receive ledger heads.
    - **Ringlock / Layher SpeedyScaf** — German system, rosette receives wedge connectors.
    - **Faster assembly** vs tube + coupler.
    - **Less flexibility** dla unusual configurations.
  - **Frame (fabricated tubular) scaffold** — pre-welded H-frames + cross braces:
    - **Easy assembly** (consumer-grade lub light commercial).
    - **Limited dla industrial** — pipe rack access.
  - **Mobile (rolling) scaffold** — frame scaffold z casters:
    - **Inside-the-plant** dla machinery access.
    - **Height limit** (typowo 4× base width minimum).
  - **Suspended scaffold** — hung from above (cables + winch):
    - **Vessel exterior + tank work**.
- **Components**:
  - **Tubes** — 1.9" OD (48 mm) standard, galvanized steel lub aluminum.
  - **Couplers**:
    - **Right-angle clamps** — connect tubes at 90°.
    - **Swivel clamps** — connect at any angle.
    - **Sleeve couplers** — join tubes end-to-end.
    - **Putlog couplers** — light-duty.
  - **Base plates** — distribute load do ground.
  - **Mud sills** — wooden planks under base plates (soft ground).
  - **Adjustable jacks** — fine-tune height + level.
  - **Planks** — typowo 2×10 lub 2×12 wood, lub aluminum / steel deck.
  - **Toe boards** — 4" high planks at edges preventing tool falls.
  - **Guardrails** — 39-45" handrail + mid-rail (per OSHA).
  - **Ladders** — internal access between levels.
- **Load capacity** (OSHA 1926.451):
  - **Light duty**: 25 psf (122 kg/m²).
  - **Medium duty**: 50 psf (244 kg/m²).
  - **Heavy duty**: 75 psf (366 kg/m²).
  - **Special design**: > 75 psf, engineering required.
- **Tag system** (KRYTYCZNY OSHA):
  - **Green tag** = **READY TO USE**:
    - Inspected by competent person.
    - All safety features installed.
    - Date + inspector name on tag.
  - **Yellow tag** = **USE WITH CAUTION**:
    - Modifications in progress lub limited use.
    - Specific restrictions listed.
    - Special PPE required (fall harness mandatory).
  - **Red tag** = **DO NOT USE**:
    - Unsafe condition.
    - Erection / disassembly in progress.
    - Damaged components.
  - **Tag location**: at access point (ladder / stair entrance).
  - **Daily verification** — competent person re-inspects + updates.
- **Competent Person** (per OSHA):
  - **Training** w OSHA scaffold regulations + scaffold-specific hazards.
  - **Authority** do stop work + make corrections.
  - **Daily inspection** before shift + after any events (rain, vehicle impact, modifications).
- **Inspection items**:
  - **Verticality + level** of frames.
  - **Tight couplers** + base plates.
  - **Planks intact** + uniform width.
  - **Guardrails + toe boards** complete.
  - **Access ladders** secured.
  - **No damaged components** w use.
  - **Adequate ground support**.
- **Fall protection** dla pipefitter on scaffold:
  - **Guardrails** at 39-45" + mid-rail 21".
  - **Personal Fall Arrest System (PFAS)** required when guardrails not feasible:
    - **Full body harness** + lanyard + anchor.
    - **6 ft maximum free fall**.
    - **Self-retracting lifeline (SRL)** preferred.
  - **Scaffold-internal anchor points** typowo NOT acceptable dla PFAS — separate structural anchor needed.
- **Erection / dismantling**:
  - **OSHA-trained crew** required.
  - **Top-down erection** + reverse dismantling.
  - **Above scaffold work** — fall protection mandatory.
  - **Inspect new components** before installation.
- **Site planning considerations**:
  - **Access from grade** — clear path.
  - **Material hoisting** — typowo hand-up by crew lub crane assist.
  - **Tool tethering** — fall protection dla wrenches + tools (cause of "struck-by" injuries below).
  - **Worker count limit** based on scaffold rating.
- **Standards / regulations**:
  - **OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451** — general requirements.
  - **OSHA 1926.452** — additional requirements specific scaffold types.
  - **OSHA 1926.453** — aerial lifts.
  - **ANSI / SSFI** — standards dla scaffold components (similar to ASTM).
- **Vendors / suppliers**:
  - **Layher** — German premier system scaffold.
  - **PERI / Doka** — formwork + scaffolding.
  - **SafeWay Scaffolds** — USA.
  - **Sunbelt Scaffold** — rental + sales.
  - **United Rentals** — industrial scaffold service.

## Iteration 67 — 2026-05-26

### TTTTTTTT. ESD (Emergency Shutdown) Valves + Spring-Return Fail-Safe Actuators
- **ESD (Emergency Shutdown) valve / ESDV** = **last-line defense** valve that **automatically closes (lub opens) under emergency conditions** preventing pipeline / process catastrophe. **Fail-safe design** — must function even with loss of power, control air, hydraulic.
- **Triggers** dla ESD activation:
  - **SIS (Safety Instrumented System)** signal — automatic from process sensors.
  - **Manual emergency stop** — operator-initiated button.
  - **Loss of power / control air** — fail-safe automatic activation.
  - **Fire detection** — heat / flame detectors trigger isolation.
  - **Gas detection** — H₂S, LEL, toxic gas above thresholds.
- **Fail-safe configurations**:
  - **FC (Fail-Close)** — valve closes on loss of signal/power. **STANDARD dla flammable + toxic services**.
  - **FO (Fail-Open)** — valve opens on loss. Used dla relief systems, cooling water (loss of cooling = greater hazard).
  - **FL (Fail-Locked / Last position)** — valve holds last commanded position. Niche applications.
- **Actuator types** dla ESD valves:
  - **Spring-return pneumatic actuator** — STANDARD:
    - **Compressed air pressure** holds valve open against **powerful spring**.
    - **Loss of air** → spring drives valve closed.
    - **Single-acting** — one air port (other side is spring chamber).
    - **Reliable** — purely mechanical fail-safe.
  - **Spring-return hydraulic actuator** — heavy-duty:
    - Same concept as pneumatic, ale **hydraulic oil** (much higher pressures available).
    - **Used dla large valves** (DN 600+) requiring large operating force.
    - **Slower than pneumatic** (less compressible fluid).
  - **Accumulator-based actuator**:
    - **Hydraulic accumulator** stores pressurized fluid + nitrogen pre-charge.
    - On ESD signal, **accumulator releases** pressure do drive valve.
    - **Fast closure** — full stroke w seconds.
    - **High reliability** — accumulator stays charged even z power lost.
  - **Hybrid (spring + accumulator)** — best of both:
    - Spring dla primary fail-safe.
    - Accumulator dla rapid response.
  - **NIE dla critical ESD**:
    - **Pure electric actuator** — fails to last position w power loss (NOT acceptable dla flammable). Limited do non-critical ESD.
- **Critical sizing**:
  - **Closure time**: typowo **≤ 2-4 seconds** dla flammable gas line ESD. PHMSA + IGEM regulations.
  - **Torque margin**: actuator output > valve required torque z 1.5-2× safety factor.
- **Actuator components**:
  - **Cylinder body** — hydraulic / pneumatic chamber.
  - **Piston + rod** — converts pressure to linear motion.
  - **Scotch yoke** lub **rack & pinion** — converts linear do rotary (dla quarter-turn valves).
  - **Springs** — multiple parallel springs dla high force.
  - **Position transmitter** — feedback do DCS / SIS.
  - **Solenoid valves** — control air / hydraulic to actuator.
  - **Quick-exhaust valve** — speeds up closure (rapid air dump).
- **SIL ratings** (Safety Integrity Level):
  - **SIL 1**: 90-99% probability of fail-safe operation.
  - **SIL 2**: 99-99.9%.
  - **SIL 3**: 99.9-99.99% — critical applications.
  - **SIL 4**: 99.99%+ — extremely rare.
  - **ESD valves** typowo **SIL 2 or SIL 3** required.
- **Testing requirements**:
  - **Partial stroke testing (PST)** — valve moves 10-15% partial closure w running. **Online verification** without shutdown.
  - **Full stroke testing** — annual lub bi-annual full close/open.
  - **Response time measurement** — record actual closure time.
  - **Records mandatory** dla regulatory compliance.
- **Position indication**:
  - **Limit switches** — open + closed feedback.
  - **Position transmitter** — analog (4-20 mA) position signal.
  - **Visual indicator** — flag visible from ground showing valve position.
- **Standards**:
  - **IEC 61508** — Functional safety general framework.
  - **IEC 61511** — Process industry SIS.
  - **API 6FA / 607** — Fire-test (covered iter 9).
  - **API 6D** — Pipeline valves (covered iter 41).
- **Vendors**:
  - **Bettis (Emerson)** — actuator + valve packages.
  - **Rotork** — UK premier actuator.
  - **Cowan Dynamics** — heavy-duty ESD actuator systems.
  - **Advanced Actuators** — hydraulic ESD specialist.

### UUUUUUUU. Slug Catcher — Pipeline Inlet Gas/Liquid Separation
- **Slug catcher** = vessel lub multi-pipe assembly at **pipeline arrival point** (LNG terminal, gas processing plant) that **separates incoming gas + liquid slugs** before processing.
- **Why needed**:
  - **Long pipelines** transport mixed-phase fluid (gas + condensate + water).
  - **Pigging** displaces accumulated liquid slugs (large volumes arriving suddenly).
  - **Two-phase flow regime** — slug flow generates intermittent liquid waves.
  - **Process equipment downstream cannot handle slugs** — pumps cavitate, dehydrators flood, compressors take liquid (catastrophic).
- **Two main types**:
  - **Vessel-type slug catcher**:
    - **Conventional pressure vessel** dla separation.
    - **Cyclonic inlet** lub **inlet baffle**.
    - **Mist eliminator** at top dla droplet removal.
    - **Liquid level controller** managing outlet.
    - **Easier dla small-medium volume** (< 100 m³ liquid storage).
    - **More expensive** dla high pressure (thick wall vessel).
  - **Finger-type slug catcher (multi-pipe)** — **PREMIER design** dla high-pressure / large-volume:
    - **Pipe array** instead of single vessel.
    - **2-8 horizontal pipes** (typowo DN 600-1200) at **sloped angle** (1-3°).
    - **Liquid drops do bottom** of each pipe; gas remains above.
    - **Pipes act as both** separator + liquid storage.
    - **Lower cost** dla high-pressure service (multiple small pipes < one large vessel walls).
    - **Larger storage** (km of pipe possible).
- **Finger-type configuration**:
  - **Inlet manifold** — splits incoming flow into 2-8 fingers.
  - **Fingers** — horizontal pipes, slightly sloped downward toward outlet.
  - **Separation section** — first portion of finger, gas-liquid separates by gravity.
  - **Intermediate section** — gas risers tap off top of pipe.
  - **Slug storage section** — bottom of finger holds accumulated liquid.
  - **Liquid header** — perpendicular do fingers, collects liquid from all.
  - **Gas equalization lines** — connect tops of fingers, balance pressure.
  - **Outlet**: gas → mist eliminator → downstream process; liquid → stabilizer / storage.
- **Sizing variables**:
  - **Maximum slug volume** expected from pipeline (function of pipe size, length, flow pattern).
  - **Pigging slug volume** — typowo dominant sizing case.
  - **Design pressure** — matches pipeline.
  - **Surge time** — desired residence dla level control to respond.
- **Materials**:
  - **CS API 5L X65/X70** — typowo dla onshore.
  - **Sour service grades** (NACE MR0175) dla H₂S.
  - **CS w corrosion allowance** lub clad CRA dla CO₂ + water.
- **Pressure ratings**:
  - **High-pressure pipeline arrival**: 70-100+ bar.
  - **Fingers + manifold** rated dla full pipeline pressure.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **LNG receiving terminals** — pipeline gas separated z any liquid carry-over.
  - **Natural gas processing plants** — pipeline inlet processing.
  - **Offshore platform gathering** — separation post collection.
- **Operating considerations**:
  - **Pig receiver integration** — pig arrival surge handled by slug catcher.
  - **Level control critical** — too high = liquid carry-over, too low = gas blow-by.
  - **Solid handling** — sand + scale particles settle dla periodic drain.
  - **Slug arrival monitoring** — pressure / level sensors w each finger.
- **Standards**:
  - **API 12J** — Specification dla oil + gas separators (vessel-type).
  - **GPSA Engineering Data Book** — design recommendations.
  - **NORSOK Standards** — Norwegian Continental Shelf.
- **Vendors / EPC**:
  - **Taylor Forge Engineered Systems** — slug catcher specialty.
  - **Saudi Arabia Pipeline projects** — large installations.
  - **Sulzer, Koch-Glitsch** — internals (mist eliminators).

### VVVVVVVV. Demineralized Water Piping — Boiler Feedwater + Ultrapure Service
- **Demineralized water (demin water, DM water, polished water)** = water z **virtually all dissolved minerals removed**. **Critical dla boiler feedwater**, semiconductor manufacturing, pharma, laboratory.
- **Why demineralized**:
  - **Boiler scale prevention** — minerals (Ca, Mg) form scale on heat exchanger tubes, reducing efficiency + causing tube failures.
  - **Steam purity** — minerals carry over w steam, deposit w turbines (efficiency loss + erosion).
  - **Electronics + pharma** — ultrapure water dla product quality.
- **Conductivity levels**:
  - **Tap water**: 200-800 µS/cm.
  - **RO permeate**: 5-20 µS/cm.
  - **Single-stage demin**: 1-5 µS/cm.
  - **Mixed-bed polishing**: < 0.1 µS/cm (0.055 µS/cm = theoretical pure water).
  - **EDI (Electrodeionization)**: < 0.1 µS/cm continuous.
- **Production methods**:
  - **Ion exchange** — cation + anion + mixed bed resins.
  - **RO (Reverse Osmosis)** — semi-permeable membrane.
  - **EDI (Electrodeionization)** — continuous ion exchange z DC electric field.
  - **MED (Multi-Effect Distillation)** — thermal separation.
- **Why demin water is CORROSIVE** (counterintuitive!):
  - **Negative Langelier Saturation Index** — water has no buffering minerals.
  - **Pure water hungry** dla dissolved materials → leaches metal ions z piping.
  - **Removes protective passive layer** from stainless steel.
  - **Higher CO₂ absorption** w some demin → carbonic acid formation.
- **Material selection dla demin piping**:
  - **SS 304 / 304L** — **STANDARD** dla cold demin water (T < 60°C):
    - Provides corrosion resistance.
    - **Welds critical** — pickle + passivate w field welds.
  - **SS 316 / 316L** — premium dla:
    - **Hot demin water** (> 60°C).
    - **Aerated water** (some O₂ present).
    - **High-purity polishing** loops.
  - **Duplex SS** — extreme service (high T + high purity).
  - **Titanium** — rare, ultra-corrosive conditions.
  - **HDPE / PVC** — cold demin only, NOT high pressure.
  - **NIE używać** dla demin: CS (corrodes), galvanized (Zn leach), copper (Cu leach), brass.
- **Joining**:
  - **Welded preferred** — minimize crevices.
  - **Orbital GTAW** (per iter 6) — clean ID, no contamination, consistent quality.
  - **NIE NPT threaded** dla critical (crevice corrosion + Teflon tape contamination).
  - **Flanges** — flat face lub raised face z PTFE / EPDM gaskets (NIE graphite — contaminates).
- **Surface finish**:
  - **High-purity demin (semiconductor)**: ID polished Ra < 0.5 µm, electropolished (similar do BPE per iter 6).
  - **Boiler feedwater grade**: standard mill ID acceptable.
- **System cleanliness practices**:
  - **No carbon steel tools** w SS piping (Fe contamination).
  - **Dedicated SS workspace** — separate from CS fabrication.
  - **Pickling + passivation** post-welding (per iter 4 — nitric + HF acid).
  - **Pre-installation flushing** z demin water — verify outlet conductivity matches inlet (no contamination).
- **Common system components**:
  - **Make-up demin tank** — typowo SS 304, conical bottom, gravity drain.
  - **Recirculation loop** — pumps + polishing mixed-bed exchanger.
  - **Distribution piping** do users (boiler, process, lab).
  - **Sample stations** — conductivity, silica, pH monitoring.
- **Conductivity monitoring**:
  - **Inline sensors** at multiple points.
  - **Tracks water quality** + leaks (dissolved CO₂ enters → conductivity rises).
  - **Alarms** dla excursions.
- **Power plant feedwater treatment** (typical sequence):
  - **Raw water** → **Multimedia filtration** → **Carbon filter** (chlorine removal) → **RO** (1st stage 95% removal) → **EDI / Mixed Bed** (final polish) → **Demin tank** → **Boiler feed pump** → **Boiler economizer** → **Steam drum**.
- **Storage tank design**:
  - **Gas blanketing** — N₂ over water (excludes O₂ + CO₂).
  - **Sealed vents** — bladder vents prevent atmospheric contamination.
  - **Heated** (some applications) — prevents biological growth.
- **Standards**:
  - **ASTM D1193** — Reagent Water (laboratory grades).
  - **ASME PTC-19.11** — Steam + Water Sampling.
  - **EPRI** — Industry guidance dla power plants.
  - **ISPE GAMP** — Pharma demin water.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Eurowater / Grundfos**.
  - **Veolia Water Technologies**.
  - **Ovivo (formerly GE)**.
  - **Nijhuis Saur Industries**.
  - **ChemTreat / Suez WTS**.

## Iteration 68 — 2026-05-26

### WWWWWWWW. Hydraulic Piping System — High-Pressure Fluid Power
- **Hydraulic piping** = system carrying **pressurized oil** (lub water-glycol, phosphate ester) dla **fluid power transmission**. Different from process piping:
  - **Small bore** typowo (DN 6-50 mm).
  - **VERY high pressure** (100-700 bar typowy, do 1000 bar dla specialty).
  - **Pulsating flow** (pump cycles).
  - **Tubing + fittings** dominant (rare welded joints).
- **Applications**:
  - **Mobile equipment** — excavators, dozers, cranes, agricultural.
  - **Industrial machinery** — presses, injection molders, balers.
  - **Aerospace** — flight controls, landing gear actuation.
  - **Marine** — steering, hatch covers, lifeboat davits.
  - **Industrial automation** — process equipment positioners.
  - **Wind turbines** — blade pitch control, brakes.
- **System components**:
  - **Pump** — gear, vane, piston, axial piston.
  - **Reservoir / tank** — oil storage + cooling + air separation.
  - **Filter** — typowo 10-25 µm absolute.
  - **Cooler** — oil cooler (air lub water heat exchanger).
  - **Hydraulic motor** lub **cylinder** — actuator (linear lub rotary).
  - **Directional control valve** — solenoid lub manual.
  - **Pressure relief valve** — overpressure protection.
  - **Accumulator** — pulsation dampening + emergency reserve.
  - **Hydraulic hoses** — flexible connections.
  - **Hydraulic tubing** — rigid lines.
- **Tubing materials**:
  - **Cold-drawn seamless steel** (SAE J356, DIN 2391) — STANDARD.
  - **Stainless steel 316L** — marine + corrosive environments.
  - **Bright annealed tubing** — soft, easily bent.
  - **High-pressure variants** — specialty alloys, thicker walls.
- **Tubing sizes** (typical metric):
  - **6 × 1 mm** (6 mm OD × 1 mm wall) — small actuators.
  - **8 × 1.5, 10 × 1.5, 12 × 1.5** — medium service.
  - **15 × 2.5, 18 × 3, 22 × 3, 28 × 4** — heavy duty.
  - **35 × 5, 42 × 6** — main pump lines.
- **Working pressure rating** (function of wall thickness + material):
  - **Light-duty (L series)**: up to 250 bar.
  - **Medium-duty (M series)**: up to 320 bar.
  - **Heavy-duty (S series)**: up to **400-630 bar**.
- **Fitting types** (high-pressure tube connections):
  - **JIC 37° flare** — SAE standard (USA), male/female with 37° cone.
  - **ORFS (O-Ring Face Seal)** — flat face z O-ring seal, very reliable.
  - **DIN 24°** — German standard, cone + sleeve compression.
  - **ISO 6149 (M series)** — metric thread + O-ring seal. MODERN standard.
  - **DIN 2353 / ISO 8434-1** — compression fitting standard.
  - **BSPP (British Standard Pipe Parallel)** — older British standard.
  - **NPT** — limited do low-pressure hydraulic only (NIE preferred high-pressure).
- **ISO 6149** (most modern metric):
  - **Metric thread (M14×1.5, M18×1.5, etc.)** parallel.
  - **O-ring face seal** — eliminates Teflon tape / sealant.
  - **Working pressure**: do 630 bar dla non-adjustable, 400 bar adjustable.
  - **Three parts**: 6149-1 (ports), 6149-2 (heavy-duty S series), 6149-3 (light-duty L series).
- **Fitting installation**:
  - **Cut tube square** — burrs removed inside + outside.
  - **Thread inspection** — no damage.
  - **O-ring lubrication** z compatible hydraulic oil.
  - **Hand-tight** + final wrench torque per spec table.
  - **NIE over-tighten** — crushes O-ring lub deforms threads.
- **Hose vs tubing**:
  - **Tubing** — permanent installations, no flex required.
  - **Hose** — moving applications (cylinder z extending arm).
  - **Hose construction**: inner tube + reinforcement (wire braid + spiral) + outer cover.
  - **Hose pressure ratings**: 70-700 bar depending on construction.
- **Common problems**:
  - **Contamination** — most common failure cause. Particles ruin pumps + valves.
  - **Heat** — degrades oil, embrittles hoses, leaks at fittings.
  - **Cavitation** — pump damage z entrained air.
  - **Pulsations** — pump cycles fatigue lines.
- **Cleanliness standards**:
  - **ISO 4406** rating — particle count w 3 sizes (e.g., 18/16/13).
  - **Hydraulic system flushing** to target cleanliness w startup.
- **Test methods**:
  - **Hydrostatic test** — 150% working pressure dla certification.
  - **Burst test** — 4× working pressure minimum.
  - **Pulsation test** — cyclic dla fatigue verification.

### XXXXXXXX. Vapor Recovery Unit (VRU) — Storage Tank Emissions Control
- **VRU (Vapor Recovery Unit)** = compression-based system **capturing fugitive hydrocarbon vapors** from storage tanks + low-pressure equipment. **Compresses vapors back into pipeline** lub processing system. **Methane emission reduction** — key EPA / climate compliance technology.
- **Why VRU needed**:
  - **Storage tank vapor space** w hot weather → product evaporates → vents do atmosphere = lost product + emissions.
  - **Methane** = potent greenhouse gas (~25× CO₂ effect over 100 years).
  - **VOC (Volatile Organic Compounds)** = smog precursors + health hazards.
  - **EPA regulations** — 40 CFR Part 60 Subpart OOOO ("Quad O") + OOOOa/b/c demand emission control.
  - **Revenue recovery** — captured vapors are valuable product (gas, LPG).
- **EPA requirements (Quad O / OOOOa)**:
  - **95% vapor capture** dla regulated storage tanks.
  - **Continuous operation** typowo required.
  - **Periodic testing + reporting**.
- **VRU process flow**:
  1. **Vapor source** (storage tank, separator, dehydrator):
     - **Low pressure**: 0.25-2 psig typical.
     - **Connected via** vapor return line to VRU.
  2. **Suction scrubber (liquid separator)**:
     - Removes condensed liquids from saturated vapor.
     - Liquids return do tank lub disposal.
  3. **Compressor**:
     - **Increases vapor pressure** to discharge requirement.
     - **Pressure ratio**: typowo 30-150× (e.g., 1 psig → 50 psig).
     - **Types**: rotary screw (most common), scroll, sliding vane, piston.
  4. **After-cooler** — removes compression heat.
  5. **Discharge separator** — final liquid removal.
  6. **Outlet** — back do gas pipeline, fuel gas system, lub processing unit.
- **VRU types**:
  - **Single-stage rotary screw VRU** — STANDARD, oilfield + tank battery.
  - **Two-stage VRU** — higher compression ratios.
  - **VRT (Vapor Recovery Tower)** — gas-tight tank atop storage, captures vapors at source.
  - **Diaphragm compressor VRU** — sensitive vapor (no oil contamination).
- **Piping system design**:
  - **Vapor return line** — sized dla expected vapor volume + low pressure drop:
    - **DN 50-200 typical** dla single tank.
    - **DN 200-400** dla multi-tank manifold.
  - **Vapor line slope**: 1:100 minimum toward knockout drum (drainage).
  - **Liquid traps** at low points.
  - **Pressure relief valves** — protect tank from over-pressure / vacuum.
  - **Flame arrestors** — prevent flame propagation back to tank.
  - **Block valves** dla isolation during VRU maintenance.
- **Material selection**:
  - **CS pipe** dla wet vapor (need water knockout).
  - **SS** dla aggressive vapor (H₂S, mercaptans).
  - **PVC / FRP** dla low-pressure non-corrosive vent lines.
- **Sizing parameters**:
  - **Vapor volume** — function of tank breathing (working losses) + storage standing losses.
  - **API E&P E14** — VRU sizing guidelines.
  - **VRU capacity** typowo 5-500 SCFM dla oilfield, larger dla refineries.
- **Safety features**:
  - **High-pressure shutdown** — protects compressor.
  - **Low-pressure shutdown** — prevents vacuum (air ingress to tank).
  - **Flame arrestors** mandatory.
  - **Vapor explosion-proof** electrical (Class 1 Div 1 lub Zone 0/1).
  - **Gas detection** w VRU enclosure.
- **Common issues**:
  - **Liquid carryover** — damages compressor (needs robust knockout drum).
  - **Tank pressure cycling** — VRU on/off cycles. Optimize z larger vapor balance line + accumulator.
  - **Cold weather** — vapors condense in vapor line. Heat tracing required.
  - **Sulfur compounds** — corrosive vapors, premium materials needed.
- **Economic justification**:
  - **Recovery value**: 100-500 MCF/year per tank typowo recovered.
  - **Payback**: 1-3 years dla typical oil + gas storage applications.
- **Standards / vendors**:
  - **API E&P E14** — VRU sizing.
  - **EPA Natural Gas STAR** — methane reduction technology recommendations.
  - **GENERON** — vapor recovery + permeation separation.
  - **Whirlwind / Power Service**.
  - **DNOW (Distribution NOW)** — distribution.

### YYYYYYYY. Welder Cooling Vest — Heat Stress Management dla Hot Work
- **Welder heat stress** = serious occupational hazard. Heavy PPE (jacket, gloves, helmet) + radiant heat z welding arc + ambient industrial heat → core body temperature rise.
- **Heat illness progression**:
  - **Heat rash** — mild irritation, skin moisture issue.
  - **Heat cramps** — electrolyte depletion z sweating.
  - **Heat exhaustion** — heavy sweating, fatigue, dizziness (core T 38-39°C).
  - **Heat stroke** — life-threatening (core T > 40°C). **Medical emergency**.
- **Risk factors**:
  - **Hot ambient** (> 30°C).
  - **Humidity** (sweat evaporation impeded > 70% RH).
  - **Heavy PPE** w fire-resistant clothing trapping heat.
  - **Physical exertion** — welders carrying tools, climbing scaffolding.
  - **Confined spaces** — limited ventilation, heat accumulation.
  - **Personal factors** — age, fitness, hydration, alcohol use.
- **Cooling vest technologies**:
  - **Phase Change Material (PCM)** — most popular:
    - **Encased gel packs** w pockets distributed across vest body.
    - **Melt at specific temperature** (typowo 14-28°C / 58-82°F).
    - **Absorbs body heat** during melting (latent heat).
    - **Provides 2-4 hours cooling** before re-freezing required.
    - **Re-cools w refrigerator** lub ice water in 15-60 min.
  - **Ice pack cooling**:
    - **Frozen gel packs** lub ice cubes.
    - **Very cold initial** but shorter duration.
    - **Higher temperature differential** can cause discomfort lub frostbite.
  - **Evaporative cooling**:
    - **Vest soaked w water** evaporates as worker moves.
    - **Cheap** ($25-75) — economic option.
    - **NIE effective w high humidity** (no evaporation possible).
    - **Requires dry environment + air flow**.
  - **Active cooling**:
    - **Battery-powered fans** circulating air through vest.
    - **Liquid-circulating** — chilled water through vest tubing.
    - **More expensive** ($300-1000).
    - **Longer cooling duration** (battery-dependent).
  - **Hybrid (PCM + evaporative)**:
    - **EZ-Cool Max** type — combined technology.
    - **Best of both worlds**: PCM core cooling + evaporative outer layer.
- **PCM specifications**:
  - **Melt temperature options**:
    - **14°C (58°F)** — most aggressive cooling, longest body cooling effect.
    - **20°C (68°F)** — comfortable, less shock.
    - **28°C (82°F)** — mild, longer duration ale less intensive.
  - **Material**: organic paraffin wax lub salt hydrate (non-toxic).
  - **Pack size**: 200-500 g per pack typowo.
  - **Vest holds**: 8-16 packs distributed across torso.
- **Welder-specific design**:
  - **FR (Flame-Resistant) outer shell** — wymagana dla welding environment.
  - **Nomex** lub modacrylic blends.
  - **No exposed plastic** at welding zones.
  - **Compatible z hi-vis (per iter 64)** — combo FR + Hi-Vis + cooling vest.
- **Application rates** (welder cooling):
  - **Ambient 25-30°C**: PCM 28°C vest provides comfort.
  - **30-40°C**: PCM 14°C ice pack vests + frequent rest breaks.
  - **> 40°C** confined space: active cooling (liquid circulating) preferred.
- **Best practices**:
  - **Pre-cool vest** before shift start.
  - **Multiple pack sets** dla rotation (one in freezer while other in use).
  - **Hydration** — water + electrolytes throughout shift.
  - **Shade breaks** — 10-15 min every 2 hours w extreme heat.
  - **Buddy system** — workers monitor each other dla heat illness symptoms.
- **OSHA recommendations**:
  - **OSHA Heat Illness Prevention Campaign** — water, rest, shade.
  - **NIOSH WBGT** (Wet Bulb Globe Temperature) screening — 28-32°C = high alert.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Ergodyne Chill-Its** — premier.
  - **Allegro Industries**.
  - **EZCooldown**.
  - **Steele Vest**.
  - **HyperKewl (TechNiche)** — evaporative.
- **Cost**:
  - **Evaporative vest**: $25-100.
  - **PCM vest + packs**: $150-400.
  - **Active cooling**: $500-1500.

## Iteration 69 — 2026-05-26

### ZZZZZZZZ. Plate Heat Exchanger (PHE) — Gasketed Frame Design + Installation
- **Plate Heat Exchanger (PHE)** = **stacked thin plates** z corrugated pattern między which two fluids flow w **alternating channels**. **Different from shell + tube** (TEMA per iter 56):
  - **Compact**: 5-25× smaller niż equivalent shell + tube.
  - **Higher heat transfer coefficient** (3-5×).
  - **Easy maintenance** — open frame, replace plates.
  - **Limited pressure / temperature** vs shell + tube.
- **Types**:
  - **Gasketed PHE (GPHE)** — STANDARD, most common:
    - **Removable plates** z elastomeric gaskets.
    - **Easy cleaning** by opening frame.
    - **Pressure**: do 25 bar typowo (specialty do 40 bar).
    - **Temperature**: −40 do +180°C (depending on gasket).
  - **Brazed PHE (BPHE)** — plates brazed permanently:
    - **Copper lub nickel braze**.
    - **No gaskets** = higher pressure (do 45+ bar).
    - **NOT cleanable** by opening — chemical clean only.
    - **Compact** dla HVAC, refrigeration.
  - **Welded PHE (WPHE)** — laser-welded plate stacks:
    - **No gaskets**, no brazing.
    - **High pressure + high temperature** (300°C+).
    - **Specialty applications** — corrosive media, high-T process.
- **GPHE construction**:
  - **Frame plate (fixed)** — back end z inlet/outlet ports.
  - **Pressure plate (movable)** — front, holds plate stack against frame.
  - **Carrying bar (upper)** — supports plates.
  - **Guide bar (lower)** — aligns plates.
  - **Tightening bolts** (4-12 sztuk) — compress plate stack.
  - **Support column** — structural.
- **Plates**:
  - **Material**: SS 316L most common, also Hastelloy, titanium, AL-6XN dla corrosive.
  - **Pressed pattern** (chevron lub washboard) — turbulence + heat transfer enhancement.
  - **Port holes** — 4 corners, alternating fluid flow.
  - **Gasket groove** around perimeter + ports.
- **Gaskets** (KRYTYCZNE):
  - **Material selection** based on service:
    - **NBR (Nitrile)** — water, oils, glycols. Limit: 100°C.
    - **EPDM** — water, steam, dilute acids. Limit: 150°C.
    - **HNBR** — improved NBR, do 130°C.
    - **Viton (FKM)** — chemicals, hydrocarbons. Limit: 200°C.
    - **PTFE / Kalrez** — extreme chemicals, premium pricing.
  - **Attachment styles**:
    - **Glue-bonded** — older, requires gluing during gasket replacement.
    - **Snap-in / clip-on** — modern, faster replacement.
    - **Multi-point fastening** (5+ points) — premier Alfa Laval — better seal.
- **Flow distribution** — KRYTYCZNE dla performance:
  - **Distribution area** between port + main heat transfer:
    - **CurveFlow** (Alfa Laval patented) — curved channels distribute flow evenly.
    - **Chocolate pattern** — dimpled, dla turbulence.
  - **Maldistribution** = reduced effective heat transfer + localized fouling.
- **Sizing**:
  - **Number of plates**: 5-700+ depending on duty.
  - **Plate area**: 0.05-3.5 m² per plate.
  - **Approach temperatures**: typowo 2-5°C (close approach possible).
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **HVAC** — chiller condensers, heat recovery.
  - **District heating** — heat exchange w network substations.
  - **Food + beverage** — pasteurization (especially milk).
  - **Marine** — engine cooling.
  - **Industrial** — process cooling, lube oil cooling.
- **Installation considerations**:
  - **Access space** — front of frame requires 50%+ of unit length dla plate removal.
  - **Drip pan** below frame — gasket leak collection.
  - **Foundation** — supports unit weight + thermal cycling.
  - **Piping**: 4 connections (2 inlet, 2 outlet) — typically flanged.
- **Bolt tightening** (assembly):
  - **Even compression** — measure plate stack dimension (A measurement).
  - **Diagonal pattern** torquing — same as flange (covered iter 53).
  - **Specific A dimension** per plate count + model — manufacturer table.
  - **Over-tightening** crushes gaskets.
  - **Under-tightening** = leaks.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Open frame** — loosen bolts, slide pressure plate back.
  - **Inspect plates** — pitting, scale, deformation.
  - **Clean** chemically lub mechanically (soft brush).
  - **Replace gaskets** if worn / damaged.
  - **Re-assemble** + pressure test.
- **Performance monitoring**:
  - **Pressure drop trending** — increase = fouling.
  - **Temperature approach** — widening = reduced efficiency.
  - **CIP (Clean-In-Place)** for food / beverage applications.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Alfa Laval** (Sweden) — market leader.
  - **Tranter** — premier industrial.
  - **APV / SPX FLOW** — sanitary applications.
  - **Sondex / Danfoss**.
  - **Mueller Industries**.

### AAAAAAAAA. Steam Jet Ejector — Vacuum System Design + Piping
- **Steam jet ejector** = **vacuum-creating device** using **high-velocity steam jet** to entrain + compress process gases/vapors. Based on **Venturi / ejector-venturi principle**. No moving parts, simple, reliable.
- **Different from mechanical vacuum pump**:
  - **No mechanical components** — pure fluid mechanics.
  - **Powered by steam pressure** (10-30 bar typowy).
  - **Lower achievable vacuum** vs liquid-ring lub rotary vane (~ 1 mbar absolute typowy minimum w multi-stage).
  - **Higher steam consumption** vs electric motor power.
  - **Cheaper capital** dla certain duties.
- **Operating principle**:
  - **High-pressure steam** enters **converging-diverging nozzle** — accelerates to supersonic velocity (Mach 2-3).
  - **High velocity creates low-pressure zone** at nozzle exit (Venturi effect).
  - **Process gas drawn in** through suction port — entrained w steam jet.
  - **Mixture enters diffuser** — kinetic energy converts back do pressure.
  - **Discharge** at higher pressure (atmospheric lub condenser back-pressure).
- **Components**:
  - **Steam chest** — receives motive steam.
  - **Steam nozzle** — converging-diverging design (Laval nozzle).
  - **Suction head** — process gas inlet.
  - **Diffuser** — discharge cone.
  - **Body / casing** — encloses ejector.
- **Multi-stage configuration**:
  - **Single stage**: vacuum to ~75 mmHg absolute typowy.
  - **Two-stage**: ~5-10 mmHg.
  - **Three-stage**: ~1 mmHg.
  - **Four-stage**: ~0.1 mmHg (100 microns).
  - **Five-stage**: ~0.01 mmHg (specialty).
- **Inter-stage condensers** (multi-stage):
  - **Condense water vapor** between stages.
  - **Reduces vapor load** dla next ejector → smaller, less steam consumption.
  - **Two types**:
    - **Direct contact (barometric)** — water sprayed directly into vapor.
    - **Surface (shell + tube)** — water-cooled, no mixing.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Vacuum distillation** — refineries (crude vacuum towers), chemical plants.
  - **Steam turbine condensers** — air ejectors maintain vacuum.
  - **Freeze drying / dehydrating** — pharma, food.
  - **Degassing** — boiler feedwater deaeration.
  - **Crystallization processes** — chemical.
  - **Vapor evacuation** — chemical reactor system.
- **Piping system design**:
  - **Suction line diameter**: ≥ ejector first-stage suction connection (NIE smaller).
  - **NO low points** w vacuum piping — condensate + solids accumulate, block flow.
  - **Slope toward ejector** — typowo 1:100 + drain pots at low points.
  - **Insulation** w vacuum lines dla preventing condensation.
  - **Material**: CS dla water service, SS dla aggressive vapors, RP-grade dla high purity.
- **Motive steam piping**:
  - **High-pressure steam line** to ejector.
  - **Steam pressure regulator** — maintains constant motive pressure.
  - **Steam trap + drain** — ensures dry steam at ejector (wet steam reduces efficiency).
  - **Strainer / Y-strainer** — particulates removed.
- **Cooling water piping** (dla condensers):
  - **Cooling water inlet** to condenser shell lub spray nozzles.
  - **Hot water outlet** do cooling tower lub waste.
  - **Flow control** dla optimization.
- **Limitations**:
  - **High steam consumption** — typowo 1-3 kg steam per kg vapor evacuated.
  - **Wet steam** reduces capacity significantly.
  - **Backpressure sensitive** — discharge condenser pressure affects performance.
  - **Cold ambient** condenses too much steam early — performance drop.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Croll Reynolds** — premier USA dla steam ejectors.
  - **Schutte Koerting** — industrial ejectors.
  - **GEA Wiegand**.
  - **Graham Corporation**.

### BBBBBBBBB. Anti-Galling Thread Compounds — Stainless Steel Fastener Protection
- **Galling** = severe form of friction-induced damage gdy **two surfaces slide z high contact pressure**. **Stainless steel particularly susceptible** — austenitic SS (304, 316) is **soft + has tendency to weld microscopically** under pressure.
- **Galling mechanism**:
  - **Asperity contact** at high pressure → **cold welding** of microscopic high points.
  - **Continued sliding** rips welded points → surface damage.
  - **Cascading damage** — once started, gets worse rapidly.
  - **Result**: thread destruction, seized fastener, often need cutting / drilling out.
- **Why stainless prone to galling**:
  - **Low hardness** — surface deforms easily.
  - **Low thermal conductivity** — heat builds up at contact points.
  - **Tough oxide film** breaks down z friction — fresh metal-to-metal contact = welding.
  - **Stainless-to-stainless** combination worst.
- **Anti-galling compound types**:
  - **Nickel-based anti-seize** (Jet-Lube Nikal, Loctite LB 8150):
    - **Carrier**: petroleum + nickel flake.
    - **Forms thin nickel film** on threads after evaporation.
    - **Temperature range**: −20 do +1425°C.
    - **STANDARD dla stainless steel** w most applications.
  - **Molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) compound**:
    - **MoS2 platelets** — lamellar structure, slides easily.
    - **Excellent extreme pressure**.
    - **Temperature range**: −185 do +650°C.
    - **Cold conditions** dobry, dla high-T (> 650°C) MoS2 oxidizes.
  - **Copper-based anti-seize** (CRC Copper, Permatex Copper):
    - **Cu flake** w lithium grease.
    - **Temperature**: do 980°C.
    - **NIE dla**: stainless near hydrogen service (catalytic effect).
    - **NIE dla**: nickel alloys (galvanic concern).
  - **Graphite-based**:
    - **Graphite suspended w oil**.
    - **Excellent dla low-cost applications**.
    - **NIE dla**: SS near food / pharma (carbon contamination).
  - **PTFE / Teflon-based**:
    - **Inert** — most chemicals OK.
    - **Lower temperature** rating (200°C).
    - **Cleaner** dla food / pharma.
  - **Boron nitride (hBN)**:
    - **Premium** dla extreme T (> 1000°C).
    - **Specialty** applications.
- **NIE używać**:
  - **Anti-seize compounds with silicon** dla stainless — silicon **promotes galling** w SS threads.
  - **Petroleum grease alone** — bez additives, NIE prevents galling adequately.
- **Application**:
  - **Apply to MALE threads only** — sparingly.
  - **Cover entire thread engagement length**.
  - **NIE excessive** — squeeze-out contamination + uneven torque.
  - **Wear gloves** — some compounds have nickel sensitization concerns.
- **Torque effect**:
  - **Anti-seize REDUCES friction** → **higher preload at same torque**.
  - **Adjust torque downward** ~20-25% z anti-seize vs dry threads.
  - **Spec dla lubricated threads** w torque tables.
- **Reusability**:
  - **Stainless fasteners with anti-seize** can be reused multiple times if compound intact.
  - **Clean + reapply** dla critical applications.
- **Galling prevention** beyond compounds:
  - **Slow assembly** — high speed wrench increases friction T.
  - **Different alloys** — SS bolt + brass nut, lub SS bolt + SS nut z different grade.
  - **Heat treatment** — harden one surface (typowo bolt) to prevent micro-welding.
  - **Cold-forming** — work-hardened threads less prone.
  - **Surface coatings** — Xylan, fluoropolymer coatings.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Flange bolts** w stainless piping.
  - **Pump impeller fasteners**.
  - **Sanitary clamps** (BPE, food industry).
  - **Spark plugs** w aluminum heads (galling between SS spark plug + Al head).
  - **High-T fasteners** — exhaust manifold bolts.
- **Vendors / brands**:
  - **Jet-Lube** — Nikal, AP-100 premier.
  - **Loctite (Henkel)** — LB 8150, 51269.
  - **Molykote (Dow Corning)** — anti-seize pastes.
  - **Permatex** — economy.
  - **Bostik Never-Seez** — nuclear grade.
  - **Saf-T-Eze** — stainless-specific.
- **Safety considerations**:
  - **Nickel sensitization** — some workers allergic, requires gloves.
  - **MSDS review** dla each compound.
  - **Disposal** — used containers typowo hazardous waste classification.

---

## Iteration 70 — 2026-05-26

### CCCCCCCCC. PSV / PRV — Pressure Safety / Relief Valves (API 520/521/526)
- **PSV (Pressure Safety Valve) / PRV (Pressure Relief Valve)** = mechanical device opening at **set pressure** dla pressure relief, **closing automatically** when pressure drops. **Different from rupture disk** (single-use, full burst per iter 65) — PSV is **reusable, partial-stroke**.
- **Terminology**:
  - **PSV** — typowo gas/vapor service (snap-action opening).
  - **PRV** — liquid service (gradual opening).
  - **PRD** (Pressure Relief Device) — generic term covering PSV, PRV, rupture disk.
  - **Safety relief valve (SRV)** — combination (gas+liquid).
- **API standards** (definitive references):
  - **API 520 Part 1** — **Sizing + selection** dla PSV.
  - **API 520 Part 2** — **Installation** practices.
  - **API 521** — **System pressure relief loads** + **flare network sizing**.
  - **API 526** — **Standardized valve dimensions** + orifice letter sizes D-T.
  - **API 527** — Seat tightness testing.
  - **ASME Section VIII** — pressure relief w pressure vessels.
- **Operating principle**:
  - **Spring-loaded disc** held against seat by spring force.
  - **Pressure under disc** acts on disc area.
  - **At set pressure**, pressure force > spring force → disc lifts.
  - **Pop action** (snap full open) dla gas service.
  - **Modulating opening** dla liquid (gradual).
  - **When pressure drops**, spring re-seats disc.
  - **Blowdown** = pressure differential between popping + reseating.
- **Types**:
  - **Conventional spring-loaded PSV** — most basic:
    - **Backpressure sensitive** — discharge pressure affects opening.
    - **Use** when back-pressure stable.
  - **Balanced bellows PSV**:
    - **Internal bellows** isolates spring chamber from discharge.
    - **Resistant do variable back-pressure**.
    - **Premium dla flare systems** (variable backpressure).
  - **Pilot-operated PSV (POSV)**:
    - **Pilot valve** controls main valve (similar do hydraulic master cylinder).
    - **Tight shut-off** near set pressure (95-98% of set vs conventional 90%).
    - **Used dla**: gas services z high operating pressure ratio do set.
    - **Common dla pipeline + processing applications**.
  - **Safety valve (steam service)**:
    - **Pop-action** dla full opening.
    - **ASME Section I** dla boilers.
- **Orifice sizing** (API 526 — standard letter designations):

| Letter | Orifice Area (in²) |
|--------|-------------------:|
| D | 0.110 |
| E | 0.196 |
| F | 0.307 |
| G | 0.503 |
| H | 0.785 |
| J | 1.287 |
| K | 1.838 |
| L | 2.853 |
| M | 3.60 |
| N | 4.34 |
| P | 6.38 |
| Q | 11.05 |
| R | 16.0 |
| T | 26.0 |

- **Inlet + outlet sizes** standardized — each letter has specific flange dimensions.
- **Sizing calculation overview** (gas service, API 520):
  - **A_req = (W / (C × Kd × Kb × P1)) × √(Z × T / M)** where:
    - W = required mass flow [lb/h].
    - C = gas-specific constant.
    - Kd = discharge coefficient (typowo 0.975).
    - Kb = backpressure correction.
    - P1 = relieving pressure [psia].
    - Z = compressibility.
    - T = absolute temperature [°R].
    - M = molecular weight.
- **Set pressure + accumulation**:
  - **Set pressure** = pressure at which valve starts to open.
  - **Single PSV** — set at MAWP (Maximum Allowable Working Pressure).
  - **Multiple PSVs** — primary at MAWP, secondary at 105% MAWP.
  - **Accumulation** — pressure rise above MAWP during relief (typowo limited to 10-20% per code).
  - **Overpressure** — pressure during relief above set (typowo 10% dla single, 16% dla fire case).
- **Set pressure tolerance** (ASME):
  - **±3%** dla set pressures > 70 psig.
  - **±5%** dla set pressures < 70 psig.
- **Installation requirements (API 520 Part 2)**:
  - **No isolation valve between vessel + PSV** (CRITICAL — prevents disabling).
  - **Lockable car-sealed open (CSO)** isolation valve sometimes allowed dla redundant valves.
  - **Inlet pressure drop**: ≤ **3% of set pressure** at relieving flow (prevents chatter).
  - **No throttling**: NIE valves between PSV + vent line.
  - **Discharge piping** — straight as possible, sized dla design relief flow.
  - **Mounted vertical** typowo, stem up.
- **Common discharge destinations**:
  - **Atmosphere** — safe area dla non-toxic + non-flammable.
  - **Flare header** — flammable + toxic services.
  - **Containment vessel** — small relief (small drums + condensate flash).
- **Testing requirements**:
  - **Annual bench test** typowo (more frequent dla critical service).
  - **Bench test** verifies set pressure, blowdown, seat tightness.
  - **In-situ pop test** dla some installations (where bench impossible).
  - **API 527** — seat tightness test method.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong set pressure** — incorrect calibration.
  - **Improper discharge piping** — restricts flow, chattering.
  - **Stem stuck** — corrosion, scale.
  - **Spring fatigue** — long service without recertification.
  - **Backpressure exceeded** — modifications elsewhere increase header pressure.
- **Vendors**:
  - **Crosby (Emerson)** — premier USA.
  - **Anderson Greenwood (Emerson)**.
  - **LESER (Germany)** — European premier.
  - **Sempell**.
  - **Consolidated (Baker Hughes)**.

### DDDDDDDDD. Refinery Open + Closed Drain Systems
- **Refinery drain systems** = network of piping collecting + safely disposing **liquids + spilled products** w hazardous + non-hazardous areas. **Critical safety infrastructure** dla:
  - **Spill containment** w process areas.
  - **Equipment drain disposal** during maintenance.
  - **Hazardous chemical handling**.
  - **Vapor / fire control**.
- **Two main systems** (KRYTYCZNE separation):
  - **Open drain system (OPD)** — atmospheric / vented:
    - **Funnels, catch basins, floor drains** w process areas.
    - **Open hatch / grating** accessible.
    - **Vented do atmosphere** lub low-pressure receiver.
    - **Handles**: rainwater, spilled product, equipment drains.
    - **Hazardous Open Drain (HOD)** subset — handles flammable liquids.
    - **Non-Hazardous (NOD)** — water + non-flammable.
  - **Closed drain system (CDS)** — sealed, pressure-rated:
    - **Sealed equipment drains** — drained directly do CDS bez exposure.
    - **Pressure-rated piping** — handles hot, pressurized fluids.
    - **Flare lub recovery** dla vapors.
    - **Used dla**: hydrocarbons containing H₂S, hot products, toxic chemicals.
    - **Prevents vapor escape** do atmosphere.
- **Why separate systems**:
  - **Vapor segregation** — closed drain vapors do flare; open drain vapors do atmosphere lub VRU.
  - **Pressure differences** — CDS handles 50-200 psig; OPD essentially atmospheric.
  - **Code requirements** — fire / explosion prevention.
  - **HSE compliance** — preventing vapor cloud formation w process areas.
- **OPD system components**:
  - **Floor drains** — channels w paved process areas.
  - **Trench drains** — long collection w heavy traffic zones.
  - **Catch basins** — accumulation + initial settling.
  - **API separator** — primary oil-water separation.
  - **Junction boxes** — gravity flow connections.
  - **Header pipes** — sloped 1:100 minimum.
- **OPD design rules**:
  - **Branch line ≥ DN 100 (4")** minimum.
  - **Header line ≥ DN 150 (6")** minimum.
  - **Gravity flow** primarily — no pumps w OPD (except final transfer).
  - **Slope continuously toward outlet**.
  - **Cleanout points** every 30-50 m.
  - **Vent stacks** for major lines (prevent siphon).
- **CDS system components**:
  - **Closed drain drum (CDD)** — pressure vessel receiving equipment drains.
  - **CDS piping** — typowo DN 50-200, depending on service.
  - **Hot drain header** — separate dla high-T fluids (avoid flashing).
  - **Cold drain header** — separate dla cold fluids.
  - **Outlet pump** — transfers liquid do slop tank lub recycle.
  - **Vapor outlet** — do flare header lub VRU.
- **Slop oil tank**:
  - **Final receiving** dla recovered hydrocarbons.
  - **Separation** — water + oil + sediment.
  - **Recovery** — oil pumped back do crude tank lub reprocessing.
  - **Water** — to API separator → wastewater treatment.
  - **Sediment** — periodic cleaning dla hazardous waste disposal.
- **Critical design rule** — **OPEN DRAIN MUSI być oddzielony od CLOSED DRAIN**:
  - **Liquid seal** between systems (water seal, U-trap z minimum 1 m level).
  - **Prevents vapor migration** from CDS do OPD area.
- **Pump considerations**:
  - **Slop pumps** — handle dirty service, robust:
    - **Vertical centrifugal** lub **submersible** dla sump duty.
    - **Diaphragm pumps** dla solids-laden.
    - **NPSH** crucial (sump levels variable).
- **Materials**:
  - **CS** dla water + light hydrocarbons.
  - **SS** dla aggressive chemicals.
  - **HDPE / FRP** dla acidic services.
  - **Cast iron** historical, less used now.
- **Standards**:
  - **API RP 2350** — overfill protection (tank).
  - **API 650** — storage tanks (covered iter 52).
  - **Local environmental regulations** — discharge permits.
- **Slope rules**:
  - **OPD**: 1:50 to 1:100 typowo dla gravity flow.
  - **CDS**: 1:200 minimum (closed system tolerates less slope).
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Refineries** — process unit drainage.
  - **Chemical plants** — similar systems.
  - **Tank farms** — bund drainage + rainwater removal.
  - **Offshore platforms** — separate hazardous (process) + non-hazardous (deck wash) drains.

### EEEEEEEEE. Steam Header Drip Legs + Trap Drainage System
- **Drip leg** = vertical pocket on steam piping collecting **condensate (water)** dla removal by **steam trap**. **Critical** dla preventing **water hammer** + **erosion** + **superheater damage**.
- **Why drip legs needed**:
  - **Steam pipes lose heat** to ambient → condensation w pipe.
  - **Condensate accumulates w low points** lub on slope.
  - **At velocity** (steam flow ~30-50 m/s), pooled condensate gets entrained → slug flow → **water hammer**.
  - **Damaged components**: control valves, regulators, turbines.
- **Drip leg construction**:
  - **Vertical pocket pipe** below main steam line.
  - **Diameter sized** per main line size.
  - **Height** ≥ 28" (700 mm) preferred dla 1 psi head pressure on trap.
  - **Bottom outlet** to steam trap (covered iter 11).
- **Sizing rules** (best practices, FCI ST110):
  - **Main steam line ≤ DN 100 (4")**: drip leg **same size** as main.
  - **Main steam line > DN 100**: drip leg **= 1/2 main diameter**, minimum DN 100.
  - **Examples**:
    - DN 50 main → DN 50 drip leg.
    - DN 100 main → DN 100 drip leg.
    - DN 200 main → DN 100 drip leg.
    - DN 400 main → DN 200 drip leg.
- **Why height = 700 mm**:
  - **Head pressure** = 28" × 0.036 psi/inch = 1 psi.
  - **Pushes condensate** through trap reliably.
  - **Provides "trap reservoir"** dla intermittent traps.
- **Spacing dla drip legs**:
  - **150-300 ft (45-90 m)** along straight runs.
  - **Before key components**:
    - **Pressure-reducing valves** (PRVs).
    - **Control valves** + regulators.
    - **Steam-turbine inlet**.
    - **Heat exchanger inlet** (vital — wet steam damages tubes).
    - **End of long branches**.
  - **Low points** — gravity collection.
  - **Direction changes** — change of slope, before vertical risers.
- **Trap connection**:
  - **Side outlet** from drip leg z 100-200 mm above bottom (avoid sediment trap).
  - **Strainer Y-type** before trap (filters scale).
  - **Test valve / nipple** for trap testing access.
  - **Bypass piping** dla maintenance.
- **Trap selection** (per service):
  - **Thermodynamic (TD)** — common dla drip leg drainage, drip service.
  - **Float & thermostatic (F&T)** — heat exchanger drains.
  - **Inverted bucket (IB)** — high-pressure, robust.
- **Slope of steam main**:
  - **Toward drip legs** — slope 1:100 minimum.
  - **Downward** in flow direction typowo.
  - **Drip legs at bottom** of slope sections.
- **Steam main start-up**:
  - **Cold start** — extensive condensation w pipe.
  - **Drip legs critical** dla initial drainage.
  - **Slow warm-up** — open trap bypass dla immediate condensate flow.
  - **Gradual pressurization** — limit thermal shock.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Undersized drip leg** — overflow, water carryover.
  - **Drip leg horizontal** — NIE gravity flow.
  - **Missing drip legs** — water hammer episodes.
  - **Failed traps not replaced** — silent failure, condensate builds, eventually fails.
- **Drip leg flange connections** (dla maintenance):
  - **Inlet flange** to drip leg (replaceable).
  - **Outlet flange** to trap.
  - **Drain valve** at very bottom dla cleanout.
- **Standards / guidelines**:
  - **FCI ST110** — Fluid Controls Institute, drip leg + trap piping.
  - **ASME B31.1** — Power Piping (general).
  - **TLV best practices** — Japanese steam systems leader.
  - **Spirax Sarco** — UK steam systems expertise.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Visual inspection** — daily during operation.
  - **UT trap testing** (acoustic) — verify proper cycling.
  - **Pyrometer / IR** — temperature differential inlet vs outlet (failed trap = same temp = steam loss).
  - **Annual trap survey** — replace 5-15% failed traps typowo.

---

## Iteration 71 — 2026-05-26

### FFFFFFFFF. HVAC Refrigerant Piping — Oil Return, P-Traps, Double Suction Risers
- **Problem**: refrigerant systems (split AC, chillers, walk-in freezers) używają **R-410A, R-32, R-454B** + **POE oil** (polyolester). Olej miesza się z czynnikiem w skraplaczu i parowniku, ale na powrocie do sprężarki **musi wrócić** — inaczej **starvation** sprężarki + zatarcie. Critical: dobór średnic + slope + trapy.
- **Suction line sizing** — kluczowa dla powrotu oleju:
  - **Min velocity**: **5 m/s (1000 ft/min)** na poziomych odcinkach, **7-12 m/s (1500-2400 ft/min)** na pionowych ku górze (suction riser).
  - **Max velocity**: **20 m/s (4000 ft/min)** — przekroczenie = hałas + drop ciśnienia.
  - **Pressure drop budget**: 1 psi (≈1°F SST loss) na całej długości.
- **P-trap (oil trap) na suction line**:
  - **Lokalizacja**: u podstawy każdego pionowego risera, max 6 m (20 ft) odstępu na long risers.
  - **Kształt**: U-shape z **R ≤ 1×D** (compact, mała pojemność oleju).
  - **Cel**: olej nie zalewa parownika podczas postoju (off-cycle migration); buforuje slug oleju przed sprężarką.
  - **Slope poziomego**: 1:50 (¼" per 1 ft) w kierunku sprężarki.
- **Double suction riser (dual riser)**:
  - **Kiedy**: variable capacity systems (TRV, hot-gas bypass, multi-evaporator), gdy min load < 40% max.
  - **Konstrukcja**: **mały riser** (sized for min load velocity) + **duży riser** (sized for combined). U-trap u podstawy małego, **inverted P-trap** u góry dużego.
  - **Działanie**: przy małym load olej zbiera się w dużym riserze → blokuje go → cały gaz idzie małym (wysoka prędkość = olej idzie w górę). Przy pełnym load olej wypchnięty → oba aktywne.
- **Hard-drawn copper tubing types**:
  - **Type L** (medium wall) — **standard for refrigeration**, 100-150 psi rating zależnie od średnicy.
  - **Type K** (heavy wall) — **HP applications** (CO₂ transcritical R-744, 90-130 bar), underground.
  - **Type ACR** (Air Conditioning/Refrigeration) — cleaned + capped + nitrogen-charged from factory; **3/8"-1-5/8" OD**.
  - **NIE używać Type M** (thin wall, water service tylko).
- **Brazing requirements**:
  - **Silver braze (BAg-5, BAg-7)** dla copper-to-copper z **flux** lub **BCuP-5 (15% Ag-phosphorus)** bez flux na copper.
  - **Nitrogen purge** podczas brazingu — **2-5 SCFH** flow przez rurę → zapobiega **black oxide scale** wewnątrz (zatkałby TXV/EEV).
  - **Brak purge** = filter drier + TXV w 6 m-cy zatkany.
- **Pipe insulation** — armaflex/k-flex closed-cell:
  - **Grubość**: 13 mm (½") dla -10°C SST, 19 mm (¾") dla -30°C.
  - **Vapor barrier** — wszystkie szwy klejone (Armaflex 520 adhesive), inaczej kondensacja w izolacji → korozja rury.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pipe slope toward compressor** — 1:50 minimum dla powrotu oleju.
  - **Avoid traps in liquid line** (z wyjątkiem przed zaworem rozprężnym 1.5 m wymiennika).
  - **Min suction line size DN 16 (5/8")** dla split systems > 5 kW (avoid premature failure).
  - **Pressure test 600 psi (40 bar) N₂** przed vacuum — wykryje leak przed czynnikiem.
  - **Triple evacuation** — 500 microns → break z N₂ → 500 microns → break → 250 microns final.

### GGGGGGGGG. Water Treatment Plant — Backwash Filter Piping + Valve Sequencing
- **Multimedia filter (MMF)** — vessel z warstwami filtracyjnymi do usuwania zawiesin (turbidity, Fe, Mn) z wody surowej przed RO / IX / kotłem.
- **Layered media (top-to-bottom)**:
  - **Anthracite** — lekki (1.4-1.6 SG), 0.8-1.2 mm, top layer (≈40% bed depth) — łapie duże cząstki.
  - **Silica sand** — 1.5-1.7 SG, 0.4-0.8 mm, middle (≈30%) — mid-range cząstki.
  - **Garnet** — 4.0-4.2 SG, 0.2-0.4 mm, bottom (≈10%) — fine particles + bed support.
  - **Gravel support** — 3-5 mm grit, pod garnetem, na underdrain.
  - **Underdrain** — strainer nozzles lub nozzle-plate, distribuuje wodę + zbiera filtrat.
- **Service mode** (filtration):
  - **Flow**: top-to-bottom, 5-15 m/h velocity (12-30 gpm/ft²).
  - **Differential pressure** — start ≈0.2 bar (3 psi), narasta z czasem cycle.
  - **Trigger backwash**: **ΔP ≥ 0.7 bar (10 psi)** OR turbidity outlet > 1 NTU OR czas 24-72 h.
- **Backwash sequence** (typical 6-valve manifold):
  1. **Service close** — odciąć dopływ surowej wody, zamknąć outlet (filtrat) — valves V1, V2 ZAMK.
  2. **Air scrub (opcjonalnie)** — kompresowane powietrze 60-90 s, 25-35 Nm³/h/m² — rozluźnia kek + odkleja zawiesinę. Valve V3 (air inlet), V4 (rinse drain) OTW.
  3. **Slow rinse** — backwash water od dołu, 5 m/h, 60 s — przepłucz powietrze.
  4. **Fast backwash** — backwash 15-20 m/h (30% bed expansion), 8-15 min — fluidyzuje złoże, wypłukuje zawiesinę przez **drain V4**.
  5. **Settle** — 30-60 s, wstrzymanie flow, media osiada warstwami (anthracite najpierw na górze dzięki gęstości).
  6. **Forward rinse** — service flow top-to-bottom przez 2-5 min, drain V4 nadal otwarty — wypłucz resztę zmętnienia.
  7. **Return to service** — V4 ZAMK, V2 (outlet) OTW.
- **Valve types**:
  - **Butterfly valves** z **pneumatic actuators** + **limit switches** (open/closed feedback do PLC).
  - **Spring-return fail-safe** — fail-closed na inlet, fail-open na drain (safe state przy power loss).
  - **Cycle time**: 5-15 sek per valve, sequence z dead-band dla overlap protection.
- **Backwash water source**:
  - **Filtered water tank** (najlepiej) — gwarantuje quality.
  - **Raw water** — tylko dla zgrubnej filtracji (nie po RO).
  - **Flow rate** — 2-3× rate serwisowy.
- **Backwash drain**:
  - **DN 1.5× inlet pipe** (mniej oporu, szybkie spłukanie).
  - **Atmospheric break** — siphon-breaker, prevent cross-contamination.
  - **Settling basin / lagoon** — przed odpływem do kanalizacji (turbid water).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Niedostateczny backwash flow** — bed nie fluidyzuje → kek twardnieje → channeling → breakthrough.
  - **Brak air scrub** — biofilm + cement na media → "mud balls" → trzeba media replacement.
  - **Valve fails partially open** podczas backwashu — short-circuit, część surowej idzie do filtratu.
  - **Brak settle step** — wymieszane media, fine cząstki na górze → high ΔP po starcie.
- **Underdrain types**:
  - **Nozzle plate** — strainer nozzles (0.2-0.5 mm slot), distribute air + water.
  - **Lateral pipe** — DN 25-50, slotted, z hub i ramionami.
  - **Air-water washable** — false bottom z plenum dla równomiernej dystrybucji.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Inlet/outlet flanges DN 100-300** zależnie od capacity, ANSI 150 carbon steel rubber-lined lub PVC dla soft water.
  - **Sight glass** na backwash drain — operator widzi clarity (mętna → czysta = gotowe).
  - **Sampling cocks** — przed/po filtrze, dla turbidity testing.
  - **Vent valve na top** — usuwa powietrze podczas fill po backwashu.
  - **PRV / safety valve** — chroni przed over-pressure jeśli outlet zamknięty during service.

### HHHHHHHHH. Pump Priming + Foot Valves + Suction Lift Limits
- **Problem**: centrifugal pump **nie pompuje powietrza** — vane spinning w air → brak prime → brak flow. Pre-fill obudowy + suction line kluczowy.
- **Atmospheric pressure → suction lift limit**:
  - **14.7 psi at sea level** = **34 ft (10.34 m)** equivalent water column theoretical max.
  - **Practical max suction lift**: **6-8 m (20-25 ft)** z powodu:
    - **Friction loss** w suction pipe + fittings (1-2 m).
    - **NPSHr** (required) — pump-specific, 2-5 m typically.
    - **Vapor pressure margin** — woda 20°C ≈ 0.23 m, 60°C ≈ 2 m, 80°C ≈ 4.8 m.
    - **Safety margin** (NPSHa - NPSHr ≥ 1 m).
  - **Wniosek**: pumping zimnej wody dopuszczalne **5-7 m lift**, gorącej wody (>60°C) **0-2 m** lub flooded suction wymagane.
- **Foot valve** = check valve z **strainer** na końcu suction pipe (na dnie zbiornika/studni):
  - **Funkcja 1**: prevent backflow → utrzymuje prime po wyłączeniu pumpy.
  - **Funkcja 2**: strainer (mesh 1-10 mm) → nie zasysa kamieni, gałęzi, ryb.
  - **Konstrukcja**: spring-loaded disc lub flapper, cast iron / brass / SS body, screen z brass/SS.
  - **Kp (cracking pressure)**: 0.05-0.5 m water column — niska, żeby nie utrudniała ssania.
  - **Submergence min**: **2-3× pipe diameter pod powierzchnią** — inaczej vortex + air entrainment.
- **Self-priming pump** — alternatywa, **NIE potrzebuje foot valve**:
  - **Konstrukcja**: pump z **wbudowanym water reservoir** wokół wirnika.
  - **Princip**: przy starcie woda w reservoir + powietrze są wirowane → powietrze separuje się i wychodzi przez delivery, woda recyrkuluje przez suction → po 30-180 s prime osiągnięty.
  - **Limit**: też 6-8 m suction lift (atmospheric).
  - **Plusy**: brak foot valve do utrzymania (brak ryzyka stuck/zatkany strainer), self-recovery po stracie prime.
  - **Minusy**: większy fizycznie, droższe (2-3× standard centrifugal), wymaga **regular water fill** w reservoir przed pierwszym startem.
- **Priming methods** (standard centrifugal):
  1. **Foot valve + manual fill** — odkręć vent na obudowie + napełnij z hose, zamknij vent, start.
  2. **Vacuum priming pump** — osobny vacuum pump (rotary vane lub liquid ring) wyciąga powietrze z casing → woda zassana z dołu.
  3. **Ejector priming** — water/steam ejector tworzy vacuum w pump casing.
  4. **Self-priming pump** — built-in (above).
- **Suction pipe design rules**:
  - **Średnica**: o **1 size większa niż inlet flange** pumpy (np. 4" pump → 5" suction).
  - **Eccentric reducer** przy flansze pumpy z **flat top** — żaden air pocket.
  - **Slope**: 1:100 ku **upward direction toward pump** — bąble idą do pumpy, nie zostają w pipe.
  - **Min length straight pipe**: 5-10× D przed flange pumpy — uniform flow profile.
  - **Brak high points** w suction line — air trap = prime loss.
  - **Min bends** — każde 90° dodaje friction loss + turbulencję.
- **NPSH (Net Positive Suction Head)**:
  - **NPSHa (available)** = atmospheric pressure + static head − friction loss − vapor pressure.
  - **NPSHr (required)** — z curve producenta pumpy.
  - **Margin**: NPSHa ≥ NPSHr + 1 m (lub +20%).
  - **Cavitation** gdy NPSHa < NPSHr — implosion bubbles → erosion impeller, vibracje, hałas (gravel/marbles), spadek wydajności.
- **Foot valve troubleshooting**:
  - **Pump loses prime overnight** → foot valve nieszczelny (debris pod disc, zużyta sprężyna).
  - **Test**: zamknij dyszę delivery, zmierz drop ciśnienia w suction line — szybki spadek = leak.
  - **Strainer zatkany** → brak flow, kawitacja → inspect + clean periodically.
- **Practical rules**:
  - **Walk-around check** — przed startem upewnij się że priming valve zamknięty + delivery valve **zamknięty** (start w "closed" pozycji → minimum power + brak water hammer).
  - **Vent valve highest point** suction housing → wypuść powietrze przed startem.
  - **Pressure gauges** — suction (compound: vacuum + pressure) + delivery — diagnostyka leak / cavitation.
  - **Run dry protection** — float switch w studni / sucker rod / pressure switch — wyłącz pump przy braku wody, inaczej seal + impeller zniszczone w minutach.
  - **Suction line dla cold service** — izoluj + heat trace jeśli ryzyko zamarznięcia (frozen pipe = brak prime + ruptured pipe).

---

## Iteration 72 — 2026-05-26

### IIIIIIIII. Pulsation Dampener (Suction Stabilizer) — Reciprocating Pump Piping
- **Problem**: pompy tłokowe (simplex, duplex, triplex, quintuplex) generują **pulsacje ciśnienia + przepływu** — fluid acceleration/deceleration → **acceleration head loss** w suction + **shock waves** w discharge. Bez tłumika: kawitacja po stronie ssania, hammer + zmęczeniowe pękanie rurociągu po stronie tłoczenia.
- **Mechanizm dampener (bladder/diaphragm type)**:
  - **Gaz N₂** (pre-charged) oddzielony **elastomerową błoną** (NBR/EPDM/Viton/PTFE) lub **PTFE bellows** lub **SS bellows** od cieczy procesowej.
  - **Discharge stroke**: błona ściskana → gaz absorbuje peak ciśnienia.
  - **Suction stroke**: gaz rozpręża się → wypycha ciecz do linii → utrzymuje flow.
  - **Efekt**: redukcja pulsacji **80-95%** (residual ripple **<5%**).
- **Sizing — displaced volume above mean per stroke**:
  - **Simplex (1 cyl)**: ≈ **60%** total displacement above mean.
  - **Duplex (2 cyl)**: ≈ **30%** above mean (pół simplexu).
  - **Triplex (3 cyl)**: ≈ **6%** above mean.
  - **Quintuplex (5 cyl)**: ≈ **2%** above mean.
  - **Rule of thumb**: V_dampener ≥ **10-20×** displaced volume above mean per stroke.
- **Lokalizacja**:
  - **Discharge dampener** — **JAK NAJBLIŻEJ** pump discharge nozzle (≤5×D od flange). Im dalej, tym mniej skuteczny (pulse już rozpropagowany).
  - **Suction stabilizer (inlet dampener)** — przy pump inlet, magazynuje ciecz, oddaje przy ssaniu (kompensuje acceleration head loss). Critical przy long suction lines (>3 m).
- **Pre-charge ciśnienie N₂**:
  - **Discharge**: **80% średniego ciśnienia roboczego** (np. system 100 bar → precharge 80 bar).
  - **Suction**: **50% statycznego ciśnienia inlet** (znacznie niższe).
  - **Bez ciśnienia procesowego!** — pomiar na **odłączonej** pompie lub przez **gas valve** (Schrader-style) na top.
- **Hole passage / port**:
  - **CRITICAL**: średnica otworu dampener → linia procesowa **musi być pełnowymiarowa** (nie redukowana).
  - **Redukcja portu** → bottleneck → tłumik nie ma czasu napełnić/oddać objętości → spadek skuteczności **80%+**.
  - **Mounting**: bezpośrednio na rurociągu, **branch tee full-bore**, nie skinny stub.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Niewłaściwy precharge** — za niski: gaz ciągle ściśnięty, dampener nieaktywny. Za wysoki: błona uderza w wall, zniszczenie + brak action.
  - **Sprawdzanie precharge raz/kwartał** — diffusion N₂ przez bladder.
  - **Brak isolation valve** — niemożliwy serwis bez wyłączenia całej pompy.
  - **Wrong elastomer** — NBR fail w aromatic hydrocarbons, EPDM fail w petroleum, Viton dla większości chemii (ale drogi).
- **Bladder vs no-bladder (gas-over-liquid)**:
  - **Bladder type** — gaz separated, brak gas absorption do liquid.
  - **No-bladder (gas pot)** — gaz bezpośrednio nad cieczą; stopniowo gaz rozpuszcza się + ucieka → wymaga **periodic re-charging** (manual lub auto z N₂ line).
  - **Bladder** standardowy dla większości aplikacji; no-bladder dla wysokich T (>120°C) gdzie elastomers fail.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Quarterly precharge check** — pump down, isolate, vent process, mierz N₂.
  - **Bladder replacement** — co 1-3 lata zależnie od duty cycle, T, chemii.
  - **Inspect dla**: cracks, hardening, deformation.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Acoustic filter (Helmholtz)** — alternatywa lub dodatek do dampener dla specific frequency tuning.
  - **NPSHa calculation** dla recip pump **MUSI** uwzględniać acceleration head:
    - Ha = (L · V · n · C) / (K · g) gdzie L=suction length, V=velocity, n=RPM, C=pump constant (0.066 simplex, 0.040 duplex, 0.0085 triplex).
    - **Acceleration head** może być **5-10× friction head** dla triplexu, **30×+** dla simplexu.
  - **Suction stabilizer** dramatycznie redukuje wymagane NPSHa (zerowa zmienna velocity widziana przez pompę).
  - **Discharge piping**: minimum **3 average pipe diameter** przed pierwszym kolanem od dampener.

### JJJJJJJJJ. Pickling + Passivation Stainless Steel — ASTM A380
