  - **Spring hanger** — vertical movement compensation (steam lines, hot/cold cycles).
  - **U-bolt** — small pipes, clamp ku beam.
- **Fireproofing**:
  - **Steel struktura** → expansion w pożarze → collapse w 15 min @ 700°C.
  - **Fireproofing material**:
    - **Concrete-encased** kolumny (50-100 mm) — passive, robust.
    - **Cementitious sprayed (sprayed-applied fireproofing — SAF)** — np. Pyrocrete, K-Mass.
    - **Intumescent paint** — expands w fire, insulates.
  - **Fire rating**: typically **2 h** dla pipe rack columns (UL 263).
  - **Extent**: kolumny + braces do **lower tier** elevation + 0.6 m above. Top tier nie wymaga (above fire zone typically).
  - **Equipment over rack** — extend fireproofing do those supports.
- **Common errors**:
  - **No future expansion space** — adding new pipe w 5 lat impossible, nowy rack required.
  - **Wrong tier dla utility** — process line w utility tier, contamination risk + maintenance complications.
  - **No expansion loop space** — long straight runs (200+ m) bez expansion → thermal stress damage.
  - **Cable tray under pipes** — leak → short circuit → power loss.
  - **Insufficient guide spacing** — pipes buckle pod axial compression w hot state.
  - **Anchor too close to equipment** — thermal force concentrated → nozzle damage.
- **Materials**:
  - **Steel** — najczęściej, ASTM A36/A992. Hot-dip galvanized lub painted (3-coat system: primer + intermediate + topcoat).
  - **Concrete** — heavy industrial (coastal, corrosive atmosphere), more durable, no painting, ale higher CAPEX.
  - **Combined** — concrete columns + steel beams (best of both).
- **Maintenance access**:
  - **Walkways** at each tier dla maintenance access.
  - **Drop-out areas** dla flange spreader, valve replacement.
  - **Drains** at low points — rainwater off platform.
  - **Lighting** — explosion-proof dla hazardous areas.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-fab spool** w shop, lift onto rack — minimize field welds + scaffolding hours.
  - **Lifting points** clear — crane access dla future maintenance.
  - **Color code pipes per service**: yellow (gas), green (water), red (fire water), blue (air) — z direction arrow.
  - **Sloping toward drain** for all pipes — eliminate water trap (winter freeze).
  - **Drip pan** under valves/flanges containing hazardous medium.

### ZZZZZZZZZ. TEG (Triethylene Glycol) Gas Dehydration Unit — Piping
- **Po co**: natural gas zawiera **water vapor** (saturated z reservoir). Free water w pipeline → **hydraty** (covered iter 76), **corrosion**, **freezing** (Joule-Thomson cooling). **Dehydration** = obniżenie water content do <**7 lb/MMscf** (pipeline spec) lub <**0.1 ppm** (LNG spec).
- **TEG vs alternatywy**:
  - **TEG** — najczęstsze, do **dew point depression 30-50°C**, OPEX moderate.
  - **DEG (Diethylene Glycol)** — niższa T tolerance, less common.
  - **MEG (Mono Ethylene Glycol)** — hydrate inhibition (injection), nie dehydration absorption.
  - **Molecular sieve** — dehydration do **<0.1 ppm** dla LNG, ale CAPEX 3-5× TEG.
  - **Silica gel** — small scale, batch.
- **TEG właściwości**:
  - **C₆H₁₄O₄**, MW 150.
  - **Boiling point 287°C, decomposition above 400°C**.
  - **Hygroscopic** — strong affinity dla water.
  - **Low vapor pressure** — small carryover w dry gas.
  - **Lean TEG**: 98-99.5% purity (water <1.5%).
  - **Rich TEG**: 92-96% purity (water 4-8% absorbed).
- **System overview** — 2 sekcje:
  1. **Contactor section** (high pressure) — absorbs water.
  2. **Regeneration section** (low pressure, hot) — strip water, recycle TEG.
- **Contactor tower (absorber)**:
  - **Vertical column**, **30-100 bar** operating pressure.
  - **Diameter** 0.5-3 m, height 8-20 m.
  - **Internal**: structured packing (Mellapak, Sulzer BX) lub trays (bubble cap, valve).
  - **Wet gas IN bottom** — rises.
  - **Lean TEG IN top** — falls.
  - **Counter-current contact** — TEG absorbs water progressively, gas dries top-to-bottom.
  - **Dry gas OUT top** — to pipeline.
  - **Rich TEG OUT bottom** — to regeneration.
- **Regeneration skid** — komponenty:
  - **Flash tank** — niskie ciśnienie (4-7 bar), hydrocarbon flash off (recovered to fuel gas).
  - **Filter (solids)** — 5-25 µm cartridge, usuwa rdza, scale.
  - **Carbon filter** — activated carbon, usuwa hydrocarbons + corrosion inhibitors.
  - **Lean/Rich heat exchanger** — rich TEG (zimne) preheats od lean TEG (gorące) → energy saving.
  - **Reboiler (still column)** — boiler heats TEG do **204°C (400°F)** (just below decomposition).
  - **Still column (regenerator)** — distillation column, water vapor leaves overhead, lean TEG bottoms.
  - **Reflux condenser coil** — partial condensation overhead, returns liquid TEG back, lets vapor exit.
  - **Stripping gas** (optional) — dry gas/CO₂ injected do reboiler bottom, **lower partial pressure water** → higher TEG purity (99.7-99.9%).
  - **Surge tank / accumulator** — TEG hold-up volume.
  - **TEG cooler** (water/air) — cools lean TEG do **40-50°C** przed wejściem do contactora.
  - **Booster pumps** — circulates TEG (typically positive displacement, plunger).
- **Piping considerations**:
  - **Contactor inlet/outlet**: large diameter, high P (CS A106 Gr B, ANSI 600 lub 900).
  - **Rich TEG line** (contactor bottom → regeneration): może zawierać **dissolved hydrocarbons** + slight pressure → flash precautions.
  - **Reboiler** (atmospheric or low P): CS, but **204°C T** → not insulated dla cool zones (man-protection).
  - **Vapor line** (still overhead): T 100°C, low P, often **SS** dla corrosion (water + acid gas).
  - **Stripping gas line**: dry, hot — CS.
- **Glycol contamination + degradation**:
  - **TEG degradation** — accelerated above 204°C, oxygen, acids.
  - **Thermal degradation** → glycol breaks → glycolic acid → corrosion + foam.
  - **Salt contamination** (formation water) → solid deposits w reboiler, fouling.
  - **Air ingress** → oxidation → TEG turns dark, viscous, acidic.
  - **Solution**: N₂ blanket na surge tank, periodic filter change, oxygen scavengers if needed.
- **Reboiler design**:
  - **Heat source**: fire-tube (gas-fired), steam, hot oil, electrical.
  - **Fire-tube reboiler**: most common dla onshore gas wells.
  - **Temperature control** crítico — 204°C max (TEG decomposition 207°C).
  - **Heat flux** <8 kW/m² — avoid hot spots.
- **Hot fume vent + glycol losses**:
  - **Reboiler vent** → atmosphere lub flare lub VRU.
  - **VOC emissions** (BTEX, hexane) absorbed from gas → released w regenerator → environmental concern.
  - **Aerated cooler** dla vapors before vent → condenses heavy HC.
  - **EPA reporting** — glycol units regulated dla benzene, BTEX.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Too low TEG circulation rate** — incomplete absorption, off-spec gas.
  - **Too high TEG circulation rate** — excessive reboiler duty, fuel waste.
  - **TEG circulation rate rule**: **2-5 gal TEG per lb water removed** (rough).
  - **Reboiler temp >207°C** — TEG decomposition, glycolic acid, corrosion.
  - **Salt build-up reboiler** — heat transfer drops, hot spots, decomposition.
  - **Wrong contactor packing** — channeling → poor contact → off-spec gas.
- **Common contaminants**:
  - **H₂S/CO₂ z gas** → acid gas in TEG → corrosion.
  - **Inhibitors / surfactants** z upstream → foaming w contactor.
  - **Particulates** (sand, scale) → erosion, plugging filters.
- **Performance monitoring**:
  - **Dew point analyzer** dry gas outlet — verify spec compliance.
  - **TEG concentration** lab test weekly — verify lean TEG purity.
  - **Reboiler temp**, **stripping gas rate**, **TEG circulation rate** — recorded.
  - **Acid number** TEG (KOH titration) — degradation indicator.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **All piping welded** preferred (limit flanges) — small leaks costly (TEG ekspensive).
  - **Insulate hot lines** (reboiler, still column) z mineral wool — safety + energy.
  - **Drain valves** at low points dla maintenance.
  - **Sample ports**: lean TEG, rich TEG, dry gas — dla quality monitoring.
  - **Pressure gauges + temperatures** at all major sections.
  - **Pressure relief**: contactor (HP) → flare; reboiler (atm) → vent.
  - **N₂ purge connections** dla each vessel dla shutdown / startup.
  - **Heat trace** dla outdoor cold-climate installations (winter TEG viscosity high).

---

## Iteration 78 — 2026-05-26

### AAAAAAAAAA. Acoustic-Induced Vibration (AIV) + Flow-Induced Vibration (FIV) — Flare Headers + Relief Systems
- **Problem**: gaz przepływający przez **pressure-reducing device** (PSV, control valve, restriction orifice, choke) z **wysokim ΔP** generuje energię akustyczną do **160-180 dB** wewnątrz rury. Energy excitates wall → high-frequency vibration → **fatigue failure małych branch connections** w godziny/dni.
- **Failure history**:
  - Piper Alpha (1988) — nie AIV bezpośrednio, ale podobne mechanizmy.
  - **Common location**: small-bore connection (instrument tap, vent, drain) **na branch tee** wzdłuż relief headera.
  - **Czas do failure**: 8-200 godzin po starcie procesu z wysokim ΔP.
- **AIV vs FIV**:
  - **AIV** — **acoustic energy** powodowany high-velocity gas + ΔP. Częstotliwość **1-5 kHz**, energy excites pipe shell (transverse modes) → high-frequency cycle fatigue.
  - **FIV** — **flow turbulence**, vortex shedding, slugging, two-phase impingement. Częstotliwość **<200 Hz**, low-frequency, excites pipe span beams modes.
- **Sound Power Level (PWL/SPL)** — kluczowa metryka:
  - **Carucci-Mueller equation** (najczęstsza):
    - PWL = 10·log₁₀[(P₁−P₂)²·M·T/MW·(P₁²)] + 126.1 + 10·log(W) − 60
    - W = mass flow rate (kg/s), M = Mach no, T = temp, MW = molar weight.
  - **Threshold**:
    - **PWL <155 dB** — safe, no further action.
    - **PWL 155-165 dB** — risk zone, LOF (Likelihood Of Failure) calculation.
    - **PWL >165 dB** — high risk, mandatory mitigation.
    - **PWL >170 dB** — almost certain failure w hours.
- **Energy Institute Guidelines (EI Guidelines)** — industry standard:
  - **"Guidelines for the Avoidance of Vibration Induced Fatigue Failure in Process Pipework"** (2nd Ed 2008, 3rd Ed 2026).
  - **LOF calculation** based on:
    - PWL.
    - **D/t ratio** (pipe diameter / wall thickness) — większy D/t = niższa shell stiffness = wyższy stress.
    - **Branch connection geometry** — set-on, weldolet, sweepolet, reducing tee.
    - **Distance** od source.
  - **CONCAWE 85/52** — older European guidance, similar approach.
  - **DEP (Shell), Norsok L-002** — operator-specific standards.
- **Vulnerable locations**:
  - **Small-bore connections (SBC)** ≤2" — main fatigue location.
  - **Instrument taps** (PT, TT, gauges) na main relief header.
  - **Vent + drain valves** z stub piping.
  - **Lateral takeoffs** z high-velocity branch.
  - **Reducing tees** — diameter change creates turbulence + pressure peaks.
- **Mitigation strategies** (Energy Institute hierarchy):
  1. **Reduce source energy** — niższy ΔP across valve, multi-stage pressure reduction.
  2. **Larger downstream pipe** — niższa velocity, niższy Mach, niższy PWL.
  3. **Thicker wall** — wyższa pipe stiffness, niższe shell stress (zmień D/t z 70 do 40).
  4. **Geometry mitigation** branch connections:
     - **Sweepolet** (best dla AIV resistance) — smooth contour, low stress concentration factor (SCF ≈1.5-2.5).
     - **Reducing tee** (full-wrap) — najlepsze, SCF 1.0-1.5.
     - **Pipet®** (Bonney Forge) — mid-tier, SCF 2.5-3.5.
     - **Weldolet** — SCF 3-4.
     - **Set-on / Stub-on** — najgorsze, SCF 4-6.
     - **AVOID Threadolet i sockolet** dla high AIV — fatigue notch.
  5. **Add full-wrap reinforcement pad** — increases local thickness.
  6. **Reroute SBC** od main header (further from high-energy source).
  7. **Remove dead legs** — small terminating branches z plugs.
- **Mitigation podczas eksploatacji**:
  - **Multi-stage choke** — split ΔP across 2-3 stages → niższe PWL każda.
  - **Silencer** — packed muffler na vent/blowdown lines.
  - **Pipe wrap** — viscoelastic damping material (Vibradamp, 3M ISD).
  - **Pipe support stiffener** — clamp z pad na vulnerable section.
- **Inspection & monitoring**:
  - **Visual inspection** SBC junction po pierwszym startupie.
  - **MT/PT** wszystkie weld toes branch connections.
  - **Accelerometers** czasowo w wysoka-PWL sekcji — measure vibration amplitude.
  - **Strain gauge** dla short-term stress measurement w fatigue location.
  - **Long-term**: SDOF (single degree of freedom) wireless sensors na critical branches.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **PSV outlet sizing** — outlet pipe ≥ PSV outlet flange size, NIE reduce downstream PSV.
  - **Brake plate** lub **diffuser** at flare tip — reduces noise + vibration retreating.
  - **NDT 100%** dla branch connections w PWL >155 dB zonie.
  - **Tag critical branches** z paint code / warning label — visual reminder.
  - **As-built drawings** include SBC schedule (location, geometry, SCF) — dla future audit + inspection program.
  - **AIV/FIV review** w PHA/HAZOP — formalna identyfikacja w design.

### BBBBBBBBBB. HF Alkylation Unit — Monel Piping + Refinery HF Service
- **Po co HF alkylation**: refinery proces tworzenia **alkylate** (high-octane gasoline blend) z isobutane + olefins (propylene, butylene) używając **HF (hydrofluoric acid)** jako katalizator. Alternatywa H₂SO₄ alkylation, ale HF zazwyczaj tańszy + lighter handling logistics.
- **HF właściwości** — KRYTYCZNE bezpieczeństwo:
  - **Anhydrous HF** (>99%) — gaz powyżej 19.5°C, liquid below.
  - **Density**: 0.99 g/cm³ (lighter than water).
  - **Toxicity**: PEL 3 ppm (OSHA), IDLH 30 ppm. **Penetrates skin** bez bólu, zabija przez **hipokalcemia** (binds Ca²⁺) — cardiac arrest.
  - **Vapor cloud**: dense, hugs ground, kilometers drift dla large release.
  - **Skin contact**: 25 cm² = potential fatality. **Calcium gluconate gel** first aid critical.
  - **Industry track record**: Texas City 1987 release, Husky Superior 2018, Philadelphia 2019 — multiple incidents.
- **Materials selection** — concentration + T + water content critical:
  - **Carbon Steel (CS)** — **najczęściej** w low T (<35°C) + high HF concentration (>65%) + low water (<4%):
    - **Mechanizm**: surface reacts → **iron fluoride (FeF₂) layer** = protective barrier.
    - **Loss rate**: ~0.05-0.5 mm/year normal.
    - **Embrittlement risk**: high-strength steels (yield >340 MPa) susceptible to HF SCC.
    - **Spec**: **A105 N + A106 Gr B** carbon equivalents controlled. Residual Ni + Cu ≤0.15% (max). V ≤0.02%, Nb ≤0.02% (avoid hydrogen-induced cracking).
    - **PWHT** na wszystkich welds — usuwa residual stress + hardness.
  - **Monel 400 (Cu-Ni alloy 67/33)** — **acid regenerator, rerun tower, locations z high T (>50°C) lub high water**:
    - Excellent HF corrosion resistance across wide concentrations + temperatures.
    - **Price**: ~5-7× CS, używaj selectively.
    - **Joint**: GTAW z ER-NiCu-7 filler, no PWHT needed.
  - **Alloy 20 / Hastelloy C-276** — specialty dla oksydujących HF (rare).
  - **NIE: Cu, brass** — high corrosion + galvanic. **NIE: Aluminium** — violently reacts.
- **Critical components**:
  - **Acid settler**: vessel gdzie HF i hydrocarbon separują się (HF na dnie, HC na górze).
  - **Reactor**: typically vertical, multi-zone, intensive mixing HC + HF.
  - **Acid regenerator**: removes acid sludge, water buildup.
  - **Rerun tower**: distillation HF + HC.
  - **Defluorinator**: removes residual HF od product.
  - **HF storage tanks**: insulated, refrigerated, multi-layered tertiary containment.
- **Valves**:
  - **Body**: CS lub Monel.
  - **Internals (seat, plug)**: Monel + spring-loaded.
  - **Packing**: Teflon graphite (anti-corrosive).
  - **Sealing**: triple stem seal + live-loading (continuous Belleville washer compression).
  - **Producent**: **Cameron, Velan, KITZ-K1V** — specjalnie kwalifikowani dla HF.
- **Gaskets** — alky-spec:
  - **Standard**: spiral wound z **Monel 400 Kamprofile** primary + **expanded PTFE pillow** secondary barrier.
  - **NIE used**: rubber, fiber, spiral wound z SS (galvanic).
  - **Wymiana**: każde otwarcie flange = nowa gasket.
- **Piping schedule**:
  - **Min Sch 80** dla all HF piping, **Sch 160** dla high P lines.
  - **No threaded connections** — wszystko welded lub flanged.
  - **No screwed nipples / unions** — fail points.
- **Welding wymagania**:
  - **WPS specific dla HF service** (API RP 751).
  - **Low-H electrode** (E7018-H4 lub H8).
  - **PWHT 100%** dla all CS welds — usuwa residual stress.
  - **Hardness HV10 ≤200** — soft enough dla HF resistance.
  - **NDT 100%** RT lub UT.
  - **Hold time** PWHT: 1h per inch wall, min 1 h.
- **Inspection program**:
  - **API RP 751** — comprehensive inspection dla HF alky.
  - **CS pipe**: UT thickness annually, **statistical sampling 100 points/segment** (large areas).
  - **HF accumulator**: WFMT (wet fluorescent MT) na all welds.
  - **High-risk areas**: vapor space (water condensation), elbows, downstream control valves (high velocity erosion).
  - **Corrosion under insulation (CUI)** — biggest hidden risk; insulation removal po 5 latach dla inspection.
- **Safety design**:
  - **Tertiary containment** — primary + secondary + tertiary catch basins.
  - **Water spray** system (deluge) — w wykorzystaniu HF release: spray na vapor cloud, knock down + dilute.
  - **HF detection** — multiple sensors, alarm @1 ppm, evacuation @5 ppm.
  - **Wind sensors** + **dispersion model** — predict cloud direction.
  - **HF mitigation systems** w post-Texas City designs:
    - Acid mitigation (ARS) — large water spray network.
    - Acid recovery system — captures release before dispersion.
  - **Emergency isolation valves** — fast-closing actuators (10-30 sec).
- **Personnel safety**:
  - **Level A suit** (fully encapsulating, SCBA) dla anything > work permit threshold.
  - **Calcium gluconate gel** + IV at fixed locations.
  - **No solo work** — always 2-person rule near HF.
  - **Pre-shift HF training** + certification annually.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **No grinding/cutting** w live area — fire/spark + HF release.
  - **Hot work permits** — strict, w designated cold-cut area only.
  - **Verify decontamination** before work — measure HF residue z Drager tubes.
  - **N₂ purge** entire system przed work — verify oxygen + HF zero readings.
  - **Multiple isolation** (block + bleed + double block) — never single valve.
  - **Tools**: SS, Monel only — żaden Cu, brass.
  - **Don't break flanges blindly** — even after purge, residual liquid w pockets.

### CCCCCCCCCC. Sanitary CIP / SIP Piping — Food + Pharma Hygienic Design
- **Po co**: food + pharmaceutical procesy wymagają **bioburden control** — eliminuje bacteria, biofilm, residue zanim contamination batchu produktu (e.g., milk pasteurization, vaccine production, beer brewing). **CIP (Clean-in-Place)** = circulate cleaning chemicals + water through equipment **bez disassembly**. **SIP (Steam-in-Place)** = sterilization z saturated steam (>121°C, 15 min).
- **Standardy** (krytyczne dla compliance):
  - **3-A Sanitary Standards** (USA, dairy + food) — wszystkie equipment + piping designs certified.
  - **EHEDG** (European Hygienic Engineering & Design Group) — EU standard.
  - **ASME BPE** (Bioprocessing Equipment) — pharma standard.
  - **FDA 21 CFR** dla pharma in USA, **EU GMP Annex 1** dla aseptic manufacturing.
  - **USP <88> Class VI** materials biocompatibility test.
- **Surface finish wymagania**:
  - **3-A / EHEDG minimum**: **Ra ≤0.8 µm (32 µin)** — mechanically polished.
  - **ASME BPE SF-3 (most common)**: **Ra ≤0.5 µm (20 µin)** mechanically polished.
  - **ASME BPE SF-4**: **Ra ≤0.4 µm + electropolished** — premium pharma.
  - **ASME BPE SF-5 / SF-6**: ≤0.25 µm — high-end (vaccines, biologics).
  - **Why finish matters**: rough surface (>1 µm) → bacteria adhesion → biofilm formation, CIP ineffective.
- **Material selection**:
  - **316L SS standard** dla wszystkich wet contacts (L = low carbon ≤0.030%, avoid sensitization na welds).
  - **AL-6XN** dla high chloride environments (brewing).
  - **Hastelloy C-22** dla aggressive chemistry (whey, fermentation).
  - **NIE 304** — chlorides → pitting w months.
  - **NIE galvanized, Cu** — toxic, corrosion.
  - **Welding**: GTAW z **back-purge** Ar (oxide-free root). NO sugar, no scale, no porosity.
- **Dead leg rule** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **L_dead ≤ 1.5 × D** (depth of dead leg ≤ 1.5× pipe diameter).
  - **Reasoning**: bacteria w stagnant zone, CIP flow nie reach turbulent w deep pocket → biofilm.
  - **Examples**:
    - DN 25 line → dead leg max 38 mm głębokości.
    - DN 50 → max 75 mm.
  - **Common dead leg violations**:
    - Branch tee z capped pipe (vent/drain) — too long stub.
    - Instrument tap with extended thermowell.
    - Spectacle blind cavity.
    - Sample valve port too far od main flow.
  - **Solution**: use **flush-mounted valves** (zero dead leg), instrument **flush-bore** (no protrusion).
- **Drainage**:
  - **Pipe slope minimum 1:100** (1% lub ¼" per foot).
  - **No low points without drain valve**.
  - **Eccentric reducer** flat-bottom (concentric eccentrics create dead pocket).
  - **No traps** (U-bends) — water + product accumulation.
- **Connections**:
  - **Tri-clamp (TC) / sanitary clamp** — preferred dla disassembly + inspection. Ferrule + gasket + clamp.
  - **Aseptic flanges (DIN 11864-2/3)** — high-end pharma, gasket compressed metal-to-metal stop.
  - **Welded** — best dla permanent, no crevice.
  - **NO threaded** — crevice + biofilm hidden under threads.
- **CIP cycle** (typical):
  1. **Pre-rinse**: water flush, 5-10 min, removes loose residue.
  2. **Caustic wash**: 1-2% NaOH @70-85°C, **15-30 min** — emulsifies fats, dissolves proteins.
  3. **Intermediate rinse**: water 5-10 min.
  4. **Acid wash** (opcjonalnie): 0.5-1% HNO₃ lub H₃PO₄ @60°C, **10-20 min** — dissolves minerał scale (Ca/Mg deposits).
  5. **Final rinse**: water (sometimes WFI - Water For Injection w pharma).
  6. **Air blow** (opcjonalnie): compressed air, dries pipework.
- **CIP flow + velocity**:
  - **Min velocity**: **1.5 m/s (5 ft/s)** dla turbulent flow + shear stress w wall.
  - **Re >4000** in piping for effective CIP.
  - **Spray balls** w tanks — rotating, cover 100% interior.
- **SIP cycle**:
  - **Saturated steam** (100% RH) @ **121-135°C** (15-30 psig).
  - **Hold time**: 15-30 min @ 121°C dla typical microbial reduction.
  - **F₀ value**: lethality measure, target F₀ ≥15-30 min equivalent @ 121°C.
  - **Cool-down**: filtered sterile air przed loss-of-sterility scenario.
- **Hygienic valves**:
  - **Diaphragm valve** (Saunders type) — najczęstsze, weir-type, no metal-to-metal seating, fully drainable.
  - **Mix-proof valve** (Alfa Laval, Tetra Pak) — two seats z drainable cavity, prevent cross-contamination.
  - **Aseptic ball valve** — encapsulated stem seal, drainable cavity.
  - **AVOID**: gate valves, plug valves z lubrication, screwed bonnet (crevice).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Dead legs >1.5D** — sanitation fails, ROD (root cause of deviation).
  - **Threaded connections** — cleaning hides residue.
  - **No slope** — pooling water → corrosion + biofilm.
  - **Insufficient surface finish** — bacterial adhesion.
  - **Concentric reducer** w horizontal line — pocket dla product.
  - **Mismatched ID** (pipe-to-fitting) → step → soil accumulation.
  - **Overheated PWHT** sensitizes 304 → chloride pitting.
- **Validation + Documentation** (pharma-specific):
  - **IQ/OQ/PQ** (Installation/Operational/Performance Qualification).
  - **CIP cycle validation**: swab tests + ATP bioluminescence (limit <50 RLU).
  - **Riboflavin spray test**: UV indicator, spray walls, run CIP, verify NO residue UV-visible.
  - **Documentation chain**: weld map, weld procedure record, NDT, surface finish certs.
- **Surface finish testing**:
  - **Profilometer** — direct Ra measurement (contact stylus or non-contact optical).
  - **3-D scanner** — surface topology.
  - **Random sampling** — every 10-20 m + każdy critical joint.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Orbital welding (GTAW automated)** — uniform bead, no spatter, repeatable.
  - **Back-purge** z Ar dla każdego root pass — oxide-free.
  - **Pre-cleaned pipe** (acid-pickled + passivated) przed install.
  - **No flux** w weld root (becomes contamination).
  - **Inspection access** — 100% borescope dla all welds wewnątrz pipe.
  - **Tag-out / sanitize tools** — dedicated dla sanitary work (no cross-contamination z carbon steel shop).
  - **Final passivation** po welding — citric or nitric acid passivation per ASTM A380.
  - **Documentation pack** — drawings, certs, NDT, surface finish reports.

---

## Iteration 79 — 2026-05-26

### DDDDDDDDDD. PVC / CPVC Solvent Cement Bonding — Primer + Cure Time
- **Po co solvent cement**: PVC + CPVC plastic pipes joined przez **chemical fusion**, NIE gluing. Solvent dissolves surface molecules pipe + fitting → mixed molecular layer → po evaporation solvent → fused joint **mocniejszy niż pipe sam**.
- **Materials**:
  - **PVC** (Poly Vinyl Chloride) — najpopularniejszy plastic dla water, sewage, irrigation. Max T 60°C.
  - **CPVC** (Chlorinated PVC) — rozszerzony T range do 93°C, dla hot water + mild chemicals.
  - **NIE PE, PP, PVDF** — wymagają **fusion welding** (heat), NIE solvent cement.
  - **ABS** — separate solvent type (NIE compatible z PVC cement).
- **ASTM standards**:
  - **ASTM D2564** — PVC solvent cement.
  - **ASTM F493** — CPVC solvent cement.
  - **ASTM F656** — PVC primer.
  - **ASTM D2855** — making solvent cemented joints (procedure).
- **Cement categories** (per F493 / D2564):
  - **Regular bodied** — small/thin pipes (≤1.5"), low viscosity, fast set.
  - **Medium bodied** — common, 0.5-6" pipes.
  - **Heavy bodied** — 3-12" large pipes, high gap-filling capacity.
  - **Extra heavy bodied** — >12" pipes, maximum gap fill.
- **Primer**:
  - **Wymagany** dla heavy-bodied cements (less solvent w cement → primer pre-softens).
  - **Color** — typically **purple** (visible inspection — verify że primed).
  - **Clear primer** dostępny, ale trudniej w QA (no visual confirmation).
  - **NIE pomijaj primer** na CPVC nawet jeśli "no-primer" cement dostępny — inspectors check primer color.
- **Standard joint procedure** (ASTM D2855):
  1. **Cut square** — pipe cutter wheel lub fine-tooth saw, deburr inside + outside.
  2. **Test fit dry** — pipe powinien wsuwać się **1/3 do 1/2** dla socket depth ("interference fit"). Loose = wymień fitting/pipe. Bottom-out całkowicie = za luźne.
  3. **Clean surfaces** — wipe dry, dla heavy soil użyj cleaner (MEK).
  4. **Apply primer** obu surfaces (pipe OD + fitting socket ID) — even coat, working into pores. **Dwell time 15-30 sec**.
  5. **Apply cement** **while primer still wet** — heavy coat on pipe, light on fitting (avoid pooling at bottom).
  6. **Insert pipe** into fitting with **1/4 turn** twist → distribute cement evenly.
  7. **Hold for set time** — 30 sec (small) do 5 min (large).
  8. **Wipe excess** cement — bead 360° around joint visible = good fill.
  9. **Don't move** for cure time.
- **Set time / cure time** (zależy od size + T + RH):
  - **Set time** (handle without disturbing): 30 sec (½") - 5 min (12") @ 21°C.
  - **Initial cure** (light handling, water test low P): 1 h (½") - 24 h (12") @ 21°C.
  - **Full cure** (full pressure, working): 24 h (½") - 168 h / 7 days (12") @ 21°C.
- **Cure rate factors**:
  - **T**: ↑ T → ↑ cure rate. **Zimne <5°C** → cure 2-4× slower lub stop. **Hot >40°C** → flash off solvent too fast → weak joint.
  - **RH (humidity)**: high RH ↓ cure rate. >90% może wstrzymać cure.
  - **Pipe size**: bigger ID = slower solvent escape = slower cure.
  - **Fit**: tighter fit = faster cure.
- **Pressure testing**:
  - **NIE testuj przed full cure** — joint może exploded violently.
  - **Hydrotest** preferred over pneumatic (NIE pneumatic z plastic — failure katastrofalne).
  - **Test pressure** 150% design, ale nie więcej niż pipe rating.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak primera** dla heavy-bodied cement — weak joint, eventual failure.
  - **Za dużo cementu** — pools w socket, weakens joint w bottom.
  - **Za mało cementu** — uneven coverage, leak path.
  - **Suchy primer** przed cement — straca pre-softening effect.
  - **Wilgoć / dirty surfaces** — interferuje z fusion.
  - **Cold install** (<5°C) — cement nie aktywuje properly.
  - **Hot install** (>40°C) — solvent flashes before joint complete.
  - **Move joint przed set** — break bond, weak joint.
  - **Pressure test before cure** — burst.
  - **Wrong cement** dla material (PVC cement na CPVC = fail).
- **Storage + shelf life**:
  - **Cement + primer**: 2-3 years od date of manufacture sealed.
  - **Open container**: 6 m-cy max — solvent evaporates, viscosity rośnie.
  - **Discard if**: thickened (jak miód), separated, gelled.
  - **Storage**: 4-35°C, sealed tightly.
- **Schedule 80 specific**:
  - **Heavier wall** = więcej material to soften = potrzebuje heavy-bodied cement.
  - **Larger socket depth** — cement musi reach pełnej depth.
  - **Cure time longer** vs Sch 40.
- **Solvent cement vs gasketed (push-on) connections**:
  - **Solvent cement** — permanent, pressure-rated, sanitary OK.
  - **Gasketed** (rubber ring seal, push-on) — non-pressure, sewer/storm only, faster install ale lower performance.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Daubers** matching cement viscosity — small dla ½" pipes, large dla 6"+.
  - **Two-person job** dla pipes ≥4" — one applies cement, other holds + pushes.
  - **Don't drag pipe** przez dirt po cementing — contamination.
  - **Cap unused ends** podczas install — prevent debris w pipe.
  - **Mark cure date** na fresh joint — operator wie kiedy testować.
  - **Ventilation** — solvent fumes (THF, MEK) — flammable + toxic. Outdoor or forced ventilation.
  - **NO smoking** — solvent fumes ignite.
  - **PPE**: nitrile gloves (NIE latex), safety goggles, respirator dla enclosed spaces.

### EEEEEEEEEE. Pipe Spring Hangers — Variable + Constant Load (MSS SP-58)
- **Po co**: pipes ze znaczącym **thermal movement** (steam, hot oil, cryogenic) **nie mogą być rigidly supported** — ekspansja generowałaby destrukcyjne siły. Spring hangers **pozwalają vertical movement** podtrzymując pipe weight + minimizing load variation.
- **Standardy**:
  - **MSS SP-58** — pipe hangers + supports materials, design, manufacture.
  - **MSS SP-69** — pipe hangers + supports selection + application.
  - **MSS SP-89** — pipe hangers fabrication + installation.
  - **ASME B31.1** — power piping (steam systems → main use).
- **Types**:
  - **Variable spring hanger** — load variation **<25%** dla hot vs cold position.
  - **Constant load hanger** — load variation **<6%**, dla critical/sensitive equipment.
  - **Rigid hanger** — żadnego vertical movement (anchors, guides nie spring).
- **Variable spring hanger**:
  - **Konstrukcja**: helical spring w can/housing, indicator scale, load adjustment ring.
  - **Działanie**: pipe vertical movement → spring compresses/extends → force varies linearly z displacement (F = k·x, Hooke's law).
  - **Load travel chart**:
    - **Cold position** — pipe na ambient T, spring relaxed.
    - **Hot position** — pipe na operating T, spring compressed (or extended).
    - **Load variation** = |F_hot − F_cold| / F_hot × 100% ≤ 25%.
  - **Aplikacja**: typical movement **<75 mm**, non-critical lines (utility steam, secondary process).
  - **Cost**: 30-50% constant hanger.
- **Constant load hanger**:
  - **Konstrukcja**: helical spring + **cam mechanism** (eccentric pivot lever).
  - **Działanie**: spring force × moment arm = constant force at pipe attachment point.
  - **Mechanism**: jak spring compresses (więcej force), moment arm decreases proportionally → net force unchanged at pipe.
  - **Load variation**: <6% throughout travel range.
  - **Aplikacja**: critical lines (main steam, turbine connections, where stress sensitivity critical), large movements >75 mm.
  - **Cost**: 2-3× variable hanger.
- **Selection process** (MSS SP-58):
  1. **Calculate pipe load** = (pipe weight + insulation + content + ice + valve/flange concentrated loads) for span.
  2. **Calculate vertical movement** δ = α × L × ΔT (linear expansion factor).
  3. **Variability check**: jeśli δ <75 mm + load variation <25% → variable spring OK.
  4. **Jeśli > 75 mm** lub critical → constant load hanger.
  5. **Catalog selection** — Lisega, PSSI, Anvil, Bergen, PT&P catalogs — choose model meeting load + travel range.
- **Sizing margins**:
  - **Travel range** spring ≥ **1.5× calculated movement** — accounts dla unexpected thermal cycles, install tolerance.
  - **Load range** middle of travel band — install at center, allow both up + down movement.
  - **Stops** (top + bottom) limit spring range — pipe nie wisi free if support fails.
- **Cold position vs hot position installation**:
  - **Install cold** — pipe at ambient, adjust spring na "cold load":
    - Spring sized so że pri hot operation, spring deflects, taking hot load.
    - **Travel direction**: jeśli pipe grows up (hot), spring compresses (load increases) → pre-set spring lower (cold).
  - **Lock-down screws** during install — prevent premature deflection.
  - **Release lock-down** po pipe filled + tested + ready for hot service.
- **Load indicator**:
  - **Pointer + scale** na can — visual indicator current load.
  - **Cold-set / hot-set** marks — installer-set markings.
  - **Reading position** od pipe ↔ spring connection.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong load calculation** — pipe weight underestimated → spring bottom-out (no support).
  - **Wrong travel** — pipe expansion exceeds spring travel → spring bottoms-out → rigid + force concentration.
  - **No lock-down release** po hydrotest — spring nie functions, rigid support → thermal stress damage.
  - **Spring corroded** — galvanic / atmospheric → loses spring constant → wrong load.
  - **Wrong direction** install — vertical pipe grows up, spring set assuming down movement → wrong load.
- **Installation sequence**:
  1. **Pre-install** spring hanger z **all-thread rods** + clamps + clevis.
  2. **Position pipe** w cold position.
  3. **Connect spring hanger** to pipe (clamp/clevis) + structure (above).
  4. **Lock down stops** engaged (preventing spring deflection podczas install).
  5. **Hydrotest** — water adds weight, but lock-down still on (rigid).
  6. **Drain hydrotest water**.
  7. **Fill pipe z process medium** (steam, oil) + heat-up gradually.
  8. **Release lock-down stops** when pipe at operating T.
  9. **Verify indicator at hot position** — within design range.
  10. **Document settings** — cold load, hot load, travel direction.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Annual visual** — pointer position, corrosion, leak (water trap).
  - **5-year load verification** — load cell measurement at indicator.
  - **Lubricate cam mechanism** (constant hanger) — periodically.
  - **Replace** corroded springs — never paint over (paint shifts spring constant).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Lisega catalog** — premier supplier, comprehensive engineering catalogs.
  - **PSSI / Anvil / Bergen** alternatywne.
  - **Snubbers / sway braces** — separate dla seismic/water hammer (NIE same jak spring hanger).
  - **Sliding shoes + guides** dla horizontal restraint — separate components.
  - **Indicator gauge** wisible z grade (operator can verify bez climbing).
  - **Spring hanger tag** z load, travel, position od engineering — permanent metal tag.
  - **Insulate spring** w cold service (cryogenic) — prevent ice formation w mechanism.

### FFFFFFFFFF. Horizontal Directional Drilling (HDD) — Pipeline Crossings
- **Po co HDD**: instalacja pipeline pod **przeszkodą** (rzeka, autostrada, lotnisko, miasto, wetland) **bez wykopu** — minimum surface disturbance + environmental impact + traffic disruption.
- **HDD process** — 3 etapy:
  1. **Pilot bore** — small-diameter steerable bore z entry point do exit point.
  2. **Reaming** — progressive enlargement, pilot → final size.
  3. **Pullback** — product pipe pulled przez reamed hole.
- **Pilot bore**:
  - **Drill rig** at entry point — angled 8-15° down.
  - **Drill rod**: 3-5 m segments steel rod (S-135 grade, 4.5" lub 5.5" OD typical).
  - **Steerable head** (slant face mud motor) — drilling direction adjustable.
  - **Tracking**:
    - **Walkover (1.5-15 m depth)** — operator z hand-held detector follows transmitter w drill head, surface signal.
    - **Wire-line (deep, >15 m)** — magnetic field-based, no surface tracking required.
    - **Gyro-based** — accurate dla very deep / underwater bores.
  - **Steering**: drill rod **rotates** dla straight bore; **stops rotation** + slant face deflects → curves direction. **Push without rotation** = directional change.
- **Reaming**:
  - **Progressive sizes** — start z pilot (4.5"), expand do 8", 12", 18", 24" do final (~1.2-1.5× pipe OD).
  - **Reamer types**:
    - **Hole opener** (rock formations) — carbide teeth.
    - **Fly cutter** (sticky clay) — paddle-style, mixes clay.
    - **Bullet reamer** (sand) — round profile, less mud aggressive.
    - **Hybrid** — versatile mixed soils.
  - **Reaming direction**: typically **back-ream** (pull reamer od exit point toward entry while rotating).
  - **Final bore**: **1.2-1.5× pipe OD** dla pullback clearance.
- **Drilling mud (bentonite slurry)**:
  - **Cel**:
    1. **Cool + lubricate** drill bit + reamer.
    2. **Transport cuttings** od cutting face do entry/exit.
    3. **Stabilize bore wall** — prevent collapse.
    4. **Reduce friction** during pullback.
  - **Skład**:
    - **Bentonite clay** (10-12% by weight) — base.
    - **PHPA** (partially hydrolyzed polyacrylamide) — shale inhibitor, viscosity.
    - **PAC** (polyanionic cellulose) — filtrate reducer.
    - **Soda ash** — pH adjuster.
    - **Water** (rest of volume).
  - **Properties** (typical pipeline crossing):
    - **Marsh funnel viscosity**: **50-55 sec/qt** (viscosity).
    - **Mud weight**: **8.7-9.2 ppg** (density, lb/gal).
    - **Gel strength**: 10-second + 10-minute readings — suspension ability.
    - **Filtrate**: <10 mL / 30 min API filter press.
  - **Mud recycle system** — solids control:
    - **Shale shaker** — primary screen, separates large cuttings.
    - **Desander** (4" hydrocyclones) — removes sand-size particles.
    - **Desilter** (2" hydrocyclones) — removes silt.
    - **Centrifuge** — final cleaning, fine solids removal.
    - **Cleaned mud** back to pump.
- **Pullback**:
  - **Final swab pass** — clean reamer pulled through, smooths wall.
  - **Pipe preparation** at exit point:
    - **Welded sections** (long string for crossing).
    - **Hydrotested + coated** beforehand.
    - **Capped end** z pull head + swivel.
  - **Swivel** — separates pipe rotation od pullback direction (allows pipe rotation under tension).
  - **Pullback speed**: 0.5-2 m/min typical, **slow** dla long crossings (>500 m).
  - **Continuous mud injection** podczas pullback — fill annulus, reduce friction.
  - **Tension monitoring** — load cell on pull rod, alarm if exceeds pipe rating.
- **Pipe protection during pullback**:
  - **Concrete-coated** dla offshore lub negative buoyancy.
  - **FBE + abrasion-resistant outer (ARO)** dla rocky soils — extra protection.
  - **Centralizers** — keep pipe centered in bore (less wall contact).
  - **Heavy-duty coating** — survive abrasion against bore wall.
- **HDD rig sizes**:
  - **Mini-HDD** (10-40k lb pullback) — utility crossings, water/gas distribution.
  - **Midi** (40-100k lb) — medium pipelines.
  - **Maxi** (100-500k lb+) — large pipelines, river crossings, long-distance.
  - **Tier sizes**: 100×100, 250×400, 500×750 (UNI nomenclature — pullback × torque).
- **Common challenges**:
  - **Cobbles / boulders** — drill rod rejection, reamer damage.
  - **Frac-out** (inadvertent mud return) — mud surfaces in field/river → environmental incident.
  - **Stuck pipe** — pull tension exceeds capacity → pipe coating damaged or string lost in bore.
  - **Bore collapse** — mud quality insufficient, fast pace.
  - **Misalignment** — exit point >2 m off design — re-bore.
- **Quality control**:
  - **As-drilled profile** logged — depth, GPS coordinates each rod.
  - **Pullback tension log** — graph load vs distance.
  - **Pre-pullback hydrotest** pipe — verify integrity.
  - **Post-installation**:
    - **Hydrotest** pipe installed at 150% design.
    - **Caliper / smart pig** dla geometry verification.
    - **Holiday detection** coating (jeeping) — verify no damage during pullback.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pipe staging area** — minimum 1.2× crossing length (room for welded string).
  - **Centerline survey** entry + exit + every 10-20 m intermediate.
  - **Mud disposal plan** — large volumes (1000-10,000 m³ dla long bore).
  - **Frac-out monitoring** — visual + GPR (ground penetrating radar) if sensitive areas.
  - **Permit requirements** — environmental, crossing-specific (river → environmental, road → DOT).
  - **Daily reports**: rod count, bore depth, mud parameters, tension readings.
  - **Standby crew** dla emergency response (stuck rod, frac-out).

---

## Iteration 80 — 2026-05-26

### GGGGGGGGGG. Plant Air / Compressed Air System — Compressor, Dryer, Filter Layout
- **Po co**: industrial plants potrzebują **clean dry compressed air** dla: pneumatic actuators (control valves), instrument signals (zanim ISA wired), spray painting, pneumatic tools (impact wrench, drills), product transfer (pneumatic conveying), purging.
- **Typy systemów**:
  - **Plant air (PA)** — utility air, lower quality, dla tools, cleaning, drying. **Dew point** 5-15°C ok.
  - **Instrument air (IA)** — wyższa jakość, **dew point ≤ -40°C**, no oil, no particles >0.01 µm. Krytyczny dla control valves + instrumentation.
  - **Process air** — dla product (food, pharma), wymaga **medical-grade** quality + filtration + sterility.
- **System overview** (typowy IA system):
  - **Air intake** → **compressor** → **aftercooler** → **moisture separator** → **pre-filter** (coalescing) → **dryer** → **after-filter** → **receiver tank** → **distribution piping** → **end users**.
- **Compressor types**:
  - **Reciprocating (piston)** — small capacity (<100 cfm), oil-lubricated typically.
  - **Rotary screw** — medium-large (50-3000+ cfm), continuous duty, oil-flooded lub oil-free.
  - **Centrifugal** — large (>500 cfm), high-pressure, oil-free.
  - **Vane** — small-medium, simple, oil-flooded.
  - **Selection**: instrument air zazwyczaj **oil-free rotary screw** lub **centrifugal** (zero oil contamination).
- **Discharge T**:
  - **Out of compressor**: 80-120°C (sprężanie podnosi T).
  - **After aftercooler**: ~T_ambient + 10-15°C (water lub air cooled).
  - **Cooler removes ~60-70% saturation moisture** (drops out as liquid).
  - **Moisture separator / knockout pot** drains liquid water.
- **Air dryers** — 3 typy:
  - **Refrigerated dryer**:
    - **Cools air to 2-5°C** w refrigerant evaporator → moisture condenses → drained.
    - **Pressure dew point (PDP)**: typically **+3°C** (rated).
    - **Cheap CAPEX + OPEX**, **non-cycling** vs **cycling** (energy-efficient at partial load).
    - **Aplikacje**: plant air, general industrial, **NIE outdoor cold climates** (PDP +3°C → freeze if ambient lower).
  - **Desiccant dryer**:
    - **Adsorbs moisture** na **silica gel** lub **activated alumina** lub **molecular sieve**.
    - **Twin-tower design** — one online (drying), one offline (regenerating).
    - **Regeneration**: heatless (purge z dried air, 15% loss), heated (electric/steam, 5% purge loss), heated blower (zero purge).
    - **PDP**: **-40°C** (standard), **-70°C** (premium) — bardzo suche powietrze.
    - **CAPEX 3-5× refrigerated**, **OPEX higher** (purge air loss + heating energy).
    - **Aplikacje**: instrument air, outdoor cold climates, critical processes.
  - **Membrane dryer**:
    - **Selective permeation** przez hollow-fiber membrane — H₂O permeates faster than air.
    - **Compact**, no moving parts, low maintenance.
    - **PDP**: -25 do -40°C.
    - **Small flow** (5-150 cfm), often **point-of-use** dla critical instruments.
- **Filters** — multistage:
  - **Pre-filter (particulate)**: 1-5 µm filter, protects dryer od dust + bulk water.
  - **Coalescing filter (pre-dryer)**: 0.01-1 µm, removes **water aerosols** + oil droplets. **Forces fine droplets to combine** + drain. Wymiana co 6-12 m-cy.
  - **Coalescing filter (post-dryer)**: po dryer, secondary cleanup, 0.01 µm.
  - **Activated carbon filter** (opcjonalnie): removes **oil vapor** (NIE liquid). Critical dla breathing air, food/pharma.
  - **Sterile filter** (food/pharma): 0.01 µm absolute, biological barrier.
- **Receiver tank**:
  - **Cel**: dampens pressure fluctuations, provides demand buffer (short-term high flow), settles out moisture (additional cooling), reduces compressor cycling.
  - **Volume**: typically 1-3× compressor cfm = receiver gallons (np. 100 cfm compressor → 100-300 gal receiver).
  - **Drains**: timer-operated lub electronic level-sensor drain at bottom — removes accumulated water.
  - **Multiple receivers** dla redundancy + zonal storage.
- **Piping**:
  - **Material**: galvanized CS (typical), aluminium (Transair, Atlas Copco) lub copper (small clean), SS 316 (food/pharma, instrument air).
  - **Sloping** 1:100 toward drain — collect condensate (zwłaszcza dla long horizontal runs).
  - **Drip legs** at low points + drains.
  - **Branch takeoff od TOP of main** — moisture stays in main, branches feed dry air.
  - **NO threaded joints** dla SS sanitary; threaded acceptable dla CS plant air <10 bar.
- **Pressure drops**:
  - **Compressor outlet**: 7-10 bar typowo (100-150 psig).
  - **Through dryer**: 0.1-0.3 bar.
  - **Through filters**: 0.2-0.5 bar (each, fresh element).
  - **End-of-line target**: 6-7 bar @ point of use.
  - **1 psi ↑ pressure = ~0.5% energy** — keep pressure as low as feasible.
- **Common errors**:
  - **No moisture separator before dryer** — water slugs into dryer → desiccant ruined / refrigerant frozen.
  - **Wrong dryer for ambient T** — refrigerated PDP +3°C, outdoor winter -10°C → freeze in pipe.
  - **Filters undersized** — high ΔP, energy waste, eventual fail.
  - **Wrong filter element compatibility** with compressor lubricant — element dissolves.
  - **Receiver too small** — compressor short-cycles, wear.
  - **Brak slope toward drain** — water pools, corrosion, sudden slug to control valves.
  - **Dryer bypassed during outage** — wet air to instruments → control valve failures.
- **Sizing rules**:
  - **Compressor**: peak demand × 1.25 safety factor, account dla leak rate (5-10% typical).
  - **Dryer**: rated capacity at **expected inlet conditions** (PSIG + inlet T) — NIE nominal-only.
  - **Receiver**: gallons = 1× cfm (continuous duty) lub 3× cfm (cyclic loads).
  - **Distribution piping** velocity ≤ **6 m/s** dla pressure stability, ≤ **9 m/s** dla mains.
- **Maintenance**:
  - **Compressor**: oil change per OEM (200-500 hours), filter intake quarterly.
  - **Dryer**:
    - Refrigerated: refrigerant level, condenser cleaning annually.
    - Desiccant: replace desiccant every 3-5 years (lifetime degrades, dust generation).
  - **Filters**: ΔP gauge monitoring → replace when 0.5 bar drop.
  - **Drains**: weekly check operation, manual drain backup.
  - **Audit annually**: ultrasonic leak detection (15-30% air loss typical, savings $$).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Hot work permit** for hot tap on plant air — ostrożnie z spark + flammable lubricant residue.
  - **Bypass piping** dla dryer maintenance (continuous service capability).
  - **Pressure gauges** at every major component — diagnostic visibility.
  - **Drain piping** to scrubber lub oil-water separator — environmental compliance (wash water).
  - **Outdoor lines insulated + heat-traced** if PDP could be exceeded.
  - **Color code**: light blue ASME A13.1 dla compressed air; safety yellow dla instrument air variant.

### HHHHHHHHHH. Restriction Orifice (RO) — Single + Multi-stage Plates
- **Co to**: **fixed orifice plate** w pipeline ze stałym otworem, **reduces pressure** lub **limits flow** bez moving parts. Tańsza alternatywa do control valve dla fixed-rate applications.
- **Vs orifice flow meter**:
  - **Flow meter orifice** — measure flow (ΔP across measured + correlated).
  - **Restriction orifice (RO)** — control flow / pressure (utilizes ΔP intentionally).
  - **Similar geometry**, ale RO sized dla maximum ΔP at design flow, flow meter dla minimum ΔP + linearity.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Pump minimum flow recirculation** — limits flow do bypass dla pump protection.
  - **Vent line restrictor** — prevents over-velocity.
  - **Drain line** — controlled drainage rate.
  - **Pressure reducer** — fixed-ratio pressure drop dla critical flow conditions.
  - **Flare lines** — limits flow do flare tip capacity.
  - **Cooling water flow balancing** — multiple parallel users.
- **Single-stage RO**:
  - **Concentric** — round hole na centerline. Standard dla liquid + gas (mid-pressure drop).
  - **Eccentric** — hole offset (typically bottom dla wet gas, top dla slurry — prevent solids accumulation/bubble trap).
  - **Segmental** — half-moon orifice opening — dla slurries.
- **Sizing — flow + ΔP**:
  - **Beta ratio (β)**: β = d (orifice ID) / D (pipe ID). **Typical β = 0.3-0.7**.
  - **Niższy β** = mniejszy otwór = wyższy ΔP, wyższe noise + cavitation risk.
  - **Wyższy β** = większy otwór = niższy ΔP, mniej restriction.
  - **Equation**: Q = Cd × A × √(2·ΔP/ρ) — discharge coefficient × area × √(pressure drop term).
- **Critical flow (choked flow)** dla gas/vapor:
  - **P₂/P₁ < critical pressure ratio (~0.528 dla air)** → flow choked at orifice.
  - **Mass flow nie wzrasta** z further P₁/P₂ decrease.
  - **Velocity = sonic velocity** at vena contracta.
  - **Noise generation** maksymalne (high-frequency, AIV risk).
- **Multi-stage RO** — dla **high ΔP** (e.g., 50-100 bar drop):
  - **Single-stage** może powodować:
    - **Cavitation** (liquid): pressure at vena contracta < vapor pressure → bubbles → collapse → erosion + noise.
    - **Sonic / choked flow** (gas): velocity Mach 1 at orifice → noise (130-150 dB) + AIV risk.
  - **Multi-stage**: 2-5 plates w serii, **step-wise pressure drop**.
  - **Stages spaced D (one pipe ID) apart** — allow flow reattachment + pressure recovery between stages.
  - **Eccentric orientation** — alternating up/down between stages, **avoid jet alignment** (zone of jet impingement on next plate damaged).
- **Multi-hole RO** (within single plate):
  - **Multiple smaller holes** instead of single large.
  - **Total area = single hole area**, ale **distributed**.
  - **Reduces noise** — smaller jets, **higher frequency** (atenuated faster w pipe wall).
  - **Reduces vena contracta cavitation** — multiple smaller jets, less localized.
- **Materials**:
  - **Plate**: SS 304/316 standard, 6-12 mm thickness.
  - **Hastelloy / Inconel** dla aggressive medium.
  - **Tungsten carbide / Stellite-faced** dla erosion service (slurries, high velocity).
- **Plate thickness sizing**:
  - **Minimum**: pressure design (ASME B16.5 dla pressure class).
  - **Wedge taper** downstream side (45° typical) — reduces re-attachment + pressure recovery losses.
- **Installation**:
  - **Between flanges** — wsuwany jak gasket, gripped by flanges.
  - **Tab visible** — extends past flanges, **stamped with**:
    - Service tag (e.g., "RO-1234").
    - Bore diameter.
    - Material.
    - Class rating.
    - Flow direction arrow.
  - **Upstream straight run**: minimum 10× pipe diameter (more dla flow meter accuracy, less dla RO).
  - **Downstream straight run**: 5× pipe diameter.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong orientation** — flow direction reversed → wrong sizing, cavitation.
  - **Tab in wrong location** — flange spreader force concentrated.
  - **Wrong material** — erosion w months z slurry service.
  - **Single-stage dla high ΔP** — cavitation + noise + AIV.
  - **No allowance for fouling** — orifice plugs partially (scale, particulates) → ΔP wzrasta → cracking pressure exceeded.
  - **Brak inspection ports** — can't verify wear without flange remove.
- **Noise mitigation**:
  - **Multi-stage** + **multi-hole** — reduces sound power level.
  - **Acoustic lagging** downstream — viscoelastic damping wrap.
  - **Silencer / muffler** — w gas service, downstream of RO.
  - **Pipe schedule increase** downstream — dilute velocity, lower Re.
- **NDT post-install**:
  - **Visual** — bore measurement (caliper) periodically (annual dla slurry, 5-year dla clean service).
  - **Hydrotest** assembled flanges — verify gasket seal (no leak around tab).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Always document RO position** w as-built — easy to miss in walkdown (looks like normal gasket).
  - **Pressure gauge upstream + downstream** — verify ΔP matches design.
  - **Multi-stage spacing** EXACT (one D) — closer = less recovery, further = pressure drop incomplete before next stage.
  - **Vibration monitoring** at high-velocity outlets — early AIV indicator.
  - **Spare plates inventory** — replacement same size + material + ID for emergency.

### IIIIIIIIII. Hydrogen Service Piping — HTHA, Nelson Curves, API RP 941
- **Hydrogen damage mechanisms** (multiple):
  1. **HTHA (High-Temperature Hydrogen Attack)** — @T >204°C + P_H₂ — **molecular H₂ dissociates → atomic H penetrates steel → reacts with carbon/carbides → methane (CH₄)** → trapped, decarburizes + fissures + crack.
  2. **Hydrogen embrittlement (HE)** — @low T, **atomic H absorbed** → reduces ductility → cracking under stress. Affects high-strength steels (yield >550 MPa).
  3. **Sulfide stress cracking (SSC)** — H₂S environments, anodic H formation → embrittlement.
  4. **Hydrogen-Induced Cracking (HIC)** — wet H₂S, H trapped at inclusions → blistering + cracks.
  5. **Stepwise cracking (SWC)** — multiple parallel HIC cracks linking → through-wall.
- **API RP 941** — "Steels for Hydrogen Service at Elevated Temperatures and Pressures":
  - **Nelson curves** — graphs of **T vs P_H₂** dla each steel alloy.
  - **Below curve** = safe.
  - **Above curve** = HTHA likely → use higher alloy.
  - **Curve elements**:
    - X-axis: H₂ partial pressure (bar / psia).
    - Y-axis: temperature (°C / °F).
    - Family of curves: CS, 1.0Cr-0.5Mo, 1.25Cr-0.5Mo, 2.25Cr-1Mo (P22), 3Cr-1Mo, 5Cr-0.5Mo, 9Cr-1Mo (P9), 304/321 SS, etc.
- **Material progression z hydrogen partial pressure**:
  - **Carbon steel** — do ~200°C + low P_H₂ (depending).
  - **C-0.5Mo** — slight improvement (depreciated — many failures historically).
  - **1.25Cr-0.5Mo (P11)** — bigger envelope.
  - **2.25Cr-1Mo (P22)** — standard for hot hydroprocessing.
  - **2.25Cr-1Mo-0.25V** (mod) — enhanced HTHA resistance.
  - **9Cr-1Mo (P9 / P91)** — high T, but creep more relevant.
  - **300 series SS** — fully resistant HTHA at typical refinery T.
- **2016 API 941 update** (important!):
  - **C-0.5Mo curve REMOVED** — multiple failures showed inadequate resistance.
  - **Non-PWHT CS new curve** added — **50°F lower** than PWHT'd CS curve.
  - **Implication**: many older units operating in "now unsafe" zone → reassessment needed.
  - **Tighter inspection requirements** dla HTHA-susceptible equipment.
- **API RP 941 limits**:
  - **Above 204°C (400°F)** — HTHA potential, follow Nelson curves.
  - **Below 204°C** — HE concern (different mechanism), separate guidelines.
- **Aplikacje refinery**:
  - **Hydrotreater** — diesel, gasoline desulfurization (H₂ + S → H₂S).
  - **Hydrocracker** — heavy oils cracking (>100 bar H₂, >400°C).
  - **Catalytic reformer** — naphtha → gasoline + H₂.
  - **Hydrogen plants (steam methane reformers)** — produce H₂.
  - **Ammonia plants** — N₂ + H₂ → NH₃.
- **Welding considerations**:
  - **Low-H electrodes** mandatory (E7018-H4 or H8) — reduces H₂ pickup w weld.
  - **PWHT requirements**:
    - **CS**: PWHT @ 595-650°C × 1 h/in, especially high-restraint joints.
    - **Cr-Mo alloys**: PWHT @ 690-720°C × 1 h/in.
    - **PWHT removes residual H₂** + relieves residual stress (HIC primary defense).
  - **Hardness limits HV10**:
    - **HIC service** (wet H₂S): max **HV10 = 248**.
    - **HTHA**: hardness not primary concern (stress + T + P_H₂ dominate).
  - **NACE MR0175** dla sour service welding qualifications.
- **Inspection program** (HTHA-susceptible equipment):
  - **API RP 941 + API RP 941 Annex** — recommended NDT methods.
  - **Visual + WFMT** dla cracks.
  - **AUT (Automated UT) ToFD** lub **phased array** dla fissures (HTHA signature).
  - **Replica metallography** (in-situ) — verify microstructure, fissure presence.
  - **In-situ hardness** — fissures lower hardness w decarburized zone.
  - **Frequency**: based on operating envelope vs Nelson curve margin.
- **High-pressure hydrogen specific**:
  - **Pipe size + thickness** — high P_H₂ design, ASME B31.3 + B31.12 (NEW for H₂).
  - **B31.12 introduces** factors dla H₂ embrittlement of pipe + welds.
  - **Material certifications** strict — composition, mill tests, NDT records.
  - **Threads / bolts** — high-strength bolts (>1240 MPa) prohibited — H₂ embrittlement → bolt cracking.
- **CSB findings** dla HTHA incidents:
  - Tesoro Anacortes 2010 — naphtha hydrotreater, **failed C-0.5Mo heat exchanger** killed 7 workers.
  - **Cause**: C-0.5Mo Nelson curve was inadequate (now removed from API 941).
  - **Lessons**: aggressive Nelson curve margin, proactive material upgrades, frequent inspection.
- **Material upgrade path** (when inspection findings indicate HTHA):
  - **CS → 1.25Cr-0.5Mo** (P11) — modest upgrade.
  - **CS → 2.25Cr-1Mo (P22)** — robust dla most refinery hydroprocessing.
  - **P22 → 2.25Cr-1Mo-V (P23)** — high creep + HTHA.
  - **All-alloy → 300 SS** — full HTHA immunity (ale creep limits T).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Hydrogen service procedure (HSP)** specific WPS dla każdej kombinacji material + thickness.
  - **Strict pre-weld cleanliness** — moisture, oil = H₂ pickup w weld.
  - **Baked low-H electrodes** — 230°C × 2 h immediately before use, kept warm in oven.
  - **PWHT verify** thermocouple records — gaps in heating curve = potentially incomplete relief.
  - **NDT 100%** including UT + RT for thick walls.
  - **Material identification** — PMI (positive material identification) every component, check Cr% + Mo% match spec.
  - **Hardness HV10** post-weld 100% on welds + HAZ.
  - **Hot bolting** schedule po startup (after thermal expansion) — verify bolt torque maintained.
  - **Operational monitoring**: T + P_H₂ trends → flag drift toward Nelson curve boundary.

---

## Iteration 81 — 2026-05-26

### JJJJJJJJJJ. Amine Treater (MDEA / MEA / DEA) — Gas Sweetening Piping
- **Po co**: natural gas + refinery gas streams zawierają **H₂S** i **CO₂** ("acid gases") — toxic + corrosive. Pipeline spec: **H₂S <4 ppm** (US, "sweet gas spec") + **CO₂ <2-4%**. Amine treater absorbs acid gas, returns clean gas.
- **Mechanizm chemiczny**:
  - **Amine (lean)** + H₂S → ammonium hydrosulfide salt (reversible).
  - **Amine** + CO₂ + H₂O → bicarbonate / carbamate (reversible).
  - **Heat** + **reduced pressure** = reverses reaction → strips acid gas → amine regenerated.
- **Amine selection** (różne aminy = różna selektywność + corrosion):
  - **MEA (monoethanolamine)** — primary amine, najszybsze kinetyka, **highest CO₂ + H₂S absorption**.
    - **Concentration**: 15-20 wt% max.
    - **CONS**: highest corrosion, degradation z O₂ + COS + CS₂, high regeneration energy.
  - **DEA (diethanolamine)** — secondary amine, średnio.
    - **Concentration**: 25-30 wt% max.
    - **More resistant** do degradation niż MEA, lower corrosion.
  - **MDEA (methyldiethanolamine)** — tertiary amine, **selective dla H₂S** w obecności CO₂.
    - **Concentration**: 40-50 wt% max (highest loading).
    - **Najniższy heat of reaction** (600 BTU/lb CO₂, 522 BTU/lb H₂S) = **lower regeneration energy** = ~30% OPEX savings vs MEA.
    - **NIE absorb CO₂ szybko** (slow kinetics) — used w aplikacjach gdzie CO₂ "slip" OK.
    - **Higher resistance** do degradation, lower corrosion.
  - **DGA (diglycolamine)** — used dla cold climates (DEA freezes <8°C).
  - **Specialty / activated MDEA** — MDEA + piperazine activator → fast CO₂ uptake + low energy.
- **System components**:
  - **Inlet separator** — usuwa free liquids (water, condensate) + slugs.
  - **Inlet filter coalescer** — removes solids + aerosols (protects amine od fouling).
  - **Contactor (absorber) tower**: vertical, 30-80 bar, 30-50°C.
    - **Sour gas IN bottom**, **lean amine IN top**.
    - **Internals**: structured packing (Mellapak, Sulzer BX) lub valve trays (15-25 trays).
    - **Sweet gas OUT top**, **rich amine OUT bottom**.
  - **Rich amine flash drum** — 4-7 bar, drops some absorbed hydrocarbons (recovered to fuel gas).
  - **Lean/Rich heat exchanger** — preheats rich amine, cools lean (energy recovery, **>70% duty saving**).
  - **Regenerator (stripper) tower**:
    - **Atmospheric** lub low P (1-2 bar).
    - **Steam-heated reboiler** at bottom (115-130°C).
    - **Overhead condenser** (water/air cooled) — refluxes some water.
    - **Acid gas OUT top** → to Claus sulfur recovery / flare / incineration.
    - **Lean amine OUT bottom** → cycled back.
  - **Lean amine cooler** — air/water cooler, cools to 40-50°C przed contactor (lower T = better absorption).
  - **Surge tank** — amine inventory, makeup.
  - **Booster pumps** — circulate amine.
  - **Filtration** — 10-25 µm + activated carbon (removes degradation products).
- **Piping materials**:
  - **Lean amine (clean)**: **carbon steel** (A106 Gr B) — minimal corrosion w lean side.
  - **Rich amine (acid gas-loaded)**: **carbon steel** w lower T, **304/304L SS** w higher T (>80°C).
  - **Acid gas line** (regenerator overhead → condenser): **304L SS** lub **dual-grade SS** (wet H₂S + CO₂).
  - **Sour gas inlet/sweet gas outlet**: CS standard.
  - **NEVER aluminum, brass, copper** — galvanic + amine attack.
- **Critical piping rules**:
  - **NACE MR0175** dla wet H₂S service:
    - **Hardness HV10 ≤248** dla CS + welds.
    - **PWHT all welds** w sour service piping — relieves residual stress.
    - **Low-S steel** (S ≤0.005%) — prevents HIC (hydrogen-induced cracking).
  - **Sloping toward drains**: 1:100 minimum.
  - **No dead legs** — amine degradation accelerates w stagnant zones (high T).
  - **Block valves + bleed valves** dla maintenance isolation.
- **Velocity limits**:
  - **Lean amine pipe**: 1-2 m/s (laminar, no foam entrainment).
  - **Rich amine pipe**: 0.5-1.5 m/s (lower velocity → less erosion/corrosion).
  - **Acid gas pipe**: 5-15 m/s gas velocity (avoid sonic + AIV).
- **Common corrosion locations**:
  - **Cross exchanger** (lean/rich) — **two-phase flow** od flash gas → erosion-corrosion.
  - **Reboiler tubes** — local hot spots + amine degradation products (heat-stable salts — HSS).
  - **Regenerator overhead piping** — wet acid gas, high CO₂/H₂S partial pressure.
  - **Pump suction** — vacuum + dissolved gas → cavitation.
- **Amine degradation products** (cause corrosion):
  - **Heat-stable salts** (HSS) — formed z O₂ + HCN + organic acids; nie regenerable.
  - **Common HSS**: formate, acetate, oxalate, thiocyanate.
  - **HSS levels**: <0.5% acceptable, >2% → reclaim amine (vacuum distillation, ion exchange).
- **Foaming** — major operational issue:
  - **Causes**: liquid HC carry-over, surfactants, fines, HSS, degradation products.
  - **Symptoms**: pressure spikes, off-spec sweet gas, level instability.
  - **Mitigation**: anti-foam injection (silicone-based, 5-50 ppm), pre-filtration, slug catcher upstream.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong amine** dla service — overdosing CO₂ z MDEA (too slow kinetics).
  - **No NACE compliance** — H₂S SCC w months.
  - **No PWHT** sour service welds — SSC + HIC failures.
  - **Air ingress** podczas maintenance → amine oxidation → degradation.
  - **Filter neglected** → fouling → channeling → off-spec.
  - **Reboiler temp too high** (>135°C dla MEA, >145°C dla MDEA) → thermal degradation.
- **Inspection program**:
  - **NACE TM0284 HIC test** dla new piping plate.
  - **Wet H₂S inspection**: WFMT all welds biennial.
  - **UT thickness** at known erosion locations.
  - **Amine analysis** monthly: concentration, HSS, degradation products.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **No threaded connections** — flange or weld only (leak paths in sour service).
  - **Blowdown procedures** — always N₂ purge before opening (residual H₂S).
  - **H₂S monitoring** — personal monitors mandatory.
  - **Hot work permit** — strict; many ignition sources eliminated.
  - **Sample valves** — sample point dla amine quality monitoring.
  - **Vapor space N₂ blanket** na surge tank — prevent oxidation.
  - **Pump seal flush** — Plan 32 or 53B (external flush) — prevents amine degradation product accumulation.

### KKKKKKKKKK. Underground Fire Water Main — NFPA 24 + AWWA + Hydrants
- **Po co**: private fire mains supply **fire hydrants, sprinkler systems, deluge systems, monitor towers** w industrial plants + commercial. **NFPA 24** governs design + installation in USA.
- **Pipe materials** (NFPA 24 approved):
  - **Ductile iron pipe (DI)** — **najpopularniejsze**, AWWA C151.
    - **Cement-mortar lined** (AWWA C104) — corrosion resistance.
    - **Pressure class** 150/200/250/350 psi (1.0-2.4 MPa).
    - **Size range**: DN 75-1200 (3"-48").
    - **Joints**: push-on (Tyton), mechanical, restraint joints.
  - **HDPE (high-density polyethylene)** — flexible, butt-fused, **DR 11 lub DR 13.5** dla fire service.
    - **Listed product** dla fire (UL, FM).
    - **NSF 61** certified dla potable water.
  - **PVC AWWA C900** — Class 100/150/200 psi.
    - **Restricted** w niektórych jurisdykcjach dla fire.
  - **Steel pipe** (rare) — corrosion concerns, used dla specific applications.
- **Depth of cover** (NFPA 24 §10.4.3):
  - **Minimum 2.5 ft (760 mm)** below grade.
  - **More for frost line areas** — below frost depth + 200 mm safety.
  - **Below traffic loads**: 3-4 ft depending on truck loading + soil.
- **Joint types**:
  - **Push-on (Tyton)**: gasket compresses w bell, simple install, **NOT thrust-restrained** alone.
  - **Mechanical (MJ)**: gland + bolts + gasket. **NOT thrust-restrained** unless using restraint glands (EBAA Iron Megalug, etc.).
  - **Restrained joint** (Flex-Ring, TR-Flex, Lok-Ring) — built-in restraint via locking ring. Eliminates need for thrust blocks at fittings.
  - **Flanged** — at valves, hydrant connections, above-ground transitions.
- **Thrust blocks** — KRYTYCZNE dla non-restrained joints:
  - **Po co**: pipe under pressure produces **unbalanced force** at fittings (tees, elbows, reducers, dead-ends, valves) → tendency to "blow apart" joint.
  - **Force calculation**: F = 2·P·A·sin(θ/2) dla bend, F = P·A dla dead-end.
    - P = pressure (psi), A = pipe area (in²), θ = bend angle.
    - 8" pipe @ 200 psi, 90° elbow → ~14,000 lb force.
  - **Thrust block design**:
    - **Bearing area** = F / (allowable soil bearing).
    - **Concrete block** poured against undisturbed soil, opposite direction of force.
    - **Soil bearing**: 1500 psf (sand) - 8000 psf (dense clay), depends on tests.
    - **Concrete**: 3000 psi compressive strength minimum.
  - **Hydrant tees** — always thrust block + restraint on hydrant lateral.
- **Restraint vs thrust block**:
  - **Restrained joints**: more expensive pipe/fittings, but no concrete + faster install + no settling issues.
  - **Thrust blocks**: cheap concrete but problematic w wet soils + need cure time (28 days for full strength) + can't trench parallel utilities later.
  - **Combination**: restraint joint at fittings, thrust blocks at dead-ends.
- **Hydrants**:
  - **Dry-barrel** — preferred dla frost areas; main shutoff valve below frost line, hydrant drains when closed.
  - **Wet-barrel** — warm climates; water always at hydrant nozzles.
  - **Materials**: cast iron body, bronze working parts, stainless internals.
  - **Pumper port** 4.5" (NST thread), side ports 2.5" (NST).
  - **Storz adapter** — quick connect, used in many fire departments now.
  - **Color code**:
    - Body: typically red (or per jurisdiction).
    - Bonnet/caps: per NFPA 291 color = flow rating (light blue ≥1500 gpm, green 1000-1499, orange 500-999, red <500).
- **Hydrant installation**:
  - **Branch off main** with tee (DI + restraint).
  - **Aux valve** (gate valve) on hydrant lateral, below grade w valve box — allows isolation for hydrant repair.
  - **Hydrant body** above grade, **breakaway flange** at frost level — vehicle impact breaks flange (not pipe).
  - **Distance from main**: typically 5-15 ft, depending on plot.
  - **Lateral pipe**: 6" minimum dla single hydrant.
  - **Drainage gravel** at hydrant base — drainage water (dry-barrel) doesn't pool + freeze.
- **Valves** (in fire main):
  - **Gate valves** (resilient seated) — main line + branches.
  - **OS&Y (Outside Screw & Yoke)** — for above-ground; position visible.
  - **NRS (Non-Rising Stem)** w valve boxes — below grade.
  - **Indicator post** — above-grade marker dla isolation valve location + visible position (target).
  - **Backflow preventer** — DCDA (Double Check Detector Assembly) at service connection (prevents fire water reentering potable supply).
- **Flow testing** (NFPA 25):
  - **Annual flow test** at most-remote hydrant.
  - **Measure**: static pressure (before flow), residual pressure (during flow), flow rate (pitot at hydrant).
  - **Determine available water supply** at 20 psi residual (sprinkler minimum).
- **Hydrostatic test** (acceptance):
  - **200 psi or 150% working pressure** minimum, 2 h hold.
  - **Allowable leakage**: per NFPA 24 / AWWA C600 formula (small allowance for joints).
  - **Flushing** prior to test — remove debris, scale, soil intrusion.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak thrust blocks** at dead-ends / fittings → joint failure under pressure surge.
  - **Insufficient cover** → freeze damage winter.
  - **No drainage** at dry-barrel hydrant → water freezes w barrel, hydrant inoperable.
  - **Aux valve buried bez valve box** → can't operate without excavation.
  - **Indicator post missing/blocked** → fire department can't find isolation valve in emergency.
  - **DI pipe scratched** in trench → cement lining damaged → corrosion years later.
  - **No marker tape / tracer wire** above pipe (HDPE/PVC) → future excavation hits pipe.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Plumbing tape (caution)** 0.3-0.5 m above pipe — warns future excavators.
  - **Tracer wire** dla non-metallic pipe (HDPE/PVC) — RFID locatable from surface.
  - **Bedding sand** 150 mm around pipe — protects from rock damage.
  - **Backfill in lifts** — 150 mm layers + compaction → no settlement.
  - **Pre-installation pressure test** of valve assemblies before backfill — easier to fix above-grade.
  - **Maintain isolation** during construction — never break fire protection without coverage (temporary).
  - **Post-installation training** — operators learn each valve location.

### LLLLLLLLLL. Pneumatic Conveying Piping — Dilute + Dense Phase
- **Co to**: transport **bulk solids** (powders, granules, pellets) **air-blown** w piping. Alternatywa do mechanical conveyors (belt, screw, drag). **No moving parts** w transport line, route flexibility (around corners, vertical), dust-free.
- **2 main phases**:
  - **Dilute phase** — high velocity, low pressure, solid:gas ratio LOW.
    - **Velocity**: **15-30 m/s** (700-1000 m/min, 2400-3200 fpm).
    - **Pressure**: 0.1-1.5 bar gauge (15 psig max typically).
    - **Loading ratio**: 1-15 kg solid / kg air (low).
    - **Particles suspended in air**, **turbulent flow**.
    - **Long distance** (do 500 m).
    - **Aplikacje**: light powders, food, plastic pellets na konwencjonalnym Distance.
  - **Dense phase** — low velocity, high pressure, solid:gas ratio HIGH.
    - **Velocity**: **0.5-5 m/s** (100-1000 fpm).
    - **Pressure**: **1-3.5 bar gauge** (15-50 psig).
    - **Loading ratio**: 15-100+ kg/kg (very high).
    - **Material moves as slug / plug** or **slow-moving bed**.
    - **Less wear, less degradation, less energy/ton**.
    - **Aplikacje**: cement, fly ash, abrasive material, fragile particles (plastic pellets at quality requirement).
- **Conveying modes** (sub-types):
  - **Pressure system**: pressure source (blower/compressor) pushes material od pickup → destination.
  - **Vacuum system**: vacuum source pulls material od pickup → receiver.
  - **Combination (push-pull)**: vacuum pickup + pressure transport (long systems).
- **Pipe materials**:
  - **Carbon steel** — najpopularniejsze, low cost.
  - **Stainless steel** — food/pharma, mild abrasive (sugar, salt).
  - **Hardened steel** (AR400, Hardox) — abrasive (fly ash, sand, cement).
  - **Ceramic-lined** (alumina tiles) — very abrasive, long-life w bends.
  - **HDPE** — mild non-abrasive (plastic pellets), small cost.
  - **Glass-lined** — fragile particles, prevent contamination (pharma).
- **Pipe sizing**:
  - **Dilute phase**: typically **DN 50-200**, larger as distance + capacity grow.
  - **Dense phase**: **DN 25-150** typowo (smaller — high pressure, plug flow).
  - **Velocity calculation**: v = Q_air / A_pipe.
- **Bends — main wear location**:
  - **Long-radius (LR) elbows**: R/D = 10-20, lower wear vs SR.
  - **Short-radius (SR)**: cheap ale szybciej zużywa się.
  - **Blind-T elbows** (Pocket-T) — material packs w blind end, **further material slides on cushion of itself** — minimal wear, w abrasive service preferowane.
  - **Hammerhead / vortex elbows** — patented designs minimizing impact angle.
  - **Replacement zones**: bends, valves, diverters — 80% of wear.
- **Wear rates** (typical, dla cement / fly ash dilute):
  - **CS bend**: 100-1000 g material per mm wear (czas ~6-24 m-cy).
  - **Hardened bend**: 5-10× longer life.
  - **Ceramic-lined**: 50-100× longer life vs CS.
- **Critical velocity**:
  - **Minimum conveying velocity** dla material — below = saltation (material drops out, plugs).
  - **Saltation velocity** dla typical materials:
    - **Cement / fly ash (fines)**: 8-12 m/s minimum dilute.
    - **Plastic pellets**: 18-22 m/s dilute.
    - **Sand**: 25-30 m/s dilute.
  - **Pickup velocity** typically 1.5× saltation velocity (safety margin).
- **System components**:
  - **Material feeder**: rotary airlock valve (RAV), screw feeder, eductor.
  - **Conveying air**: compressor (oil-free dla food) lub blower (Roots, 1-3 bar).
  - **Transport piping**: described above.
  - **Bends + diverters**: route material between destinations.
  - **Receiver / cyclone separator**: separates material from air at destination.
  - **Bag filter** / **HEPA filter**: cleans exhaust air before vent.
  - **Pressure relief**: dla blocked line scenario (vacuum / pressure release).
- **Hazards**:
  - **Dust explosion** (combustible dust):
    - **MEC (Minimum Explosive Concentration)**: 30-100 g/m³ dla typical combustible powders.
    - **Coal, flour, sugar, wood, plastic** — wszystkie potential.
    - **Ignition source**: static electricity, hot surface, spark od metal-on-metal.
    - **NFPA 654** dla combustible dust hazards.
    - **Bonding + grounding**: ground all pipe, components.
    - **Explosion vents** + **suppression** dla high-risk applications.
  - **Static charge**: pipe + material build up charge — grounding mandatory.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Velocity za niska** → plug / saltation. Restart difficult.
  - **Velocity za wysoka** → particle degradation (plastic pellets break, snake-skin formation), excessive wear.
  - **SR bends w abrasive** → quick wear-through.
  - **Brak grounding** → static buildup → ignition + explosion.
  - **Wet material w dense phase** → cement-like plug.
  - **Brak filter maintenance** → blocked → pressure builds → system stop.
  - **Rotary airlock leakage** — air leak through valve clearances → pressure loss + dust release.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Wear indicator**: ultrasonic thickness gauges at bends — monitor + replace before failure.
  - **Inspection hatches** at strategic bends.
  - **Diverter valves**: routing material między destinations — bullet-type, slide gate.
  - **Slugging/pulsation control**: dense phase — pressure cycles, design supports + pipe accordingly.
  - **Sample valves** at receiver — verify material quality, contamination check.
  - **Long-radius preferred** dla all bends — lower wear + lower pressure drop.
  - **Vertical risers**: plug-flow tendency, smaller diameter to maintain velocity vs horizontal section.

---

## Iteration 82 — 2026-05-26

### MMMMMMMMMM. API 650 Storage Tank Nozzles + Manways + Reinforcing Pads
- **Aplikacja**: **API 650** standard dla **welded atmospheric storage tanks** (above ground, vertical, cylindrical) dla petroleum + chemical industry. Tanks od DN 1.5 m do >100 m diameter, **atmospheric to 18 kPa internal pressure**. Crude, products, water, chemicals.
- **Tank construction**:
  - **Bottom plate** — flat lub cone-down, 6-12 mm CS.
  - **Shell** — cylindrical, multiple courses (rings) o wzrastającej thickness ku dnu (hydrostatic pressure).
  - **Roof** — fixed (cone, dome, flat) lub floating (external lub internal pontoon).
  - **Annular ring** — wzmocnione plate pod nuts shell ku bottom.
- **Nozzles na shell**:
  - **Inlet / outlet** — product transfer, typowo near bottom.
  - **Mixer / agitator** — side entry, mid-elevation.
  - **Drain / sump** — lowest point bottom.
  - **Vent** — top, breathing.
  - **Overflow** — emergency vent, top.
  - **Sample / instrument** — temperature, level, sample valve.
  - **Heating coil** — internal coil w large tanks, external coil for smaller.
  - **Manway** — personnel access (24" minimum), shell or roof.
- **Nozzle specifications** (API 650 §5.7):
  - **Standard sizes** od ¾" do 24" (some up to 48" dla special).
  - **Standard "stand-out"** dystans od shell:
    - **Small nozzle** (≤4"): 100 mm od shell.
    - **Large nozzle** (6-24"): 150-300 mm.
  - **Standard flanged**: ANSI 150 typowo (low pressure tank).
  - **Higher class** (300+): only dla high-stress nozzles.
- **Reinforcing pad** — krytyczne dla nozzle integrity:
  - **Po co**: nozzle opening creates **stress concentration** w shell + reduces shell strength locally. **Pad redystrybuuje stress** + dodaje material w obszarze opening.
  - **Wymóg API 650 §5.7.2**: reinforcement area ≥ removed shell area (A_removed = d × t_shell, where d = opening diameter, t = shell thickness).
- **Reinforcement methods**:
  1. **Excess shell thickness** — jeśli shell wystarczająco gruby (powyżej design min) → reinforcement built-in, no pad needed.
  2. **Reinforcing pad** (zewnętrzne RF pad) — most common, plate wokół nozzle welded to shell.
  3. **Heavier nozzle neck** — thicker pipe wall provides reinforcement (≥70% yield + ≥80% tensile of shell plate).
  4. **Combination** — pad + heavier neck.
- **Pad shapes**:
  - **Round pad** — najprostsze, low-stress nozzles.
  - **Tombstone / rathole** — owalne lub D-shaped, dla **low-type** nozzles blisko bottom (avoid bottom plate intersection).
  - **Diamond / rectangular** — high-stress, distributes load preferentially.
- **Pad sizing**:
  - **Outer diameter**: typically **2× nozzle ID** (covers stress field).
  - **Thickness**: equal to shell course thickness (typically).
  - **Limits**: pad nie może być wider than 2.5× nozzle ID, ani thicker than 2.5× shell thickness.
- **Welds dla nozzle installation**:
  - **Inner fillet weld** — nozzle to shell (or pad).
  - **Outer fillet weld** — pad to shell.
  - **NDT**:
    - **MT/PT** all welds.
    - **RT lub UT** dla nozzles > 6" (API 650 §8).
    - **Vacuum box test** dla bottom-plate welds.
- **Test ports / telltale holes**:
  - **Small drilled hole** (¼" NPT z pipe plug) na pad → vent for **soap test** + reveal leakage from inside-shell weld.
  - Critical dla service detection — leak between pad + shell wykrywa się przez telltale.
- **Manway design**:
