  - **Standard 24" diameter** (minimum dla personnel entry).
  - **Bolted cover** z stiffener pad — pressurized lub vacuum during cleaning.
  - **Davits** dla cover lifting — heavy (200+ kg dla large manway).
  - **Manway gasket**: typically Klingerit / aramid / graphite, replaceable every entry.
  - **Bottom manway** — shell course 1, opposite drain (avoid sludge accumulation at manway).
  - **Roof manway** — for top entry, vacuum tank breathing.
- **Nozzle orientation**:
  - **Side entry mixer**: orient blade vertical, **outside dike** position dla removable bundle.
  - **Inlet nozzle** — typically with **diffuser pipe** internal (slows velocity, prevents bottom sediment disturbance + emulsification).
  - **Outlet nozzle** — with **vortex breaker** (cross plates 100 mm above nozzle CL) preventing vortex formation @ pump suction.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Pad too small** — insufficient reinforcement → cracking under thermal cycles.
  - **Skip telltale hole** — pad-to-shell leak nieoperator-detectable.
  - **No internal projection** check — nozzle may need internal pipe extension dla downstream installations.
  - **Mixer orientation wrong** — bundle can't be pulled without dike removal.
  - **Outlet bez vortex breaker** — vortex, pump cavitation.
  - **Bottom plate weld interference** — low nozzle pad overlaps bottom plate → cracking od differential thermal expansion.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-fab nozzle** w shop — bevel pipe, fit pad, tack tig prep, then ship to site.
  - **Mark nozzle locations** clearly on shell before cutting — orientation, elevation, projection.
  - **Heat-treat sensitive** nozzles z PWHT — vessel-grade nozzles po API 650 Annex M.
  - **Settlement allowance** — tank settles 100-300 mm during fill + service; nozzle elevations design for that.
  - **Anchor bolts** dla high winds / seismic (API 650 Annex E).
  - **Cathodic protection** dla bottom (sacrificial anodes or impressed current) — bottom plate corrodes against soil.

### NNNNNNNNNN. Subsea Pipeline Lay — S-Lay, J-Lay, Reel-Lay
- **Po co subsea pipelines**: oil + gas transport offshore (platform-to-platform, field-to-shore), submarine power cables, water/sewage outfalls. Length kilometers do hundreds km. Depths 10 m (shore) do **3500+ m** (ultra-deepwater).
- **3 main installation methods**:
  - **S-Lay** — shallow/intermediate water, horizontal weld station.
  - **J-Lay** — deepwater, vertical weld station.
  - **Reel-Lay** — pre-fabricated long string spooled, fast install.
- **S-Lay method** — najpopularniejsze dla shallow + medium depth:
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Lay vessel** (barge) z **firing line** (linia weld + NDT stations) HORIZONTAL na pokładzie.
    - **Pipe joints** (12 m sections) welded sequentially → continuous string exits stern.
    - **Stinger** (steel framework z rolls) **at stern** wykrzywia rurociąg w **overbend** ku dnu.
    - **Tensioners** (caterpillar-track grippers) trzymają pipe z określoną siłą, kontrolują **sagbend**.
  - **Geometry**: pipe assumes **S-shape** between vessel + seabed:
    - **Overbend**: top curvature kontrolowana przez stinger (large radius).
    - **Sagbend**: bottom curvature ku touchdown.
    - **Touchdown point**: gdzie pipe meets seabed.
  - **Depth range**: efficient do **600-800 m** (limit z stinger length + tension capacity).
  - **Production rate**: 3-5 km/day typical (8-10 joints/h × 12 m = 120 m/h sustained).
  - **Major operators**: Saipem (Castorone, Castoro Sei), Allseas (Solitaire), Subsea 7.
- **J-Lay method** — deepwater:
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Vertical tower** na pokładzie barge (40-100 m wysokości).
    - **Single weld station** w tower — joint welded vertically z above.
    - **Pipe descends straight down** — large-radius bend tylko at touchdown.
  - **Geometry**: **J-shape** — straight section vertical, small curvature at seabed.
  - **Advantages**:
    - **Lower tension required** — pipe weight supports itself (catenary).
    - **No overbend** — no stinger needed.
    - **Deepwater capable** — 1000-3500 m+.
    - **Better dla heavy-wall + insulated pipe**.
  - **Disadvantages**:
    - **Slower** — single weld station vs multiple in S-lay (1-2 joints/h).
    - **Limited dla concrete-coated pipe** (verticals stations limit handling).
  - **Examples**: Saipem 7000 (J-lay capable), Heerema Aegir.
- **Reel-Lay method** — fast + efficient dla deep:
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Onshore spool-base** — pipe sections welded into very long strings (km), NDT, hydrotest.
    - **Spooled onto reel** (8-20 m diameter), reel mounted on barge.
    - **Pipe unspools** during installation, **straightened** w straightener.
    - **Tensioner + chute** dla S-lay or J-lay configuration.
  - **Reel orientation**:
    - **Horizontal reel** → S-lay configuration.
    - **Vertical reel** → J-lay (or S-lay).
  - **Advantages**:
    - **Najszybsze** — no welding on barge, just unspool (10-15 km/day possible).
    - **Quality control** at onshore base (better than offshore conditions).
    - **No weather sensitivity** dla welding.
  - **Disadvantages**:
    - **Pipe size limited** — typical max **18" OD, 30 mm wall** (bending strain limit on reel).
    - **Plastic deformation** of pipe — pipe yields when spooled, then unyielded when straightened (cyclic load history).
    - **Coating compatibility** — flexible coatings only (3LPE/3LPP, but not concrete).
    - **Specialty vessels** rzadkie (Subsea 7 Seven Oceans, Technip CSO Apache).
- **Common features all methods**:
  - **Davit lifting** dla buoys + accessories (anchors, anodes).
  - **ROV operations** — Remotely Operated Vehicle dla inspection touchdown + repairs.
  - **DP (Dynamic Positioning)** — thrusters keep vessel on track without anchors (deepwater).
  - **Bend stiffeners** at touchdown — prevent over-bending at point where pipe contacts seabed.
- **Welding station** specifics:
  - **Multiple stations** w S-lay (4-8 stations sequential): root, hot pass, fill, cap, NDT, FJC.
  - **Automated welding** (Pipeline Welding Tractors, RMS Automatic) — GMAW, FCAW, multi-torch.
  - **Speed**: 5-15 min per joint (vs 30-60 min manual).
  - **NDT inline**: AUT (automated UT) — real-time, between weld passes.
  - **FJC station**: field joint coating — heat shrink sleeves applied + cured.
- **Pre-installation activities**:
  - **Route survey** — multibeam bathymetry, magnetometer (UXO), side-scan sonar.
  - **Geotechnical** — soil cores along route.
  - **Pre-lay trenching / dredging** — w shallow areas dla burial.
  - **Pre-lay anchors** — mooring posts dla S-lay (in shallow w/o DP).
- **Post-installation**:
  - **As-laid survey** — exact pipe location, GPS + ROV.
  - **Anchor / weighting** — concrete mattresses, rock dump.
  - **Tie-ins** — connect pipeline ends to platform riser, well manifolds.
  - **Hydrotest** — dewater, pressure test.
  - **Pre-commissioning**: dewater, dry, gauge pig.
- **Common challenges**:
  - **Weather windows** — dla S-lay limited to calm sea (Hs <3 m).
  - **Buckle / kink** — over-bending → permanent deformation, repair section cut out.
  - **Lost pipe** — emergency abandonment + recovery (A&R) procedure.
  - **Trawl gear interaction** — fishermen catch nets → pipe damage.
  - **Free spans** — pipe over depression → vortex shedding → fatigue.
  - **Touchdown soils** — soft mud → pipe sinks; rock → not contact ground enough → free span.
- **Practical fitter rules** (offshore-specific):
  - **24/7 operation** — shift work, accelerated production rates.
  - **NDT 100%** — every weld AUT or RT, no exceptions.
  - **Spare parts pre-stocked** — anchor handlers, buoys, anodes pre-installed at field.
  - **Crew specialization** — welder, NDT operator, ROV pilot, navigator — distinct roles.
  - **HSE strict** — offshore environment + heavy equipment + sea state hazards.

### OOOOOOOOOO. ASME A13.1 Pipe Identification + Labelling — Color Code, Direction Arrow
- **Po co**: piping w plant zawiera **różne medium** (steam, water, gas, chemicals) — operator + emergency responders **muszą szybko identyfikować** zawartość rurociągu. Pomyłka → wrong valve → release / explosion / injury.
- **ASME A13.1 (USA)** najnowsze wydanie **2026** lub **2023**. Equivalents internationally:
  - **ISO 14726** — international (uses substance-specific colors).
  - **BS 1710** — UK.
  - **DIN 2403** — Germany.
- **5 elements każdej label** (per A13.1):
  1. **Legend** — substance name (e.g., "STEAM 250 PSI", "ACID CONC.").
  2. **Color background** — hazard category.
  3. **Size** — letter height + label length per pipe OD.
  4. **Directional arrow** — flow direction.
  5. **Placement** — where label is mounted.
- **Color coding** (ASME A13.1 — hazard-based, NOT substance-based):
  - **Flammable / oxidizing liquid + gas** — **white text on brown** (Pantone 4515).
  - **Combustible liquids / gases** — **white text on yellow** (Pantone 109) — alternate dla some flammables.
  - **Oxidizing fluids** — **white text on yellow** (Pantone 109).
  - **Toxic / corrosive** — **white text on orange** (Pantone 152).
  - **Fire-quenching (fire water)** — **white text on red** (Pantone 186).
  - **Compressed gas (other than air)** — **white text on blue** (Pantone 285).
  - **Combustible / non-hazardous liquids (cooling water)** — **white text on green** (Pantone 348).
  - **Compressed air** — **white text on blue** (typical, or sometimes own coding).
  - **Vacuum** — **black text on white** (some plants).
- **Letter height + label length** (per pipe OD):
  - **¾" - 1¼" OD pipe**: letter height **½"**, label length **8"**.
  - **1½" - 2" OD**: ¾" letters, 8" length.
  - **2½" - 6" OD**: 1¼" letters, 12" length.
  - **8" - 10" OD**: 2½" letters, 24" length.
  - **>10" OD**: 3½" letters, 32" length.
  - **Insulated pipe**: use OD over insulation (jacket) for sizing.
- **Direction arrow** — wymagana z każdą legend:
  - **Single arrow** — unidirectional flow.
  - **Double arrow** (both directions) — bidirectional flow.
  - **Arrow extends beyond legend** to clearly show direction.
- **Placement rules** (where to install):
  - **Both sides** of wall, floor, ceiling penetrations.
  - **At all valves** (both sides if accessible from two directions).
  - **At branch connections + tees** — each branch leg labeled.
  - **Near equipment connections** (pumps, exchangers, vessels, tanks).
  - **At direction changes** (elbows, bends, offsets).
  - **Straight runs**: every **25 ft (7.6 m)** intervals.
  - **Visible from normal operator approach** — face toward walkway / access route.
- **Label materials**:
  - **Self-adhesive vinyl** — najtaniej, dobry dla indoor.
  - **Aluminum tag** — outdoor, harsh environment.
  - **Stencil + paint** — direct na pipe (less common, hard to maintain).
  - **Heat-shrink wrap** — pipe-wrapping label, durable.
  - **UV-resistant** dla outdoor — fade w 1-2 lata bez UV protection.
- **Wrap-around vs strip labels**:
  - **Strip** label (rectangular) — most common, single side visibility.
  - **Wrap-around** (band around pipe) — multi-angle visibility, but może zakrywać identification od jednej strony.
- **Combination labeling**:
  - **Legend + color band** — both required, color band wystaja poza legend.
  - **Hazard pictogram** opcjonalnie — added clarity (e.g., flame = flammable).
  - **Process detail** — temperature, pressure, line number — added if relevant.
- **Line numbering**:
  - **Plant-specific** scheme (np. "12-PA-1234-CS-150" = 12" Plant Air line 1234 Carbon Steel ANSI 150).
  - **Includes**: size, service, line number, material, rating.
  - **Critical**: line number on label allows operator to find drawings.
- **Valve identification**:
  - **Plate tag** on valve handle (metal stamped, plastic engraved).
  - **Includes**: valve tag (e.g., "HV-1234"), service ("FW SUPPLY"), position (CSO/CSC/LO).
  - **Color match** to pipe color code.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Faded labels** w outdoor — UV degradation, replace co 3-5 lat.
  - **Wrong color** for substance — operator confusion → wrong valve → incident.
  - **No direction arrow** — operator nie wie flow direction → wrong isolation.
  - **Labels removed during maintenance** + nie przywrócone — pipe identification lost.
  - **Cross-language plants** — labels in only English w international operations → emergency confusion.
  - **Cover labels with insulation** — invisible to operator.
  - **Wrong size dla pipe OD** — too small letters = not visible from required distance.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Annual labeling audit** — walkdown wszystkich pipe, identify faded/missing/wrong labels.
  - **Pre-installation labeling** — install label before insulation jacket finished.
  - **Spare label inventory** — common substances + sizes available na site.
  - **Crayon/marker temporary** dla under-construction lines — replace z permanent po commissioning.
  - **Color-blind considerations** — text legend primary, color secondary (some operators colorblind).
  - **MOC for line repurpose** — if line changes service, MUST update all labels along entire run.
  - **3D plant model** ekspresuje labels — match field installation.

---

## Iteration 83 — 2026-05-26

### PPPPPPPPPP. Hot Bolting / Live Flange Tightening — ASME PCC-1, Stud Replacement
- **Co to**: **sequential removal + replacement bolts** w **pressurized flanged joint** — bez shutdown procesu. Pozwala na maintenance galled bolts, leak repair, gasket replacement (w niektórych warunkach) bez full unit outage.
- **Klasyfikacja** (ASME PCC-1 §10):
  - **"Hot bolting"** — pressurized system, **reduced operating pressure** (typowo ≤50% MAOP), one bolt at a time.
  - **"Online tightening / retorque"** — bolts already installed, just tightened to spec while pressurized (no removal).
  - **"Single stud replacement (SSR)"** — replacing one stud at a time, system still online.
- **High-risk activity** — qualifies as **hot work** w wielu plants:
  - **Hot work permit** required.
  - **Risk assessment + JSA** (Job Safety Analysis) — każda procedura specific.
  - **Qualified personnel only** — typically certified Bolt Technicians.
  - **Backup procedures** — emergency shutdown ready jeśli leak develops.
- **Standard ASME PCC-1**:
  - **Appendix M** — hot bolting procedure.
  - **Appendix O** — single stud replacement.
  - **Conditions**:
    - **Service**: gases preferred (less catastrophic z leak than liquid).
    - **Temperature**: limit per material — bolts może creep przy high T.
    - **Pressure**: typically ≤25% MAOP (operator dependent).
    - **Service exclusions**: H₂S above thresholds, anhydrous HF, oleum, fuming acids — too dangerous.
- **Stud relaxation** — root cause hot bolting need:
  - **Mechanism 1: Embedment** — surface asperities flatten in first hours post-install → load loss 5-15%.
  - **Mechanism 2: Creep** (high T) — bolt material elongates over time → load loss ongoing.
  - **Mechanism 3: Gasket compression set** — gasket loses thickness → bolt elongation lost.
  - **Mechanism 4: Thermal cycling** — repeated heat-cold cycles → cumulative relaxation.
  - **Cumulative load loss**: 20-50% after years of service.
- **Relaxation pass** — preventive technique (during install, not hot bolting):
  - **After initial torque** to 100% spec, pause **20 min - 4 h**.
  - **Repeat circular pass** at 100% target torque — captures embedment + initial relaxation.
  - **Verify visible torque rotation** during relaxation pass — if no rotation, joint already settled.
  - **Improves long-term joint reliability**.
- **Hot bolting procedure** — typical sequence:
  1. **Plan in star pattern** — identify bolt sequence.
  2. **First bolt**:
     - Loosen + remove (slow, watch dla gasket leak indicators).
     - Inspect bolt — galling, corrosion, thread damage. **Replace** if damaged.
     - **Clean threads** — wire brush.
     - **Re-lubricate** — anti-seize on threads + flange face nut.
     - **Reinstall + torque** to spec (use **hydraulic wrench** preferred dla repeatability).
  3. **Wait 45 min** — allows thermal stabilization gasket + flange.
  4. **Next bolt across star pattern** — repeat sequence.
  5. **Continue until all bolts processed** (typically 12-32 bolts on large flange = 9-24 hours).
  6. **Final relaxation pass** — circular re-torque check after all complete.
- **Tools required**:
  - **Hydraulic torque wrench** (Hytorc, Enerpac, Norbar) — accurate, repeatable.
  - **Calibrated gauges** — pressure → torque calibration verified.
  - **Backup wrench** — counter-hold opposite end nut.
  - **Anti-seize compound** — Cu-based (Permatex) lub Ni-based dla high T.
  - **Bolt heater** (opcjonalnie) — high T service, heated bolt extends → easier removal.
- **Bolt patterns**:
  - **8-bolt flange**: standard star pattern (1-5-3-7-2-6-4-8 sequence).
  - **12-bolt**: 1-7-4-10-2-8-5-11-3-9-6-12.
  - **Hot bolting**: same star pattern, but one bolt fully processed before next.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Multiple bolts removed simultaneously** — gasket compression lost → leak.
  - **Wrong sequence** — uneven gasket loading → blow-out.
  - **Insufficient lubrication** — under-torqued joint (friction varies).
  - **Damaged thread reuse** — galls during retorque, joint fails.
  - **No risk assessment** — surprise leak, no backup plan.
  - **High pressure / high T service** — should NOT be done hot, requires shutdown.
- **Single stud replacement (SSR)** — specific case:
  - Po galling discovered, **one stud completely lost** (broken, can't remove).
  - **Drill out** broken stud — easy out lub EDM (electrical discharge machining).
  - **Retap** threads jeśli damaged.
  - **Install new stud + torque**.
  - **Hot bolting** dla balance bolts (verify gasket integrity).
- **Hot bolting limits**:
  - **NIE wymieniać gasket** podczas hot bolting (system pressurized — gasket exposed → leak).
  - **Bolts only**, nie nuts replacement.
  - **Limited iteracja** — frequent hot bolting same joint = gasket cumulative damage.
  - **Critical service** (jak hydrogen, ammonia) — special permitting + extra safety measures.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-job inspection** — verify bolts visible, accessible, no obstructions.
  - **Spare bolt inventory** — same grade + size + length at job site.
  - **Backup gas / fire suppression** — incipient leak escalation control.
  - **Hot work fire watch** dla duration.
  - **Documentation chain** — every bolt logged (date, technician, torque, observation).
  - **Post-job inspection** — leak test (soap bubbles) + thermal imaging dla high T.
  - **Schedule maintenance** — plan hot bolting w periods of low process volatility (steady-state operation).

### QQQQQQQQQQ. Stainless Steel Back Purge — Dams + Inflatable Bladders + Argon
- **Po co**: spawanie **stainless steel + reactive metals** (titanium, zirconium, Inconel, super duplex) wymaga **bezpełnej** **inert gas atmosphere** wewnątrz rury podczas welding root pass. Bez tego:
  - **Oxidation** wewnętrznej rootu → "sugar" formation, scale, czarna oksydacja.
  - **Loss of corrosion resistance** — Cr depleted w surface zone → korozja w months/years.
  - **Cracking risk** — porosity + crack initiation.
  - **Cosmetic + integrity issue** — pharma/food fail inspection (visible oxide w pipe inside).
- **Back purge** = filling **wewnątrz rury** (root side) z **argon** (lub helium) podczas root weld pass.
- **Methods** (od taniego do drogiego):
  1. **Tape + foil** — duct tape over openings + Al foil patches. Manual, cheap, czesto leaky.
  2. **Water-soluble paper** — Argweld® Solu-Foil, dissolved later z water. Convenient, eliminate need to retrieve.
  3. **Foam plugs** — polyurethane foam discs sliced to size.
  4. **Inflatable bladders** — premium, fastest, most reliable. **Industry standard now**.
- **Water-soluble paper / dams** (Argweld Solu-Foil, Tite-R-Wett):
  - **Material**: cellulose paper z water-soluble bonding agent.
  - **Aplikacja**: cut to pipe ID, taped/wedged on both sides of weld zone.
  - **Drawbacks**:
    - **Contains water** (15-30% moisture content) — outgases dla weld zone → forms oxides z hot metal.
    - **Slow to dissolve** — flush z hot water 5-10 min po welding.
    - **NIE używać dla**: titanium, zirconium (water = catastrophic — H absorption + brittleness).
  - **Best dla**: low-criticality SS, sanitary that will be CIP-flushed anyway.
- **Foam plugs**:
  - **Material**: polyurethane foam — porous, contains air + moisture.
  - **Drawbacks**:
    - **Outgasses** moisture + air through pores → contamination weld zone.
    - **Burns** if hot (>200°C) → smoke, soot, contamination.
    - **Tight fit difficult** — gaps allow gas leakage.
  - **Best dla**: large pipe (>4"), short welding times, non-critical SS.
- **Inflatable bladder systems** (Argweld Quick Purge, COB Industries, Aquasol I-Purge):
  - **Materiał**: silicone rubber (heat-resistant do **150-200°C**) lub specialty composites.
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Dual bladders** (one upstream, one downstream) — separated by **purge chamber** ~50-150 mm.
    - **Air inflation** za pomocą hand pump lub compressed air to seal.
    - **Argon supply port** between bladders — fills purge chamber.
    - **Outlet vent** dla initial atmosphere flush.
  - **Setup**:
    1. Insert bladders + air hoses + Ar lines through pipe end.
    2. Position centered on weld zone (each bladder 75-300 mm from weld).
    3. Inflate bladders to seal (4-8 psi typical).
    4. Argon flush 10-30 min (until O₂ <0.1%).
    5. **Weld root pass**.
    6. Vent argon, deflate bladders.
    7. Withdraw via inlet hose.
  - **Speed**: 5-8× faster than paper / foam dla large pipes.
  - **Reliable**: tight seal, low gas consumption, **clean weld every time**.
- **Argon flow + monitoring**:
  - **Flow rate**: 20-50 L/h dla 4-12" pipe, larger for bigger diameters.
  - **Argon (>99.99% purity)** — welding-grade.
  - **Helium** alternative dla deep welds (lower density, settles?? — actually rises, displaces O₂ slower).
  - **Oxygen analyzer** at outlet — **target <0.1% (1000 ppm) O₂** dla typical SS, **<0.01% (100 ppm)** for super duplex / titanium.
  - **Continuous monitoring** during weld — alert if O₂ rises (leak indicator).
- **Purge time before welding**:
  - **3-5 pipe volumes** of argon before O₂ <0.1%.
  - **Pipe volume calculation**: V = π·(D/2)² × L (Marsalis equation simplified).
  - **Wait for analyzer readout** — don't rely on time alone.
- **Color indicator** during welding:
  - **Silver / shiny gold** — perfect purge.
  - **Straw / light tan** — slight oxidation (still OK dla SS, ale not super duplex).
  - **Dark blue / purple** — heavy oxidation, **REJECT + re-weld**.
  - **Black scale** — full oxidation, "sugar", reject.
- **High-temperature dams** (>200°C service welds):
  - **Ceramic blanket** wokół silicone bladder dla heat protection.
  - **Water-cooled bladders** dla extreme T.
  - **Argweld High-Temp** rated do 300°C.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Insufficient purge time** — first welds oxidized, "rainbow" colors.
  - **Leaky bladders** — air ingress, contamination.
  - **Wrong bladder size** — too loose = leak, too tight = damage during withdrawal.
  - **Forget to vent before deflation** — pressure trap, bladder rupture.
  - **No O₂ monitoring** — assumes purge complete (often not).
  - **Foam plugs hot** — burnt foam contaminates next welds.
  - **Water-soluble paper na titanium** — H absorption → catastrophic brittle fracture.
- **Withdrawal w long pipe runs**:
  - **Bladders w długiej rurze** — może być trapped if welds completed downstream.
  - **Plan extraction route** before installation.
  - **Modular bladder** (segments z connectors) — extract one segment at a time.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Backup dla bladder failure** — spare bladders na site, secondary argon supply.
  - **Argon supply** — large bottles (200 ft³) lub dewar dla long jobs.
  - **No grease / oil** on bladders — fire hazard.
  - **Pre-test inflation** off-pipe — verify no leaks.
  - **Surface prep** weld bevel — wipe acetone (no oil/grease).
  - **Documentation**: O₂ readings, argon flow, weld pass times — QA trail.
  - **Post-weld inspection** root pass borescope — verify no sugar, no oxidation.

### RRRRRRRRRR. Cooling Tower (CT) Water Piping — Supply + Return + Makeup
- **Po co**: cooling tower removes heat **od process water** by evaporative cooling. CT supplies cool water do **condensers, heat exchangers, jackets**. Return warm water → CT → evaporative cooling → repeat. Plant-wide loop, **largest water consumer** w typical industrial facility.
- **System overview**:
  - **CT basin** — sump z cool water (process water reservoir).
  - **CT pumps** (booster) — pump water od basin do plant users.
  - **Supply header** — cool water do plant.
  - **Plant users** — process exchangers cool z CT water.
  - **Return header** — warm water back to CT.
  - **CT distribution** — atomizers spray water down through fill.
  - **Air flow** (induced lub forced draft) — fans pull air through wet fill.
  - **Evaporation** — water cools (2-3°C below ambient wet-bulb T).
  - **Cool water collects** w basin → repeat.
- **Critical components**:
  - **Makeup water** — replaces losses (evaporation + drift + blowdown).
  - **Blowdown / bleed-off** — drains concentrated water (TDS / chloride buildup) → fresh water added.
  - **Chemical injection** — biocides, scale inhibitors, corrosion inhibitors.
  - **Filtration** — side-stream filter (5-10% flow) removes solids, debris.
- **Materials**:
  - **CT basin + structure**: FRP (fiberglass), concrete, pultruded structural.
  - **Piping** dla CT water (open loop):
    - **CS galvanized** (hot-dip) — najtaniej, lifespan 15-25 lat.
    - **CS internally coated** (epoxy, ceramic) — longer life dla aggressive water.
    - **HDPE / FRP** — non-metallic, no corrosion, common dla larger sizes.
    - **SS 316** — high chloride / brackish water, food / pharma.
    - **Cu-Ni 90/10** — seawater CT (offshore).
  - **NIE używać**: ungalvanized CS — rust + scale w months.
- **Open loop vs closed loop**:
  - **Open (evaporative)** — most CT systems. Water contacts air → evaporation cools. Concentration of dissolved solids + biological growth.
  - **Closed (dry cooler)** — water in closed loop, air-cooled heat exchanger. No evaporation. Used dla critical processes requiring quality water (no scaling).
- **Velocity limits**:
  - **Supply piping**: 2-3 m/s — economical balance między pipe size + pump head.
  - **Return piping**: similar.
  - **Above 3.5 m/s** — high erosion + noise + pressure drop.
  - **Below 1 m/s** — settling of suspended solids.
- **Piping layout**:
  - **CT basin level**: pump suction below basin water level (flooded suction, no priming needed).
  - **Vertical riser**: short as possible (NPSHa).
  - **Header sizing**: largest diameter (5-15 m/s? actually 2-3 m/s preferred), branches off.
  - **Loop configuration** (ring main): redundant flow paths, allows isolation w sections.
- **Pump sizing**:
  - **Flow**: 5-15 GPM per ton refrigeration (HVAC), 50-200 GPM/process exchanger.
  - **Head**: 60-100 ft typowo (lift + friction + heat exchanger ΔP).
  - **NPSHa critical** — pump suction near basin level.
- **Makeup water**:
  - **Sources**: city water, well, river — depends on plant.
  - **Treatment**: softening (Ca/Mg removal), filtration.
  - **Flow rate** — replaces:
    - **Evaporation** (~1% per 5°C ΔT cooling, 1-3% typical).
    - **Drift** (~0.001-0.01% of circulation — wind-driven mist losses).
    - **Blowdown** (varies, kept od cycles concentration).
  - **Total makeup**: 2-5% of circulation rate typical.
  - **Float valve** in basin — auto-fills do level set point.
- **Blowdown system**:
  - **Cel**: control **cycles of concentration (CoC)**:
    - CoC = TDS_circulation / TDS_makeup.
    - Typical CoC = 4-8 (more = less water consumption + more chemicals; less = more water but less risk fouling).
  - **Continuous blowdown** — small flow always open, drains 1-3% circulation.
  - **Conductivity controller** — automatic blowdown when TDS > setpoint.
  - **Blowdown destination**: sewer, lagoon, evaporation pond, salinity-tolerant downstream user.
- **Chemical injection**:
  - **Biocide** — chlorine (bleach), bromine, isothiazolinones — kontrolują bacteria, algae, biofilm.
  - **Scale inhibitor** — phosphonates, polymers — keeps Ca/Mg in solution.
  - **Corrosion inhibitor** — typically nitrate / molybdate (some applications) lub phosphate.
  - **pH adjuster** — H₂SO₄ (lower pH), NaOH (raise pH).
  - **Injection points**: typically into makeup or return line (good mixing).
- **CT water issues** (common):
  - **Legionella** — bacteria w warm water + biofilm → respiratory disease (CTs are #1 source).
    - **Mitigation**: maintain biocide, monitor counts, system flushing.
  - **Scale** (CaCO₃, CaSO₄) — białawe deposits w exchangers → reduced heat transfer.
  - **Corrosion** — pitting, general loss → leaks.
  - **Biofouling** — slime w pipes + exchangers → flow restriction.
  - **MIC (Microbiologically Influenced Corrosion)** — SRB (sulfate-reducing bacteria) → H₂S + corrosion.
- **CT supply temperature**:
  - **Wet-bulb approach**: CT supply ≈ wet-bulb T + 3-5°C.
  - **Summer**: 28-32°C typical (depends on climate).
  - **Winter**: 5-15°C (CTs may cycle off, basin heater w cold climates).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak isolation valves** dla individual heat exchangers — can't service without shutdown entire CT loop.
  - **No drain points** — winter freeze damage.
  - **Insufficient strainer** at pump suction — debris damages pump.
  - **Wrong material** (ungalvanized CS) — corrosion in months.
  - **No chemical injection automation** — manual feed inconsistent, biofilm develops.
  - **No blowdown** — TDS rises, scaling, fouling.
  - **No filter** — solids accumulate, exchanger plugging.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Drains at low points** — winterization + repair.
  - **Vents at high points** — bleed air during fill / startup.
  - **Sampling ports** — at suction, supply, return — water quality monitoring.
  - **Pressure gauges** — verify pump head, friction losses.
  - **Flow meters** — supply + return — energy + heat balance.
  - **Thermal expansion** dla long runs (1-2 km plant loops) — expansion loops or joints.
  - **Pipe supports** — large pipes, full of water (3-10× pipe weight).
  - **Insulation** dla high-T or critical service (energy efficiency).
  - **Strainer wash** — backwash strainers monthly.
  - **Tower inspection** — annual: nozzles, fill condition, drift eliminators, fan blades.

---

## Iteration 84 — 2026-05-26

### SSSSSSSSSS. Pump Minimum Flow Bypass / Recycle Line — ARC Valves + Continuous Bypass
- **Po co**: centrifugal pumps przy **low flow** (poniżej "minimum continuous flow" / MCF / Q_min):
  - **Overheating** — kinetic energy converts do heat, no flow to carry it away → impeller seal damage, vapor lock.
  - **Hydraulic instability** — recirculation w pump casing, **suction recirculation** (cavitation-like damage).
  - **Radial thrust** wzrasta dramatycznie (off-BEP operation) → shaft bending, bearing damage.
  - **Vibration** — wzrasta, mech seal failure.
  - **Dry running risk** — przy zero flow + dead-head, pump w 1-5 min może destroyed.
- **Q_min calculation** (per OEM curve):
  - **Thermal minimum** (heat removal limit): ~30% BEP (Best Efficiency Point).
  - **Stable minimum** (vibration limit): ~50% BEP dla single-stage, 70-80% dla multi-stage.
  - **Manufacturer publishes** specific Q_min na pump curve.
- **Solutions** dla maintaining Q_min:
  1. **Continuous bypass z restriction orifice** — najtaniej, ale always wastes energy.
  2. **Modulated bypass z control valve** — bypass opens only at low flow.
  3. **Automatic Recirculation Valve (ARV/ARC)** — single multifunction valve.
  4. **VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) z low-flow shutdown** — slow pump down + stop dla zero demand.
- **Continuous bypass z orifice** (simplest):
  - **Connection**: pump discharge → orifice → return to suction source (storage tank, not directly to pump suction!).
  - **Orifice sized** dla Q_min flow at ΔP = pump head − suction P + line losses.
  - **Always flowing** — ~10-30% pump capacity (energy waste).
  - **Aplikacja**: small pumps, simple systems, low-cost installations.
  - **Energy penalty**: 10-30% pump motor power dissipated jako heat (recirculation tank musi mieć cooling lub volume).
- **ARV / ARC valve** — premium solution:
  - **3 funkcje w jednym valve**:
    1. **Main flow check valve** — prevents reverse flow when pump off.
    2. **Bypass valve** — opens automatically when main flow < setpoint, closes when sufficient flow.
    3. **Pressure reducing** (bypass side) — orifice / pressure-reducing stages built-in dla high ΔP services.
  - **Producent**: Yarway® (Schroedahl, oryginalny owner ARC® trademark), Yarway (acquired), Schroedahl, IMI CCI, Mokveld.
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Disc + spring** w main line — disc lifts proportional to flow.
    - **Bypass port** z spring — opens when disc position indicates low main flow.
    - **Multi-stage cage** w bypass — handles high ΔP without cavitation.
  - **Cost**: 5-20× simple orifice install, ale **major energy savings** (bypass only flows when needed).
  - **Aplikacja**: boiler feedwater pumps, large process pumps, critical service.
- **Sizing ARV**:
  - **Q_min bypass flow** = pump MCF.
  - **Main flow rated** = pump capacity at design point.
  - **ΔP across bypass** = pump head + suction pressure + line losses.
  - **Material**: typically high-grade SS or alloy dla wear resistance + high-pressure trim.
- **Bypass destination**:
  - **NEVER back to pump suction directly** — fluid w recirculation gets hotter w each cycle (no heat removal) → thermal runaway.
  - **Back to source tank** — large vessel acts as heat sink + degassing.
  - **Bypass + cooling line** — heat exchanger in bypass dla critical service.
- **Common errors**:
  - **No min flow protection** — pump dead-heads on shutdown, destroyed w minutes.
  - **Bypass returns to suction** — short-circuit, no heat removal.
  - **Undersized bypass** — Q_min nie reached, pump still overheats.
  - **Orifice fouls** — debris in bypass line → blocked → no min flow protection → pump damage.
  - **No ARV maintenance** — disc/seat wears, valve stuck open (continuous bypass = energy waste) or closed (no protection).
- **Indication + monitoring**:
  - **Flow switch** in bypass line — verify bypass active when needed.
  - **Pump T** monitoring (RTD on casing) — alarm at MCF + 5°C above normal.
  - **Vibration sensor** — detect operating off-BEP.
  - **ARV position indicator** — visual confirmation flow path.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Bypass line size** — ≥ 1.5× nominal flow line size dla low pressure drop.
  - **Bypass connection** — top of pump casing (not flange ports — could vent dry).
  - **Isolation valves** — bypass + main, dla maintenance.
  - **Check valve** w bypass — prevents backflow from tank when pump off.
  - **Drain valve** at low points — winterization, debris removal.
  - **Insulate hot bypass** — high-T pumping → bypass return line also hot.
  - **NDT + functional test** after install — verify ARV operates at low flow.

### TTTTTTTTTT. Pipe Field Bending — Cold + Hot + Induction Bending
- **Po co bending zamiast elbows**:
  - **Fewer welds** = less NDT cost + faster install.
  - **Lower pressure drop** (large radius bend vs 90° elbow).
  - **Less ID restriction** dla pigging service.
  - **Better dla pipeline construction** — wymagana ciągła profile.
- **3 typy field bending**:
  1. **Elastic / cold bending (no permanent set)** — bending within elastic limit, **pipe springs back**.
  2. **Cold bending (plastic, permanent)** — bend pipe past elastic, permanent shape.
  3. **Hot / induction bending** — heat pipe locally, bend, cool.
- **Elastic / cold sag bending** (no machine):
  - **Pipeline construction**: pipe **string laid + cold sagged** to follow terrain naturalnie. Used for **vertical undulations** (hills, valleys).
  - **Curvature**: very large R (>50× D) — within elastic limit, no permanent deformation.
  - **No special tools** — leverage pipe weight + lifting equipment dla shape.
- **Cold plastic bending** (with machine):
  - **Min bend radius (R)**:
    - **Standard CS, schedule 40-80**: R ≥ **30-40D** (D = nominal pipe size).
    - **Higher grade pipe (X65, X70)**: R ≥ **40-60D** (less ductile).
  - **Equipment**:
    - **Roll bender** (3-roll lub 4-roll) — large pipe diameter, smooth bend.
    - **Pyramid bender** (hydraulic) — field portable, OK dla DN 100-300.
    - **CRC Evans field bender** — popular dla long-distance pipeline.
  - **Process**:
    1. Position pipe on bender bed.
    2. Apply gradual ram force at center, supports at ends.
    3. Bend incrementally (30-50 mm increments).
    4. Measure ovality + bend angle.
    5. Repeat dla larger bend angle (multiple positions on pipe).
  - **Ovality limit** (per API 5L, B31.4): **5% max** (D_max − D_min) / D_nominal.
  - **Wall thinning** at extrados (outer of bend): typowo 5-15%.
  - **Wrinkles at intrados** (inner of bend): can develop if R too small or material too stiff.
- **Hot bending** (workshop, dla tighter R):
  - **Heat pipe** at bend zone with gas torches lub furnace.
  - **Heat zones**: ~D-wide circumferential band.
  - **Temperature**: 700-900°C dla CS (austenitizing range), 1100-1200°C dla SS.
  - **Bend** while hot — much less force required, less wrinkling/ovality.
  - **Cool** — water quench (for some applications) lub air cool.
  - **Min R**:
    - **CS hot bending**: 0.7-1.5× D (very tight!).
    - **Most alloys**: 1.5-3× D.
  - **PWHT after hot bending** (HAZ + bend zone) — typowo wymagane dla critical service.
- **Induction bending** (premium method):
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Induction coil** wokół pipe at bend zone — heats narrow band (75-300 mm).
    - **Pipe pushed** through coil while bending arm pivots around center of curvature.
    - **Heating + bending continuously** — bend zone hot, balance pipe cold (rigid).
    - **Water spray cooling** behind coil — controlled cooling rate.
  - **Process**:
    - **Heat T**: 850-1100°C zależnie od material.
    - **Bending rate**: 25-200 mm/min (depending on D + R).
    - **Continuous** vs incremental — smooth bend, uniform properties.
  - **Capabilities**:
    - **R as small as 1.5D**.
    - **Diameter range**: DN 50 do >2400 (HD bend machines).
    - **Wall thickness up to 100+ mm**.
  - **Quality**:
    - **Ovality**: <3% typical, <1% premium.
    - **Wall thinning**: 5-10% controllable.
    - **Wrinkles**: NONE — controlled heating prevents.
    - **No mandrel** required (vs cold bending).
  - **Aplikacje**: pipelines (X70, X80 high-strength), refinery transfer lines, process piping, headers.
- **Material limits**:
  - **API 5L X grade**: cold bending OK do X70 z care, induction preferred for X80+.
  - **P22 / P91 Cr-Mo**: hot bending preferred, PWHT mandatory.
  - **304/316 SS**: cold OK, induction for tight R.
  - **Duplex SS**: induction preferred (cold causes ferrite/austenite ratio shift).
- **Post-bend inspection**:
  - **Ovality** — caliper measurements at multiple points.
  - **Wall thickness** — UT, especially at extrados.
  - **Hardness** (induction bends) — verify no over-hardening (martensite formation).
  - **NDT**: PT/MT for surface cracks at intrados (compressive yielding zone).
  - **PWHT verification** (if applied) — temperature charts.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong R** specified — too tight for material → wrinkles + cracks.
  - **No PWHT after hot bend** — residual stress + non-tempered microstructure.
  - **Excessive ovality** — pig restrictions, fluid dynamics issues.
  - **Cold bend at low T** (winter outdoor) — material brittle, cracks possible.
  - **No supports** during cold bending — pipe shape uncontrolled, kinks.
- **Code compliance**:
  - **ASME B31.3** §332 — bending requirements.
  - **ASME B16.49** — factory-made elbows + bends.
  - **API 5L** — bending limits dla line pipe.
  - **ISO 15590-1** — induction bends.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Bend marking** — clear stamp z bend angle, R, direction.
  - **Inspect for cracks** prior to bending — defective pipe magnified.
  - **Bend allowance** w cutting — bend has some shrinkage axially (1-3%).
  - **Avoid weld near bend zone** — keep welds in straight section, 1-2D away from bend tangent.
  - **Test bend** specimen for new procedures — verify mechanical properties retained.
  - **Marking direction** — outer of bend (extrados), inner (intrados) — for inspection records.
  - **Field touch-up** — paint after bend for corrosion protection.

### UUUUUUUUUU. Slurry Pipeline — Mining / Tailings / Coal Transport
- **Co to**: rurociąg transportujący **mieszaninę cieczy + cząstek stałych** (solids fraction 10-70% by weight) na large distances. Aplikacje:
  - **Mining**: ore concentrate (Fe, Cu, Au, phosphate) → port lub processing.
  - **Tailings**: waste solid → impoundment lub backfill.
  - **Coal slurry**: coal + water → power plant lub processing.
  - **Sand slurry**: sand + water → backfill, beach replenishment.
  - **Dredging**: sediment + water → disposal area.
- **Slurry classification**:
  - **Newtonian** — viscosity constant z shear rate (low solids + fine particles).
  - **Non-Newtonian** — viscosity zmienia się z shear (high solids, certain materials):
    - **Bingham plastic** — wymaga yield stress dla start flow.
    - **Pseudoplastic** — viscosity decreases z shear rate (shear-thinning).
    - **Dilatant** — viscosity increases z shear rate (rare).
- **Settling vs non-settling**:
  - **Settling slurry** — particles tend to deposit, **MUST maintain min velocity** to prevent bed formation.
  - **Non-settling** — homogeneous, particles small enough that suspended by Brownian + turbulence even at low velocity.
- **Critical settling velocity (V_L)**:
  - **Vital design parameter** — velocity poniżej której solids form **stationary lub sliding bed**.
  - **Consequences**:
    - **Reduced cross-section** dla flow → pressure rise.
    - **Sliding bed abrasion** — bottom of pipe gets uneven wear.
    - **Pipeline plug** (worst case) — total flow stop, restart difficult.
  - **Formula** (Durand-Condolios approx.): V_L = F_L × √(2·g·D·(s−1)).
    - F_L = 1.0-1.5 typically (depends on particle size + concentration).
    - g = 9.81 m/s², D = pipe ID, s = specific gravity solids / liquid.
  - **Typical V_L**:
    - **Fine sand (200 µm)**: 1-2 m/s.
    - **Coarse sand (2 mm)**: 3-5 m/s.
    - **Gravel (10 mm)**: 6-8 m/s.
- **Operating velocity**:
  - **Min**: V_L + 0.5 m/s safety margin.
  - **Max**: ~7-8 m/s — above = excessive wear.
  - **Optimum**: 1-1.5× V_L (balance pressure drop, wear, safety).
- **Pipe materials** (wear resistance critical):
  - **Carbon steel (CS) bare** — najtaniej, ale fast wear w abrasive service. **Lifetime 1-5 years**.
  - **Hardened steel (AR400, Hardox)** — 5-10× longer life.
  - **HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene)** — popular dla tailings, mid-velocity.
    - **Velocity limit**: 6-8 ft/s (2-2.5 m/s) typical for abrasive.
    - **Wear** by gradual erosion — replace section when worn through.
  - **Rubber-lined pipe** — natural rubber lub neoprene liner 6-25 mm, w CS shell.
    - **Excellent wear** — elasticity dissipates impact energy.
    - **Aplikacje**: coal slurry, slurry pumps internals.
  - **Ceramic-lined** (alumina, basalt) — premium wear resistance.
    - **Aplikacje**: severe service (>6 m/s w bend, fine abrasive).
    - **Cost**: 5-10× CS.
  - **UHMW-PE (Ultra-High Molecular Weight Polyethylene)** — w CS shell, ultra-wear resistant.
- **Wear locations** (highest stress):
  - **Bottom of horizontal pipe** — sliding bed wear (1 o'clock to 5 o'clock zone).
  - **Bends** — impingement wear at extrados.
  - **Joints / welds** — turbulence + uneven surface → erosion.
  - **Diverters / valves** — flow direction change → wear hot spot.
- **Pipe rotation maintenance**:
  - **Rotate pipe** every 6-12 months (mining ops) — distributes wear around circumference.
  - **2x lifetime extension** w typowych operations.
  - **Marker stripe** on pipe indicates position.
- **Wear monitoring**:
  - **UT thickness gauging** — quarterly, focus on bottom + bends.
  - **Pipe wear coupons** — replaceable test sections inline.
  - **Acoustic emission** — early detection thinning.
  - **Replacement criteria**: 50% wall loss typowo (factor of safety for variability).
- **Hydraulic design**:
  - **Pumps**: centrifugal slurry pumps (heavy-duty, rubber-lined casing/impeller) lub positive displacement (piston, peristaltic).
  - **Stages**: long pipelines (50-500 km) wymagają **booster stations** every 50-100 km.
  - **Pressure**: 10-150 bar zależnie.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Velocity below V_L** → settling → blocked pipeline → costly recovery.
  - **Velocity above 8 m/s** → excessive wear → replacement every 6 m-cy.
  - **Wrong material dla service** — CS w highly abrasive → fail w months.
  - **No pipe rotation** — bottom wears through, top still new.
  - **Sudden velocity drop** (pump trip) → solids settle in pipeline → "starved start" próby restart = blockage.
- **Restart after shutdown** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Water flush first** — clear settled bed.
  - **Slow ramp** — increase velocity gradually, allow solids re-suspension.
  - **Avoid full slurry restart** — pumps overloaded, plug formation.
- **Common slurry transport materials + design data**:
  - **Iron ore tailings** — fine, easy to keep suspended, but high specific gravity (~3.5).
  - **Coal slurry (1:1 water-coal)** — coarse, settling, requires high velocity.
  - **Phosphate slurry** — moderate abrasion, moderate velocity.
  - **Bauxite slurry** — moderate.
  - **Hydraulic fill (sand + water)** — short distance, high velocity, very abrasive.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Flow indicators** — magnetic flowmeter (no obstruction), Doppler ultrasonic.
  - **Pressure gauges** — multiple points, gradient analysis dla blockage detection.
  - **Sample valves** — periodic sampling dla density + solids check.
  - **Drain valves** at low points — flush + cleanout.
  - **Pigging launchers** — periodically clean pipe (foam pigs).
  - **Backup capacity** — alternate route or storage dla maintenance shutdown.
  - **Annual hydrotest** — verify integrity (slurry deposits hide cracks).
  - **NDT focus**: external + internal (where accessible) corrosion + wear.

---

## Iteration 85 — 2026-05-26

### VVVVVVVVVV. Centrifugal Compressor Anti-Surge Recycle Line — Cold + Hot Bypass
- **Co to surge**: dynamic instability w centrifugal/axial compressors gdy operating point **left of surge line** (low flow, high pressure ratio). Flow REVERSES momentarily → pressure collapses → flow surges forward → repeats z **5-20 Hz frequency**. Konsekwencje:
  - **Impeller damage** — bending, blade tip rubs.
  - **Bearing failure** — radial loads cycle.
  - **Seal damage** — dynamic axial loads.
  - **Vibration** — alarm levels exceeded, vessel shake.
  - **Process upset** — pressure pulsations propagate downstream.
  - **W severe cases**: total destruction w sekundy.
- **Anti-surge control system**:
  - **Surge line** — manufacturer curve, P_discharge/P_suction = f(Q_inlet).
  - **Control line** = surge line + safety margin (5-10%).
  - **Anti-surge valve (ASV)** — modulating valve w recycle line.
  - **Surge controller** (Compressor Control Corp, GE Mark VIe, Triconex) — high-speed (50 ms response).
  - **Operation**:
    - Operating point monitored real-time z flow + pressure transducers.
    - As point approaches control line → ASV opens proportionally.
    - Recycles discharge gas back to suction → wzrasta Q_inlet → operating point moves away od surge.
    - When demand returns → ASV closes.
- **Cold bypass (Anti-Surge Recycle Line)**:
  - **Konfiguracja**: tap **downstream of aftercooler** (gas już cooled) → przez **ASV** → back to suction scrubber (przed inlet).
  - **Cold gas** wraca → niska T temperatura suction nie rośnie → continuous operation possible.
  - **Aplikacja**: **normal operation**, **low demand** scenarios, daily turn-down.
  - **Valve**: globe lub characterized ball valve, modulating actuator (pneumatic or electric).
  - **Sizing**: full compressor flow capability (worst-case turn-down do 100% recycle).
  - **Drainage**: knockout drum downstream of cooler removes condensate przed wracaniem do suction (prevent liquid carryover).
- **Hot bypass (Hot Gas Bypass — HGBV)**:
  - **Konfiguracja**: tap **upstream of aftercooler** (gas HOT, just out of compressor) → przez **HGV (Hot Gas Valve)** → directly back to suction.
  - **Hot gas** wraca → suction T natychmiast wzrasta → operating point shifts left to right na curve.
  - **Aplikacja**: **emergency surge**, **ESD (Emergency Shutdown)**, **fast surge recovery**.
  - **Operation w ESD**: HGV opens 100% w 1-2 sek → compressor "killed" → pressure rise instantly stopped → bezpieczne shutdown.
  - **Why hot bypass?**:
    - **Faster response** — no aftercooler / piping delays (cold bypass has 3-10 sec time lag).
    - **No hydrate formation risk** — w cold bypass, ΔP through ASV powoduje JT cooling → wet gas → hydrates → blockage. Hot bypass eliminates this.
  - **Drawback**: hot gas to suction → znaczna T rise → cooler downstream of compressor overloaded → can't sustain long-term. Tylko transient use.
- **Combined system** — most large compressors:
  - **ASV** dla normal turn-down + slow surge events.
  - **HGV** dla emergency / ESD events.
  - **Surge controller** sequences both — ASV first, HGV jeśli ASV insufficient.
- **Recycle line components**:
  - **Tap point**: typically perpendicular to main flow (no debris in branch).
  - **Anti-surge valve** — close-coupled to compressor (minimize volume between compressor + valve).
  - **Silencer** — high pressure drop → high noise → silencer downstream of valve (or specific noise-reducing valve trim).
  - **Check valve** — w main discharge line (prevent reverse flow do compressor podczas surge).
- **ASV characteristics**:
  - **Stroke speed**: ≤2 sec full stroke (open or close).
  - **Linear** flow characteristic preferred (predictable control).
  - **Fail-open** position (safe state — recycle = surge protection).
  - **Trim**: low-noise (multi-stage cage), anti-cavitation if liquid carryover possible.
  - **Actuator**: pneumatic z spring (fail-open) lub electric (z UPS dla fail-open).
- **Recycle line sizing rules**:
  - **Pipe size**: based on full recycle flow + acceptable pressure drop.
  - **Velocity**: ≤25 m/s gas dla AIV avoidance.
  - **Pressure rating**: ANSI rating of discharge side (full pressure during turn-down).
  - **Material**: same as discharge piping (high P, high T potential).
- **Surge events**:
  - **Cause 1**: process upset → flow drops fast.
  - **Cause 2**: ESD (emergency shutdown) → discharge valve closes.
  - **Cause 3**: cooler fouling → ΔP rises → operating point shifts left.
  - **Cause 4**: power loss + slow rundown z trapped gas.
  - **Common case**: ESD without HGV → surge w 2-5 cykli → mechanical damage.
- **Indicators of surge**:
  - **Vibration spike** (compressor casing, bearings).
  - **Flow reversal** (flow meter readings negative briefly).
  - **Pressure pulsation** (transients @5-20 Hz).
  - **Audible** — compressor "huffs" lub "barks" sound.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak HGV** w large compressor — ESD events damage compressor.
  - **Slow ASV** — surge events propagate before ASV opens.
  - **Recycle tap too far downstream** — large volume between compressor + valve → control lag.
  - **Aftercooler bypass** dla cold bypass — recycle gas hotter than expected → suction T rises → derating.
  - **Knockout drum undersized** — liquid carryover do suction → impeller damage.
  - **ASV stuck closed** — surge protection nieoperative — verify quarterly z partial stroke test.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Recycle piping straight runs** — 10D upstream + 5D downstream of ASV dla flow stability.
  - **Heat trace recycle line** w cold climate (avoid condensation/freeze).
  - **Insulation** dla hot bypass — operator burn protection.
  - **Drain valve** at low point dla maintenance.
  - **Vent valve** dla pressurization/depressurization.
  - **Maintenance schedule**: ASV full stroke test annually, surge controller calibration every 2-3 years.
  - **Operator training** — recognize surge symptoms, manual intervention if controls fail.

### WWWWWWWWWW. Concentrated Sulfuric Acid (98% H₂SO₄) — Piping + Materials
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Sulfuric acid production plant** — produkt finalny.
  - **Acid alkylation** (refinery, alternatywa HF).
  - **Battery manufacture**.
  - **Mining + metallurgy** — leaching.
  - **Fertilizer** — phosphate, ammonium sulfate.
  - **Cleaning + pickling** stainless steel.
- **H₂SO₄ właściwości**:
  - **98% H₂SO₄** = "concentrated" lub "98% acid" (commercial form).
  - **Specific gravity**: 1.84 (very heavy — fully density water × 1.84).
  - **Freezing point**: 3°C @ 98% (oh - close to ambient!).
  - **Boiling point**: 338°C @ atmospheric.
  - **Viscosity**: ~25 cP @ 20°C (much higher than water).
  - **NOT volatile** at ambient (low vapor pressure, no breathing risk z H₂SO₄ z 98%).
  - **VERY hygroscopic** — absorbs water od air → exothermic dilution → bardzo dangerous (heat + corrosion).
- **Concentration matters** dla materials:
  - **<70%** (dilute / battery acid): **VERY corrosive** dla CS, requires alloy/lined pipe.
  - **70-93%** (moderate): CS slow attack, w warunkach z high velocity / T limited.
  - **93-99%** (concentrated): **CS forms PASSIVE SULFATE FILM** = corrosion rate **0.2-1 mm/year** acceptable.
  - **>99% (oleum / fuming acid)**: special materials, very low velocity.
  - **CRITICAL**: small water contamination spoils passive layer → katastrofalny corrosion.
- **Passive film mechanism**:
  - **Initial corrosion** rate high — Fe reacts z H₂SO₄.
  - **Iron sulfate (FeSO₄)** forms na surface → insoluble (w concentrated acid).
  - **Film grows** → blocks further attack → corrosion rate drops dramatically.
  - **Film integrity** = passivation. **Film disturbance** = local catastrophic corrosion.
- **Velocity limits** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Max 0.9 m/s (3 ft/s)** dla 98% H₂SO₄ w CS pipe — **passive film stays intact**.
  - **Above 1 m/s** → film erodes → fresh metal exposed → rapid attack.
  - **At 2 m/s** → corrosion rate 10-50× higher.
  - **W bends**: lokalne wear → "hydrogen grooving" — long grooves wzdłuż pipe wzdłuż dna.
- **Material selection**:
  - **Concentrated 93-99% @ ambient T, V<0.9 m/s**: **carbon steel** (A106 Gr B), **Schedule 80** (corrosion allowance).
  - **Concentrated @ elevated T (>50°C) OR V>0.9 m/s**: **SS 316L** preferred.
  - **Dilute acid (< 70%)**: **Alloy 20**, **Hastelloy B/C**, **PTFE-lined**, **PVDF**.
  - **Very dilute (<30%) + high T**: **rubber-lined CS** lub **glass-lined**.
  - **Oleum (>100% — z free SO₃)**: SS 304 OK przy low T, but be careful.
- **NIE używać**:
  - **Aluminum** — burns vigorously z dilute acid.
  - **Copper, brass** — high corrosion + cross-contamination.
  - **Galvanized** — Zn rapidly attacked.
- **Pipe schedule**:
  - **Minimum Sch 80** dla concentrated acid w CS — 0.125" corrosion allowance dla 20+ year design life.
  - **Sch 40 OK** dla SS (less corrosion).
- **Joints**:
  - **Welded preferred** — fewer leak paths.
  - **Flanges**: spiral wound z **graphite filler** + **carbon steel windings** (PTFE bad — degrades z acid).
  - **NIE rubber gaskets** — degraded.
- **Slope** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Slope toward drain** 1:50 (more steep than water, dla heavy fluid drainage).
  - **NO POCKETS** — acid trapped → concentrates impurities → local corrosion.
  - **Eccentric reducers**: flat-top horizontal (z drain bottom).
- **Welding**:
  - **Same procedure jako CS** (sour/wet H₂S nie applies w concentrated acid).
  - **NO PWHT required** dla CS concentrated H₂SO₄ piping (residual stress nie issue at low corrosion rate).
  - **Internal weld bead smooth** — protrusion creates turbulence → high local velocity → film disrupted → local corrosion.
  - **Grind weld smooth** if possible — improves passive film stability.
- **Valves**:
  - **Body**: CS lub SS 316.
  - **Plug / disc**: PTFE-lined lub SS 316 (limit velocity).
  - **Diaphragm valve** preferred dla isolation — full bore, no obstruction.
  - **NO ball valves z metal seats** — point contact → wear → leak.
- **Tank materials**:
  - **98% H₂SO₄ storage tank**: **carbon steel**, no lining, well-passivated.
  - **Concentration drop** below 93% (humidity ingress) → tank starts corroding rapidly → control breathing system.
  - **Tank breather**: silica gel desiccant dryer dla ingress air → prevents dilution.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong velocity** — sizing dla water flow → 2-3 m/s → film disrupted → corrosion w months.
  - **Water contamination** — pipe washing z water bez drying → diluted acid → CS corroded.
  - **Brak isolation** during maintenance → mixing z water → exothermic → injury risk.
  - **Galvanized fittings** by mistake → rapid Zn release + acid contamination.
  - **Hot work near acid** w sealed system — heat → pressure rise → leak.
  - **Quick draining** w shutdown — turbulence + film damage.
  - **Cold weather** (<3°C) — acid freezes solid → heat trace required.
- **Anodic protection** — premium dla CS:
  - **Apply small voltage** (50-200 mV vs reference) → maintains passive film actively → corrosion ↓ 10-100×.
  - **Used** dla acid coolers, transfer pumps, intermediate storage tanks.
- **Safety**:
  - **PPE level B/C** dla maintenance — splash suit, face shield, neoprene gloves + boots.
  - **Eyewash + safety shower** w 10 sec range.
  - **Emergency response** — large spills overwhelm normal water rinse; **soda ash** lub **lime** for neutralization.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Mark velocity limit** clearly on P&ID — operator awareness.
  - **Flow restricting orifice** w discharge dla protection vs pump runaway speed.
  - **Sample valve** — periodic acid concentration check (drift indicates water ingress).
  - **Tank breather check** — silica gel replacement annually.
  - **Cathodic protection** dla buried CS acid lines (sulfate-reducing bacteria!).
  - **Insulation** + heat trace dla cold climates (freeze point 3°C is risky).
  - **Inspection**: UT wall thickness annually at high-stress zones (bends, downstream of valves, pump discharge).

### XXXXXXXXXX. Vortex Breaker — Pump Suction + Tank Outlet Nozzle
- **Po co**: gdy tank/vessel drainuje od bottom nozzle, **swirling motion** can form **vortex** (jak w sink/bathtub) → centerline vortex sucks **air/vapor** od top → **gas entrainment** w outlet flow → **pump cavitation** lub **two-phase flow** downstream.
- **Consequences**:
  - **Pump cavitation** — vapor in suction → impeller damage.
  - **NPSH loss** — effective head reduced.
  - **Flow oscillation** — process upset.
  - **Air entrainment** — if oxygen-sensitive process (food, pharma, hot oil), contamination.
  - **Premature emptying** — operator sees vortex z above, stops emptying tank w 30% remaining.
- **Vortex formation factors**:
  - **Low submergence** — depth liquid above outlet decreases → vortex risk wzrasta.
  - **Outlet velocity** — high velocity at draining → strong vortex.
  - **Tangential flow** — w tank z side inlets at angle creates swirl.
  - **Tank geometry** — round more prone niż square (no corners to disrupt rotation).
  - **Rotation imparted** by mixers, agitators, asymmetric inlets.
- **Vortex breaker types**:
  - **Disc / plate** — flat disc 100-300 mm above nozzle.
    - **Diameter**: **2-3× nozzle ID**.
    - **Height above nozzle**: **0.5-1× nozzle ID**.
    - **Blocks axial vortex** — interrupts vertical "tornado" core.
    - **Allows radial flow** under disc → liquid enters nozzle.
    - **Simple**, common dla horizontal exit.
  - **Cross plates** — 2-4 vertical plates intersecting at center over nozzle.
    - **Length**: **1.5× nozzle ID** each plate.
    - **Height**: **0.5-1× nozzle ID**.
    - **Most common** dla industrial tanks.
    - **Disrupts rotation** w 4 quadrants.
  - **Grid plates** — multiple orthogonal grids stacked.
    - **Most effective** dla strong rotation.
    - **Aplikacje**: heavy industrial, mixing tanks.
  - **Eccentric (offset)** breakers — special dla offset nozzles.
- **Submergence requirements** (minimum liquid depth above nozzle):
  - **Without vortex breaker**: **S_min = D + 2.3·D·√Fr** gdzie:
    - D = nozzle ID.
    - Fr = Froude number = V / √(g·D).
    - V = nozzle inlet velocity (m/s).
  - **Przykład**: 6" nozzle (D=0.15 m), V=2 m/s → Fr = 2/√(9.81×0.15) = 1.65 → S_min = 0.15 + 2.3×0.15×√1.65 = 0.59 m **bez breakera**.
  - **Z vortex breaker** → submergence requirement reduces do **0.5-1.5× D**.
- **Design rules**:
  - **Disc diameter** = 2-3× nozzle ID (minimum 2D).
  - **Height above nozzle** = 0.5-1× nozzle ID.
  - **Material**: same jako tank (CS, SS, FRP).
  - **Welded to bottom plate** or supported on nozzle internal extension.
  - **Holes drilled** dla drainage (avoid trapped liquid below disc po emptying tank).
- **Cross-plate design**:
  - **4-plate cross** (most common): plates 90° apart.
  - **Plate width**: 1.5× nozzle ID.
  - **Plate height**: 0.5-1× nozzle ID.
  - **Welded** directly to tank bottom + each other at center.
  - **Drainage holes** w lower edge dla complete emptying.
- **NPSH considerations**:
  - **Without vortex breaker**: liquid level + vapor pressure margin → high required level.
  - **Z breaker**: can drain do **20% nominal level** before vortex forms.
  - **Critical dla pump-out operations** — maximize usable tank volume.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak vortex breaker** + low NPSH — pump cavitates routine.
  - **Disc too small** (< 2D) — vortex still forms above smaller area.
  - **Disc too high** above nozzle — vortex forms below disc.
  - **No drain holes** — liquid trapped between disc + tank bottom (especially w batch processes z product changes).
  - **Misaligned cross-plate** — uneven flow disruption.
  - **Wrong material** — corrosion → breaker collapses → vortex returns.
  - **Forgot during retrofit** — old tank, new pump higher capacity → vortex now possible.
- **Manhole inspection** — verify breaker condition during turnaround:
  - **Visual**: corrosion, damage from agitator strikes.
  - **Dimensional**: still meets design specs.
  - **Repairs**: weld patch, replace.
- **Aplikacje specyficzne**:
  - **Reactor/CSTR outlet** — strong mixing creates rotation.
  - **Crude oil tank** — slow drainage but large volume → vortex z low level.
  - **Heat exchanger drain** — fast emptying → vortex.
  - **Sump pump suction** — gravity flow do sump → entering water rotates.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Anti-rotation baffles** w large tanks (vertical strakes on inner wall) — break rotation before reaching outlet.
  - **Multiple nozzles** spaced — distribute flow, no single high-velocity exit.
  - **Sumped nozzles** — recessed below tank bottom → less vortex.
  - **Air-entry vent** od top — prevents vacuum w tank during drainage (which can promote vortex).
  - **Mark min operating level** with high-level alarm — operator stops draining before vortex.
  - **Pre-startup inspection** new installations — verify breaker present + correct.
  - **Test pump at low level** during commissioning — verify no cavitation.

---

## Iteration 86 — 2026-05-26

### YYYYYYYYYY. Welder PPE — Helmet Shade Numbers, Auto-Darkening, PAPR + Fume Respirator
- **Welding hazards**:
  - **Arc radiation** — UV (200-400 nm) + visible + IR. **UV powoduje "arc eye" (photokeratitis)** w sekundy bez protection. Skin burns ("welder's flash") z prolonged exposure.
  - **Fumes + gases** — metal oxide particles (Cr, Mn, Ni, Zn, Pb), ozone (from UV), nitrogen oxides, phosgene (z chlorinated solvents).
  - **Heat + spatter** — hot metal droplets, ignition risk.
  - **Electrical shock** — open circuit voltage 50-100 V (touch hazardous).
  - **Noise** — air carbon arc gouging do 110 dB+.
- **Welding helmet** — primary protection:
  - **ANSI Z87.1+** standard (USA) lub **EN 175 + EN 169/EN 379** (EU).
  - **Z87+** = high-impact rated (vs Z87 basic).
  - **CSA Z94.3** (Canada).
  - **Auto-darkening filter (ADF)** — switches ze "passive shade 3-4" (clear-ish) do "active shade 8-13" w **0.1-0.5 ms** (faster = lepsze).
- **Shade numbers** (lens darkness):
  - **Higher number = darker** (logarithmic scale).
  - **Selection based on**:
    - Process type.
    - Current (amperage).
    - Electrode size.
  - **Shade chart** (standard ranges):
    - **Oxy-fuel cutting / brazing** (no arc): 3-6.
    - **SMAW** (stick welding): **shade 10 @ <100A**, **11 @ 100-150A**, **12 @ 150-250A**, **13 @ 250-350A**, **14 @ >350A**.
    - **GMAW (MIG)**: **10 @ <100A**, **11-12 @ 100-200A**, **13 @ 200-400A**.
    - **FCAW**: same jako GMAW.
    - **GTAW (TIG)**: **8 @ <30A**, **9-10 @ 30-100A**, **11-12 @ 100-200A**, **13 @ 200-400A**.
    - **Plasma cutting**: 9-12 zależnie od current.
    - **Carbon arc gouging**: **13-14** (very intense arc).
  - **Min shade dla aluminum** (more UV): +1 vs CS.
- **ADF (Auto-Darkening Filter) features**:
  - **Variable shade** dial — set per task.
  - **Sensitivity** — how sensitive trigger to arc light (set niski w sun, wysoki indoor).
  - **Delay** — how long after arc stops before lens lightens (0.1-1 sec).
  - **Grind mode** — locks shade ~4 dla grinding (no darkening flicker).
  - **Solar + battery powered** — usually self-charging.
- **Helmet types**:
  - **Fixed shade** — najtaniej, single shade only.
  - **ADF passive** — single fixed shade ADF.
  - **ADF variable** — adjustable shade 5-13 typowo, programowalne.
  - **Pancake hood** — flat shape, dla pipe welding (closer to workpiece).
- **Respiratory protection** — KRYTYCZNE dla welding fumes:
  - **OSHA PEL Cr(VI)**: **5 µg/m³** (very low, often exceeded w SS welding).
  - **OSHA PEL Mn**: **5 mg/m³** (frequently exceeded w heavy fab).
  - **NIOSH IDLH Cr(VI)**: **15 mg/m³**.
- **Respirator types**:
  - **N95 disposable** — particles only, no fit checking, OSHA min dla short exposures.
  - **Half-mask APR** (Air-Purifying Respirator) z P100 filters — **APF 10**.
  - **Full-face APR** — APF 50.
  - **PAPR (Powered Air-Purifying Respirator)** — battery blower forces air through HEPA filter, blows clean air do welding helmet shroud:
    - **APF 25 (loose-fitting)** lub **APF 1000 (tight-fitting)**.
    - **Positive pressure** inside helmet → fume nie wchodzi.
    - **Cooler** = comfort, less fatigue.
    - **Battery 4-8 h** zwykle.
  - **Supplied air respirator (SAR)** — fed od compressor lub bottle, **APF 1000-10,000**, dla highest hazard (confined space, sandblasting).
- **PAPR + welding helmet combo**:
  - **Integrated unit** — manufacturer (Optrel, Miller, Lincoln, ESAB, 3M Speedglas).
  - **Cool air** circulates wnętrze helmetu — comfort welder zwiększony 40-50%.
  - **OSHA preferred** dla SS welding (Cr-VI exposure).
  - **Cost**: $1,500-3,500 per unit (vs $200-500 standard helmet).
  - **Maintenance**: filter replacement co 200-500 h, battery yearly.
- **Other PPE**:
  - **Welding gloves** — chrome-tanned leather (heat resistant). **Long gauntlet TIG**, **short MIG**, **heavy SMAW**.
  - **Welding jacket / sleeves** — flame-resistant cotton (FR), leather, FR cotton-wool. Cover arms, torso.
  - **FR pants** — no synthetic (melts onto skin).
  - **Steel-toe boots** + spats — protect feet od spatter.
  - **Welder's cap (snood)** — under helmet, fire-resistant cotton, soaks sweat.
  - **Hearing protection** — earplugs lub muffs dla loud processes (gouging, plasma).
- **Safety glasses pod helmet**:
  - **Z87+ rated** safety glasses underneath ADF.
  - **Protect from ricochet sparks**, slag chips when helmet lifted.
  - **NIE używać prescription glasses zamiast Z87** — break easily.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong shade** dla amperage — too dark = can't see weld; too light = arc eye.
  - **ADF battery dead** — lens stuck w light state → exposure.
  - **Damaged ADF** (cracked screen) — light penetration → exposure.
  - **Helmet inadequate dla overhead work** — looking up, sparks fall behind helmet edge.
  - **No respirator** dla SS welding — chronic Cr-VI exposure.
  - **Stand in fume plume** — even z respirator, others exposed.
  - **Synthetic clothing under FR** — burns w fire scenario.
  - **No eye protection** podczas grinding (ADF assumes welding).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Annual lens inspection** — visible cracks, scratches, peripheral defects.
  - **Battery test** before shift (ADF auto-test mode).
  - **Spare lens** w toolbox dla emergencies.
  - **Local exhaust ventilation (LEV)** — fume extractor near arc, primary control.
  - **Confined space** — supplied air mandatory (oxygen + fume).
  - **Pre-shift fit test** — PAPR/SAR confirm seal.
  - **Hexavalent chromium** training — required for SS welding (OSHA).
  - **Hot work permit** — verify before each new location.
  - **Fire watch** during + 30 min after welding (covered iter 4).

### ZZZZZZZZZZ. Pipe Spool Prefabrication — Shop Workflow + Tracking
- **Co to "spool"**: prefabricated piping assembly, typically including:
  - Pipe sections.
  - Fittings (elbows, tees, reducers).
  - Flanges.
  - Sometimes valves, instrument connections.
  - **Length**: max ~12 m (limited dla truck transport, lifting).
- **Po co prefab vs field weld**:
  - **Controlled shop environment** — better weld quality (ground, indoor, jigged).
  - **Productivity 3-5×** vs field welding.
  - **Lower cost** — shop overhead vs scaffolding + access.
  - **QC easier** — NDT machines stationary, inspectors permanent.
  - **Field installation** — minimum welds (just spool-to-spool joints).
  - **Schedule compression** — parallel shop fab while site prep ongoing.
- **Typical project split**:
  - **80% spool weld w shop**.
  - **20% field welds** (spool-to-spool, spool-to-equipment, tie-ins).
- **Workflow stages**:
  1. **Design** — isometric drawings + Material Take-Off (MTO).
  2. **Material procurement** — pipe, fittings, flanges, gaskets, bolts.
  3. **Material receipt + inspection** — quantity check, mill test reports, PMI (Positive Material ID).
  4. **Spool design + drafting** — isometrics split do shippable lengths.
  5. **Cutting** — pipe od bulk lengths do required + bevel.
  6. **Fitting** — components positioned, tack welded.
  7. **Welding** — full weld z qualified procedure (WPS) + welder.
  8. **NDT** — VT, MT, PT, RT, UT per spec.
  9. **PWHT** (if required) — local stress relief.
  10. **Hydrostatic test** — usually deferred to system test field, but shop test option dla critical.
  11. **Surface prep + painting** — primer + topcoat per spec.
  12. **Dispatch** — load on trucks, deliver to site.
  13. **Field install** — pipe fitters position + weld spool-to-spool.
- **Shop layout**:
  - **Receiving area** — incoming materials.
  - **Storage racks** — pipe + fittings sorted by size/grade.
  - **Cutting bay** — saws (band saw, plasma cutter), pipe profiling machines.
  - **Bending bay** (if any field bends needed).
  - **Fitting tables** — pipe positioners (turning rolls) + jigs.
  - **Welding booths** — segregated (no cross-contamination dla SS vs CS).
  - **NDT bay** — dark room dla RT, separate dla noise (UT).
  - **PWHT bay** — induction or furnace.
  - **Paint shop** — separated, ventilated.
  - **Storage / dispatch area** — finished spools ready ship.
- **Pipe positioners + jigs**:
  - **Turning rolls** — variable speed, hold pipe horizontally, rotate dla weld access.
  - **Manipulators** — pipe positioning arms, multi-axis.
  - **Jigs** — fixed dimensions, hold components dla repeatable spool.
- **Welding automation**:
  - **Orbital welding** — for SS, sanitary, critical service. Automated heads circle pipe.
  - **Submerged Arc Welding (SAW)** — large pipe longitudinal seams (covered iter 77).
  - **Rotational welding** — pipe rotates, torch stationary (turn the work, not the torch).
- **Quality control**:
  - **Weld ID** każdy weld — unique identifier (e.g., spool-A123-W04).
  - **Welder ID** + qualification record per weld.
  - **Heat number traceability** — pipe + filler metal heat numbers logged.
  - **NDT records** — radiographs, UT scans, MT/PT reports stored, linked to weld ID.
  - **Inspection sign-off** — QC inspector signs each step.
- **Software / MES (Manufacturing Execution System)**:
  - **PCF (Piping Component File)** — industry standard data exchange format dla isometrics.
  - **Modern shops** parse PCF z CAD (SmartPlant, AutoCAD Plant 3D) → auto extract: cut lists, weld list, bend list, material BOM.
  - **MES tools** (PipeCloud, MSUITE, FabPro): track each spool digital twin, real-time progress, bottleneck detection.
- **Traceability requirements**:
  - **Material certificate** — pipe, fittings, weld consumables linked to spool.
  - **Welder certificates** — current qualifications per welder per process.
  - **PWHT chart** — temperature/time records for each PWHT'd weld.
  - **NDT reports** — bound w spool documentation package.
  - **Final inspection certificate** — released for shipment.
- **Pre-fab vs stick-build comparison**:
  - **Pre-fab**: 30-50% cost savings dla typical project, 30-40% schedule reduction.
  - **Stick-build (all field)**: more flexibility, no transport limits, but slower + costlier.
  - **Hybrid common** — large straight sections field, complex spools shop.
- **Transport considerations**:
  - **Truck max length**: 13-15 m typowo (longer = special permit, escort vehicles).
  - **Crane reach** at field — limit spool weight (typically <5-10 tons).
  - **Lifting points** dla each spool — marked clearly, designed dla CG.
  - **Padding** dla coated/lined pipe — protect during transport.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong cut dimensions** — fitting won't align w field.
  - **Incorrect orientation** dla branch off main — wrong nozzle direction.
  - **PWHT skipped** — shop forgot, found w field NDT.
  - **Coating damage** podczas transport — repair w field.
  - **Wrong material** — pipe spec mismatch (X42 vs X65) — caught przez PMI.
  - **Welder qualification expired** — weld rejected, redo.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-fab review meeting** — designers + shop manager + site supervisor — verify shippable, installable.
  - **Spool numbering** — match isometric numbering dla traceability.
  - **Tag every spool** at shop completion — laminated label z spool no, drawing no, weight, lifting point indicators.
  - **Photos before shipping** — record condition (claims dispute later).
  - **Site staging area** — receive + verify before installation begins.
  - **Hydrotest in shop** dla critical service — saves field hydrotest scaffolding cost.
  - **Standard fittings inventory** — common elbows, tees, reducers always stocked.

### AAAAAAAAAAA. Radiographic Testing (RT) — Film, Digital, IQI/Penetrameters
- **Po co**: NDT method dla **subsurface defects** w welds + materials. **Gamma ray (Ir-192, Co-60, Se-75) lub X-ray** penetrates metal, exposes film/sensor — defects visible as dark spots (more transmission through reduced material).
- **Defects detected**:
  - **Porosity** (round dark spots) — gas inclusions.
  - **Slag inclusions** (irregular dark) — trapped flux/oxide.
  - **Lack of fusion / penetration** (linear darks) — incomplete welding.
  - **Cracks** (sharp linear) — most critical.
  - **Undercut** (linear adjacent to weld toe).
  - **Burn-through** (large dark).
  - **Internal misalignment / mismatch** (geometry).
- **Source types**:
  - **X-ray** (electrically generated):
    - **Tube voltage**: 100-500 kV — varied dla thickness.
    - **Pros**: variable energy, no radioactive material (no source disposal).
    - **Cons**: bulky equipment, electrical, not portable.
    - **Used**: shop installations, computed/digital radiography.
  - **Gamma ray** (radioactive isotopes — capsule sealed source):
    - **Ir-192 (iridium)** — most common, 380-600 keV, thickness 6-75 mm steel.
    - **Co-60 (cobalt)** — 1.17-1.33 MeV, thickness 50-150 mm steel, dla thick sections.
    - **Se-75 (selenium)** — 100-400 keV, thickness 3-30 mm, dla thin sections.
    - **Yb-169 (ytterbium)** — low energy, thin steel + Al.
    - **Pros**: portable (camera + crank-out source), no electrical (field-friendly).
    - **Cons**: source decays (Ir-192 half-life 74 days, Co-60 5.27 years), radioactive permits.
- **Film radiography** — traditional method:
  - **Film placed behind pipe** (opposite source side).
  - **Lead screens** (front + back) intensify image + filter scattered radiation.
  - **Exposure time**: seconds do minutes zależnie od thickness + source intensity.
  - **Develop film** w chemistry (D-19 developer, fixer, water rinse).
  - **Interpretation**: light box + magnifier.
  - **Storage**: film archived dla 5-10 lat dla critical service.
- **Computed Radiography (CR)** — intermediate technology:
  - **Imaging plate** (phosphor screen) zamiast film.
  - **After exposure**, plate scanned z laser → digital image.
  - **Plate reusable** (vs single-use film).
  - **Cost intermediate** vs film and DR.
- **Digital Radiography (DR)** — newest method:
  - **Detector** (flat panel, line array) → digital signal → computer image.
  - **Real-time** (or near-real-time) image.
  - **No chemicals**, no film handling.
  - **Higher sensitivity** dla some applications.
  - **Image enhancement** software possible (contrast, edge detection).
- **IQI (Image Quality Indicator) / Penetrameter** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Po co**: verify że radiograph **DEMONSTRATES required sensitivity** — without IQI, radiograph nieoważny.
  - **Placed on source side** of weld, w field of view.
  - **Types** (per ASME V):
    - **Hole-type (ASTM E1025)** — flat plate z 3 drilled holes (1T, 2T, 4T diameter, T = plate thickness).
    - **Wire-type (ASTM E747 / EN 462)** — set of 6-7 wires of decreasing diameter.
- **Wire IQI selection** (ASME V T-276):
  - **Material thickness + group** determines which wire set.
  - **6 wires** w each set, diameters z 0.05 mm do 4.0 mm.
  - **Sensitivity = (visible wire diameter) / (specimen thickness) × 100%**.
  - **Typical requirement**: 2% sensitivity — smallest visible wire ≤2% specimen thickness.
- **Hole IQI levels** (1-1T, 2-2T, 4-2T):
  - **"2-2T"** = 2% thickness IQI, 2T hole visible.
  - **Required for most welding** code applications.
  - **Higher = more critical** (X-ray @1-2T = better resolution required).
- **Exposure technique**:
  - **Single Wall Single Image (SWSI)**: source one side, film other side → image one wall. Standard dla flat plates lub accessible pipe.
  - **Double Wall Single Image (DWSI)**: source outside, film inside lub vice versa → both walls superimposed → interpretable for small diameter (≤3.5"). Both walls in image.
  - **Double Wall Double Image (DWDI)**: source outside z offset position → both walls shown separately (elliptical exposure). Most common dla small pipe (no internal access).
- **Source-to-Film Distance (SFD)**:
  - **Minimum**: usually 7× weld length (geometric unsharpness limit Ug ≤0.5 mm).
  - **Calculation**: Ug = (focal spot size × specimen-to-film distance) / SFD.
  - **More SFD** = better image quality but longer exposure.
- **Radiation safety**:
  - **NCRP / ICRP** dose limits — public 1 mSv/year, occupational 50 mSv/year.
  - **Exclusion zone** — calculated radius z source activity, time, distance.
  - **Survey meter** — Geiger counter, verify zero dose at exclusion boundary.
  - **TLD badges** dla radiographers — personal monitoring.
  - **Source storage** — concrete bunker (camera "exposure pit").
- **Common errors**:
  - **Insufficient density** (film too light) — over-exposed lub wrong technique.
  - **Excessive density** (too dark) — under-exposed.
  - **No visible IQI** — radiograph rejected, retest.
  - **Geometric unsharpness** — fuzzy edges, can't detect small defects.
  - **Scatter** — fogs film, reduces contrast → lead screens + collimators help.
