  - **Wrong exposure time** — chart + experience dla pipe size + thickness + source.
- **Radiograph interpretation**:
  - **Density check** — film d 2.0-3.5 standard (per ASME V).
  - **IQI check** — required holes / wire visible.
  - **Defect indications** — measured + classified per applicable code:
    - **ASME B31.3 §344.5** dla process piping.
    - **API 1104** dla pipelines.
    - **AWS D1.1** dla structural welds.
- **Acceptance criteria** (typical ASME B31.3):
  - **Cracks**: ZERO tolerance.
  - **Porosity**: cumulative diameter ≤ 1.5× t / 12T (varies).
  - **Slag**: cumulative length ≤ ½T per Tx (length).
  - **Lack of fusion / penetration**: ZERO at root.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Coordinate z RT crew** — schedule welds w batches dla source efficiency.
  - **Mark weld direction** + start point — operator references same in radiograph.
  - **Clean weld surface** — slag/spatter removed (creates artifacts).
  - **Identification** — lead numbers on film (welder ID, weld ID, date) — incorporated w image.
  - **NDT report** — link to weld map, traceability.
  - **Time + distance + shielding** — radiographer safety triad.
  - **Hold time** — radiographer announces "shoot" — area cleared, people evacuate.

---

## Iteration 87 — 2026-05-26

### BBBBBBBBBBB. Phased Array Ultrasonic Testing (PAUT) — Pipe Weld Inspection
- **Co to**: advanced UT method, **multi-element probe** (32-128 piezo elements) z elektronicznie sterowanymi **time delays** każdy element → forms steered + focused ultrasonic beam. Vs conventional UT (single-element).
- **Vs RT (radiography)**:
  - **No radiation** — no exclusion zone, no permits → 24/7 work możliwe.
  - **Real-time** wyniki — vs RT film/digital processing.
  - **Better dla planar defects** (cracks, lack of fusion) — RT misses tight cracks parallel to beam.
  - **Worse dla volumetric** (porosity, slag) — RT lepsze.
  - **Modern code (ASME B31.3 2022)**: AUT/PAUT acceptable substitute dla RT w wielu cases.
- **Po co PAUT vs UT**:
  - **Multiple angles** w jednym probe — covers wider weld area + crown + root w one pass.
  - **Faster inspection** — 3-5× szybsze niż conventional UT.
  - **Permanent digital record** — recorded scan z encoder, can re-analyze.
  - **Detailed sizing** — defect length, height, depth z mm accuracy.
  - **Imaging** — S-scan (sectorial), B-scan (cross-section), C-scan (top view).
- **Komponenty systemu**:
  - **Probe** — 16-128 element transducer array (1-5 MHz typowo).
  - **Wedge** — angled plastic block (Rexolite, lucite) attaches probe → directs sound beam at angle do pipe.
  - **Couplant** — gel / glycerine / water → acoustic coupling pipe ↔ wedge.
  - **Encoder** — wheel rolling along pipe → measures probe position → links scan data do location.
  - **Scanner** — manual lub motorized fixture trzymający probe.
  - **Acquisition unit** — Olympus OmniScan, Eddyfi Mantis, Phoenix ISL.
- **Scan types** (visualizations):
  - **A-scan**: time vs amplitude (classic UT) — basic, one element view.
  - **B-scan**: cross-section through pipe wall — depth vs length.
  - **C-scan**: top-down view — defect location + size mapped on weld.
  - **S-scan (Sectorial)**: fan of angles z one probe position (40-70° typical) — detects defects at various orientations.
  - **L-scan (Linear)**: parallel beams od successive elements → covers wide area at fixed angle.
  - **TFM (Total Focusing Method)** — newer, computes image z full matrix capture (FMC), even higher resolution.
- **Beam angles** dla welds:
  - **45°** — common, good general purpose.
  - **60°** — better dla LOF root.
  - **70°** — wider coverage, but reduced signal.
  - **S-scan 40-70°** — covers all angles automatically.
- **Probe element configuration**:
  - **Linear array** — elements w row, scans linearly.
  - **Matrix array (2D)** — elements w grid, 2D beam steering.
  - **Annular** — circular ring elements, focus z varying depths.
  - **Curved** — pre-shaped dla pipe OD (no wedge needed dla small radius).
- **Scan plan**:
  - **Designed before inspection** dla each weld geometry:
    - Pipe OD + wall thickness.
    - Weld centerline + width.
    - Beveling type (J-prep, V-prep, U-prep).
    - Material (sound velocity różni się: CS 5900 m/s, SS 5790, Al 6300, Cu 4660).
  - **Software** — ESBeamTool, OmniScan setup wizard — input pipe + weld data → recommend probe + wedge + angles.
  - **Coverage verification** — ray tracing shows beam paths, verify all weld zones reached.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **In-service weld inspection** — pipe online, no shutdown.
  - **Corrosion mapping** — wall thickness via C-scan over wide area.
  - **New construction welds** — code-compliant inspection (per ASME V Article 4).
  - **Crack detection + sizing** — particularly cracks parallel do pipe axis.
- **Calibration**:
  - **Reference block** z known reflectors (notches, side-drilled holes, flat-bottom holes).
  - **Sensitivity calibration** — set gain at reference response.
  - **TCG (Time-Corrected Gain)** — adjusts dla beam spread + attenuation z depth.
  - **Recalibration**: każda zmiana setup, every 4 h shift, after probe damage.
- **Code coverage**:
  - **ASME V Article 4** — PAUT procedure qualification.
  - **ASME B31.3 §344.5.1** — PAUT acceptable substitute dla RT (z restrictions).
  - **API 5L Annex K** — PAUT dla line pipe.
  - **DNV GL** dla offshore.
- **Defect characterization** (sizing methods):
  - **6 dB drop** — amplitude method dla length.
  - **20 dB drop** — wider area, dla irregular defects.
  - **Tip diffraction** — for crack height (depth od top to tip).
  - **TOFD (Time-of-Flight Diffraction)** — often combined z PAUT dla complete inspection (lateral wave + back wall + tip signals).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Poor calibration** — false sizing, missed defects.
  - **Improper couplant** — insufficient ultrasonic transmission.
  - **Wrong wedge angle** dla pipe geometry — coverage gaps.
  - **Probe damage** (cracked face, loose elements) — false indications.
  - **Encoder slip** — wrong location of indication.
  - **Surface roughness** — couplant pools, false signals.
  - **Magnetic / electrical interference** — w electrical / motor environment.
- **Cost vs RT comparison**:
  - **PAUT equipment**: $30,000-100,000 upfront.
  - **RT equipment**: $50,000-150,000 upfront + ongoing source replacement.
  - **PAUT operator** ASNT Level II/III certified — higher pay rate.
  - **Per-weld cost**: PAUT ~30-50% lower than RT dla typical pipe.
  - **Schedule**: PAUT faster (no exposure time, no film developing).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Surface prep**: clean, grind smooth (Ra <12.5 µm), no spatter at scan area.
  - **Couplant cleanup** po inspekcji — corrosive jeśli left.
  - **Probe orientation** marked — index point reference dla position consistency.
  - **Scan plan** w QC documentation — auditable per code.
  - **Operator certification** — ASNT SNT-TC-1A Level II minimum, Level III dla complex applications.
  - **Inspection record retention** — 5-10 lat per project requirements.

### CCCCCCCCCCC. Pipe Insulation Installation — Mineral Wool, Calsil, Jacketing
- **Po co**: thermal insulation pipes dla:
  - **Energy conservation** — reduce heat loss (hot lines) lub heat gain (cold lines).
  - **Personnel protection** — hot pipe surface burns (skin contact >70°C = burn w sekundy).
  - **Process stability** — maintain T (cooling oil, heat tracing redukcja).
  - **Condensation control** — cold lines (chilled water, LNG, refrigerant).
  - **Freeze protection** — water lines w cold climates.
  - **Noise reduction** — gas pipes z flow noise.
- **Insulation materials** — typowo wybierane przez T range:
  - **Mineral wool / stone wool** (Rockwool):
    - **T range**: -100°C do **+750°C** (some products do +1000°C).
    - **Density**: 60-160 kg/m³.
    - **Thermal conductivity**: 0.035-0.050 W/(m·K) @ 50°C.
    - **Fire-resistant** (non-combustible, melt point 1100°C).
    - **Water repellent** but vapor-permeable.
    - **Aplikacje**: hot lines, steam, hot oil, general industrial.
  - **Calcium silicate (Calsil)**:
    - **T range**: do **+650°C (1200°F)** (some Type V do +815°C).
    - **High compressive strength** (>1000 kPa).
    - **Rigid pre-formed shapes** — half-pipe sections + blocks.
    - **Aplikacje**: high-T steam, pipe supports (high compressive resistance), areas requiring abuse resistance.
    - **Class A fire rating**.
  - **Cellular glass (Foamglas)**:
    - **T range**: -260°C do +482°C.
    - **Vapor-impermeable** (closed cells).
    - **Aplikacje**: cryogenic, cold lines (LNG, ethylene), where moisture critical.
  - **Polyurethane foam (PUR/PIR)**:
    - **T range**: -260°C do +120°C.
    - **Excellent insulation** (low k = 0.022-0.028).
    - **Aplikacje**: cold lines, district heating, refrigeration.
  - **Aerogel** (Pyrogel, Cryogel):
    - **T range**: -270°C do +650°C.
    - **Highest insulation value** (k = 0.013-0.025).
    - **Thin** (3-10 mm vs 50-100 mm mineral wool dla same effect).
    - **Aplikacje**: space-restricted, retrofit, energy upgrades.
    - **Cost**: 10-50× mineral wool.
  - **Elastomeric foam (Armaflex)**:
    - **T range**: -50°C do +110°C.
    - **Closed cell** — built-in vapor barrier.
    - **Aplikacje**: HVAC, refrigeration, chilled water.
  - **Aramid (Nomex)** + **silica blanket** — high-T fireproofing.
- **Insulation form factors**:
  - **Pipe sections** (half-shells) — pre-formed dla standard pipe ODs.
  - **Blanket / batt** — flexible roll, wrapped around pipe.
  - **Loose fill** — poured/pneumatic, dla irregular shapes.
  - **Spray foam** — applied liquid, expands + cures (PUR).
- **Layered installation**:
  - **Inner layer** — material specific to T (calcium silicate dla hot, foam dla cold).
  - **Outer layer** — opcjonalnie second layer dla extra thickness, joint offset.
  - **Vapor barrier** (jeśli wymagany) — between insulation + jacket lub on cold side.
  - **Jacket / cladding** — outer protection.
- **Joints**:
  - **Stagger joints** — adjacent layers offset 90° lub at least 50 mm.
  - **Glue seams** dla closed-cell foams (adhesive).
  - **Wire / banding** — galvanized wire lub stainless straps, every 200-300 mm.
- **Vapor barrier** — KRYTYCZNE dla cold lines:
  - **Cel**: prevents water vapor migration od warm side → cold pipe → condensation w insulation → wet insulation = ineffective + corrosive.
  - **Materiały**:
    - **Aluminum foil-faced kraft paper** (FSK).
    - **PVC vapor barrier**.
    - **Mastic coating** (asphalt or rubber).
  - **Continuous** — joints sealed z tape lub mastic.
  - **Inside jacket** — protected od physical damage.
  - **Hot lines NO vapor barrier** — moisture exits, no condensation risk.
- **Outer jacket (cladding)**:
  - **Aluminum (Al)** — najpopularniejsze, ekonomiczne, **0.4-0.8 mm** grubości:
    - Smooth, stucco-embossed (Tex Tex), corrugated (3/16" cross-crimped) finishes.
    - **Polyfilm moisture barrier** laminate (3 mil) na wewnętrznej stronie — corrosion protection.
    - **NOT for sea coast** (salt corrosion).
  - **Stainless steel (304/316)** — przemysł chemicalny, marine, **0.5-1.0 mm**:
    - Corrosion resistant.
    - More expensive.
    - **Required dla** chloride environments, food/pharma, high-grade processes.
  - **PVDF-coated aluminum** (Kynar) — premium, UV-resistant, chemical-resistant.
  - **PVC jacket** — temporary, indoor only.
  - **GRP / FRP** — composite, corrosion-resistant.
- **Jacket installation**:
  - **Cut to length** for each pipe section.
  - **Overlap** — minimum **75 mm** dla horizontal joints.
  - **Sheet metal screws** OR **bands** dla fastening (depending on jacket material).
  - **Self-tapping screws** (SS for SS jacket) — pre-drilled holes optional.
  - **Sealant** at seams — silicone or polysulfide.
- **Insulation thickness sizing**:
  - **Thermal calculation**: heat loss limit (W/m), or surface T target (<60°C for personnel).
  - **Typical thicknesses**:
    - **Steam DN 50**: 50-75 mm.
    - **Steam DN 200**: 100-150 mm.
    - **Steam DN 500**: 150-200 mm.
    - **Cold lines** smaller (lower T differential typically).
  - **Software**: 3E Plus (NAIMA) free, calculates optimal thickness.
- **Special locations**:
  - **Flanges + valves** — removable insulation jackets (Velcro lub buckle) — można otwierać dla maintenance.
  - **Elbows** — pre-formed pieces, lub mitered segments.
  - **Tees** — special pre-formed, lub field-cut + sealed.
  - **Equipment** (vessels, exchangers) — wrap-around blankets lub spray foam.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Brak vapor barrier** na cold line → wet insulation → freeze + expand → cracks → ineffective.
  - **Wrong material dla T** — mineral wool nad melts above limit.
  - **Compressed insulation** (squeezed tight z bands) — reduces k value (lower density better dla thin insulation).
  - **Gaps + joints not sealed** — thermal bridges.
  - **Personnel protection skipped** — burn injuries.
  - **Jacket installed loose** — wind blow off w outdoor (storm damage).
  - **Bands too tight** — cuts through insulation, especially soft mineral wool.
  - **Wrong-pipe shape** (jacket dla larger pipe) — voids inside, moisture pockets.
- **CUI (Corrosion Under Insulation)** — common, expensive problem:
  - **Mechanism**: water enters jacket (rain, washdown, condensation) → wets insulation → in contact z pipe metal → corrosion + crevice corrosion.
  - **Worst zones**: 60-150°C carbon steel (water present, T high enough dla rate increase).
  - **Mitigation**:
    - Closed-cell foam if T allows.
    - Vapor barrier maintained.
    - Periodic insulation removal + inspection.
    - Pipe coating + paint under insulation.
    - Designed drainage at low points.
- **Hot lines vs cold lines key differences**:
  - **Hot lines**: NO vapor barrier; sloped to shed water from above; weep holes at low points.
  - **Cold lines**: vapor barrier CRITICAL; sealed all joints; condensation drains.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-installation**: pipe surface clean, dry, painted/primed for CUI protection.
  - **Heat trace BEFORE insulation** — tracer cables installed first.
  - **Trace adhesive** — between heat trace + pipe for thermal coupling.
  - **Inspection access**: removable jackets at strategic points (NDT zones, valves).
  - **Identification markers** — flag tags showing flow direction, contents, T extremes.
  - **Don't damage existing insulation** — repair/reapply if disturbed for work.
  - **PPE for installer**: respirator (mineral wool dust), gloves, long sleeves.

### DDDDDDDDDDD. Confined Space Entry — Vessel + Pipe Internal Work
- **Definicja (OSHA 1910.146)**: confined space = wszystkie 3 criteria:
  1. **Large enough** dla employee to enter + perform work.
  2. **Limited/restricted means for entry/exit**.
  3. **Not designed for continuous occupancy**.
- **Permit-Required Confined Space (PRCS)** — additionally has 1+ z:
  - **Hazardous atmosphere** (oxygen, flammable, toxic).
  - **Engulfment potential** (loose material, liquid).
  - **Configuration trap** (slope, taper to suffocation).
  - **Any other recognized serious hazard** (mech, electrical, radiation).
- **Common examples**:
  - **Storage tanks**, vessel interiors, manholes, sewers, tunnels, pits, large pipe sections (>DN 800 entered), heat exchanger channels.
- **Hazards inside confined space**:
  - **Oxygen deficiency** — most common cause death. <19.5% = impaired, <16% = severe. Causes: combustion, oxidation, displacement by inert gas (N₂ purge), microbial activity.
  - **Oxygen enrichment** >23.5% — fire/explosion hazard, materials burn violently.
  - **Flammable gases / vapors** — methane, H₂, propane — measure LEL.
  - **Toxic gases** — H₂S, CO, NH₃, Cl₂ — measure ppm.
  - **Engulfment** — grain bins, sand, sludge.
  - **Heat stress** — poor ventilation + hot work = collapse.
- **Atmospheric testing** — KRYTYCZNE (order matters):
  1. **Oxygen FIRST** — acceptable **19.5-23.5%**. (Other meters need O₂ to work correctly.)
  2. **Flammable** — LEL must be **<10%** (per OSHA).
  3. **Toxic** — H₂S **<10 ppm** (PEL), CO **<25 ppm**, NH₃ **<25 ppm**, others per substance.
  - **Test multiple levels**: top, middle, bottom (gases stratify — H₂S heavier than air, sinks; NH₃ lighter, rises).
  - **Continuous monitoring** podczas entry — 4-gas detector worn by entrant.
- **Permit-to-work**:
  - **Written permit** required przed entry.
  - **Validity**: typowo 8 h shift.
  - **Information**:
    - Space description + location.
    - Hazards.
    - Atmospheric test results.
    - Ventilation requirements.
    - PPE required.
    - Communication method.
    - Rescue plan.
    - Entrant + attendant + supervisor names.
- **Roles** (OSHA 1910.146):
  - **Entrant** — person entering, qualified, monitor self + communicate.
  - **Attendant (watch person)** — outside space, **NEVER enters**, maintains communication, can summon rescue.
  - **Entry supervisor** — authorizes entry, verifies all controls in place.
  - **Rescue team** — qualified, equipment ready, response time <5 min.
- **Ventilation**:
  - **Mechanical ventilation** — forced air (blower) lub natural draft.
  - **Air changes**: minimum **5-7 per hour** (some applications 10+).
  - **Position** — air supply 0.6-1 m above floor, exhaust opposite side.
  - **Type**:
    - **Supply** (positive pressure) — pushes fresh air in.
    - **Exhaust** (negative pressure) — pulls hazards out.
    - **Combined** — push fresh in, pull contaminated out.
  - **Hot work**: continuous ventilation throughout work + 30 min after.
- **PPE inside confined space**:
  - **Respirator**:
    - **APR** (air-purifying respirator) — only if hazard known + filter rated.
    - **SAR (Supplied Air Respirator)** — z compressor lub bottle, **REQUIRED** dla IDLH (Immediately Dangerous to Life and Health) atmospheres.
    - **SCBA (Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus)** — back-mounted air bottle, dla rescue or short tasks.
    - **Egress bottle** — emergency 5-15 min supply, dla escape.
  - **Body harness** — full body z dorsal D-ring, attached to retrieval line.
  - **Retrieval line** — mechanical hoist if entrant >1.5 m below entry.
  - **Hard hat**, gloves, eye protection, FRC clothing.
  - **Hearing protection** dla noisy work.
- **Rescue equipment**:
  - **Tripod / davit** + winch — non-entry retrieval.
  - **Retrieval line** attached do harness — pull out without re-entry.
  - **SCBA** + spare bottles dla rescuers.
  - **First aid + AED** at entry point.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Single gas detector** (just LEL) — misses O₂, toxic — must be 4-gas (or specific per hazards).
  - **Entrant alone** — no attendant — fatal.
  - **Attendant enters dla rescue** — 60%+ of confined space deaths are would-be rescuers without proper procedure.
  - **Inadequate ventilation** — gases concentrate.
  - **Hot work without permit** — ignition w flammable atmosphere.
  - **Stratification not measured** — top OK, bottom toxic.
  - **No isolation of process** — pipe valve passing → gas/liquid enters space.
- **Pre-entry preparation** dla pipe / vessel:
  - **Isolation**: blind flanges, lock-out-tag-out (LOTO), disconnect lines lub spectacle blinds set.
  - **Drain + flush** — remove residual material.
  - **Wash + steam** — clean residue (carcinogens, biofilm).
  - **Ventilate** — air blower 30+ min before testing.
  - **Atmospheric test** — verify acceptable.
  - **Visual inspection** — known hazards (sharp objects, exposed wiring).
  - **Heat dissipation** — cool surfaces przed entry (recent service).
- **Hot work inside confined space** — additional permits:
  - **Hot work permit** + confined space permit + JSA.
  - **Fire watch** — at entry point.
  - **Extra ventilation** — fumes + sparks.
  - **Spark / slag containment** — fire blankets nearby.
- **Communication**:
  - **Voice contact** if space small + open.
  - **Radio / intercom** dla larger spaces.
  - **Hand signal protocol** — pre-arranged signals.
  - **Continuous monitoring** — attendant calls entrant every 5-15 min.
- **Time limits**:
  - **Continuous occupancy** limited — heat stress, oxygen consumption.
  - **Rotation crews** if work >2-4 h.
  - **Rest breaks** outside confined space.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Annual training** — refresher dla all entrants + attendants.
  - **Practice rescue drills** annually — verify equipment, response time.
  - **Calibrate gas detectors** daily — bump test before use.
  - **Multi-gas alarm** — auditory + visual when limits exceeded.
  - **Continuous radio** dla isolated spaces.
  - **Buddy system** — never solo work in confined space.
  - **Stop work authority** — anyone can call STOP for safety concerns.
  - **Post-job review** — lessons learned, improve procedures.

---

## Iteration 88 — 2026-05-26

### EEEEEEEEEEE. Magnetic Particle Testing (MT) + Dye Penetrant Testing (PT) — Surface NDT
- **Po co**: detect **surface + near-surface defects** w welds + base material. **MT** dla **ferromagnetic** materials (CS, low-alloy, martensitic SS). **PT** dla **all materials** w tym non-magnetic (300-series SS, Al, Cu, Ti, plastics).
- **Defects detected**:
  - **MT**: surface cracks (najczulsze do **0.1 mm głębokie**), near-surface (do **3-6 mm** below surface), seams, laps, laminations.
  - **PT**: surface-breaking only — cracks, porosity, pinholes, leaks, lack of fusion at weld toe.
- **Vs RT/UT**:
  - **MT/PT**: surface + slightly subsurface only.
  - **RT**: volumetric (porosity, slag inclusions, voids).
  - **UT/PAUT**: volumetric + planar (cracks, LOF) anywhere w cross-section.
  - **Często combination** — RT/UT dla bulk + MT/PT dla surface finish quality.

### Magnetic Particle Testing (MT) — szczegóły

- **Princip**: pipe **magnetized** (electric current lub yoke z permanent magnet/electromagnet) → defekt na powierzchni **breaks magnetic field** → creates **flux leakage** → **iron particles (magnetic ink)** cluster at leakage point → visual indication.
- **Methods**:
  - **Yoke method** — najpopularniejsze, portable. Electromagnet z dwoma legs ("U" shape), forms localized magnetic field between legs. **Limited area** per shot (75-200 mm dla typical yoke).
  - **Prods (contact electrodes)** — wbijane do pipe surface, AC current flows → magnetic field around prods. Larger area, ale **arc burns** risk (small spark @ contact point).
  - **Coil method** — pipe inserted w coil, longitudinal magnetic field. Used dla bars, small parts.
  - **Central conductor** — cable z high current run inside pipe → circumferential magnetic field. **Best dla circumferential defects** w pipe.
- **Magnetic field orientation** matters:
  - **Defekt perpendicular do field lines** = visible (cuts through field).
  - **Defekt parallel do field lines** = invisible.
  - **2 perpendicular shots required** dla complete coverage.
- **Particle types**:
  - **Dry powder** — fine iron particles (Fe₃O₄), shaken on surface — gravity does work. Best dla rough surfaces, large parts, no UV.
  - **Wet suspension** — particles suspended w oil lub water → sprayed → indication wzbiera.
  - **Fluorescent particles** — UV (black light) inspection — much more sensitive — used z dark room or shroud.
- **AC vs DC current**:
  - **AC** — surface defects only, "skin effect" concentrates current at surface.
  - **DC / half-wave DC** — penetrates deeper (1.5-3 mm below surface), detects near-surface.
  - **Mixed AC + DC** — best of both, sometimes used.
- **Procedure** (ASME V Article 7):
  1. **Surface prep** — clean weld + 25 mm beyond, remove scale/spatter/coating.
  2. **Demagnetize** if previously magnetized (ironwork = false retention).
  3. **Apply magnetizing force** (yoke contact, current via prods).
  4. **Apply particles** during magnetizing (continuous method) lub immediately after (residual method).
  5. **Inspect** w 1000 lux white light (white) lub 1000+ µW/cm² UV (fluorescent).
  6. **Repeat z perpendicular orientation** of field.
  7. **Demagnetize** (especially carbon steel, dla welding/machining downstream).
- **Acceptance** (per ASME B31.3 §344.3):
  - **Linear indication** (length >3× width): **2-6 mm** depending on material thickness.
  - **Rounded indications**: **<6 mm dia** typical.
  - **Cracks**: **ZERO tolerance**.
- **Yoke vs prods comparison**:
  - **Yoke**:
    - **Easy use**, no electrical hazards.
    - **AC magnetizing** (surface only).
    - **Limited area** per shot (75-200 mm).
    - **Field strength** verified z lifting test (10 lb lift @ 75 mm gap).
  - **Prods**:
    - **DC/AC current** capability.
    - **Larger area** (up do 600 mm spacing).
    - **Arc burn risk** at contact points (defect possible od this).
    - **Not used na critical welds** w high-quality service.

### Liquid Penetrant Testing (PT) — szczegóły

- **Princip**: liquid dye **wicked into surface-breaking defect** by capillary action → excess removed → developer pulls dye out → indication appears.
- **Penetrant types**:
  - **Visible color (red)** — daylight inspection.
  - **Fluorescent** — yellow-green dye, UV inspection — **3-10× more sensitive**.
- **Removal types**:
  - **Solvent removable** — wiped z solvent (most field use).
  - **Water washable** — built-in emulsifier, water spray removes.
  - **Post-emulsifiable** — separate emulsifier applied before water.
- **Standard procedure** (ASME V Article 6):
  1. **Surface prep** — clean, dry, free of paint/oil/scale (penetrant won't enter contaminated defect).
  2. **Apply penetrant** — spray, brush, lub dip.
  3. **Dwell time** — **10-30 min** (varies z material + temperature). Allows penetration into defects.
  4. **Remove excess** — wipe z solvent (don't flush directly with solvent — washes penetrant out of cracks).
  5. **Apply developer** — fine white powder lub liquid. Acts as blotter, draws penetrant out of defects.
  6. **Develop time** — 7-30 min — dye bleeds out, indication grows.
  7. **Inspect** w white light (visible) lub UV (fluorescent).
- **Developer types**:
  - **Dry powder** — sprinkled, simple.
  - **Wet (water-based suspension)** — sprayed, more uniform coverage.
  - **Solvent suspension (non-aqueous)** — spray, evaporates leaving white film. Most sensitive.
- **Pre-cleaning** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Mechanical cleaning** (wire brush) — pushes dirt/scale INTO defect, blocking penetration.
  - **Chemical cleaning** preferred — solvent wipe, vapor degrease.
  - **Surface contamination** = false rejection (no penetration where dirt is) lub missed defect.
- **Temperature limits**:
  - **Standard penetrants**: **10-50°C** application range.
  - **Below 10°C**: penetrant viscous, slow penetration.
  - **Above 50°C**: dries before reaching defect.
  - **High-T penetrants** dostępne dla weld inspection on hot pipe (do 200°C).
- **Acceptance criteria** — same as MT (ASME B31.3 §344.3).
- **Common errors MT**:
  - **Insufficient field strength** — small defects missed.
  - **Only one orientation** — defects parallel to field invisible.
  - **Contaminated particles** (oxide, dust) — false indications.
  - **No demagnetization** — interferes z downstream welding (arc blow).
  - **Inspection from too far** — small indications missed (max 0.6 m viewing distance).
- **Common errors PT**:
  - **Insufficient dwell** — penetrant nie reach defect bottom.
  - **Too long dwell** — penetrant dries → bleed-out fails.
  - **Wrong cleaning** (mechanical) — defects blocked.
  - **Wrong developer thickness** — too thick blocks bleed, too thin = no contrast.
  - **Inspection over UV cutoff** (sunlight in fluorescent test) — masks indications.
  - **Reapply penetrant w defect found before re-test** — false positive (contaminated).
- **Inspection lighting**:
  - **Visible dye**: minimum **1000 lux** white light at inspection surface.
  - **Fluorescent**: minimum **1000 µW/cm²** UV-A (365 nm) + dark room (<20 lux ambient).
- **MT + PT selection guide**:
  - **CS/low-alloy welds**: **MT preferred** (more sensitive, cheaper, faster).
  - **SS 300-series, Al, Cu, Ti, plastics**: **PT only option** (non-magnetic).
  - **Plant-quality service**: PT 100% on welds + MT supplemental.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **MT yoke battery check** — drained = weak field = missed defects.
  - **Lift test** verification (10 lb @ 75 mm) — start of shift.
  - **PT temperature compatibility** — verify product spec dla pipe T.
  - **Cleanup** post-inspection — penetrant residue → can hide future defects, MT particles → corrosion.
  - **Penetrant disposal** — hazardous waste per EPA.
  - **Personnel certification** — ASNT SNT-TC-1A Level II minimum, Level III dla procedure approval.

### FFFFFFFFFFF. Pipe Hydrostatic Test Procedure — ASME B31.3
- **Po co**: **proof test** integrity ukończonego rurociągu before commissioning:
  - **Verify strength** — pressure exceeds design.
  - **Detect leaks** — at joints, welds, fittings.
  - **Qualify joints** not covered by NDT (e.g., flanged + threaded).
  - **Validate installation** — pipe doesn't move/bend under pressure.
- **Test pressure** (ASME B31.3 §345.4):
  - **Standard**: **1.5× design pressure** w temperaturze ambient.
  - **Adjustment dla temperature**: P_test = 1.5 × P_design × (S_test/S_design), gdzie S = allowable stress.
  - **Practical**: dla typical CS @ 20°C, S_test/S_design ≈ 1, → **P_test = 1.5 × P_design**.
  - **Conservative limit**: P_test nie może przekroczyć **yield stress** of pipe (avoid permanent deformation).
- **Hold time**:
  - **Minimum 10 min** per ASME B31.3 §345.4.2(c).
  - **Practice**: often **1-4 h** for reliable leak detection + verification.
  - **Code req**: visual examination during hold dla leaks.
- **Test fluid**:
  - **Water default** — incompressible (low stored energy), readily available, non-flammable, non-toxic.
  - **Alternative liquids** if water causes issues:
    - **Freezing risk** (-T) → **methanol-water mix** lub **glycol-water**.
    - **Stainless steel** (chloride-free water required) → DI water lub potable z Cl⁻ <50 ppm.
    - **Process compatibility issue** → process medium itself (rare).
- **Hydrostatic vs pneumatic**:
  - **Hydro (water)**: **default**, safer (low stored energy, large rupture = low blast).
  - **Pneumatic (air/N₂)**: only if hydro impractical (no water source, cryogenic line, large vacuum vessel).
  - **Pneumatic risks**: stored energy 1000× water, catastrophic rupture risk → strict controls + exclusion zones.
- **Pre-test preparation**:
  - **Visual inspection** completed — all welds NDT'd, joints assembled, supports installed.
  - **PWHT completed** if required.
  - **Loose items removed** — temporary supports, packing.
  - **Vent + drain points** identified — at high points (vent air) + low points (drain).
  - **Test boundaries** — isolation valves closed lub blinded (don't test downstream equipment that isn't rated).
  - **Equipment exclusions** — rotating equipment (pumps, compressors), instrumentation (sensors), expansion joints often excluded from test pressure (lower rating).
- **Filling procedure**:
  1. **Open all vents** at high points (allows air escape).
  2. **Slowly fill from lowest point** — fluid rises naturally, no entrapment.
  3. **Fill rate** ~1-2 m/min liquid rise — too fast = air trapped.
  4. **Close vents** as water exits each one — final water-only pipe.
  5. **Vibrate pipe slightly** lub walk around tapping pipe — releases trapped air pockets.
- **Air entrapment** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Air trapped** → upon pressurization → air compresses → stored energy → upon failure, air drives water explosively.
  - **All vents must release water** before sealing.
  - **High-point automatic vents** — if installed.
- **Pressurization**:
  1. **Apply pressure** in stages — typically 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%, **125%, then 150%**.
  2. **Hold at each stage** — observation period (5-10 min) for leak detection.
  3. **Pressure rise rate** — ≤1 bar/min approaching test pressure (slow = controlled).
  4. **Test pressure** reached — hold for required time (10 min min, typically longer).
- **Chart recorder / pressure gauge**:
  - **Calibrated gauge** — within last 6 months minimum, certificate.
  - **Range**: test pressure 25-75% of gauge full-scale (best accuracy zone).
  - **Resolution**: minimum 0.1 × test pressure subdivisions.
  - **Two independent gauges** preferred — verify each other (single gauge can fail).
  - **Chart recorder** — continuous pressure + time graph, archived dla project record.
- **Leak detection methods**:
  - **Visual** — water dripping/wetness at joints. Primary method.
  - **Soap solution** at joints — bubbles indicate leak.
  - **Pressure decay** — gauge drops without obvious cause = leak somewhere.
  - **Ultrasonic leak detector** — for very small leaks (rare for hydro, more dla pneumatic).
- **Acceptance**:
  - **No visible leakage** at joints/welds.
  - **No permanent deformation** of pipe (verify with calibrated dimensional check).
  - **Pressure stable** w hold period — code allows small drop dla temperature drift compensation.
- **Failure scenarios**:
  - **Leak found** — drain, repair, re-test. Don't try to "tighten" valves at high pressure.
  - **Rupture** — drain immediately, root cause investigation, replace section.
  - **Gauge drift** w hold — calculate temperature compensation (water expands +0.21% per °C → pressure drops).
- **Blowdown / drain after test**:
  - **Slow depressurization** — open small vent, lower 1-5 bar/min.
  - **Drain water** — open low-point drains, allow gravity.
  - **Final dry** — air-blow lub N₂ for sensitive service (vacuum dry dla cryogenic, instrument air).
  - **Water disposal** — depends on contamination level (potable water OK to sewer; preservative-treated → industrial waste).
- **Special considerations**:
  - **Stainless steel + chloride water** — pickling/passivation required po test, lub use chloride-free water.
  - **Cold weather** — risk freeze damage during fill, heat-trace if -T.
  - **Subsea / buried pipelines** — modify procedure (account dla static head, soil pressure).
  - **High-strength steel** — yield-to-tensile ratio important, test pressure margin smaller.
- **Test documentation**:
  - **Test plan** — sketch, pressure stages, hold times, gauge calibration cert.
  - **Test record** — chart, observations, sign-offs.
  - **Witnesses**: contractor QC, owner inspector, insurance (sometimes).
  - **Certified by**: PE / Lead Mechanical Engineer.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Air not fully vented** — pressure spike during pressurization, possible failure.
  - **Wrong test pressure** — too low = doesn't validate; too high = damages.
  - **Equipment exposed to test pressure** — burst disks, expansion joints, low-rated valves.
  - **Wrong fluid** — water in alloy steel with chloride issue.
  - **No isolation** — adjacent system pressurized accidentally.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-test walkdown** — verify all hangers, supports, anchors, instrumentation as-built.
  - **Hammer test** — light tap z hammer at flange joints — listens for "dead" sound (good seal) vs "ringing" (loose).
  - **Soap bubble** before high-stage (low pressure leak detection).
  - **Communication** — radio z all team members during test (clear exclusion zone).
  - **Emergency depressurization plan** — visible vent valve everyone knows.

### GGGGGGGGGGG. Heat Exchanger Tube Bundle Pulling — Maintenance + Cleaning
- **Po co**: shell-and-tube heat exchangers fouled (process deposits, scale, biofilm) → need **shell-side cleaning** lub **tube replacement**. Bundle must be **extracted from shell** dla access.
- **Heat exchanger types** (recap dla context):
  - **Fixed tube sheet** — both tube sheets welded do shell, **CANNOT pull bundle** (only chemical clean shell side).
  - **U-tube** — bundle pulls od one end (channel side opens, bundle slides out).
  - **Floating head** — internal floating tubesheet, bundle pulls completely after removing floating head + shell cover.
  - **Removable bundle (TEMA P-T-U types)** — pullable dla maintenance.
- **Tube bundle puller (extractor)**:
  - **Cel**: pull heavy bundle (1-50+ tons) out of shell horizontally lub vertically.
  - **Konstrukcja**:
    - **Hydraulic ram** — force generator, 15-50 tons typical, stroke 3-6 m.
    - **Guide rails** — bundle slides on rollers, prevents binding.
    - **Frame** — supports puller, attached do exchanger shell flange.
    - **Pull head** — attached to floating tubesheet, distributes pulling force.
    - **Control panel** — remote-controlled hydraulics dla operator safety.
  - **Types**:
    - **Truck-mounted** — mobile, drive to location.
    - **Skid-mounted** — semi-permanent.
    - **Aerial / cherry picker mounted** — elevated exchangers.
    - **Hand-portable hydraulic** — small bundles.
- **Pulling sequence** (typowy floating head exchanger):
  1. **Process isolation + LOTO** — both shell + tube sides locked out.
  2. **Drain + flush** — water/steam flush, remove residual material.
  3. **Atmospheric test** confined space (channel head + shell).
  4. **Remove channel cover** + channel head — expose tubesheet, tube side.
  5. **Remove shell cover** (rear) — expose floating head assembly.
  6. **Disconnect floating head** od bundle (bolts).
  7. **Attach puller** — frame to shell flange, pull head to tubesheet.
  8. **Apply hydraulic force** — slow pull, observe alignment.
  9. **Extract bundle** — pull length = bundle length (3-6 m typically).
  10. **Transport bundle** to cleaning area (crane lift, dolly, rail).
- **Cleaning bundle** (after extraction):
  - **Shell side (outside tubes)**:
    - **High-pressure water jet** (10,000-15,000 psi) — removes most fouling.
    - **Chemical cleaning** — citric acid, EDTA dla scale; biocide dla biofilm.
    - **Mechanical** — wire brushes between tubes (limited access).
  - **Tube side (inside tubes)**:
    - **Hydroblast** with tube lances — 10,000-30,000 psi water rotating through each tube.
    - **Mechanical scrapers** — wire brushes pulled through.
    - **Chemical** — circulating cleaning solution.
- **Inspection during pull**:
  - **Visual** — corrosion, wear, broken tubes, baffle damage.
  - **Eddy current testing (ECT)** — internal tube inspection — detects cracks, corrosion, wear thinning.
  - **IRIS (Internal Rotating Inspection System)** — UT od inside, measures wall thickness.
  - **Hydrotest individual tubes** — leak detection (cap one end, pressurize, look dla bubbles).
- **Repair options**:
  - **Tube plugging** — cap failed tubes (max **10-15%** before bundle re-tube required).
  - **Tube replacement** — pull failed tubes, install new, expand into tubesheet.
  - **Re-tubing** — replace entire tube bundle (new tubes, reuse tubesheet if OK).
  - **Re-shelling** (rare) — replace entire shell.
- **Reinstallation**:
  1. **Inspect shell internals** — wash, remove debris.
  2. **Lubricate guide rails** — facilitate sliding.
  3. **Align bundle** carefully w shell entrance.
  4. **Push bundle** in z puller (reverse direction).
  5. **Install floating head** — re-bolt, gasket new.
  6. **Reinstall shell cover** + channel head.
  7. **Hydrostatic test** — verify integrity at design pressure.
  8. **Return to service**.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Pulling too fast** — binding, tubesheet damage.
  - **Misalignment** — bundle drags na shell wall, scratches tubes.
  - **No guide rails** — bundle drops, expensive repair.
  - **Skipped baffle inspection** — fouled baffles → poor heat transfer post-cleaning.
  - **Old gasket reused** — leak after reassembly.
  - **No tube expansion** verification w retubing — tubes loose at tubesheet.
  - **Operating off-design** — overworking exchanger leads to fouling, requires more frequent pulling.
- **Safety considerations**:
  - **Heavy lift** — bundle 5-50 tons → crane + spreader bar + rigging certification.
  - **Hydraulic pressure** — pull system 10,000+ psi → hose burst, blocked exit zone.
  - **Confined space** entry channel head + shell — covered iter 87.
  - **Chemical exposure** — residual process material on bundle (especially refinery: H₂S, asphaltenes, carcinogens).
  - **PPE**: hard hat, FRC suit, respirator, eye protection, gloves.
- **Bundle puller failure modes**:
  - **Hydraulic seal leak** — loss of pull capacity.
  - **Rail binding** — bundle stops, requires patience + repositioning.
  - **Tubesheet failure** — pull head connection breaks → bundle drops back.
  - **Hose burst** — high-pressure release, evacuate.
- **Maintenance frequency**:
  - **Heavy fouling service**: every 1-2 years (cooling water, sour water).
  - **Moderate**: every 3-5 years.
  - **Clean service**: 5-10 years (oil-oil, clean gas).
  - **Performance-driven** — monitor ΔP + thermal performance, pull when degraded >15%.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-extraction planning** — bundle weight, length, lifting points, transport route.
  - **Spare parts inventory** — gaskets, bolts, expansion joints, tubes (if retubing).
  - **Crane availability** — schedule heavy lift dla bundle removal.
  - **Cleaning facility** — high-pressure washer, chemical containment, water disposal.
  - **Tube ECT inspection** — done during turnaround, plan dla 1-2 days.
  - **Documentation** — bundle history (pulls, repairs, plugged tubes), trend over time dla replacement planning.
  - **Cathodic protection** dla buried/marine exchangers — verify CP working before reinstall.

---

## Iteration 89 — 2026-05-26

### HHHHHHHHHHH. WPS / PQR / WPQ Documentation — ASME IX Welder Qualification
- **Co to**: dokumentacyjny system **qualifying welding procedures + welders** dla pressure piping/vessel work. ASME IX (Section IX of Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) jest USA standard; EU equivalent EN ISO 15614.
- **3 kluczowe dokumenty**:
  1. **WPS (Welding Procedure Specification)** — instrukcja jak spawać dla welder.
  2. **PQR (Procedure Qualification Record)** — record qualifikacji tej procedury (raz, dokument the proof).
  3. **WPQ (Welder Performance Qualification)** — test każdego welder demonstrating skill on this WPS.
- **WPS** zawiera:
  - **Process** (SMAW, GTAW, GMAW, FCAW, SAW).
  - **Base materials** (P-Number group, material spec, grade).
  - **Filler metal** (F-Number, classification SFA 5.X, electrode dimensions).
  - **Position** (1G, 2G, 5G, 6G — pipe positions covered).
  - **Joint design** (groove type, root opening, bevel angle).
  - **Welding parameters** (current type/polarity, amperage, voltage, travel speed, heat input range).
  - **Preheat + interpass T** ranges.
  - **PWHT requirements** (T, time, ramp rates).
  - **Gas** (shielding gas type, flow rate, back purge).
  - **Technique** (string vs weave bead, pass count).
- **PQR** dokumentuje:
  - **Test weld coupon** — specific pipe + specific WPS, welded under controlled conditions.
  - **Mechanical tests** results:
    - **Tensile test** — verify joint strength ≥ base metal.
    - **Bend test** (transverse + longitudinal) — verify ductility.
    - **Notch toughness** (Charpy V-notch) — if specified, dla low T service.
    - **Hardness test** (Vickers HV10) — w HAZ, weld metal, base — verify limits.
  - **Macro-etch** — cross-section inspection — verify joint penetration, no defects.
  - **NDT** — RT/UT na coupon dla integrity.
  - **All results pass** = PQR signed by responsible engineer (PE).
- **Essential variables** — change wymaga **requalification**:
  - **P-Number change** (base metal group).
  - **F-Number change** (filler metal classification).
  - **Thickness range** (qualified range based on coupon thickness):
    - **Coupon T < 1.5 mm** → range 0.5T to 2T.
    - **1.5-10 mm** → range 1.5 mm to 2T.
    - **>10 mm** → range 1.5 mm to 200 mm.
  - **Welding process** change.
  - **Position** change (some).
  - **Preheat / PWHT** outside ranges.
  - **Heat input** (some processes).
- **Non-essential variables** — change OK, no requalification:
  - **Joint design details** (within scope).
  - **Cosmetic appearance** (within tolerances).
  - **Diameter** (no limit dla groove welds).
  - **Cleaning method**.
- **Supplementary essential variables** (gdy notch toughness required):
  - Additional essential variables (heat input, preheat, PWHT range narrower).
- **WPQ (Welder Performance Qualification)**:
  - **Test coupon** — welder welds standard plate/pipe per WPS.
  - **Visual + bend test** typical (lub RT).
  - **Pass = qualified** dla:
    - **Same process** (SMAW only OR GTAW only) — each separate.
    - **F-Number range** (filler group).
    - **Position range** (e.g., 6G test qualifies all positions).
    - **Thickness range** (similar to PQR).
    - **Diameter range** (small pipe test does NOT qualify large pipe in many cases).
  - **Renewal**:
    - **Continuity log** — welder must weld with that process **every 6 months** to stay qualified.
    - **Otherwise** — re-qualification test required.
    - **Quality issues** (3 failed welds in 6 months) — re-qualification.
- **Pipe positions** (welder qualification ranges):
  - **1G** — pipe horizontal, rotated, weld flat. Easy. Qualifies 1G only.
  - **2G** — pipe vertical, weld horizontal. Qualifies 1G + 2G.
  - **5G** — pipe horizontal, fixed, weld all positions. Qualifies 1G, 2G, 5G.
  - **6G** — pipe at 45°, fixed, weld all positions. Qualifies **ALL pipe positions** + plate.
  - **6GR (restricted)** — z restriction ring → simulates real-world access challenges. Highest qualification.
- **P-Numbers** (base metal grouping):
  - **P1**: Carbon steel — A36, A106 Gr B, A53, X42-X70 pipeline.
  - **P3**: Cr-Mo (P11, P22) — 1.25Cr-0.5Mo, 2.25Cr-1Mo.
  - **P4**: Higher Cr-Mo (P5, P9, P91).
  - **P5A/B**: Cr-Mo-V (P91 originally P5B).
  - **P8**: Austenitic SS (304, 316, 321).
  - **P10H**: Duplex SS (2205, 2507).
  - **P34**: Nickel alloys.
  - **P41**: Aluminum.
- **F-Numbers** (filler metal grouping):
  - **F1-F4**: SMAW electrodes.
  - **F5**: SMAW alloy (low-H).
  - **F6**: GTAW + GMAW solid wire CS.
  - **F22**: GTAW SS.
  - **F45**: GMAW SS.
- **Documentation flow**:
  1. **Engineering writes draft WPS** based on production needs.
  2. **Test coupon welded** per draft WPS.
  3. **Coupon tested** (mechanical + NDT).
  4. **All pass** → PQR signed.
  5. **WPS finalized** + reference to PQR.
  6. **Welder tested** per WPS → WPQ issued.
  7. **Production welding** under WPS by qualified welders.
  8. **Continuity records** maintained.
- **WPS approval responsibility**:
  - **Authorized Inspector (AI)** — third-party (insurance) for ASME-stamped vessels.
  - **PE (Professional Engineer)** — signs PQR for code applications.
  - **Quality Control / QA Manager** — internal sign-off.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Welder using WPS outside qualified range** (e.g., 5G qualified, attempts 6G work).
  - **Expired continuity** — welder welds w out-of-date qualification.
  - **Wrong filler** — welder grabs incorrect electrode, weld fails dla wrong P-number.
  - **Heat input outside WPS** — improper amperage/voltage logged.
  - **Missing essential variables** w WPS — code rejection.
  - **Material substitution** without WPS update — different grade requires new qualification.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **WPS posted at workstation** — easy reference, no memorizing.
  - **Welder qualifications check** — production foreman verifies daily.
  - **WPS-WeldID matching** — each weld assigned to specific WPS.
  - **Audit ready** — paper trail from base material certs → WPS → welder ID → NDT records → all linked.
  - **Software MES**: WPS lookup, welder qual database, weld log automated.
  - **Cross-training**: welders should have multiple WPS qualifications dla flexibility.

### IIIIIIIIIII. Hot Work Permit + Fire Watch — NFPA 51B / Refinery Procedures
- **Co to "hot work"**: operacja generująca **open flame, spark, heat** capable of ignition:
  - **Welding, cutting, brazing, soldering**.
  - **Grinding** (sparks).
  - **Torch-applied roofing**.
  - **Thawing frozen pipe** z propane torch.
  - **Spark-producing tools** — drilling, chipping w hazardous area.
  - **Open flame heating** dla bending, thawing.
- **NFPA 51B** (Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work) — USA primary standard.
  - **EU equivalent**: BS 9999, EN ISO 17672.
- **Hot work permit system**:
  - **Permanent designated areas** (welding shop) — hot work allowed without per-job permit.
  - **All other locations** — **WRITTEN HOT WORK PERMIT REQUIRED**.
  - **Permit validity**: typowo **8 hour shift**, single location, single task.
- **Permit components**:
  - **Location** — specific area, equipment, line number.
  - **Task description** — what hot work, duration.
  - **Hazard identification** — flammable materials w 35 ft radius, atmosphere.
  - **Pre-work conditions** — checklist (cleaned, ventilated, sources isolated).
  - **Fire watch** — name, equipment, duration.
  - **Atmospheric tests** — gas test results (LEL, O₂, toxic).
  - **PPE requirements**.
  - **Communication** — radio channel, supervisor contacts.
  - **Sign-offs**:
    - Permit issuer (operations supervisor).
    - Permit acceptor (work crew leader).
    - Authorizing officer (often plant engineer).
    - Fire watch acknowledgment.
- **35-foot rule** (NFPA 51B):
  - **Radius 35 ft (10.7 m)** around hot work point — all flammable materials must be:
    - **Removed** (relocated outside zone).
    - **Protected** (fire-resistant covers, welding blankets).
    - **Shielded** (welding curtains, screens).
  - **Floor** — combustible flooring → wetted lub covered.
  - **Walls/ceilings** — combustible → wetted lub covered.
  - **Floor drains** — covered (prevent sparks into sewer z flammable vapors).
- **Atmospheric testing** before hot work:
  - **LEL <10%** (some refineries <5%) — flammable gas concentration.
  - **O₂ 19.5-23.5%** — normal range.
  - **Toxic gases** — H₂S, CO, NH₃ within limits.
  - **Test multiple levels** — vapors stratify (heavy gases at floor, light at ceiling).
  - **Continuous monitoring** during work — gas detector worn by welder or attendant.
  - **Stop work** immediately if alarm triggers.
- **Equipment isolation** before hot work na live system:
  - **LOTO (Lockout-Tagout)** — energy isolation.
  - **Blind flanges / spectacle blinds** — positive isolation z process.
  - **Drain + flush** — remove residual hydrocarbon, H₂S, ammonia.
  - **Steam/N₂ purge** — displace flammable atmosphere.
  - **Cooling** — pipe wall T <50°C ideally (auto-ignition concern).
- **Fire watch**:
  - **Role**: continuous observation dla fire/spark ignition.
  - **NEVER welder himself** (welder's hand busy, eyes on weld).
  - **Equipped**:
    - **Fire extinguisher** (ABC dry chem or CO₂, minimum 20 lb).
    - **Fire hose / water spray** if available.
    - **Radio** — communicate evacuation.
    - **Atmospheric monitor**.
  - **Trained** — fire extinguisher use, alarm procedure.
  - **Duration**:
    - **During hot work** — full attention, no other duties.
    - **Post-work** — minimum **30 min** continued observation (smoldering can ignite delayed).
    - **High-risk environments** — extend **60 min or longer** (refinery, near process equipment).
- **Pre-hot work checklist**:
  - ✓ Work area cleaned + flammables removed/protected.
  - ✓ Floor wetted (if combustible).
  - ✓ Drains covered.
  - ✓ Fire extinguishers in place.
  - ✓ Atmospheric tests passed.
  - ✓ Equipment isolated + LOTO verified.
  - ✓ Permit signed all parties.
  - ✓ Fire watch positioned + equipped.
  - ✓ Emergency procedures briefed.
- **During hot work**:
  - **Continuous atmospheric monitoring**.
  - **Fire watch attention** — no distractions.
  - **Welding curtain** — protects others od arc + spatter.
  - **Sparks contained** — drip pan below cutting area.
  - **Periodic permit re-check** — atmospheric tests every 30-60 min.
- **Post-work**:
  - **30+ min fire watch** — continued observation.
  - **Inspect spatter zones** — wetted/cooled.
  - **Permit closeout** — sign by all parties.
  - **Equipment removed** — tools, hoses, materials.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Permit expired** — work continues past shift, no renewal.
  - **Fire watch leaves** to perform other tasks — fatal mistake.
  - **Skipped atmospheric test** — explosion w "clean" area not tested.
  - **Wrong fire extinguisher** — Class A water on flammable liquid = spread fire.
  - **Welding inside vessel without confined space + hot work permits** — multiple violations.
  - **Drain not covered** — sparks fall through into hazardous area below.
- **Refinery-specific hazards**:
  - **Adjacent process units** — H₂S, hydrocarbons mogą migrate.
  - **Sewer drains** — historical incidents (sparks fall in sewer, ignite vapors, blast back through plant).
  - **Insulation** — oil-soaked insulation = combustible (covered iter 86 — CUI similar).
  - **Pipe contents not fully drained** — pocket of hydrocarbon → BLEVE possible.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Fire watch trained annually** — extinguisher hands-on practice.
  - **Multiple permits** for complex jobs (hot work + confined space + LOTO + working at heights).
  - **Permit limits** — single hot work permit doesn't allow other hot work nearby.
  - **Welding screens / blankets** stocked — quick deployment.
  - **Spark arrestor** on grinders w hazardous zone.
  - **Wind direction** — fire watch upwind od work area.
  - **Lessons learned database** — review past incidents.

### JJJJJJJJJJJ. Hydrogen Pipeline Transport — ASME B31.12, X52 Material Limit
- **Po co**: rosnąca **hydrogen economy** — H₂ jako carrier energy, decarbonization. Currently small (~5000 km pipeline globally), future estimate **10,000-100,000 km** w next decades.
- **Aplikacje**:
  - **Petrochemical** — H₂ supply do refineries, ammonia plants (existing infrastructure).
  - **Fuel cell vehicles** — distribution to refueling stations.
  - **Power-to-gas** — excess renewable electricity → electrolysis → H₂ → pipeline storage/transport.
  - **Industrial heat** — replace natural gas w steel, cement, glass production.
  - **Blending z natural gas** — early transition (5-20% H₂ mix).
- **ASME B31.12** (Hydrogen Piping and Pipelines):
  - **2 parts**:
    - **Part GP (Gaseous H₂)** — gas hydrogen piping (similar to B31.3 process).
    - **Part PL (Pipeline)** — gas hydrogen pipeline (similar to B31.8 natural gas).
  - **Coverage**: H₂ piping/pipelines w industrial, commercial, distribution.
  - **2 design approaches**:
    - **Prescriptive** — conservative material, design factors, limits.
    - **Performance-based** — extensive testing, allows higher-grade materials.
- **Hydrogen embrittlement mechanism** (recap iter 80):
  - **Atomic H** absorbed into steel at high P → migrates do **stress concentrations** (notch, weld toe, crack tip).
  - **Reduces ductility** + **fracture toughness** → cracks propagate at lower stresses than expected.
  - **Worse for high-strength steels** — yield >550 MPa more susceptible.
- **Material limits (B31.12)**:
  - **Maximum proven grade dla hydrogen service**: **X52 / L360** (yield ≤359 MPa / 52 ksi).
  - **X60, X65, X70** — NOT proven dla pure H₂ — require performance-based testing + qualification.
  - **Cast iron**: PROHIBITED dla H₂ service.
  - **Low-carbon CS** (A106 Gr B, X42): generally OK z proper procedures.
- **Pipe grade implications**:
  - **Natural gas pipelines** używają **X65-X80** (modern long-distance).
  - **Converting to H₂** wymaga downgrade material lub extensive qualification.
  - **Existing infrastructure**: blending z natural gas (5-20% H₂) OK z existing X65/X70; pure H₂ wymaga upgrade.
- **Welding requirements** (B31.12):
  - **Welder qualification** — separate WPS dla H₂ service.
  - **Hardness limit**: **22 HRC max** (= ~250 HV10) — soft welds, less susceptible HE.
  - **Low-H electrodes** — E7018-H4 minimum (H content <4 mL/100g).
  - **PWHT** — generally required dla welds, even w lower strength steel.
  - **Preheat** — minimum 80°C dla H₂ service (vs 0-50°C dla natural gas typical).
  - **NDT** — 100% volumetric (RT or UT) + 100% surface (MT/PT).
- **Joint design**:
  - **Full penetration welds** — no partial-penetration.
  - **Smooth weld profile** — grind smooth, no stress concentrators.
  - **Avoid undercut** — particularly damaging w H₂ service.
  - **No backing rings** — crevice corrosion + H₂ trap site.
- **Bolting** w H₂ service:
  - **High-strength bolts** (>1240 MPa SMTS) — PROHIBITED (HE susceptible).
  - **B7 studs** (105-125 ksi SMTS) — OK with care.
  - **A193 B8M (SS)** — acceptable dla some applications.
- **Pressure design**:
  - **Location class** — same as B31.8:
    - **Class 1**: rural, low density.
    - **Class 2**: medium density.
    - **Class 3**: high density, urban.
    - **Class 4**: special — populated, near critical facilities.
  - **B31.12 explicit**: **all pipelines with material SMYS >358 MPa** = Location Class 4 (highest restriction).
  - **Design factor (F)**: 0.50 (Class 1) do 0.40 (Class 4) — conservative.
  - **vs natural gas B31.8**: F = 0.72 (Class 1) — much higher allowable.
- **Hardness limits**:
  - **Base metal HV10**: <250.
  - **Weld + HAZ HV10**: <250.
  - **Bolts**: <22 HRC.
  - **Verify z field hardness tester** during installation + inspection.
- **System pressure** considerations:
  - **Transmission**: 50-100 bar typical (vs natural gas 70-100 bar).
  - **Distribution**: 5-50 bar.
  - **Refueling stations**: 350 bar dla buses/trucks, 700 bar dla cars.
- **Operational issues**:
  - **Leak detection** — H₂ flame INVISIBLE (no carbon = no luminosity), small molecule = leaks easily through seals/threads.
    - **H₂ specific sensors** — IR thermal cameras detect flame.
    - **Helium leak detector** — used dla pinpoint detection.
    - **Ultrasonic leak detection** — high-frequency hiss.
  - **Storage** — geologic (salt cavern) lub above-ground (tube trailers, type IV composite tanks).
- **Inspection requirements**:
  - **Frequency**: every 1-3 years dla critical service (vs 5-10 years natural gas).
  - **Methods**: PAUT + ECT (eddy current) + hydro test + leak detection.
  - **Hardness mapping** — periodic verify no degradation.
- **Material upgrade paths**:
  - **Existing X65/X70 line for H₂ service** — typically derated lower (60-70% original MAOP).
  - **New construction X52 (Grade B / L360)** — proven, B31.12 compliant.
  - **Future**: HSLA z special chemistry (low S, controlled Mn, low impurity) testing dla H₂ qualification.
- **Cost implications**:
  - **H₂ pipeline costs**: **1.5-3× natural gas** dla same diameter + capacity:
    - Lower strength material → thicker walls.
    - Stricter NDT + inspection.
    - Higher safety factor (lower design factor F).
    - More PWHT requirements.
  - **Existing pipeline conversion** może być economical dla blending, not pure H₂.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Strict cleanliness** — no oil/grease (incompatible z H₂, fire hazard).
  - **PMI verification** — every component, dla material control.
  - **Strict torque control** — H₂ leaks through smallest gaps.
  - **Metal-to-metal seals** preferred over elastomers (H₂ permeates O-rings).
  - **Welder qualification specific** — H₂ service WPS, B31.12 compliant.
  - **Hardness testing in field** — verify weld + HAZ <250 HV.
  - **Cathodic protection** — H₂ entry via cathodic disbondment possible, monitor CP voltage carefully.
  - **Inspection access** — design with PI launchers/receivers (intelligent pigging).
  - **Documentation** — material traceability paramount, audit trail z mill cert do installed component.

---

## Iteration 90 — 2026-05-26

### KKKKKKKKKKK. Tank Truck Loading Rack + LACT Unit — Custody Transfer
- **Po co**: terminale, refinerie, lease oil fields wymagają **automated metering + sampling** dla transferu paliw / crude oil do trucks / barges / pipelines. **Custody transfer** = legal change of ownership → high accuracy + audit trail required.
- **Tank truck loading rack** — large infrastructure dla loading tank trucks (tankers):
  - **Petroleum terminals** — gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, crude oil distribution.
  - **Refineries** — finished product loading.
  - **Chemical plants** — bulk chemicals.
  - **Throughput**: 10-50 trucks/day przy mid-size terminals.
- **2 loading methods** — top vs bottom:
  - **Top loading** (older method):
    - Operator climbs do top truck.
    - Lowered loading arm inserted do open dome of truck.
    - **Hazards**: fall risk, vapor exposure, splash loading w air.
    - **Mostly phased out** USA dla flammable products (lightning, vapor displacement).
  - **Bottom loading** (modern standard):
    - Loading arm connects do **bottom coupler** truck.
    - **API valve** (4" poppet, spring-loaded) na truck + matching adapter loading arm.
    - **No personnel on top** — safer.
    - **Vapor recovery** możliwe (sealed system).
    - **Standardy**: **API RP 1004** (recommended practice).
- **API bottom-loading components**:
  - **API truck valve** (poppet, 4" Ø):
    - **Vapor port** + **liquid port**.
    - **Self-closing** spring-loaded — closes if coupler disconnected (safety).
    - **Materials**: aluminum body, brass internals, Buna-N seals (compatible z gasoline/diesel).
  - **Loading arm** (terminal side):
    - **API dry-break coupler** — mates z truck valve.
    - **Loading arm articulated** — 4 swivel joints dla positioning, 3-6 m reach.
    - **Counterbalanced** — operator can easily lift/maneuver.
    - **Auto-disconnect** if truck moves while connected (breakaway feature).
- **Vapor recovery system** (VRU):
  - **Cel**: capture displaced vapor (gasoline VOCs, BTEX) — reduce emissions + comply EPA.
  - **Mechanism**: as liquid loads, vapor pushed out → through return line do **VRU unit**.
  - **VRU types**:
    - **Carbon adsorption** — vapor adsorbed na activated carbon, regenerated z vacuum.
    - **Refrigerated condensation** — chill vapor, condense, return liquid.
    - **Membrane separation** — modern, selective permeation.
  - **EPA limits** — typically <80 mg VOC/L loaded fuel dla bulk terminals.
- **Overfill prevention**:
  - **Sensors w truck** — float lub thermistor based.
  - **Cable connection** terminal → truck — verify sensor type + truck ID.
  - **Auto-shutoff** at 95% full (truck-specific).
  - **OPW Civacon** — popular USA brand sensors.
- **LACT unit (Lease Automatic Custody Transfer)**:
  - **Aplikacja**: **lease metering** at oil + gas wells, custody transfer to pipeline / truck (lease operator → midstream company).
  - **Components**:
    - **Strainer** + **air eliminator** — clean fluid before meter.
    - **Sampler** — automatic composite sampling dla BS&W (Basic Sediment & Water) analysis.
    - **Meter** — Coriolis (mass) lub turbine (volumetric) — high accuracy custody-grade.
    - **Prover loop** — calibration verification — small piston-prover lub master meter.
    - **Flow computer** — calculates net volume corrected (T, P, density, BS&W).
    - **Diverter valve** — automatic divert if BS&W exceeds limit (off-spec) → recycle back to tank.
    - **Block + bleed valves** — isolate dla maintenance + leak test.
  - **Accuracy requirements**:
    - **±0.25%** typically dla custody transfer.
    - **Calibration frequency**: weekly proving (master meter prover) + quarterly full audit.
    - **Witnessing** by both buyer + seller representatives.
- **Loading sequence** — bottom loading:
  1. **Truck arrives** — driver checks in, weight ticket.
  2. **Static check** — truck grounded (clamp to grounded conductor), verify <10 Ω.
  3. **Drive into loading bay** — overhead clearance verified.
  4. **Manual valve sequence** — verify truck side valves OPEN, terminal side starts CLOSED.
  5. **Loading arm lowered** + connected do bottom coupler.
  6. **Verify overfill sensor connection** — cable plugged in.
  7. **Vapor recovery hose** connected (if required).
  8. **Pre-load test** — system pressurized briefly, leak check.
  9. **Loading commences** — flow rate ramped up 200-600 GPM typically.
  10. **Monitor flow** — supervised by terminal operator + driver.
  11. **Auto-shutoff** at sensor signal (95% full) lub manual stop at target volume.
  12. **Drain residual** od loading arm.
  13. **Disconnect** loading arm + vapor recovery.
  14. **Documentation** — ticket printed (volume, T, density, BS&W).
  15. **Truck exits** — final weight check (overweight detection).
- **Materials**:
  - **Piping**: **CS galvanized** (gasoline/diesel) lub **SS 316** (special chemicals).
  - **Loading arms**: aluminum (light) z brass/SS internals.
  - **Hoses (occasional)**: composite (Trelleborg, Continental) — fuel resistant.
  - **Gaskets**: NBR (Buna) standard, **NIE EPDM** dla gasoline (swells).
- **Safety + electrical**:
  - **Class I Div 1** electrical area classification (NEC) dla bottom loading rack.
  - **Static grounding** verified each loading.
  - **Lightning protection** — surge arrestors, bonded structure.
  - **Fire suppression** — foam system lub deluge over loading bay.
  - **Spill containment** — concrete sloped floor, drain to oil-water separator.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong product** loaded do truck — labeling + verification critical.
  - **Vapor recovery disconnected** — EPA fines.
  - **Static charge** — not grounded properly, ignition.
  - **Overflow** — sensor failure + slow operator → tank vent spills.
  - **Hot fuel** loading — wax precipitation risk (jet fuel, diesel).
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Walk-around inspection** before loading — verify hoses, valves, sensors.
  - **Pressure relief vents** on truck tanks — prevent collapse during unloading vacuum.
  - **Anti-siphon valves** — prevent backflow truck → terminal pipe.
  - **Drip pans** below loading arms — catch dribbles.
  - **Annual calibration** flow meters — custody transfer accuracy.
  - **Training**: drivers + operators per OSHA HazMat + DOT requirements.

### LLLLLLLLLLL. Eddy Current Testing (ECT) — Heat Exchanger Tube Inspection
- **Co to**: NDT method using **electromagnetic induction** — coil generates magnetic field → induces eddy currents w conductive material → defects perturb currents → measured signal change indicates defect.
- **Główne aplikacje**:
  - **Heat exchanger tube inspection** (cleaning tubes during turnaround) — primary use.
  - **Aircraft inspection** — fatigue cracks w skin.
  - **Conductivity sorting** — material grade verification.
  - **Surface defects on flat plates**.
- **Why important for heat exchangers**:
  - **Hundreds-thousands of tubes** w large exchanger — manual inspection impractical.
  - **Tube failures** w service → process contamination, cooling water leaks.
  - **Pre-emptive replacement / plugging** based on ECT findings.
- **Probe types**:
  - **Bobbin (encircling)** — ring coil that fits inside tube, moves axially. **Most common**.
    - **Differential bobbin** — two coils nearby, subtract signal → measures CHANGE w tube wall.
    - **Absolute bobbin** — single coil, measures total wall.
    - **Speed**: 0.3-1 m/s typical inspection speed.
  - **Rotating probe** (RPC — Rotating Pancake Coil) — small surface coil that rotates while moving axially.
    - **Better detection** dla:
      - Tube-tubesheet joints (high stress, cracking).
      - U-bend area (most stressed).
      - Pitting + cracks specific orientations.
    - **Slower** niż bobbin — used selectively.
  - **Array probe** — multiple coils w 360° array, single pass coverage.
    - **No rotation needed**.
    - **Faster than RPC**.
- **ECT vs RFT (Remote Field Testing)**:
  - **ECT**: works dla **non-ferromagnetic** tubes (304/316 SS, Cu, brass, Inconel, Ti, Al). NOT effective for ferromagnetic tubes (carbon steel).
  - **RFT (Remote Field Testing)**: works **dla ferromagnetic** tubes (CS, ferritic SS):
    - Uses 2 coils — transmitter + receiver — signal propagates through tube wall, around tube, back.
    - Wall thickness changes affect signal.
    - **Less sensitive** dla pitting, but good dla general wall loss.
  - **MFL (Magnetic Flux Leakage)** — alternative dla ferromagnetic tubes, fast, detects pitting.
  - **IRIS (Internal Rotating Inspection System)** — ultrasonic, gives wall thickness directly, all materials.
- **Defects detected** (ECT):
  - **Pitting** — localized corrosion, visible jako sharp signal spike.
  - **General wall thinning** — uniform corrosion.
  - **Stress corrosion cracking (SCC)** — surface-breaking cracks.
  - **Fatigue cracks** — initiates at U-bend, vibration.
  - **Steam-side erosion** — high-velocity steam wear, baffle cuts.
  - **Tubesheet defects** — cracks at tube-to-tubesheet joint.
  - **Dents** — mechanical damage.
- **Signal interpretation**:
  - **X-Y plot** (impedance plane) — vector display amplitude + phase.
  - **Phase angle** indicates depth of defect:
    - **External pit** — different phase od internal pit.
    - **Through-wall hole** — distinct signature.
  - **Amplitude** indicates **defect volume**.
  - **Calibration** z reference tube z drilled holes / EDM notches.
- **Calibration standard**:
  - **ASTM E309** (tube ECT).
  - **Reference tube** same material + size as inspection tubes, w machined defects:
    - **20% wall loss flat-bottom hole** through-wall.
    - **40% + 60% + 80%** dla full depth calibration curve.
- **Inspection workflow**:
  1. **Heat exchanger out of service** — drained, isolated.
  2. **Tube cleaning** — hydroblast inside tubes (10,000-30,000 psi) — removes scale, gives clean ECT signal.
  3. **Probe selection** — bobbin first (fast screen), RPC follow-up dla questionable areas.
  4. **Calibration** — confirm signal on reference defects.
  5. **Inspection** — probe pushed/pulled through tubes, signals recorded.
  6. **Data analysis** — software classifies indications.
  7. **Reporting** — tube-by-tube findings, recommend actions.
- **Tube actions** based on findings:
  - **Acceptable** (<20% wall loss) — return to service.
  - **Monitor** (20-40% wall loss) — re-inspect next turnaround.
  - **Plug** (>40-50% wall loss lub crack) — cap both ends, take out of service.
  - **Replace** — if plugging too many tubes, retubing whole bundle.
- **Plug types** (for failed tubes):
  - **Mechanical plug** — taper plug + ferrule expanded inside tube end.
  - **Welded plug** — disc welded over tube end (high pressure / high T service).
  - **Explosive plug** — ferrules sealed w controlled explosive charge (specialty).
- **Common errors**:
  - **Dirty tubes** — interpret false defects od scale.
  - **Wrong probe diameter** — too tight (won't enter) or too loose (false signals).
  - **Calibration off** — sizes defects incorrectly.
  - **Speed too fast** — small defects missed.
  - **Operator interpretation** — skill-dependent (more experience = better detection).
- **Reporting deliverables**:
  - **Tubesheet maps** — color-coded by defect severity per tube.
  - **Statistics**: % tubes good, marginal, failed.
  - **Trend over time** — multiple turnarounds graphed (degradation rate).
  - **Recommendations** — plug, monitor, replace, retubing schedule.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Pre-cleaning critical** — invest dla 100% pre-clean → reliable ECT.
  - **Sequence planning** — ECT after pull, before reassembly (access).
  - **Spare plugs** dla on-site plugging during inspection.
  - **NDT contractor** — third-party ECT specialist, experienced operator.
  - **Software**: Olympus MIZ, Eddyfi Magnifi, Russell NDE Ectane.
  - **Inspection frequency**: turnaround cycle 2-7 years, ECT 100% tubes każda turnaround.

### MMMMMMMMMMM. CO₂ Transport Pipeline — Dense-Phase + Supercritical CCS
- **Po co**: **CCS (Carbon Capture and Storage)** — captured CO₂ od power plants, cement/steel/refineries → transported via pipeline → injected do **geological storage** (depleted oil reservoirs, deep saline aquifers). Decarbonization strategy. **Forecast**: 10,000-50,000 km CO₂ pipelines globally w 2050.
- **CO₂ phases** w pipeline:
  - **Gas phase** — low P (<5 bar), low density, expensive transport (large diameter pipes dla mass flow).
  - **Liquid phase** — between triple point (5.18 bar @ -56.6°C) + critical point (73.8 bar @ 31°C). Higher density, ale temperature-restricted.
  - **Dense phase / Supercritical** — **>73.8 bar + >31°C** — **NOT liquid, NOT gas** — single fluid phase, **density jak liquid (700-900 kg/m³)**, **viscosity jak gas (low pressure drop)**.
  - **Optimum dla pipeline**: **dense phase**, P = 86-150 bar, T = 4-40°C.
- **Pressure-T phase diagram**:
  - **Critical point**: **73.8 bar, 31.1°C** — w wyższym P+T = supercritical.
  - **Triple point**: **5.18 bar, -56.6°C** — solid + liquid + gas coexist.
  - **Below triple T**: solid CO₂ (dry ice) forms.
  - **Above critical T**: only supercritical (no liquid/gas distinction).
- **Operating conditions** typowo:
  - **Inlet P**: 100-150 bar.
  - **Outlet P (booster station)**: 80-90 bar (above critical 73.8 bar).
  - **T range**: ambient (cold-climate may add heaters).
  - **Velocity**: 1-3 m/s w dense phase.
- **Pipe materials**:
  - **Most common**: **API 5L X65 / X70 carbon steel**.
  - **Pure dry CO₂** (<50 ppm water): **minimal corrosion** — CS adequate.
  - **Wet CO₂** (>500 ppm water): **carbonic acid (H₂CO₃)** formation → highly corrosive → need SS lub corrosion inhibitor.
  - **Dehydration critical**: typical spec **<30 ppm H₂O** (well below water dew point at operating conditions).
- **Impurities — major issue**:
  - **CO₂ captured rarely pure** — has impurities z capture process:
    - **H₂O** — corrosion (most critical impurity).
    - **N₂** — inert, OK.
    - **O₂** — reacts z SO₂/NO₂ → strong acids w water phase.
    - **SO₂, NO₂** — acid formation → very corrosive.
    - **H₂S** (od flue gas / refinery CO₂) — sulfide stress cracking risk.
    - **Hydrocarbon (HC)** — phase separation issues.
  - **Phase behavior shift** — impurities **lower critical P, raise dew point** → may unintentionally enter 2-phase region.
  - **Tighter specs needed** dla high-impurity streams.
- **Corrosion mechanism**:
  - **Dry CO₂**: corrosion <0.01 mm/year (effectively zero).
  - **Wet CO₂**:
    - **H₂O + CO₂ → H₂CO₃** (carbonic acid).
    - **pH drops** to 3-4 w aqueous phase.
    - **Local rate**: 1-10 mm/year possible.
    - **Pitting + general corrosion**.
  - **Mitigation**:
    - **Dehydration first** (TEG dehydration unit upstream — covered iter 77).
    - **CRA (Corrosion Resistant Alloy)** — 13Cr SS dla wet CO₂ service.
    - **Inhibitor injection** — film-forming amines.
- **Design standards**:
  - **ASME B31.4** — Liquid + Slurry transportation pipelines (CO₂ liquid + dense phase).
  - **ASME B31.8** — gas pipelines (CO₂ gas phase).
  - **DNV-RP-F104** — Design + operation CO₂ pipelines.
  - **ISO 27913** — Pipeline transportation systems dla CCS.
- **Welding requirements**:
  - **Similar to natural gas pipelines** dla dry CO₂ (B31.4 / B31.8).
  - **Material grade**: X65/X70 acceptable dla CCS service generally.
  - **PWHT**: typically not required dla CS pipelines z wall thickness <20 mm.
  - **Strict cleanliness** — no moisture (kompozyt z CO₂ catalyzes corrosion).
- **Operations specifics**:
  - **Phase management**: keep pressure ABOVE critical (>73.8 bar) at all times — avoid 2-phase flow.
  - **Booster stations**: at intervals 100-300 km, repressurize.
  - **JT (Joule-Thomson) cooling** dla blowdown — CO₂ expands → T drops dramatically → frost / dry ice formation:
    - **Triple point T = -56.6°C** — solid forms.
    - **Avoid rapid depressurization** — controlled blowdown.
    - **Snow / ice scale** formation possible w blowdown lines → blockage.
- **Safety considerations**:
  - **Asphyxiant** — heavier than air, accumulates w low areas.
  - **5% CO₂** — significant impairment.
  - **>10%** — unconsciousness w minutes.
  - **30%** — death.
  - **CO₂ leak** can form **vapor cloud** — invisible, no odor → silent hazard.
  - **CO₂ detection** — IR sensors (CO₂-specific).
- **Historical incidents**:
  - **Satartia, MS 2020** — Denbury pipeline rupture, hydrate plug + freeze formed during shutdown, 200+ evacuated, 45 hospitalized z CO₂ exposure.
  - **Lessons**: dehydration + temperature control + impurity limits.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wet CO₂** w CS pipe → catastrophic corrosion w 6-12 months.
  - **Phase transition** during operation → 2-phase flow → erosion + slug damage.
  - **Cold blowdown** w 1 stage → freeze + plug.
  - **Wrong material** dla impurity content.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Dehydration** before pipeline entry — TEG lub molecular sieve.
  - **Monitor water content** continuously — hygrometer.
  - **Multi-stage blowdown** — controlled T management.
  - **Booster station design** — heaters, separators dla phase control.
  - **Material selection per impurity envelope** — CO₂ purity spec critical.
  - **Pigging** dla cleaning + inspection (smart pigs, MFL/UT).
  - **Cathodic protection** — buried pipeline standard practice.
  - **Right-of-way monitoring** — leak detection (drones, fiber optic distributed sensing).
  - **Emergency response training** — operators z CO₂ vapor cloud scenarios.

## Iteration 91 — 2026-05-26

### NNNNNNNNNNN. NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 — Sour Service Material Selection
- **Po co**: gas/oil z **H₂S** (sour gas, sour crude) wywołuje **sulfide stress cracking (SSC)** w wielu metalach. **NACE MR0175** (USA, AMPP) + **ISO 15156** (international, harmonized) — primary standard dla **material selection w sour service**.
- **Po co osobny standard**: standard piping codes (B31.3, B31.4) NIE adresują H₂S-specific corrosion. NACE/ISO **dodaje wymagania** ponad standard codes.
- **3 części dokumentu**:
  - **MR0175 Part 1** — General principles dla materials selection.
  - **MR0175 Part 2** — Carbon + low alloy steels.
  - **MR0175 Part 3** — Corrosion-resistant alloys (CRA) — SS, Ni alloys, Co alloys.
- **Sour service threshold** (NACE / ISO definition):
  - **H₂S partial pressure ≥ 0.05 psi (0.3 kPa)** in gas phase OR
  - **H₂S concentration ≥ 1.45 ppm in liquid water** (saturated condition).
  - Below threshold → standard procedures OK.
  - Above → MR0175 compliance required.
- **Damage mechanisms** w sour service:
  - **SSC (Sulfide Stress Cracking)** — brittle fracture od **atomic H** absorbed during corrosion (H₂S→ H⁺ → H⁰). Worst dla **high-strength + high-hardness** materials.
  - **HIC (Hydrogen-Induced Cracking)** — atomic H accumulates at inclusions (MnS, oxide) → forms H₂ gas → blister, internal crack.
  - **SWC (Step-Wise Cracking)** — multiple HIC cracks link up → through-wall.
  - **SOHIC (Stress-Oriented HIC)** — HIC oriented perpendicular do applied stress.
- **Hardness limits** — najważniejszy element compliance:
  - **Carbon + low-alloy steels**: **max 22 HRC (≈250 HV10, ≈237 HBW)**.
  - **W welds + HAZ**: same limit max 22 HRC.
  - **Reasoning**: harder = mniej ductile + więcej residual stress + H trapping → susceptible SSC.
- **Material restrictions**:
  - **Carbon steel**:
    - **Ni content max 1%** (Ni promotes hardness).
    - **PWHT required** dla welds — relieves residual stress + reduces hardness.
    - **Hardness 22 HRC max** including welds.
  - **Stainless steel 316/316L**:
    - Compliance NIE automatic — wymaga **hardness ≤22 HRC** w wetted condition.
    - **Cold work limited** (cold work raises hardness).
    - **NACE-stamped** material certification recommended.
  - **Duplex SS (2205, 2507)**:
    - Acceptable z restrictions on partial pressure, T, chloride.
    - **Hardness limit ~28-32 HRC** (alloy-dependent).
  - **Inconel (Alloy 718, 825, 625)**:
    - Generally OK przy strict hardness control.
    - **Alloy 718 high-strength**: special procedure (precipitation hardened state).
  - **Cu/Brass**: ZABRONIONE dla wet H₂S (galvanic + cracking).
  - **High-strength bolts (>1240 MPa)**: ZABRONIONE.
- **Bolting w sour service**:
  - **B7 studs + 2H nuts**: standard CS, OK do specific H₂S partial pressures.
  - **B7M studs**: lower hardness variant B7, broader H₂S tolerance.
  - **L7 + L7M**: low-T versions.
  - **B8M (SS 316)**: acceptable, hardness controlled.
  - **B16 (Cr-Mo)**: NIE dla sour (high hardness possible).
  - **Stamping**: bolts marked "NACE MR0175" lub batch certified.
