## Iteration 100 — 2026-05-26 (FINAL)

### OOOOOOOOOOOO. Job Hazard Analysis (JHA / JSA) — Pre-Task Safety Process
- **Co to**: **step-by-step systematic review** of job → identify hazards → develop preventive controls. **JHA (Job Hazard Analysis)** + **JSA (Job Safety Analysis)** są używane **interchangeably** (OSHA preferred "JHA", many companies say "JSA").
- **OSHA reference**: **Publication 3071** — primary guidance dokument.
- **Po co JHA/JSA**:
  - **Prevent injuries + incidents** — proactive identification hazards.
  - **OSHA compliance** — many standards require z safety procedures.
  - **Training tool** — new workers learn risk-aware approach.
  - **Permit-to-work component** — JHA attached do hot work, confined space, energized work permits.
  - **Insurance + legal** — defensible documentation if incident.
- **JHA process** — 4 steps:
  1. **Select job + define scope** — specific task, not vague "maintenance".
  2. **Break job into steps** — sequential, manageable activities.
  3. **Identify hazards** at each step.
  4. **Develop controls** dla each hazard.
- **Step breakdown example** (pipe welding):
  - **Pre-job**: prepare worksite, gather tools, review permit.
  - **Material prep**: cut pipe, bevel ends, clean surfaces.
  - **Fit-up**: position pipe, install backing if needed, tack weld.
  - **Welding**: ground machine, set parameters, weld root, weld fill, weld cap.
  - **Post-weld**: NDT inspection, painting, cleanup.
  - **Each step** evaluated dla hazards + controls.
- **Hazard identification** categories:
  - **Physical**:
    - **Arc radiation** (welding) — UV burns eyes/skin.
    - **Heat / burns** — hot metal, sparks.
    - **Falling objects** — overhead work.
    - **Sharp edges** — cut hazards.
    - **Lifting + manual handling** — back injuries.
    - **Noise** — hearing damage.
    - **Vibration** — long-term nerve issues.
  - **Chemical**:
    - **Welding fumes** — Mn, Cr (VI), Ni — chronic exposure.
    - **Solvents** — degreasers, paints.
    - **Process fluids** — exposure during line opening.
  - **Electrical**:
    - **Shock** — welder, tools, machinery.
    - **Arc flash** — electrical equipment work.
  - **Ergonomic**:
    - **Awkward postures** — pipe welding overhead, in trenches.
    - **Repetitive motion** — torch operation, grinding.
  - **Environmental**:
    - **Heat stress** — outdoor summer work.
    - **Cold** — outdoor winter.
    - **Confined space**.
    - **Atmospheric** (O₂, toxic, flammable).
  - **Biological** (less common piping):
    - **Bird droppings** — outdoor piping cleaning.
    - **Sewage exposure** — wastewater work.
- **Risk evaluation**:
  - **Probability**: low / medium / high.
  - **Severity**: minor / moderate / major / catastrophic.
  - **Risk matrix** → priority dla controls.
- **Hierarchy of controls** (most effective first):
  1. **Elimination** — remove hazard entirely (e.g., relocate work).
  2. **Substitution** — replace z safer alternative (e.g., low-toxicity solvent).
  3. **Engineering controls** — physical barriers, ventilation, automation.
  4. **Administrative controls** — procedures, training, permits.
  5. **PPE** — last resort, doesn't eliminate hazard.
- **JHA worksheet** structure:
  - **Job title** + description.
  - **PPE required** (overall job).
  - **Tools / equipment**.
  - **Step-by-step** table:
    - Step number + description.
    - Hazards identified.
    - Controls (engineering + admin + PPE).
    - Responsible person.
  - **Sign-offs** — supervisor + workers.
  - **Date + revision history**.
- **Pre-task brief** ("toolbox talk"):
  - **5-15 min** at job site before starting work.
  - **JHA review** z all crew members.
  - **Questions + concerns** discussed.
  - **Sign-in sheet** — proof of attendance.
- **Living document**:
  - **Update** when:
    - **Job conditions change**.
    - **Near-miss** lub incident.
    - **New equipment** / procedure.
    - **Annual review** minimum.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Too generic** — JHA dla "maintenance" useless; needs specific task.
  - **Hazards missed** — incomplete identification.
  - **Controls vague** — "be careful" instead of specific actions.
  - **No follow-through** — JHA written, never reviewed at job site.
  - **Workers excluded** — supervisor writes alone, no input.
  - **Outdated JHA** — pre-existing, never updated.
- **Stop work authority** (SWA):
  - **Any worker** can halt job if safety concern identified.
  - **NO retribution** — encouraged proactive intervention.
  - **Re-assess JHA** if SWA invoked.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Field-level JHA review** — verify conditions match plan.
  - **Cross-functional input** — operations, safety, engineering all contribute.
  - **Simple language** — workers in different languages should understand.
  - **Photos lub sketches** — visual aids enhance comprehension.
  - **Integrate z permits** — hot work, confined space, LOTO permits reference JHA.
  - **Post-job review** — what went well, what could improve, update JHA.
  - **Lessons learned database** — incidents → JHA updates company-wide.

### PPPPPPPPPPPP. Tank Level Gauging — Radar, Servo, Hydrostatic, Magnetostrictive
- **Po co**: storage tanks (atmospheric, pressurized, cryogenic) require **accurate level measurement** dla:
  - **Inventory management** — how much product on hand.
  - **Custody transfer** — legal change of ownership (high accuracy needed).
  - **Overfill prevention** — safety against tank rupture / spill.
  - **Process control** — feed forward to downstream operations.
- **API 2350** — overfill protection standard dla atmospheric storage tanks.
- **Accuracy classes**:
  - **Operational gauging**: ±5-10 mm acceptable.
  - **Inventory**: ±2-5 mm.
  - **Custody transfer**: **±1 mm** lub better (high accuracy).
- **5 main technologies**:
  - **Float + tape** (mechanical):
    - **Float on liquid surface**, connected via tape do indicator gauge.
    - **Cheapest, oldest** technology.
    - **Accuracy**: ±5-10 mm.
    - **Wear** + maintenance issues.
    - **Aplikacje**: old installations, dropped from modern designs.
  - **Servo gauge**:
    - **Small displacer** (10-25 cm) hangs od precision motor + measurement wire.
    - **Motor positions displacer** at liquid surface using buoyancy balance.
    - **Accuracy**: ±1 mm (custody transfer capable).
    - **Multi-function**: density measurement, interface detection.
    - **Producent**: Honeywell Enraf, Endress+Hauser, Emerson Rosemount.
    - **Aplikacje**: refinery tanks, large product storage.
    - **Limitations**: moving parts → maintenance.
  - **Radar level (non-contact)**:
    - **Microwave pulse** emitted od antenna, reflects od liquid surface.
    - **Time-of-flight** (TOF) lub **FMCW (Frequency Modulated CW)** calculates distance.
    - **Accuracy**: ±2-3 mm typical, ±1 mm w specialized custody version.
    - **No moving parts** — high reliability.
    - **Wide T + P range**.
    - **Works** dla foams, mists, vapors.
    - **Doesn't contact product** — clean, no contamination.
    - **Aplikacje**: chemicals, refinery, food/pharma.
    - **Producent**: Emerson, Endress+Hauser, Vega, Krohne.
  - **Guided Wave Radar (GWR)**:
    - **Probe extends into tank** — wave travels along probe, reflects off liquid.
    - **More accurate dla**: condensing vapors, foam, agitated surface.
    - **±2-3 mm accuracy** typical.
    - **Probe types**: single rod, twin rod, cable (long tanks).
    - **Aplikacje**: chemicals, applications gdzie open radar fails.
  - **Hydrostatic** (DP transmitter):
    - **Pressure at bottom** = ρ·g·h (density × gravity × height).
    - **Single DP transmitter** lub two transmitters (bottom + vapor space).
    - **Accuracy**: ±2-5 mm — limited by density assumptions.
    - **Density compensation** required dla variable temperatura.
    - **Wetted** — process contact, maintenance issues.
    - **Aplikacje**: process tanks, less critical dla custody.
  - **Magnetostrictive**:
    - **Float z permanent magnet** travels along probe.
    - **Probe**: stainless tube containing wire.
    - **Magnetic pulse** sent down wire; magnet position creates pulse → return time measured.
    - **Accuracy**: **±0.1 mm** (highest precision dla custody transfer).
    - **Multi-product**: oil + water interface, dual measurements.
    - **Aplikacje**: precision custody transfer, petroleum terminals.
    - **Producent**: K-TEK (now ABB), MTS (Sensors).
- **Selection criteria**:
  - **Accuracy needed** → magnetostrictive (best) lub servo dla custody.
  - **Product compatibility** → radar dla aggressive media, magnetostrictive dla wetted OK.
  - **Tank height** → radar dla tall tanks (no probe length limit).
  - **Cost** → hydrostatic / radar most economical; magnetostrictive premium.
  - **Maintenance access** → non-contact (radar) preferred dla difficult-access.
- **Tank gauging system** components:
  - **Level transmitter** — primary measurement device.
  - **Temperature sensor** (RTD multi-point) — calculates average T, density compensation.
  - **Pressure sensor** — vapor space P (dla density calc).
  - **Density measurement** — sometimes inline densitometer.
  - **Flow computer / RTU** — combines all data, calculates net standard volume.
- **Custody transfer measurement** components:
  - **Level + temperature + density + pressure** — all measured.
  - **Net Standard Volume (NSV)** computed:
    - Gross observed volume.
    - Corrected dla temperature (referenced 15°C / 60°F).
    - Corrected dla pressure.
    - Corrected dla density.
  - **API 11.1 tables** dla petroleum product correction.
  - **Independent verification** — buyer + seller representatives witness.
- **Overfill protection** (API 2350):
  - **Independent high-high level switch** — separate from level gauge.
  - **Triggers ESD** — close inlet valves automatically.
  - **Visual + audible alarms** — at multiple locations.
  - **Operator response procedure** — clearly defined actions.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong technology** dla service — foamy products miss radar reflection.
  - **No regular calibration** — drift over time.
  - **Vapor effects** ignored — radar dielectric variation z product changes.
  - **Insufficient overfill protection** — single-failure path → tank overflow.
  - **No temperature compensation** — density variation w large temperature swings.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Calibration** annually minimum dla custody transfer.
  - **Reference measurement** — hand dip z calibrated tape periodically.
  - **Mounting location** — center of tank (radar), avoid disturbances (agitator, inlet).
  - **Antenna selection** (radar) — beam angle suits tank geometry.
  - **Probe length** (GWR, magnetostrictive) — must clear tank bottom (no contact).
  - **Heat trace** dla cold-climate installations — prevents seizure.
  - **Lightning protection** — surge arrestors at all instrument cables.

### QQQQQQQQQQQQ. Reactor Catalyst Loading + Unloading — Refinery Procedure
- **Po co**: refinery + petrochemical reactors zawierają **solid catalyst beds**:
  - **Fixed bed**: catalyst pellets stacked w reactor vessel.
  - **Fluidized bed**: catalyst suspended w gas flow (FCC, covered iter 95).
  - **Moving bed**: catalyst flows slowly through reactor (CCR reformer).
- **Periodic catalyst replacement**:
  - **Hydroprocessing**: 2-4 year cycles.
  - **Reforming**: 6-12 month cycles dla semi-regenerative.
  - **FCC**: continuous (catalyst circulates, fines purged).
- **Loading + unloading**: critical activities — directly affects reactor performance, run length, product quality.
- **Reactor types** (fixed bed):
  - **Down-flow** (most common): feed enters top, exits bottom.
  - **Up-flow**: feed enters bottom (less common).
  - **Radial flow**: feed flows radially through catalyst (CCR, semi-regen reformers).
- **Catalyst bed dimensions**:
  - **Diameter**: 1-5 m.
  - **Height**: 5-25 m.
  - **Catalyst volume**: 10-200 m³ per reactor.
  - **Multiple beds** (interspersed z quench injection): 2-5 beds typical hydroprocessing.
- **Loading methods**:
  - **Sock loading** (traditional):
    - **Catalyst poured** from hopper at top manway.
    - **Through canvas / rubber "sock"** — slows fall, prevents impact damage.
    - **Manual labor** — workers spread catalyst inside reactor.
    - **Pros**: cheap equipment.
    - **Cons**:
      - **Lower density** (10-15% lower than dense loading).
      - **Channeling risk** — uneven distribution.
      - **Worker exposure** inside reactor.
    - **Aplikacje**: smaller reactors, simple applications.
  - **Dense loading** (modern, preferred):
    - **Mechanical device** at top distributes catalyst uniformly.
    - **Rotating arms** lub **spreader plates**.
    - **Higher bed density** — more catalyst per volume.
    - **Better distribution** — no channeling.
    - **Reduced gas bypass** — more efficient reaction.
    - **Vendors**: CRI Catalyst Co., Albemarle, Petroval.
    - **Performance benefit**: 5-10% longer cycle length, 10-15% better activity utilization.
- **Pre-loading preparation**:
  - **Catalyst inspection** — visual check dla damage, fines.
  - **Catalyst sieving** — remove fines (<1 mm) which create high ΔP.
  - **Inert support balls** loading — at bottom + intermediate (graded layers).
  - **Internal cleaning** — vessel cleaned od previous catalyst residue.
  - **Distributor inspection** — verify spray pattern, no clogs.
- **Loading process**:
  1. **Bottom inert support balls** loaded (sized graded — large at bottom).
  2. **Bottom catalyst layer** (sometimes inert / guard layer).
  3. **Main catalyst beds** (multiple beds separated by support trays + quench injection lances).
  4. **Top support balls** — protect against feed distributor disturbance.
  5. **Feed distributor / quench distributor inspection** post-loading.
- **Inert atmosphere** — KRYTYCZNE:
  - **Nitrogen purge** during loading + unloading.
  - **Reason**: most catalysts are **pyrophoric** when activated (spontaneously ignite w air).
  - **Oxygen <1%** target dla safe entry.
  - **Continuous N₂ flow** prevents air ingress.
- **Personnel inside reactor**:
  - **SCBA / supplied air** required (inert atmosphere — no oxygen).
  - **Buddy system** + attendant outside.
  - **Specialty contractors** (CAT Tech Services, CRI, Petroval) — trained personnel.
  - **NIOSH approved breathing equipment**.
- **Loading rate**:
  - **Manual sock**: 1-2 m³/h per worker.
  - **Dense loading**: 5-15 m³/h.
  - **Big reactors**: 24-72 hours total loading time.
- **Post-loading**:
  - **Activation / sulfiding** — converts oxide catalyst to active sulfide form (for hydroprocessing).
  - **H₂S + H₂** mixed gas circulated.
  - **Controlled T ramp** — sulfides form properly.
  - **24-48 hours** typical activation.
- **Unloading methods**:
  - **Gravity dump** (most common):
    - **Bottom unloading nozzle / flange** opened.
    - **Catalyst falls by gravity** to receiver below.
    - **Spent catalyst** typically pyrophoric → **N₂ purge essential**.
    - **Receiver**: bin truck OR rail car z N₂ blanket.
  - **Vacuum unloading**:
    - **Industrial vacuum trucks** (specialized) suck catalyst od top entry.
    - **Used** dla:
      - Reactors without bottom drain.
      - Stuck / fused catalyst.
      - Top-entry-only access.
    - **Higher cost** vs gravity dump.
  - **Combination**: gravity dump bulk, vacuum dla residual.
- **Catalyst disposal**:
  - **Spent catalyst** contains:
    - **Heavy metals** (Mo, Ni, V od process) — recoverable.
    - **Sulfur compounds** — environmental concern.
    - **Carbon (coke)** — burned off OR sent to recycle.
  - **Recovery vendors**: Gulf Chemical, Eurecat, Albemarle — recycle metals + regenerate.
  - **Hazardous waste handling** — RCRA regulations USA.
- **Common errors**:
  - **Wrong loading method** dla service — sock used where dense needed.
  - **Channeling** — uneven distribution → poor reaction, hot spots.
  - **Inert atmosphere lost** — air ingress → fire + catalyst damage.
  - **Wrong catalyst position** — dla layered catalyst systems.
  - **Wet catalyst** — moisture damages activity.
  - **Personnel oxygen deficiency** — confined space + N₂ atmosphere fatalities.
- **Practical fitter rules**:
  - **Specialty contractor** — typically external company performs loading/unloading.
  - **Continuous N₂ supply** — verified by O₂ analyzer.
  - **Confined space permit** — extensive procedures (covered iter 87).
  - **Pre-loading planning** — drawings, support balls layout, catalyst arrangement.
  - **Operator coordination** — startup procedure tied do loading completion.
  - **Documentation** — loaded volumes, sieve fractions, time, conditions logged dla turnaround report.
  - **Long lead times** — catalyst ordered 6-12 months ahead, regenerated vs fresh option.

---

## PĘTLA BADAWCZA UKOŃCZONA — 100/100 ITERACJI ZREALIZOWANYCH

Plik `piping_knowledge.md` zawiera teraz **kompletną wiedzę monterską + spawalniczą** zebraną w 100 iteracjach od:

- **Iter 1-30**: podstawy spawania, ISO drawing, hot tapping, hydrotest, GTAW, pipe supports, sour service, NDT podstawy, alloy welding, refractory anchor, FRP/GRE, plastic fusion, weld defects.
- **Iter 31-70**: dissimilar metals, ultrasonic flowmeter, Coriolis, RTD/TC, expansion joints, PSV, slug catcher, demin water, hydraulic ISO 6149, VRU, plate HE, anti-galling, refinery drains, drip legs, foot valves, refrigerant piping, water backwash, pulsation dampener, pickling, spectacle blind, hot oil, pig launcher.
- **Iter 71-99**: HVAC refrigerant + oil traps, water-treatment, pump priming + NPSH, pulsation dampener + ASME PCC-1, expansion loops, spectacle blind, hot oil/Therminol, pig launcher + QOC, instrument impulse lines + manifolds, steam tracing, pipeline repair sleeves, P91/P22 alloy welding, NH₃ piping IIAR, FBE/3LPE coating, CSO/LO valves, methanol/MEG, PTFE lined pipe, SAW, pipe rack, TEG dehydration, AIV/FIV, HF alkylation Monel, sanitary CIP/SIP, PVC/CPVC solvent cement, spring hangers, HDD drilling, plant air, restriction orifice, hydrogen API 941, amine treater, fire water NFPA 24, pneumatic conveying, API 650 tank nozzles, subsea pipelay, ASME A13.1 labels, hot bolting + SS back-purge dams, cooling tower piping, pump min-flow ARV, pipe bending, slurry pipeline, compressor anti-surge, H₂SO₄ piping, vortex breaker, welder PPE, spool prefabrication, radiographic testing, PAUT, pipe insulation, confined space entry, MT+PT NDT, hydrostatic test, tube bundle pulling, WPS/PQR/WPQ, hot work permit, H₂ pipeline B31.12, LACT tank loading, eddy current ECT, CO₂ CCS pipeline, NACE MR0175, Ti/Zr welding, ASME B31 family, SMR reformer, PSSR/MOC, water hammer, SIL/ESD valves, tank PV vent, BFW boiler, distillation tower, TEMA exchanger, vacuum tower piping, pipeline leak detection, FCC piping, geothermal pipelines, PMI alloy verification, metal bellows, SAGD oil sands, SWS sour water stripper, line stop/freeze plug, RBI API 580/581, frac surface piping, FGD scrubber, pipe shoes MSS SP-58, eccentric reducer, drone UAV inspection, weld bevel V/J/U.
- **Iter 100 (final)**: Job Hazard Analysis (JHA/JSA), tank level gauging (radar/servo/magnetostrictive), reactor catalyst loading/unloading.

**Plik finalny: ~270 KB, ~12,000+ linii notatek roboczych dla monterów + spawaczy rurociągów.**
